chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Module 1: Introduction to Computer (10 Hours)

Course: CCC 90-Hour DCA | Module duration: 10 hours

1. Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Explain what a computer is and why computers are important in modern life.
  • Describe the input-process-output-storage cycle with real examples.
  • Identify common applications and types of computers.
  • Explain the evolution of computers through five generations.
  • Recognize modern IT gadgets such as smartphones, tablets, IoT devices, printers, scanners, and storage devices.
  • Identify major hardware components such as CPU, RAM, ROM, motherboard, input devices, output devices, and storage devices.
  • Differentiate hardware, software, firmware, data, information, file, and folder.
  • Understand basic memory units and number systems used in computers.
  • Connect, start, use, and shut down a computer safely.
  • Apply basic digital safety practices such as password safety, malware awareness, and data backup.
  • Prepare for CCC, DCA, PGDCA, NSQF, and beginner-level computer examinations.

2. Topic Explanation

2.1 Introduction to Computers

A computer is an electronic device that accepts input, processes it according to instructions called programs, stores data when required, and produces meaningful output. In simple words, a computer helps us convert raw facts into useful results.

For example, when a student enters marks of five subjects into a spreadsheet, the marks are input. The spreadsheet formula calculates total and average, which is processing. The saved file is storage. The displayed result or printed report is output.

Computers are important because they perform tasks with speed, accuracy, storage capacity, and consistency. A human may take several minutes to calculate hundreds of marks, but a computer can do it within seconds. Computers also help people communicate, learn, manage accounts, create documents, access government services, and control machines.

Basic Working Cycle of a Computer

Input -> Processing -> Storage -> Output
              |
           Memory
  • Input: Data or instructions given to a computer. Examples: typing text, clicking a mouse, scanning a document, touching a mobile screen.
  • Processing: The CPU works on the input according to software instructions. Examples: calculating marks, opening a file, converting typed letters into a document.
  • Memory: Temporary working area used while processing. RAM is the main temporary memory.
  • Storage: Long-term saving of files and programs. Examples: HDD, SSD, pen drive, memory card.
  • Output: Result produced by the computer. Examples: text on monitor, printed certificate, sound from speaker, downloaded PDF.

Why This Concept Is Important

The input-process-output-storage model is the foundation for understanding all computer systems. Whether you use a desktop, laptop, smartphone, ATM, railway ticket machine, or hospital billing system, the same basic cycle is followed.

Types of Computers

Type Description Main Use Examples
Microcomputer Small computer for one user Home, office, school, shop Desktop, laptop, tablet
Workstation Powerful single-user computer Design, engineering, media editing CAD workstation
Server Computer that provides services to many users Websites, LMS, banking, file sharing Web server, database server
Mainframe Very large and powerful computer Large organizations and high-volume processing Banks, railways, insurance
Supercomputer Extremely high-speed computer Weather, research, AI, simulations PARAM, Fugaku, Frontier
Embedded computer Computer built into a device Control and automation ATM, washing machine, smart TV

Historical Perspective

Early calculating devices such as the abacus and mechanical calculators performed limited arithmetic. Modern electronic computers developed when electricity, electronic circuits, and stored programs were introduced. Today, computers have become smaller, faster, cheaper, and connected to the internet. Future computers will use more artificial intelligence, cloud computing, quantum computing research, and automation.

Common Misconceptions

  • The CPU is not the full computer cabinet. The CPU is a processor chip inside the system unit.
  • A computer is not useful only for typing. It is used for communication, data analysis, design, banking, learning, entertainment, and automation.
  • More storage does not always mean faster performance. Performance depends on CPU, RAM, storage speed, software, and operating system.

Did You Know?

Modern smartphones often have more processing power and storage than many early room-sized computers.

2.2 Applications of Computers

Computers are used in almost every field because they can store large data, process information quickly, and connect people across distances.

Major Application Areas

Field How Computers Are Used Examples
Education Online classes, digital notes, exams, LMS, smart classrooms Google Classroom, LMS, e-books
Banking Account management, ATM, UPI, online banking, fraud detection UPI, net banking, passbook printing
Healthcare Patient records, lab reports, telemedicine, pharmacy billing e-Hospital, online appointments
Government Certificates, identity services, welfare schemes, digital payments DigiLocker, Aadhaar, UMANG
Retail Billing, barcode scanning, inventory, online orders POS machine, e-commerce
Manufacturing Machine control, quality checking, inventory, robotics CNC machines, automation
Communication Email, video calls, messaging, social media Gmail, WhatsApp, Zoom
IT Industry Software development, websites, apps, cybersecurity, cloud Programming, databases, testing

Real-Life Examples

  • Daily life: Paying through UPI, booking railway tickets, watching educational videos, storing photos.
  • Workplace: Preparing salary sheets, sending emails, managing customer records, creating presentations.
  • Education: Attending online classes, submitting assignments, taking quizzes.
  • Government services: Downloading certificates from DigiLocker, applying for scholarships, checking land records.

Scenario-Based Learning

A student needs to attend an online class, save notes, and submit an assignment. The student uses a smartphone or laptop as hardware, an operating system to run the device, a video meeting app for class, a word processor for notes, internet connection for communication, and storage for saving the file.

2.3 Evolution of Computers: Five Generations

Computer generations explain how computer technology changed over time. Each generation is mainly identified by the electronic technology used inside computers.

Generation Period Main Technology Features Examples
First Generation 1940s-1950s Vacuum tubes Very large, high heat, high electricity, slow ENIAC, UNIVAC
Second Generation 1950s-1960s Transistors Smaller, faster, more reliable IBM 1401
Third Generation 1960s-1970s Integrated Circuits (ICs) Multiprogramming, cheaper, more compact IBM System/360
Fourth Generation 1970s-present Microprocessors, VLSI Personal computers, GUI, networking Intel PCs, laptops
Fifth Generation Present and future AI, ULSI, parallel processing Smart systems, voice assistants, robotics AI systems, supercomputers

First Generation: Vacuum Tubes

Vacuum tubes acted like electronic switches. These computers were very large, produced heat, consumed much electricity, and were difficult to maintain. They were mainly used by governments, military, and research institutions.

Second Generation: Transistors

Transistors replaced vacuum tubes because they were smaller, faster, cheaper, and more reliable. This made computers more practical for business and scientific work.

Third Generation: Integrated Circuits

An Integrated Circuit (IC) places many electronic components on a small chip. This reduced size and cost. Computers became more reliable and could run multiple programs more effectively.

Fourth Generation: Microprocessors

A microprocessor places the CPU on a single chip. This led to personal computers, laptops, graphical user interfaces, and widespread computer use in schools, offices, homes, banks, and shops.

Fifth Generation: Artificial Intelligence

Fifth-generation computing focuses on artificial intelligence, natural language processing, machine learning, robotics, voice recognition, and intelligent decision-making. Examples include chatbots, recommendation systems, smart assistants, fraud detection systems, and autonomous vehicles.

Memory Trick

Vacuum Tube -> Transistor -> IC -> Microprocessor -> AI

Mnemonic: Very Talented Indians Make Advances

Exam Tip

Fifth-generation computers are related to AI and intelligent systems. This is different from 5G mobile network, which is a communication technology.

Common Mistakes

  • Do not confuse 5G mobile with fifth-generation computers.
  • Do not write "internet" as the main technology of fourth-generation computers. The key technology is the microprocessor.
  • Do not say vacuum tubes are modern. They belong to the first generation.

Did You Know?

ENIAC occupied a large room and consumed huge electricity, while today a small smartphone can perform many tasks faster.

2.4 IT Gadgets and Mobile Computing

An IT gadget is an electronic device used for computing, communication, storage, learning, or digital services. Mobile computing means using portable devices to access information and services from different locations.

Smartphones

A smartphone is a pocket-sized computer with a touchscreen, processor, memory, camera, speaker, microphone, GPS, sensors, internet access, and mobile operating system such as Android or iOS.

Smartphones are used for calling, messaging, online classes, UPI payments, railway booking, DigiLocker, telemedicine, learning apps, maps, photography, and email. Mobile apps are application software installed from app stores.

Tablets

A tablet is a portable touchscreen device larger than a smartphone and lighter than most laptops. Tablets are useful for reading e-books, attending video classes, writing notes with a stylus, watching tutorials, and showing presentations.

IoT Devices

IoT means Internet of Things. It refers to everyday objects connected to the internet so they can collect data, send information, or be controlled remotely.

Examples include smart bulbs, CCTV cameras, fitness bands, smart TVs, home assistants, smart meters, and GPS trackers. IoT devices usually need internet, sensors, and mobile apps.

Other Common Gadgets

Gadget Function Real-Life Use
Laptop Portable computer Study, office work, programming
Pen drive Portable storage Carry documents and photos
External HDD/SSD Large backup storage Backup videos, projects, records
Webcam Video input Online classes, video meetings
Headset Audio input/output Calls, recordings, online learning
Printer Hard copy output Certificates, bills, notes
Scanner Converts paper to digital form ID proof, old photos, application forms

Smartphone vs Tablet

Point Smartphone Tablet
Definition Portable mobile computing device mainly for communication Larger touchscreen computing device for reading and media
Size Small and pocket-friendly Larger screen
Main Purpose Calls, messaging, apps, payments Reading, learning, presentations, drawing
Advantage Easy to carry everywhere Better screen for study and videos
Disadvantage Small screen for long work Less portable than phone
Examples Android phone, iPhone iPad, Samsung Galaxy Tab

Safety Guidance for Mobile Devices

  • Use screen lock, PIN, password, or biometric lock.
  • Install apps only from trusted stores.
  • Do not share OTP, UPI PIN, ATM PIN, or passwords.
  • Keep operating system and apps updated.
  • Avoid unknown links and fake reward messages.
  • Back up important photos and documents.

Career Awareness

Understanding mobile devices helps in careers such as mobile app developer, computer operator, digital service assistant, hardware technician, customer support executive, and cybersecurity assistant.

Did You Know?

Many government services are now designed as mobile-first services because smartphones are more common than desktop computers in many areas.

2.5 Components of a Computer System

A computer system is made of hardware, software, data, users, and procedures. Hardware alone cannot work without software, and software needs hardware to run.

Visual Learning: Information Flow

Keyboard/Mouse/Scanner
        |
        v
Input -> CPU Processing -> Output
        |        |             |
        |        v             v
        |      Memory       Monitor/Printer/Speaker
        v
      Storage

If you type a letter in a word processor:

  1. The keyboard sends input.
  2. The CPU processes the key press.
  3. RAM temporarily stores the open document.
  4. The monitor shows the typed letter.
  5. The SSD or hard disk stores the saved file.

Hardware

Hardware means the physical parts of a computer that can be seen and touched.

Component Meaning Function Examples
CPU Central Processing Unit Processes instructions Intel Core, AMD Ryzen
RAM Random Access Memory Temporary working memory 4 GB, 8 GB, 16 GB
ROM Read Only Memory Stores boot instructions BIOS/UEFI chip
Motherboard Main circuit board Connects all components Desktop motherboard
Storage Permanent data storage Saves files and programs HDD, SSD, pen drive
Input Devices Send data to computer Enter text, image, sound Keyboard, mouse, scanner
Output Devices Show result from computer Display, print, audio Monitor, printer, speaker
VDU Visual Display Unit Screen output LCD, LED monitor

Software

Software is a set of programs and instructions that tell hardware what to do. Software cannot be touched physically, but it controls the computer.

Type Definition Purpose Examples
System Software Software that controls hardware and runs the computer Manages resources Windows, Linux, Android
Application Software Software used for user tasks Documents, browsing, design Word processor, browser, spreadsheet
Utility Software Software used for maintenance and protection Clean, backup, protect Antivirus, disk cleanup
Firmware Software stored in hardware Start and control device BIOS/UEFI, router firmware

Hardware vs Software

Basis Hardware Software
Definition Physical parts of a computer Programs and instructions
Can be touched? Yes No
Purpose Provides physical working parts Controls and uses hardware
Damage Can break physically Can become corrupted or infected
Examples Keyboard, CPU, monitor, printer Windows, LibreOffice, Chrome

System Software vs Application Software

Basis System Software Application Software
Meaning Runs and manages the computer Helps users complete specific tasks
Runs When Usually starts with computer Starts when user opens it
Dependence Needed for application software Depends on system software
Examples Windows, Linux, device drivers Word, Excel, browser, VLC

Common Mistakes

  • Software is not the same as a document file. A document is created using software.
  • A monitor is output hardware; it does not process data.
  • The cabinet is not the CPU. The CPU is a chip inside the cabinet.

Did You Know?

Firmware is why a computer can begin the startup process before Windows or Linux fully loads.

2.6 Central Processing Unit (CPU)

The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is the main processing component of a computer. It reads instructions, performs calculations, makes decisions, and coordinates other parts of the system. It is often called the brain of the computer because almost every action requires CPU involvement.

Main Parts of CPU

Part Full Form Function
ALU Arithmetic Logic Unit Performs arithmetic and logical operations
CU Control Unit Directs and controls operations
Registers Small high-speed memory Stores data temporarily inside CPU

How CPU Works

When you open a file, the operating system sends instructions to the CPU. The CPU fetches the instructions, decodes what needs to be done, executes the operation, and sends results to memory, storage, or output devices.

CPU Speed and Performance

CPU speed is often measured in GHz (gigahertz). A higher GHz may mean faster processing, but performance also depends on:

  • Number of cores
  • Processor generation
  • Cache memory
  • RAM capacity
  • Storage speed
  • Operating system and software efficiency

Real-Life Use

  • Opening apps and websites
  • Running spreadsheets
  • Playing educational videos
  • Editing photos or videos
  • Processing bank transactions
  • Running billing software in shops

Exam Tip

CPU has three important parts: ALU, CU, and Registers. Remember: A Computer Runs = ALU, Control Unit, Registers.

Did You Know?

Modern CPUs can contain billions of tiny transistors on one chip.

2.7 Memory

Memory is used to store data and instructions. Some memory is temporary, and some is permanent.

Types of Memory

Memory Full Form Volatile? Purpose Example
RAM Random Access Memory Yes Temporary working area Open apps and files
ROM Read Only Memory No Stores startup instructions BIOS/UEFI
Cache - Usually yes Very fast memory near CPU CPU cache
Virtual Memory - Uses storage Extends RAM when RAM is low Page file

RAM vs ROM

Basis RAM ROM
Full Form Random Access Memory Read Only Memory
Nature Temporary Permanent
Volatile? Yes, data clears when power is off No, data remains
Main Use Runs current programs Stores boot instructions
Speed Fast Slower than RAM for normal operations
Example 8 GB RAM in laptop BIOS/UEFI chip

Memory Units

Unit Value
1 Bit Smallest unit, 0 or 1
1 Byte 8 bits
1 KB 1024 bytes
1 MB 1024 KB
1 GB 1024 MB
1 TB 1024 GB

Why Memory Matters

If RAM is low, the computer may become slow when many programs are open. If storage is full, you may not be able to save new files. If cache is efficient, the CPU can access frequent data faster.

Common Mistakes

  • RAM is not permanent storage.
  • ROM is not used for storing personal documents.
  • A 1 TB hard disk does not automatically make a slow CPU fast.

Did You Know?

When you restart a computer, RAM is cleared, but saved files remain on storage.

2.8 Input and Output Devices

An input device sends data or instructions to a computer. An output device shows or produces results from a computer.

Input Devices

Device Function Example Use
Keyboard Enters text, numbers, commands Typing application forms
Mouse Selects and controls pointer Opening files and menus
Scanner Converts paper to digital image Scanning Aadhaar copy
Microphone Records sound Online class, voice search
Webcam Captures video Video meeting
Touchscreen Accepts touch input Smartphone apps, ATM
Barcode Reader Reads product codes Retail billing

Output Devices

Device Function Example Use
Monitor/VDU Displays visual output Viewing documents
Printer Produces hard copy Printing certificates
Speaker Produces sound Audio lessons
Projector Displays screen to large audience Classroom teaching
Plotter Prints large technical drawings Engineering designs

Input vs Output

Basis Input Device Output Device
Direction Sends data to computer Receives result from computer
Purpose Enter instructions/data Display/print/play result
Examples Keyboard, mouse, scanner Monitor, printer, speaker
User Action User gives data User receives result

Exam Tip

Scanner is an input device because it sends image data into the computer. Printer is an output device because it produces printed result.

Did You Know?

A touchscreen works as both input and output: it displays information and accepts touch commands.

2.9 Storage Devices

Storage is used to save data, files, programs, and operating systems permanently or for long-term use.

Common Storage Devices

Device Technology Speed Portability Common Use
HDD Magnetic disk Slower Internal/external Large low-cost storage
SSD Flash memory Fast Internal/external Faster boot and apps
Pen Drive Flash memory Medium Highly portable File transfer
Memory Card Flash memory Medium Highly portable Phone/camera storage
CD/DVD Optical Slow Portable Old media/software
Cloud Storage Internet servers Depends on internet Access anywhere Google Drive, OneDrive

HDD vs SSD

Basis HDD SSD
Full Form Hard Disk Drive Solid State Drive
Technology Spinning magnetic disk Flash memory chips
Speed Slower Faster
Durability More affected by shock More shock-resistant
Cost Cheaper per GB Costlier per GB
Noise Can make sound Silent
Best Use Large storage Operating system and fast work

Backup Importance

A backup is an extra copy of important data kept for safety. Backup protects against accidental deletion, device failure, virus attack, theft, and corruption.

Good backup practices:

  • Keep important documents in at least two places.
  • Use cloud storage carefully with strong passwords.
  • Scan pen drives before opening files.
  • Do not keep the only copy of important documents on one phone.

Did You Know?

The first hard disks stored only a few megabytes and were physically very large. Today, small SSDs can store terabytes.

2.10 Data, Information, Files, and Folders

Data means raw facts without full meaning. Information means processed data that is useful and meaningful.

Example:

  • Data: 85, 90, 78, 92
  • Information: "The student's average mark is 86.25."

Data vs Information

Basis Data Information
Meaning Raw facts Processed meaningful result
Form Numbers, text, images, symbols Report, summary, conclusion
Usefulness Limited until processed Useful for decision-making
Example List of marks Average mark and rank

Files and Folders

A file is a named collection of data stored on a storage device. A folder or directory is a container used to organize files and subfolders.

Common file extensions:

Extension File Type
.txt Text file
.docx, .odt Document
.xlsx, .ods Spreadsheet
.pptx, .odp Presentation
.pdf Portable document
.jpg, .png Image
.mp4 Video
.apk Android app package

File Management Tips

  • Use meaningful file names, such as Module-1-Notes.docx.
  • Create subject-wise folders.
  • Avoid deleting unknown system files.
  • Keep backups of certificates, mark sheets, and ID proof.

Did You Know?

File extensions help the operating system decide which application should open a file.

2.11 Number Systems

A number system is a method of representing numbers using digits and a base. Computers internally use binary because electronic circuits can easily represent two states: ON and OFF.

Number System Base Digits Use
Decimal 10 0-9 Daily counting and money
Binary 2 0, 1 Computer internal processing
Octal 8 0-7 Some computing contexts
Hexadecimal 16 0-9, A-F Memory addresses, color codes

Binary Example

1010 in binary means:

1 x 8 + 0 x 4 + 1 x 2 + 0 x 1 = 10

So, binary 1010 = decimal 10.

Why Binary Is Important

Computers use electrical signals. A signal can be represented as ON or OFF, true or false, 1 or 0. Therefore, binary is suitable for computer circuits.

Exam Tip

Remember: Bit is the smallest unit and stores either 0 or 1.

Did You Know?

Hexadecimal is often used in web color codes. For example, #FFFFFF represents white.

2.12 Connecting, Starting, and Shutting Down a Computer

Correct handling protects hardware, data, and user safety.

Connecting a Desktop PC

  1. Place CPU cabinet, monitor, keyboard, and mouse on a stable table.
  2. Connect monitor cable such as HDMI or VGA to the CPU cabinet.
  3. Connect keyboard and mouse to USB ports.
  4. Connect speaker, printer, or scanner if needed.
  5. Connect power cables to UPS or power socket.
  6. Switch on UPS or stabilizer first if available.
  7. Press the power button of CPU and monitor.
  8. Wait for the operating system login screen.

What Happens During Startup?

  1. Power reaches the motherboard.
  2. BIOS/UEFI firmware starts.
  3. Basic hardware checking happens.
  4. The operating system loads from storage into RAM.
  5. The login screen or desktop appears.

Safe Shutdown

  1. Save all work.
  2. Close open applications.
  3. Click Start -> Power -> Shut down.
  4. Wait until screen and CPU turn off.
  5. Switch off UPS after shutdown completes.

Common Mistakes

  • Do not unplug the computer while it is running.
  • Do not switch off power without saving work.
  • Do not touch cables with wet hands.
  • Do not block ventilation holes.

Digital Safety Guidance

  • Use strong passwords and do not share them.
  • Do not open unknown email attachments.
  • Use antivirus or built-in security tools.
  • Keep important data backed up.
  • Log out after using public computers.
  • Avoid downloading pirated software.

Did You Know?

Improper shutdown may corrupt open files or cause operating system errors.

3. Real-Life Examples

Daily Life

  • Using UPI to pay at a shop.
  • Searching bus or train timing online.
  • Watching a computer basics video.
  • Printing a school project.
  • Saving photos and documents on phone or cloud.

Workplace

  • Preparing salary sheets in spreadsheets.
  • Sending official emails.
  • Maintaining customer records.
  • Creating presentations for meetings.
  • Using billing software in shops.

Education

  • Attending online classes.
  • Taking LMS quizzes.
  • Downloading notes and PDFs.
  • Preparing assignments.
  • Using a projector for classroom teaching.

Government Services

  • Downloading documents from DigiLocker.
  • Applying for certificates online.
  • Using Aadhaar-based services.
  • Checking scholarship status.
  • Paying electricity or water bills online.

Scenario Practice

A shopkeeper wants to manage sales digitally. Identify the computer components involved:

  • Input: barcode scanner, keyboard, mouse.
  • Processing: CPU and billing software.
  • Storage: SSD/HDD storing sales records.
  • Output: monitor display and printed bill.
  • Software: operating system, billing application, antivirus.

4. Comparison Tables

Microcomputer vs Mainframe vs Supercomputer

Basis Microcomputer Mainframe Supercomputer
Size Small Very large Extremely large/powerful
Users One or few Many users Specialized users
Cost Low to medium Very high Extremely high
Use Home, office, school Banking, railway, insurance Research, weather, AI
Example Desktop, laptop IBM mainframe PARAM, Frontier

Hardware vs Software

Point Hardware Software
Nature Physical Logical/program-based
Touch Can be touched Cannot be touched
Examples Keyboard, RAM, CPU Windows, browser, Word
Failure Type Physical damage Corruption, virus, bugs
Relationship Runs software Controls hardware

RAM vs ROM

Point RAM ROM
Storage Type Temporary Permanent
Data after shutdown Lost Retained
Used for Running programs Startup instructions
User data storage No No
Example 8 GB RAM BIOS/UEFI

HDD vs SSD

Point HDD SSD
Mechanism Mechanical spinning disk Electronic flash memory
Speed Lower Higher
Price Cheaper Costlier
Shock resistance Lower Higher
Best for Bulk storage Fast boot and apps

Data vs Information

Point Data Information
Meaning Raw facts Processed data
Example 80, 90, 70 Average = 80
Use Input for processing Decision-making
Clarity Less meaningful alone Meaningful

Input vs Output Devices

Point Input Device Output Device
Direction User to computer Computer to user
Purpose Enter data Present result
Examples Keyboard, mouse, scanner Monitor, printer, speaker
Exam clue "Send to computer" "Receive from computer"

5. Practical Activities

Activity 1: Identify Computer Parts

  • Observation Activity: Look at a desktop or laptop and identify monitor, keyboard, mouse/touchpad, ports, speakers, and storage indicators.
  • Hands-On Activity: Connect keyboard and mouse to USB ports and test them.
  • Group Discussion: Why is the CPU often confused with the cabinet?
  • Home Assignment: Draw and label a desktop computer system.

Activity 2: Input and Output Classification

  • Observation Activity: List all devices in your home or classroom that accept input or produce output.
  • Hands-On Activity: Type a paragraph, print it, and identify input, processing, storage, and output.
  • Group Discussion: Is a touchscreen input, output, or both?
  • Home Assignment: Create a table of 5 input and 5 output devices with uses.

Activity 3: Storage and File Management

  • Observation Activity: Open This PC/File Manager and note available drives.
  • Hands-On Activity: Create a folder named DCA_Module_1, create a text file, rename it, copy it, and delete the duplicate.
  • Group Discussion: Why should important files be backed up?
  • Home Assignment: Collect 10 file extensions and write their file types.

Activity 4: Mobile Computing

  • Observation Activity: Identify apps on a smartphone and classify them as education, banking, communication, utility, or government service.
  • Hands-On Activity: Open DigiLocker or any government service app and observe login/security steps.
  • Group Discussion: What safety rules should be followed while using UPI?
  • Home Assignment: Write 5 uses of smartphones in education and government services.

Activity 5: Number System Practice

  • Observation Activity: Find where numbers are used in computer settings such as storage size, RAM, and screen resolution.
  • Hands-On Activity: Convert decimal 10, 13, 25, and 31 into binary.
  • Group Discussion: Why do computers use binary?
  • Home Assignment: Memorize memory units from bit to terabyte.

6. Industry Applications

Education

Computers are used for online learning, digital attendance, smart classrooms, computer-based tests, digital libraries, and learning management systems.

Banking

Banking systems use computers for account records, ATM transactions, UPI payments, loan processing, fraud detection, and customer service.

Healthcare

Hospitals use computers for patient registration, lab reports, digital prescriptions, telemedicine, billing, and medical imaging.

Government

Government departments use computers for Aadhaar services, land records, certificates, welfare schemes, tax portals, digital payments, and public service delivery.

IT Industry

The IT industry uses computers for programming, testing, web development, app development, cloud services, cybersecurity, database management, and technical support.

Manufacturing

Computers control machines, robots, inventory, quality checking, production planning, and safety monitoring.

Retail

Retail businesses use computers for billing, barcode scanning, GST invoices, stock management, online sales, and customer data.

Communication

Computers and mobile devices support email, video conferencing, instant messaging, online meetings, and social media communication.

Career Awareness

Career How Module 1 Helps
Computer Operator Basic hardware, software, files, typing, and safe use
Data Entry Operator Input devices, files, data accuracy, storage
Hardware Technician Components, ports, memory, storage, troubleshooting
Software Developer CPU, memory, software concepts, logic
Network Administrator Computer systems, devices, digital services
Cybersecurity Analyst Password safety, malware awareness, data protection
AI Engineer Computer generations, CPU, data, processing basics

7. Exam Tips

High-Probability Facts

  • Computer works on Input -> Process -> Output -> Storage.
  • CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.
  • CPU parts: ALU, CU, Registers.
  • RAM is volatile; ROM is non-volatile.
  • First generation used vacuum tubes.
  • Second generation used transistors.
  • Third generation used ICs.
  • Fourth generation used microprocessors.
  • Fifth generation focuses on AI.
  • Scanner is an input device; printer is an output device.
  • 1 Byte = 8 bits.
  • 1 KB = 1024 bytes.

Memory Tricks

  • Generations: Very Talented Indians Make Advances
    Vacuum Tube, Transistor, IC, Microprocessor, AI
  • CPU parts: A Computer Runs
    ALU, Control Unit, Registers
  • IPO cycle: I Process Stored Output
    Input, Process, Storage, Output

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Do not write RAM as permanent memory.
  • Do not confuse CPU with the full cabinet.
  • Do not confuse fifth-generation computers with 5G mobile network.
  • Do not call printer an input device.
  • Do not say more storage always means more speed.
  • Do not switch off a running computer directly from the power socket.

8. Important Questions

Short Answer Questions

  1. What is a computer?
  2. Define input and output.
  3. What is CPU?
  4. Write the full form of ALU and CU.
  5. Define RAM.
  6. Define ROM.
  7. What is firmware?
  8. What is IoT?
  9. What is a file?
  10. What is a folder?
  11. What is data?
  12. What is information?
  13. Write any four input devices.
  14. Write any four output devices.
  15. What is binary number system?

Long Answer Questions

  1. Explain the input-process-output-storage cycle with an example.
  2. Describe the five generations of computers.
  3. Explain the components of a computer system.
  4. Differentiate between hardware and software with examples.
  5. Explain RAM, ROM, cache memory, and virtual memory.
  6. Describe input and output devices with examples.
  7. Explain HDD, SSD, pen drive, memory card, and cloud storage.
  8. Explain the role of computers in education, banking, healthcare, and government services.

Viva Questions

  1. What happens when a computer starts?
  2. Why is RAM called volatile memory?
  3. Why is CPU called the brain of the computer?
  4. Why do computers use binary?
  5. Is touchscreen an input or output device?
  6. What is the difference between data and information?
  7. Why is backup important?
  8. What should you do before shutting down a computer?
  9. Why should OTP not be shared?
  10. What is the use of a scanner?

9. Chapter Summary

Key Takeaways

  • A computer accepts input, processes it, stores data, and produces output.
  • Hardware means physical parts; software means programs and instructions.
  • CPU performs processing and contains ALU, CU, and registers.
  • RAM is temporary working memory, while ROM stores startup instructions.
  • Input devices send data to the computer; output devices present results.
  • Storage devices save files and programs permanently.
  • Data becomes information after processing.
  • Computers use binary internally because electronic circuits work with two states.
  • Safe shutdown, strong passwords, malware awareness, and backup are important for digital safety.

Important Definitions

Term Definition
Computer Electronic device that accepts input, processes data, stores data, and gives output
Hardware Physical components of a computer
Software Programs and instructions used by a computer
CPU Main processing unit of a computer
RAM Temporary volatile memory used while working
ROM Non-volatile memory containing startup instructions
Firmware Software stored in hardware
Data Raw facts
Information Processed meaningful data
File Named collection of data
Folder Container for files and subfolders
IoT Internet-connected devices that collect or exchange data
Backup Extra copy of important data

Formulae and Conversions

  • 1 Byte = 8 bits
  • 1 KB = 1024 bytes
  • 1 MB = 1024 KB
  • 1 GB = 1024 MB
  • 1 TB = 1024 GB
  • Binary digits: 0 and 1
  • Decimal digits: 0 to 9

Exam Revision Notes

  • Read generation technologies carefully.
  • Practice RAM vs ROM, HDD vs SSD, hardware vs software, and data vs information.
  • Memorize examples of input and output devices.
  • Understand startup and shutdown steps.
  • Practice simple binary-to-decimal conversions.

10. Assessments

A. Multiple Choice Questions

1. A computer is mainly used to:

  • a) Store only pictures
  • b) Accept input, process data, store data, and produce output
  • c) Print only documents
  • d) Work without software

2. Which part is called the brain of the computer?

  • a) Monitor
  • b) Printer
  • c) CPU
  • d) Keyboard

3. ALU performs:

  • a) Arithmetic and logical operations
  • b) Printing
  • c) Sound output
  • d) File storage only

4. RAM is:

  • a) Permanent memory
  • b) Volatile memory
  • c) Output device
  • d) Printer software

5. ROM is used mainly for:

  • a) Temporary typing
  • b) Boot/startup instructions
  • c) Printing bills
  • d) Playing music

6. First-generation computers used:

  • a) Transistors
  • b) Integrated circuits
  • c) Vacuum tubes
  • d) Microprocessors

7. Fourth-generation computers are based on:

  • a) Vacuum tubes
  • b) Transistors
  • c) Microprocessors
  • d) Mechanical gears

8. Which is an input device?

  • a) Printer
  • b) Monitor
  • c) Scanner
  • d) Speaker

9. Which is an output device?

  • a) Mouse
  • b) Keyboard
  • c) Microphone
  • d) Monitor

10. IoT stands for:

  • a) Internet of Tools
  • b) Internet of Things
  • c) Input Output Technology
  • d) Internal Operating Technique

11. Binary number system uses:

  • a) 0 and 1
  • b) 0 to 7
  • c) 0 to 9
  • d) A to F only

12. 1 Byte is equal to:

  • a) 4 bits
  • b) 8 bits
  • c) 16 bits
  • d) 1024 bits

13. Processed meaningful data is called:

  • a) Hardware
  • b) Input
  • c) Information
  • d) Folder

14. Which storage device is generally faster?

  • a) SSD
  • b) DVD
  • c) Floppy disk
  • d) CD

15. A file extension for a PDF document is:

  • a) .mp4
  • b) .jpg
  • c) .pdf
  • d) .apk

B. True/False

  1. RAM keeps data permanently after shutdown.
  2. CPU contains ALU and CU.
  3. Printer is an output device.
  4. Scanner is an output device.
  5. Fifth-generation computers focus on AI.
  6. A folder can contain files and subfolders.
  7. More storage always guarantees faster processing.
  8. Touchscreen can work as both input and output.
  9. Backup protects important data.
  10. OTP should be shared with friends for help.

C. Fill in the Blanks

  1. CPU stands for __________.
  2. ALU stands for __________.
  3. RAM is __________ memory.
  4. ROM is __________ memory.
  5. The smallest unit of data is __________.
  6. 1 Byte = __________ bits.
  7. The binary number system has base __________.
  8. A device used to print output on paper is called a __________.
  9. Raw facts are called __________.
  10. Processed data is called __________.

D. Match the Following

Column A Column B
1. RAM a. Artificial Intelligence
2. Printer b. Temporary memory
3. Fifth generation c. Output device
4. Scanner d. Input device
5. SSD e. Fast storage

E. Case Study Questions

Case Study 1

Rina attends an online class on her smartphone. She uses earphones, types notes, downloads a PDF, and submits an assignment through an app.

Answer the following:

  1. Identify two input devices used in this situation.
  2. Identify one output device.
  3. Which software category does the online class app belong to?
  4. Where is the downloaded PDF stored?
  5. Write one safety rule Rina should follow.

Case Study 2

A hospital uses computers to register patients, print bills, store lab reports, and send reports through email.

Answer the following:

  1. Write two uses of computers in the hospital.
  2. Is patient age data or information before processing?
  3. Which output device prints the bill?
  4. Why is backup important for hospital records?
  5. Write one privacy rule for patient data.

F. Practical Questions

  1. Create a folder named Module_1_Practice.
  2. Create a text file named computer_components.txt.
  3. Type five hardware and five software examples.
  4. Rename the file as hardware_software_examples.txt.
  5. Copy the file to another folder.
  6. Check the storage capacity of your computer or phone.
  7. Convert decimal 13 into binary.
  8. List five apps on a smartphone and classify them.

G. Viva Questions

  1. What is the difference between RAM and ROM?
  2. Why is a scanner called an input device?
  3. What is the use of motherboard?
  4. Why should a computer be shut down properly?
  5. What is the use of antivirus?
  6. What is cloud storage?
  7. What is the difference between a file and a folder?
  8. What is the role of operating system?
  9. Name two government services available through computer or mobile.
  10. Why is binary important in computers?

Answer Key

MCQ Answers

1-b | 2-c | 3-a | 4-b | 5-b | 6-c | 7-c | 8-c | 9-d | 10-b | 11-a | 12-b | 13-c | 14-a | 15-c

True/False Answers

1-False | 2-True | 3-True | 4-False | 5-True | 6-True | 7-False | 8-True | 9-True | 10-False

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. Central Processing Unit
  2. Arithmetic Logic Unit
  3. volatile/temporary
  4. non-volatile/permanent
  5. bit
  6. 8
  7. 2
  8. printer
  9. data
  10. information

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-c | 3-a | 4-d | 5-e

11. Quick Revision Notes

One-Page Quick Revision Sheet

Topic Must Remember
Computer Input -> Process -> Storage -> Output
CPU ALU + CU + Registers
RAM Temporary, volatile, used while working
ROM Permanent startup instructions
Hardware Physical parts: keyboard, CPU, monitor
Software Programs: Windows, browser, word processor
Input Devices Keyboard, mouse, scanner, microphone
Output Devices Monitor, printer, speaker, projector
Storage HDD, SSD, pen drive, memory card, cloud
Data Raw facts
Information Processed meaningful data
Binary Base 2, digits 0 and 1
Generations Vacuum tube, transistor, IC, microprocessor, AI
Safety Strong password, backup, antivirus, safe shutdown

Final Checklist

  • I can define computer with the IPO cycle.
  • I can explain five generations of computers.
  • I can differentiate hardware and software.
  • I can identify CPU, RAM, ROM, storage, input, and output devices.
  • I can compare RAM vs ROM and HDD vs SSD.
  • I can explain data vs information.
  • I know basic file extensions.
  • I can convert simple binary numbers to decimal.
  • I can start and shut down a computer safely.
  • I can answer CCC/DCA viva questions from this chapter.

chapters.title: Practice MCQs & Labs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs & Labs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 01: Introduction to Computer

Section A — MCQ (80 questions)

  1. Full form of CPU? a) Central Process Unit b) Central Processing Unit c) Computer Personal Unit d) Control Processing Unit

  2. Brain of the computer? a) Keyboard b) Printer c) CPU d) Monitor

  3. Which memory is temporary? a) ROM b) Hard disk c) DVD d) RAM

  4. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register

  5. Input device? a) Printer b) Speaker c) Keyboard d) Monitor

  6. Output device? a) Mouse b) Scanner c) Microphone d) Printer

  7. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes

  8. Binary uses digits? a) A to F b) 0 and 1 c) 0 to 9 d) 1 to 9

  9. Software example? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) MS Word

  10. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus

  11. Pen drive is? a) ALU b) Storage device c) Input device d) Output device

  12. Full form of RAM? a) Run All Memory b) Random Active Memory c) Random Access Memory d) Read Access Memory

  13. Full form of ROM? a) Read Only Memory b) Run Only Memory c) Random Only Memory d) Read Open Memory

  14. Supercomputer used for? a) Printing bills b) Weather research c) Typing letters d) Email only

  15. Data processed into meaningful form is? a) Input b) Output c) Information d) Hardware

  16. Which is system software? a) MS Excel b) Chrome c) Tally d) Operating System

  17. ALU is part of? a) Mouse b) CPU c) Monitor d) Printer

  18. VDU stands for? a) Video Disk Unit b) Virtual Data Unit c) Visual Display Unit d) Very Digital User

  19. Multitasking means? a) One program only b) No OS c) Shutdown d) Running many programs together

  20. Virus is? a) Harmful software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Storage

  21. Smartphone is a? a) Printer b) Scanner c) Mobile computer / IT gadget d) Only phone

  22. Touchscreen is both? a) Only input b) Only storage c) Only CPU d) Input and output

  23. Evolution: first generation used? a) Vacuum tubes b) Transistors c) IC chips d) AI processors

  24. Fourth generation used? a) Abacus b) Microprocessors / VLSI c) Vacuum tubes d) Punch cards only

  25. SSD compared to HDD is generally? a) Faster b) Slower c) Same speed d) Not storage

  26. Bit is? a) 1 MB b) Smallest unit of data c) 8 bytes d) 1 KB

  27. 8 bits = ? a) 1 bit b) 1 GB c) 1 Byte d) 1 KB

  28. Motherboard function? a) Print documents b) Browse internet c) Send email d) Connects all components

  29. Scanner is? a) OS b) Input device c) Output device d) CPU

  30. Webcam is? a) Storage b) Printer c) Input device d) Output only

  31. Bluetooth in laptop is for? a) Printing only b) Storage only c) Cooling d) Wireless connectivity

  32. Tablet is an IT gadget for? a) Portable computing b) Only calls c) Only printing d) Only scanning

  33. Application software does? a) Cool CPU b) User-specific tasks c) Boot computer only d) Connect wires

  34. Firmware is? a) Virus b) Browser c) Software in hardware d) Pure hardware

  35. Cold boot starts from? a) Sleep only b) Lock screen c) Hibernate only d) Power off state

  36. GUI means? a) Graphical User Interface b) General User Internet c) Graphic Utility Input d) Global User ID

  37. File extension .docx is for? a) PowerPoint b) Video c) Word document d) Excel

  38. Backup purpose? a) Delete files b) Slow PC c) Print faster d) Protect data from loss

  39. Microcomputer example? a) None b) Laptop c) Mainframe d) Supercomputer only

  40. Cache memory is? a) Printer buffer only b) Email storage c) Very fast small buffer d) Slowest storage

  41. Which enters data into computer? a) Output device b) Monitor c) Speaker d) Input device

  42. CPU speed measured in? a) GHz b) Kg c) Inch d) Litres

  43. Decimal 5 in binary? a) 100 b) 101 c) 110 d) 111

  44. Binary 1010 in decimal? a) 12 b) 5 c) 10 d) 8

  45. Mainframe used in? a) Home gaming only b) Calculator only c) Watch only d) Banks and railways

  46. Headphones are? a) Output device b) Input device c) Storage d) CPU

  47. Joystick is used for? a) Booting b) Games / input c) Printing d) Storage

  48. Digital camera captures? a) Only text b) Only programs c) Images as digital data d) Only sound

  49. Mobile app runs on? a) Printer only b) Scanner only c) Mouse only d) Smartphone/tablet

  50. E-governance uses computers for? a) Online citizen services b) Only games c) Only music d) Only photos

  51. A computer mainly works on which cycle? a) Storage only b) Input, process, storage, output c) Print only d) Input only

  52. Second generation computers used? a) Integrated circuits b) Microprocessors c) Transistors d) Vacuum tubes

  53. Third generation computers used? a) Vacuum tubes b) Transistors c) Abacus d) Integrated Circuits (ICs)

  54. Fifth generation computers focus on? a) Artificial Intelligence b) 5G mobile network c) Vacuum tubes d) Punch cards

  55. IoT stands for? a) Internal Operating Technique b) Internet of Things c) Internet of Tools d) Input Output Technology

  56. Full form of ALU? a) Automatic Logic Unit b) Arithmetic Load Unit c) Arithmetic Logic Unit d) Advanced Logic Unit

  57. Control Unit (CU) in CPU does? a) Prints documents b) Stores files permanently c) Displays output only d) Directs and controls operations

  58. RAM is called volatile because? a) Data is lost when power is off b) It never loses data c) It is an output device d) It stores boot code

  59. Utility software is used for? a) Hardware repair physically b) Maintenance and protection c) Typing letters only d) Booting only

  60. Embedded computer example? a) Supercomputer only b) Mainframe only c) ATM machine d) Desktop PC only

  61. Server computer is used to? a) Play games only b) Type documents only c) Scan papers only d) Provide services to many users

  62. Workstation is mainly for? a) Powerful single-user professional work b) Large bank transactions only c) Weather simulation only d) Mobile calls only

  63. HDD stands for? a) Hardware Disk Drive b) Hard Disk Drive c) High Data Device d) Hard Digital Drive

  64. Cloud storage means? a) RAM storage b) Printer memory c) Files saved on internet servers d) Storage inside CPU only

  65. File extension .pdf is for? a) Spreadsheet b) Presentation c) Android app d) Portable document

  66. File extension .xlsx is for? a) Spreadsheet b) Word document c) Video file d) Text file only

  67. A folder is? a) Virus program b) Container for files and subfolders c) Same as CPU d) Output device

  68. Raw facts without full meaning are called? a) Firmware b) Output c) Data d) Information

  69. Hexadecimal number system uses base? a) 2 b) 8 c) 10 d) 16

  70. Decimal number system uses base? a) 10 b) 2 c) 8 d) 16

  71. 1 MB equals? a) 1024 GB b) 1024 KB c) 1000 bytes d) 8 bits

  72. Barcode reader is? a) Storage device b) System software c) Input device d) Output device

  73. Projector is? a) Input device b) Storage device c) CPU part d) Output device

  74. Microphone is? a) Input device b) Output device c) Storage d) Motherboard

  75. During startup, which firmware runs first? a) Antivirus only b) BIOS/UEFI c) MS Word d) Chrome browser

  76. Before shutting down, you should? a) Delete all files b) Remove RAM c) Save all open work d) Unplug power immediately

  77. OTP should be? a) Shared with friends b) Posted online c) Written on wall d) Never shared with anyone

  78. Antivirus software helps to? a) Protect against malware b) Increase RAM size c) Print faster d) Replace keyboard

  79. More storage always means faster PC — this is? a) Same as more RAM always b) False / misconception c) Always true d) Required for CPU

  80. CPU cabinet is the same as CPU chip — this is? a) Same as motherboard b) False / misconception c) True always d) Correct definition

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. What is a computer?
  2. Explain the input-process-output-storage cycle with one example.
  3. Write the full forms of ALU and CU.
  4. Differentiate between RAM and ROM.
  5. Differentiate between hardware and software with two examples each.
  6. Name the five generations of computers with their main technology.
  7. What is IoT? Give two examples.
  8. Differentiate between data and information.
  9. Write four input devices and four output devices.
  10. Why is backup important?
  11. What happens during computer startup?
  12. Why do computers use binary?
  13. What is firmware?
  14. Write two digital safety rules for mobile/UPI use.
  15. Convert decimal 13 into binary.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Identify computer parts on a desktop/laptop (monitor, keyboard, mouse, ports, speakers).
  2. Classify 5 input and 5 output devices with real-life uses.
  3. Create folder DCA_Module_1, add a text file, rename, copy, and delete duplicate.
  4. List 10 file extensions and their file types.
  5. Classify smartphone apps as education, banking, communication, utility, or government.
  6. Convert decimal 10, 13, 25, and 31 into binary.
  7. Demonstrate safe shutdown steps on a PC.
  8. Revise wrong MCQs from this practice test and note corrections.

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Full form of ROM?
questions.explanation: ROM stands for Read Only Memory — non-volatile memory that stores firmware and boot instructions.

questions.question: Supercomputer used for?
questions.explanation: Weather research is the main purpose or function described in the question. Printing bills and Typing letters are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Data processed into meaningful form is?
questions.explanation: Raw facts are called data. After processing, data becomes useful information (e.g. marks → percentage report).

questions.question: Which is system software?
questions.explanation: The Operating System (Windows, Linux) manages hardware and runs other programs — it is system software. Word, Chrome, and Tally are application software.

questions.question: ALU is part of?
questions.explanation: The ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) performs calculations and logic inside the CPU. It is not a separate monitor or printer.

questions.question: VDU stands for?
questions.explanation: VDU means Visual Display Unit — another name for a monitor/screen that displays output.

questions.question: Multitasking means?
questions.explanation: Multitasking means running several programs at the same time (e.g. browser + Word together). Modern OS supports this.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Smartphone is a?
questions.explanation: Mobile computer / IT gadget correctly answers this question. Printer and Scanner are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Touchscreen is both?
questions.explanation: A touchscreen accepts touch input and displays output on the same surface — so it works as both input and output device.

questions.question: Evolution: first generation used?
questions.explanation: First-generation computers (1940s–50s) used vacuum tubes. Transistors came in 2nd gen; IC chips in 3rd; microprocessors in 4th.

questions.question: Fourth generation used?
questions.explanation: Fourth-generation computers use microprocessors and VLSI chips — the technology in today's PCs and laptops.

questions.question: SSD compared to HDD is generally?
questions.explanation: SSD (Solid State Drive) has no moving parts and is generally faster than HDD (Hard Disk Drive) for booting and loading files.

questions.question: Bit is?
questions.explanation: A bit (binary digit) is the smallest unit of data — either 0 or 1. It is not 8 bytes or 1 KB.

questions.question: 8 bits = ?
questions.explanation: One byte equals 8 bits. A bit is the smallest unit of data (0 or 1); 8 bits grouped together form one byte.

questions.question: Motherboard function?
questions.explanation: The motherboard is the main circuit board that connects CPU, RAM, storage, and other components together.

questions.question: Scanner is?
questions.explanation: A scanner converts paper documents or images into digital data sent to the computer — it is an input device.

questions.question: Webcam is?
questions.explanation: A webcam captures video/images and sends them to the computer — it is an input device.

questions.question: Bluetooth in laptop is for?
questions.explanation: Wireless connectivity is the correct answer. Printing only and Storage only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Tablet is an IT gadget for?
questions.explanation: Portable computing is the correct answer. Only calls and Only printing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Application software does?
questions.explanation: Application software helps users do specific tasks (Word for documents, Excel for sheets). It does not boot the PC or cool the CPU.

questions.question: Firmware is?
questions.explanation: Firmware is software permanently stored in hardware (e.g. BIOS in ROM) that controls basic device functions.

questions.question: Cold boot starts from?
questions.explanation: Cold boot means starting the computer from a completely powered-off state — a full startup, not wake from sleep.

questions.question: GUI means?
questions.explanation: GUI (Graphical User Interface) lets you use icons, menus, and a mouse instead of typing only text commands.

questions.question: File extension .docx is for?
questions.explanation: The .docx extension is used for Microsoft Word documents. .xlsx is Excel; .pptx is PowerPoint.

questions.question: Backup purpose?
questions.explanation: Backup copies important files to another location so you can recover them if the original is lost, deleted, or damaged.

questions.question: Microcomputer example?
questions.explanation: A laptop is a microcomputer — a personal computer for one user. Mainframes and supercomputers serve many users or heavy science work.

questions.question: Cache memory is?
questions.explanation: Cache is very fast, small memory between CPU and RAM that stores frequently used data for quicker access.

questions.question: Which enters data into computer?
questions.explanation: Input devices (keyboard, mouse, scanner) send data to the computer. Monitors and speakers only show or play results — they are output devices.

questions.question: CPU speed measured in?
questions.explanation: CPU speed is measured in GHz (gigahertz) — how many billion cycles per second the processor can perform.

questions.question: Decimal 5 in binary?
questions.explanation: Decimal 5 = binary 101 (4 + 0 + 1). Divide by 2 repeatedly and read remainders bottom to top.

questions.question: Binary 1010 in decimal?
questions.explanation: Binary 1010 = 8 + 0 + 2 + 0 = 10 in decimal. Each position is a power of 2 from right to left.

questions.question: Mainframe used in?
questions.explanation: Mainframe computers handle huge transactions for banks, railways, and large organisations — not home gaming.

questions.question: Headphones are?
questions.explanation: Headphones receive audio signals from the computer and play sound — they are output devices.

questions.question: Joystick is used for?
questions.explanation: A joystick is an input device mainly used for games and simulations to control movement on screen.

questions.question: Digital camera captures?
questions.explanation: A digital camera captures photos as digital data (files) that can be stored and edited on a computer.

questions.question: Mobile app runs on?
questions.explanation: Mobile apps run on smartphones and tablets — portable computers — not on printers or scanners alone.

questions.question: E-governance uses computers for?
questions.explanation: E-governance delivers government services online (certificates, tax, schemes) using computers and the internet.

questions.question: A computer mainly works on which cycle?
questions.explanation: Input, process, storage, output is the correct answer. Storage only and Print only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Second generation computers used?
questions.explanation: Transistors is the correct answer. Integrated circuits and Microprocessors are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Third generation computers used?
questions.explanation: Integrated Circuits (ICs) is the correct answer. Vacuum tubes and Transistors are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Fifth generation computers focus on?
questions.explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) enables computers to perform tasks like recognition, recommendations, and language understanding.

questions.question: IoT stands for?
questions.explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

questions.question: Full form of ALU?
questions.explanation: Arithmetic Logic Unit is the correct full form of ALU. Remember abbreviations — they are common in CCC exams.

questions.question: Control Unit (CU) in CPU does?
questions.explanation: Directs and controls operations is the correct answer. Prints documents and Stores files permanently are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: RAM is called volatile because?

options.text: Data is lost when power is off

questions.explanation: Data is lost when power is off is the correct answer. It never loses data and It is an output device are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Utility software is used for?
questions.explanation: Maintenance and protection is the main purpose or function described in the question. Hardware repair physically and Typing letters only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Embedded computer example?
questions.explanation: ATM machine is a correct example for what the question asks about. Supercomputer only and Mainframe only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Server computer is used to?
questions.explanation: Provide services to many users is the main purpose or function described in the question. Play games only and Type documents only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Workstation is mainly for?
questions.explanation: Powerful single-user professional work is the correct answer. Large bank transactions only and Weather simulation only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: HDD stands for?
questions.explanation: Hard Disk Drive is the correct expansion or meaning. Learn both the short form and full form for the exam.

questions.question: Cloud storage means?
questions.explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

questions.question: File extension .pdf is for?
questions.explanation: Portable document is the correct answer. Spreadsheet and Presentation are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: File extension .xlsx is for?
questions.explanation: Spreadsheet is the correct answer. Word document and Video file are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: A folder is?
questions.explanation: Container for files and subfolders is the correct answer. Virus program and Same as CPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Raw facts without full meaning are called?
questions.explanation: Data is the correct answer. Firmware and Output are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Hexadecimal number system uses base?
questions.explanation: 16 is the correct answer. 2 and 8 are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Decimal number system uses base?
questions.explanation: 10 is the correct answer. 2 and 8 are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: 1 MB equals?
questions.explanation: The answer is 1024 KB. For number conversions, practice dividing by 2 (decimal→binary) or adding place values (binary→decimal).

questions.question: Barcode reader is?
questions.explanation: Input device is the correct answer. Storage device and System software are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Projector is?
questions.explanation: Output device is the correct answer. Input device and Storage device are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Microphone is?
questions.explanation: Input device is the correct answer. Output device and Storage are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: During startup, which firmware runs first?
questions.explanation: BIOS/UEFI is the correct answer. Antivirus only and MS Word are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Before shutting down, you should?
questions.explanation: Save all open work is the correct answer. Delete all files and Remove RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: OTP should be?
questions.explanation: Never shared with anyone is the correct answer. Shared with friends and Posted online are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Antivirus software helps to?
questions.explanation: Protect against malware is the correct answer. Increase RAM size and Print faster are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: More storage always means faster PC — this is?
questions.explanation: False / misconception is the correct answer. Same as more RAM always and Always true are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: CPU cabinet is the same as CPU chip — this is?
questions.explanation: False / misconception is the correct answer. Same as motherboard and True always are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.
