  11. Absolute reference example is __________.
  12. Key used to toggle reference type is __________.
  13. Error for divide by zero is __________.
  14. Feature to show only matching records is __________.
  15. Default Excel file extension is __________.

D. Match the Following (10)

Column A Column B
1. SUM a. Conditional count
2. AVERAGE b. Add values
3. IF c. Mean value
4. COUNTIF d. Current date
5. TODAY e. Condition result
6. Ctrl + 1 f. Format Cells
7. Alt + = g. AutoSum
8. F4 h. Absolute reference toggle
9. Filter i. Show selected records
10. Print Area j. Print selected range

E. Viva Questions (15)

  1. What is MS Excel?
  2. What is the difference between workbook and worksheet?
  3. What is a cell address?
  4. What is a range?
  5. What is the use of formula bar?
  6. What is the syntax of SUM?
  7. What is the difference between COUNT and COUNTA?
  8. What is IF function?
  9. What is absolute reference?
  10. What is conditional formatting?
  11. What is the use of filter?
  12. What is the use of sort?
  13. What chart is best for comparing categories?
  14. What is #DIV/0! error?
  15. What is LibreOffice Calc?

F. Practical Questions (10)

  1. Create a marksheet for 10 students with 3 subjects.
  2. Calculate Total, Average, and Percentage using formulas.
  3. Add Pass/Fail column using IF.
  4. Apply currency format to fee column.
  5. Sort students by total marks descending.
  6. Filter students with total greater than 200.
  7. Apply conditional formatting for fail marks.
  8. Create a column chart of totals.
  9. Set print area and repeat header row.
  10. Export worksheet as PDF.

G. Case Studies (5)

Case Study 1: Class Marksheet

A teacher has marks of 30 students in three subjects and needs total, average, grade, and a chart.

Questions:

  1. Which Excel features are required?
  2. Which functions will you use?
  3. How will you identify fail students?
  4. Which chart is suitable?

Case Study 2: Salary Sheet

An office must calculate net salary for 20 employees using fixed allowance in one cell.

Questions:

  1. Which reference type is needed for allowance cell?
  2. Which functions are useful?
  3. Why use absolute reference?
  4. How will you format currency?

Case Study 3: Inventory Alert

A shop wants to highlight items with stock below 10.

Questions:

  1. Which feature highlights low stock?
  2. Which column should be checked?
  3. Why is sorting useful?
  4. How can a chart help?

Case Study 4: Fee Due Report

A school wants to see only students with pending fees.

Questions:

  1. Which feature shows selected records?
  2. Which formula finds total due amount?
  3. Why use date format?
  4. Why export PDF?

Case Study 5: Monthly Expense Analysis

A student tracks monthly expenses by category.

Questions:

  1. Which function finds total expense?
  2. Which chart shows category share?
  3. Why use budget vs actual columns?
  4. How can dashboard cells help?

H. Mini Projects (5)

  1. School Marksheet System with formulas, grades, chart, and PDF export.
  2. Personal Monthly Budget with categories, totals, and pie chart.
  3. Inventory Management Sheet with low-stock conditional formatting.
  4. Employee Salary Register with absolute reference for allowance/tax.
  5. Sales Dashboard with totals, KPI cells, and one chart.

Answer Keys

MCQ Answers

1-a | 2-a | 3-b | 4-b | 5-a | 6-a | 7-a | 8-b | 9-a | 10-a | 11-a | 12-a | 13-a | 14-c | 15-a | 16-a | 17-a | 18-a | 19-a | 20-a | 21-a | 22-a | 23-a | 24-a | 25-a | 26-a | 27-a | 28-a | 29-a | 30-a

True/False Answers

1-True | 2-False | 3-True | 4-True | 5-True | 6-True | 7-True | 8-False | 9-True | 10-True | 11-True | 12-True | 13-True | 14-False | 15-True | 16-True | 17-True | 18-True | 19-True | 20-False

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. spreadsheet
  2. worksheets
  3. letter
  4. =
  5. Ctrl + S
  6. Ctrl + 1
  7. SUM
  8. AVERAGE
  9. COUNT
  10. COUNTA
  11. $B$1
  12. F4
  13. #DIV/0!
  14. Filter
  15. .xlsx

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-c | 3-e | 4-a | 5-d | 6-f | 7-g | 8-h | 9-i | 10-j

Case Study Model Answers

  1. Use SUM, AVERAGE, IF, sort, filter, conditional formatting, chart; identify fail using IF or conditional formatting; column chart suitable.
  2. Use absolute reference for allowance; SUM and arithmetic formulas; absolute reference keeps allowance fixed; currency format for salary columns.
  3. Conditional formatting; stock quantity column; sorting helps see lowest stock first; chart shows stock comparison.
  4. Filter; SUM for due amounts; date format for due dates; PDF for professional sharing.
  5. SUM; pie chart; budget vs actual helps control spending; dashboard totals give quick summary.

Final Self-Check

  • I can explain workbook, worksheet, cell, range, and active cell.
  • I can enter and format text, numbers, currency, dates, and percentages.
  • I can use SUM, AVERAGE, MIN, MAX, COUNT, COUNTA, ROUND, IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF, TODAY, CONCAT, AND, and OR.
  • I can use relative, absolute, and mixed references with F4.
  • I can identify and fix common formula errors.
  • I can sort, filter, and apply conditional formatting.
  • I can create column, bar, line, pie, and area charts.
  • I can prepare marksheets, salary sheets, fee reports, and inventory sheets.
  • I can set print area, print titles, and export PDF.
  • I can answer Excel practical, viva, interview, and exam questions confidently.

chapters.title: Practice MCQs & Labs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs & Labs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 04: Spreadsheet

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

  2. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word

  3. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @

  4. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print

  5. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join

  6. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date

  7. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text

  8. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets

  9. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart

  10. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM

  11. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type

  12. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email

  13. Column chart for? a) Comparing categories b) Parts of whole only c) Email d) Boot

  14. Line chart for? a) Trend over time b) Pie parts c) Database keys d) Virus scan

  15. Sort data? a) Data tab → Sort b) Insert Chart c) Review d) Draw

  16. Filter adds? a) Dropdown arrows in headers b) Virus c) RAM d) Printer

  17. Conditional formatting? a) Format by rules b) Delete rows c) Install OS d) Send email

  18. Print area sets? a) Range to print b) RAM size c) CPU d) Monitor

  19. Print titles repeat? a) Header rows each page b) Footer only c) Delete data d) Chart only

  20. Workbook contains? a) Worksheets b) Only one cell c) Only charts d) Emails

  21. Worksheet is? a) Tab/sheet in workbook b) CPU c) Printer d) Browser

  22. Fill handle used to? a) Copy formula/extend series b) Print c) Shutdown d) Email

  23. Ctrl+1 opens? a) Format Cells b) Save c) Print d) New book

  24. Merge cells? a) Combine into one cell b) Delete sheet c) Scan d) Boot

  25. COUNTIF counts? a) Cells meeting criteria b) All sheets c) Printers d) Folders

  26. SUMIF adds? a) Values matching criteria b) All text c) Charts d) BIOS

  27. TODAY() returns? a) Current date b) Sum c) Average d) Text hello

  28. ROUND(3.456,2) gives? a) 3.46 b) 3.45 c) 3.5 always wrong d) 3456

  29. Percentage formula example? a) =B2/C2*100 b) =B2+C2 c) =SUM(text) d) =IF color

  30. Remove Duplicates? a) Data tab b) Home font c) Insert d) View

  31. 3D formula SUM(Sheet1:Sheet3!B2)? a) Sum B2 across sheets b) One cell typo c) Chart d) Email

  32. Link to Sheet2 A1? a) =Sheet2!A1 b) =A1Sheet2 c) =LINK(A1) d) =WEB(A1)

  33. Relative reference when copied down? a) Adjusts row/column b) Stays fixed always c) Deletes d) Prints

  34. F4 in Excel toggles? a) Absolute/relative refs b) Save c) Print d) Close

  35. Gridlines print option in? a) Page Layout b) Insert c) Review d) Mailings

  36. Chart inserted from? a) Insert tab b) File Close c) BIOS d) Task Manager

  37. Legend in chart shows? a) Series labels b) CPU c) RAM d) Password

  38. #DIV/0! error means?

a) Divide by zero b) Success c) Saved d) Printed

  1. Currency format shows? a) ₹ or $ amounts b) Only text c) Only dates d) Charts only

  2. Bar chart similar to? a) Column chart horizontal b) Pie only c) Line only d) Email

  3. AutoSum button does? a) Quick SUM formula b) Average only c) Print d) Save PDF

  4. Freeze panes keeps visible? a) Header/label rows while scroll b) CPU c) Printer d) Desktop

  5. Data validation restricts? a) Allowed cell input b) Internet c) OS install d) Email

  6. Concatenate joins? a) Text strings b) Sheets c) Printers d) Folders

  7. Ctrl+S in Excel? a) Save workbook b) Sum c) Sort d) Print

  8. Active cell shown in? a) Name box / formula bar b) Taskbar only c) BIOS d) Printer

  9. Shift+F11 adds? a) New worksheet b) New workbook always c) Chart d) Email

  10. Calc compatible with? a) Excel formats mostly b) Only video c) Only audio d) Only PDF edit

  11. Sorting Z to A is? a) Descending b) Ascending c) Random d) Print order

  12. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Spreadsheet.
  2. List five important terms from Spreadsheet.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet.
  4. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet.
  6. Define key concept in Spreadsheet.
  7. List five important terms from Spreadsheet.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet.
  9. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet.
  11. Define key concept in Spreadsheet.
  12. List five important terms from Spreadsheet.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet.
  14. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Spreadsheet study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Spreadsheet study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

chapters.title: Practical Lab Pack — Excel
chapters.heading: Practical Lab Pack — Excel
chapters.contentHtml:
Spreadsheet — Practical Lab Pack (CCC)

Software: MS Excel or LibreOffice Calc
Total labs: 6 · Time: ~12 hours practical

Lab 1: Data Entry (2 hrs)

  1. Create workbook CCC_Marks.xlsx
  2. Columns: Name, Roll, Sub1, Sub2, Sub3
  3. Enter 8 students with sample marks (0–100)
  4. Format header row bold, centre

Lab 2: Formulas (2 hrs)

  1. Column Total = sum of 3 subjects
  2. Column Average = average of 3 subjects
  3. Column Result = IF average >= 40, "Pass", else "Fail"
  4. Verify by changing one mark — formulas update

Lab 3: Functions (2 hrs)

  1. Below table calculate:
    • Highest marks (MAX)
    • Lowest marks (MIN)
    • Class average (AVERAGE)
    • Count pass students (COUNTIF)
  2. Label each result cell

Lab 4: Sort & Filter (1.5 hrs)

  1. Sort table by Total descending
  2. Enable filter — show only Pass students
  3. Filter one subject marks > 75

Lab 5: Conditional Formatting (1.5 hrs)

  1. Highlight marks < 40 in red
  2. Highlight marks >= 75 in green
  3. Data bars on Total column (optional)

Lab 6: Chart (3 hrs)

  1. Column chart — student names vs Total marks
  2. Pie chart — Pass vs Fail count
  3. Add chart titles and axis labels
  4. Set print area; repeat header row; print preview

Trainer Sign-off Sheet

Lab Student Name Date Initial
1–6

Answer Key — Module 04: Spreadsheet

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt Answer: a) .xlsx Explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  2. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  3. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @ Answer: a) = Explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

  4. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print Answer: a) Addition/total Explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

  5. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join Answer: a) Mean value Explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  6. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date Answer: a) Largest value Explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text Answer: a) Smallest value Explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  8. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets Answer: a) Numeric cells Explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart Answer: a) Column A row 1 Explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  10. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM Answer: a) $A$1 Explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  11. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type Answer: a) Condition true/false Explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

  12. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email Answer: a) Parts of whole Explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  13. Column chart for? a) Comparing categories b) Parts of whole only c) Email d) Boot Answer: a) Comparing categories Explanation: Comparing categories — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  14. Line chart for? a) Trend over time b) Pie parts c) Database keys d) Virus scan Answer: a) Trend over time Explanation: Trend over time — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  15. Sort data? a) Data tab → Sort b) Insert Chart c) Review d) Draw Answer: a) Data tab → Sort Explanation: Data tab → Sort is the correct answer. Insert Chart and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Filter adds? a) Dropdown arrows in headers b) Virus c) RAM d) Printer Answer: a) Dropdown arrows in headers Explanation: Dropdown arrows in headers is the correct answer. Virus and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Conditional formatting? a) Format by rules b) Delete rows c) Install OS d) Send email Answer: a) Format by rules Explanation: Format by rules — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  18. Print area sets? a) Range to print b) RAM size c) CPU d) Monitor Answer: a) Range to print Explanation: Range to print is the correct answer. RAM size and CPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  19. Print titles repeat? a) Header rows each page b) Footer only c) Delete data d) Chart only Answer: a) Header rows each page Explanation: Header rows each page is the correct answer. Footer only and Delete data are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. Workbook contains? a) Worksheets b) Only one cell c) Only charts d) Emails Answer: a) Worksheets Explanation: Worksheets is the correct answer. Only one cell and Only charts are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  21. Worksheet is? a) Tab/sheet in workbook b) CPU c) Printer d) Browser Answer: a) Tab/sheet in workbook Explanation: Tab/sheet in workbook is the correct answer. CPU and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  22. Fill handle used to? a) Copy formula/extend series b) Print c) Shutdown d) Email Answer: a) Copy formula/extend series Explanation: Copy formula/extend series is the main purpose or function described in the question. Print and Shutdown are not the right choices here.

  23. Ctrl+1 opens? a) Format Cells b) Save c) Print d) New book Answer: a) Format Cells Explanation: Use Format Cells for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  24. Merge cells? a) Combine into one cell b) Delete sheet c) Scan d) Boot Answer: a) Combine into one cell Explanation: Combine into one cell is the correct answer. Delete sheet and Scan are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  25. COUNTIF counts? a) Cells meeting criteria b) All sheets c) Printers d) Folders Answer: a) Cells meeting criteria Explanation: Cells meeting criteria is the correct answer. All sheets and Printers are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. SUMIF adds? a) Values matching criteria b) All text c) Charts d) BIOS Answer: a) Values matching criteria Explanation: Values matching criteria is the correct answer. All text and Charts are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  27. TODAY() returns? a) Current date b) Sum c) Average d) Text hello Answer: a) Current date Explanation: Current date is the correct answer. Sum and Average are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. ROUND(3.456,2) gives? a) 3.46 b) 3.45 c) 3.5 always wrong d) 3456 Answer: a) 3.46 Explanation: 3.46 is the correct answer. 3.45 and 3.5 always wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Percentage formula example? a) =B2/C2100 b) =B2+C2 c) =SUM(text) d) =IF color *Answer: a) =B2/C2100* Explanation: =B2/C2*100 is a correct example for what the question asks about. =B2+C2 and =SUM(text) are not the right choices here.

  30. Remove Duplicates? a) Data tab b) Home font c) Insert d) View Answer: a) Data tab Explanation: Data tab is the correct answer. Home font and Insert are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  31. 3D formula SUM(Sheet1:Sheet3!B2)? a) Sum B2 across sheets b) One cell typo c) Chart d) Email Answer: a) Sum B2 across sheets Explanation: Sum B2 across sheets is the correct answer. One cell typo and Chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Link to Sheet2 A1? a) =Sheet2!A1 b) =A1Sheet2 c) =LINK(A1) d) =WEB(A1) Answer: a) =Sheet2!A1 Explanation: =Sheet2!A1 is the correct answer. =A1Sheet2 and =LINK(A1) are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Relative reference when copied down? a) Adjusts row/column b) Stays fixed always c) Deletes d) Prints Answer: a) Adjusts row/column Explanation: Adjusts row/column is the correct answer. Stays fixed always and Deletes are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  34. F4 in Excel toggles? a) Absolute/relative refs b) Save c) Print d) Close Answer: a) Absolute/relative refs Explanation: Use Absolute/relative refs for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  35. Gridlines print option in? a) Page Layout b) Insert c) Review d) Mailings Answer: a) Page Layout Explanation: Page Layout is the correct answer. Insert and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  36. Chart inserted from? a) Insert tab b) File Close c) BIOS d) Task Manager Answer: a) Insert tab Explanation: Insert tab is the correct answer. File Close and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  37. Legend in chart shows? a) Series labels b) CPU c) RAM d) Password Answer: a) Series labels Explanation: Series labels is the correct answer. CPU and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. #DIV/0! error means?

a) Divide by zero b) Success c) Saved d) Printed Answer: a) Divide by zero Explanation: Divide by zero is the correct answer. Success and Saved are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  1. Currency format shows? a) ₹ or $ amounts b) Only text c) Only dates d) Charts only Answer: a) ₹ or $ amounts Explanation: ₹ or $ amounts is the correct answer. Only text and Only dates are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  2. Bar chart similar to? a) Column chart horizontal b) Pie only c) Line only d) Email Answer: a) Column chart horizontal Explanation: Column chart horizontal is the correct answer. Pie only and Line only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  3. AutoSum button does? a) Quick SUM formula b) Average only c) Print d) Save PDF Answer: a) Quick SUM formula Explanation: Quick SUM formula is the correct answer. Average only and Print are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  4. Freeze panes keeps visible? a) Header/label rows while scroll b) CPU c) Printer d) Desktop Answer: a) Header/label rows while scroll Explanation: Header/label rows while scroll — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  5. Data validation restricts? a) Allowed cell input b) Internet c) OS install d) Email Answer: a) Allowed cell input Explanation: Allowed cell input is the correct answer. Internet and OS install are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  6. Concatenate joins? a) Text strings b) Sheets c) Printers d) Folders Answer: a) Text strings Explanation: Text strings is the correct answer. Sheets and Printers are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7. Ctrl+S in Excel? a) Save workbook b) Sum c) Sort d) Print Answer: a) Save workbook Explanation: Use Save workbook for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  8. Active cell shown in? a) Name box / formula bar b) Taskbar only c) BIOS d) Printer Answer: a) Name box / formula bar Explanation: Name box / formula bar is the correct answer. Taskbar only and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Shift+F11 adds? a) New worksheet b) New workbook always c) Chart d) Email Answer: a) New worksheet Explanation: Use New worksheet for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  10. Calc compatible with? a) Excel formats mostly b) Only video c) Only audio d) Only PDF edit Answer: a) Excel formats mostly Explanation: Excel formats mostly is the correct answer. Only video and Only audio are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  11. Sorting Z to A is? a) Descending b) Ascending c) Random d) Print order Answer: a) Descending Explanation: Descending is the correct answer. Ascending and Random are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  12. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt Answer: a) .xlsx Explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

Short Answer Key

  1. Define key concept in Spreadsheet. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  2. List five important terms from Spreadsheet. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  3. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  4. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  5. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  6. Define key concept in Spreadsheet. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  7. List five important terms from Spreadsheet. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  8. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  9. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  10. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  11. Define key concept in Spreadsheet. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  12. List five important terms from Spreadsheet. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  13. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  14. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  15. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

Practical Checklist Key

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Spreadsheet study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  2. Complete lab task 2 from Spreadsheet study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  6. Peer review exercise in lab. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  8. Document steps in notebook. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

questions.question: Excel extension?
questions.explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Calc is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Formula starts with?
questions.explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

questions.question: SUM() used for?
questions.explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

questions.question: AVERAGE() returns?
questions.explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MAX() returns?
questions.explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MIN() returns?
questions.explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: COUNT() counts?
questions.explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cell A1 is?
questions.explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Absolute reference?
questions.explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: IF function checks?
questions.explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Pie chart best for?
questions.explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Column chart for?
questions.explanation: Comparing categories — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Line chart for?
questions.explanation: Trend over time — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Sort data?
questions.explanation: Data tab → Sort is the correct answer. Insert Chart and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Filter adds?
questions.explanation: Dropdown arrows in headers is the correct answer. Virus and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Conditional formatting?
questions.explanation: Format by rules — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Print area sets?

questions.question: Fill handle used to?

questions.question: Ctrl+1 opens?

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Module 5: Presentation with MS PowerPoint (8 Hours)

Course: CCC 90-Hour DCA | Module duration: 8 hours
Primary software: Microsoft PowerPoint
Alternative reference: LibreOffice Impress as an open-source equivalent
Modern tools: AI-assisted presentation platforms such as ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Canva AI, and Gamma

Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Explain what a presentation is and why MS PowerPoint is used in education, business, government, and corporate communication.
  • Create multi-slide presentations manually using Microsoft PowerPoint.
  • Apply themes, layouts, slide master, and professional design principles.
  • Add and format text, bullets, images, shapes, icons, SmartArt, charts, tables, audio, video, hyperlinks, and action buttons.
  • Use slide transitions and object animations appropriately.
  • Run slideshows with keyboard navigation, presenter view, notes, and audience-friendly delivery skills.
  • Export presentations as PDF, video, handouts, and slideshow files.
  • Understand the evolution of presentation creation from manual slides to templates to AI-assisted workflows.
  • Use AI tools responsibly to generate outlines, slide content, speaker notes, and design suggestions.
  • Compare AI-generated presentations with manually created PowerPoint presentations.
  • Apply prompt engineering to create better AI presentation outputs.
  • Connect presentation skills with careers such as teacher, trainer, sales professional, project manager, and startup founder.

1. Introduction to Presentation Software

What It Is

A presentation is a sequence of slides shown to an audience to explain, teach, persuade, or inform. Microsoft PowerPoint is the most widely used presentation software in schools, colleges, offices, banks, government programs, training centers, and corporate meetings.

Why It Is Used

Presentations help speakers organize ideas visually. Instead of speaking without structure, PowerPoint allows the presenter to show headings, bullet points, images, charts, diagrams, and summaries one slide at a time.

Where It Is Used

  • Classrooms and online classes.
  • Seminars and workshops.
  • Project reviews and viva presentations.
  • Business meetings and sales pitches.
  • Government awareness campaigns.
  • Training sessions and onboarding programs.
  • Startup pitch decks and investor presentations.

MS PowerPoint Method

  1. Click Start.
  2. Search PowerPoint.
  3. Open Microsoft PowerPoint.
  4. Click Blank Presentation.
  5. Press Ctrl + S and save as my-first-presentation.pptx.

Impress Equivalent

LibreOffice Impress is an open-source alternative that saves in .odp format and supports similar slide creation, transitions, and slideshow delivery.

Real-World Example

A student creates an 8-slide presentation on Digital India for a classroom seminar, including title slide, introduction, key points, chart, image, summary, and thank-you slide.

Practice Exercise

Create a 3-slide PowerPoint presentation with title, one content slide, and thank-you slide.

2. Evolution of Presentation Creation

Presentation creation has changed significantly over time.

Traditional Era

Presenters manually created each slide by typing text, inserting images, and designing layouts from scratch. This required more time and design skill.

Template Era

Ready-made templates and themes made presentations faster and more consistent. Users could focus more on content and less on design.

AI Era

Modern AI presentation tools can generate slide structure, titles, bullet points, speaker notes, image suggestions, and design ideas from a prompt within minutes.

Example Prompt

Create a 10-slide presentation on Digital India for college students.
Include introduction, benefits, government services, challenges, and conclusion.
Add speaker notes for each slide.

AI can generate:

  • Slide outline.
  • Titles and content.
  • Design suggestions.
  • Speaker notes.
  • Summary points.

Important Lesson

AI assists presenters. AI does not replace presentation skills, audience understanding, or personal delivery ability.

3. Two Methods of Presentation Creation

Method 1: Traditional Presentation Creation

Use Microsoft PowerPoint directly with:

  • Blank presentations.
  • Built-in templates.
  • Themes and variants.
  • SmartArt.
  • Charts.
  • Images and icons.
  • Transitions and animations.
  • Slide master and presenter view.

Best for: Full control, professional branding, exam/practical work, and long-term skill building.

Method 2: AI-Assisted Presentation Creation

Use AI tools to speed up content and design:

  • ChatGPT
  • Microsoft Copilot
  • Google Gemini
  • Claude
  • Canva AI
  • Gamma
  • Tome
  • Beautiful.ai
  • SlidesAI
  • Presentations.ai
  • Prezi AI
  • Pitch AI

Best for: Fast drafts, brainstorming, outline creation, and beginner-friendly visual suggestions.

Recommended Workflow

  1. Use AI to generate outline and draft content.
  2. Review facts and remove generic text.
  3. Transfer/improve content in PowerPoint.
  4. Apply professional design rules.
  5. Practice delivery before presenting.

4. Presentation Fundamentals

Slide

One screen of content in a presentation.

Slide Layout

Predefined arrangement of placeholders such as title, subtitle, content, image, or comparison.

Theme

Coordinated set of colors, fonts, backgrounds, and effects applied across slides.

Transition

Visual effect applied between slides when moving from one slide to the next.

Animation

Visual effect applied to objects within a slide, such as text, images, or shapes.

Notes Pane

Area where presenter notes are written; visible in Presenter View but not usually shown to audience.

Slide Master

Master layout that controls consistent design across all slides.

Handout

Printed version showing multiple slides per page for audience reference.

Slideshow

Full-screen presentation mode used during delivery.

Visual Diagram

Presentation File (.pptx)
   |
   +-- Slide 1 (Title)
   +-- Slide 2 (Introduction)
   +-- Slide 3 (Key Point)
   +-- Slide 4 (Chart/Image)
   +-- Slide 5 (Summary)
   +-- Slide 6 (Thank You)

5. MS PowerPoint Interface and Views

Ribbon Interface

PowerPoint uses a ribbon with tabs such as Home, Insert, Design, Transitions, Animations, Slide Show, Review, and View.

Tab Main Use
Home Slides, font, paragraph, drawing
Insert Pictures, shapes, chart, table, video, audio
Design Themes, variants, slide size
Transitions Effects between slides
Animations Object animations
Slide Show Start slideshow, presenter view
View Normal, Slide Sorter, Notes, Reading View

Views

View MS PowerPoint Method Use
Normal Default editing view Create and edit slides
Slide Sorter View -> Slide Sorter Reorder slides
Reading View View -> Reading View Preview like audience
Slide Show F5 Present to audience
Notes Page View -> Notes Page Edit speaker notes

Impress Equivalent

Impress provides Normal, Slide Sorter, and Slide Show views with similar purposes, though menus and sidebars differ.

Practice Exercise

Open PowerPoint, switch between Normal, Slide Sorter, and Reading View, and identify the Notes pane.

6. Creating and Managing Slides

What It Is

Slide management includes creating, duplicating, deleting, reordering, and choosing layouts.

Why It Is Used

Good slide organization makes the presentation logical and easy to follow.

MS PowerPoint Method

New Slide

  • Home -> New Slide.
  • Shortcut: Ctrl + M.

Common Layouts

  • Title Slide
  • Title and Content
  • Section Header
  • Two Content
  • Comparison
  • Blank

Duplicate Slide

  • Right-click slide thumbnail -> Duplicate Slide.
  • Shortcut: Ctrl + D.

Delete Slide

  • Select slide -> press Delete.

Reorder Slides

  • Drag slide thumbnail in left pane.
  • Or use Slide Sorter view.

Text and Bullets

  • Title placeholder: main heading.
  • Content placeholder: bullet points.
  • Tab: increase bullet level.
  • Shift + Tab: decrease bullet level.

6×6 Rule

Use about 6 bullets maximum with roughly 6 words each per slide to keep slides readable.

Real-World Example

A cyber awareness presentation uses one idea per slide: phishing, malware, strong passwords, safe browsing, reporting, and summary.

Practice Exercise

Create 6 slides using different layouts and apply the 6×6 rule.

7. Design, Themes, and Professional Formatting

Themes

MS PowerPoint Method

  1. Design tab -> Themes.
  2. Choose a theme.
  3. Apply Variants if needed.

Design Ideas

In Microsoft 365, Design Ideas can suggest layouts for slides based on content.

Slide Size

  • Standard 4:3
  • Widescreen 16:9 (recommended for projectors and modern screens)

Background Formatting

Design -> Format Background -> solid, gradient, picture, or texture.

Presentation Design Best Practices

Rule Meaning
6×6 Rule Short bullet points
Visual Hierarchy Title largest, body smaller
Consistent Fonts 1-2 fonts only
Color Harmony Matching theme colors
Minimal Text Slides support speech, not replace it
High Contrast Dark text on light background or reverse
Storytelling Structure Beginning, body, conclusion
Audience-Centric Design Design for audience needs
Professional Branding Use institute/company colors consistently

Common Mistakes

  • Too much text on one slide.
  • Too many fonts and colors.
  • Distracting transitions.
  • Low-contrast text.
  • Reading slides word-for-word.

Practice Exercise

Apply one theme to all slides and improve contrast and spacing.

8. Insert Objects in PowerPoint

Object Purpose MS PowerPoint Method
Pictures Photos, logos Insert -> Pictures
Shapes Diagrams, arrows, boxes Insert -> Shapes
Icons Simple visual symbols Insert -> Icons
SmartArt Process, hierarchy, cycle diagrams Insert -> SmartArt
Chart Visual data from Excel Insert -> Chart
Table Small tabular data Insert -> Table
Audio Background or explanation sound Insert -> Audio
Video Demo clip Insert -> Video
Screen Recording Capture screen activity Insert -> Screen Recording
Hyperlink Link to website/file/slide Insert -> Link
Action Button Jump to slide/website Insert -> Action

SmartArt Use Cases

  • Process flow.
  • Organization chart.
  • Cycle diagram.
  • Relationship diagram.

Chart Use Cases

  • Subject-wise marks comparison.
  • Monthly sales trend.
  • Pass/fail proportion.

Impress Equivalent

Impress supports most insert options through Insert menu, though SmartArt equivalents may differ.

Practice Exercise

Insert one picture, one shape, one SmartArt diagram, and one chart in separate slides.

9. Slide Master, Notes, and Presenter View

Slide Master

What It Is

Slide Master controls default fonts, colors, placeholders, and footer/header design for all slides.

Why It Is Used

It ensures consistency and saves time when editing branding elements.

MS PowerPoint Method

View -> Slide Master -> edit master layout -> Close Master View.

Notes Pane

Use notes to write what the presenter will say but not show on screen.

Presenter View

What It Is

Presenter View shows current slide, next slide, timer, and notes on the presenter's screen while audience sees only the slide.

MS PowerPoint Method

Slide Show tab -> Use Presenter View -> start slideshow.

Practice Exercise

Add speaker notes to three slides and run Presenter View.

10. Transitions and Animations

Transitions (Between Slides)

What It Is

Transition is the effect when moving from one slide to another.

MS PowerPoint Method

  1. Select slide.
  2. Transitions tab -> choose effect.
  3. Set duration.
  4. Choose On Mouse Click or After time.
  5. Apply To All if needed.

Best Practices

Use simple effects such as Fade, Push, or Wipe in formal presentations.

Animations (On-Slide Objects)

What It Is

Animation affects objects such as text, images, or shapes within a slide.

MS PowerPoint Method

  1. Select object.
  2. Animations tab -> choose effect.
  3. Set Start: On Click / With Previous / After Previous.
  4. Use Animation Pane to control order.

Animation Types

Type Example
Entrance Appear, Fade, Fly In
Emphasis Pulse, Grow/Shrink
Exit Disappear, Fade Out
Motion Path Move along custom path

Real Example

Bullet points appear one by one on click during a classroom presentation.

Common Mistakes

  • Using too many spinning or bouncing effects.
  • Animating every object unnecessarily.
  • Making transitions so slow that audience loses focus.

Impress Equivalent

Impress uses Slide Transition deck and Custom Animation sidebar for similar tasks.

Practice Exercise

Apply one simple transition to all slides and animate a bullet list to appear one by one.

11. Essential MS PowerPoint Shortcuts

Presentation Management

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + N New presentation
Ctrl + O Open presentation
Ctrl + S Save presentation
Ctrl + P Print
F12 Save As
Ctrl + W Close presentation

Slide Management

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + M New slide
Ctrl + D Duplicate slide
Delete Delete selected slide
Ctrl + Shift + D Duplicate object in some contexts / duplicate formatting workflows

Slide Show

Shortcut Use
F5 Start slideshow from beginning
Shift + F5 Start from current slide
Esc End slideshow
B Black screen
W White screen
N / Page Down / Space / Enter Next slide
P / Page Up / Backspace Previous slide
Ctrl + P Pen tool in slideshow (PowerPoint)

Formatting

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + B Bold
Ctrl + I Italic
Ctrl + U Underline
Ctrl + E Center align
Ctrl + L Left align
Ctrl + R Right align

Shortcut Drills

Drill 1

Create a presentation using Ctrl + N, add slides with Ctrl + M, save with Ctrl + S.

Drill 2

Duplicate one slide using Ctrl + D and reorder it in Slide Sorter view.

Drill 3

Run slideshow with F5, move slides with arrow keys, use B for black screen, Esc to exit.

Drill 4

Apply bold and center alignment using Ctrl + B and Ctrl + E.

12. Print and Export

Print Options

  • Full page slides.
  • Handouts: 2, 3, 4, 6, or 9 slides per page.
  • Notes pages.
  • Outline.

MS PowerPoint Method

  1. File -> Print.
  2. Choose layout.
  3. Preview.
  4. Print or save as PDF.

Export PDF

File -> Save As -> PDF.

Export Video

File -> Export -> Create a Video (.mp4).

Slideshow File

Save as .ppsx to open directly in slideshow mode.

Professional Reporting Standards

  • Use clear file names such as Digital_India_Presentation_Rasmita.pptx.
  • Export PDF for submission.
  • Check spelling before final export.
  • Verify all images and charts are visible.

Impress Equivalent

Impress supports Export as PDF and slideshow export options, though video export features vary by version.

Practice Exercise

Export your presentation as PDF and create a 6-slide-per-page handout print preview.

13. AI Presentation Tools

Modern learners should understand both manual PowerPoint skills and responsible AI use.

ChatGPT

Uses

  • Generate slide content.
  • Create presentation outline.
  • Write speaker notes.
  • Summarize topics.
  • Prepare Q&A responses.

Limitations

  • Does not inherently design polished slides.
  • May generate incorrect facts.
  • Requires human review.

Microsoft Copilot for PowerPoint

Uses

  • Generate presentations directly in PowerPoint.
  • Convert Word documents into slides.
  • Suggest layouts and speaker notes.

Advantages

  • Integrated into Microsoft ecosystem.

Limitations

  • Requires Microsoft 365 licensing in many setups.

Google Gemini

Uses

  • Generate presentation content.
  • Research assistance.
  • Outline creation.

Claude

Uses

  • Structured content generation.
  • Summaries and speaker notes.
  • Alternative drafting support.

Canva AI

Uses

  • AI presentation generation.
  • Visual design.
  • Social-media-ready presentations.

Advantages

  • Extremely beginner friendly.

Limitations

  • Some features require premium plans.

Gamma

Uses

  • Generates presentations from prompts.
  • Modern web-based slide decks.

Advantages

  • Fast creation.

Limitations

  • Limited customization compared to PowerPoint.

Tome

Uses

  • Storytelling presentations.
  • AI-generated visual content.

Beautiful.ai

Uses

  • Automatic slide design.
  • Professional layouts.

SlidesAI

Uses

  • Converts text into Google Slides presentations.

Presentations.ai

Uses

  • End-to-end AI presentation generation.

Prezi AI / Pitch AI

Used for non-linear or business-style modern presentations with AI assistance.

14. AI vs Manual Presentation Creation

Factor AI Tools Manual PowerPoint
Speed Very fast Slower
Creativity Moderate High
Customization Limited Extensive
Accuracy Needs review User controlled
Learning Value Lower Higher
Professional Control Moderate Excellent
Cost Often subscription-based One-time software/license
Design Skills Needed Low Moderate
Branding Control Limited Strong
Exam/Practical Readiness Needs manual refinement Best for formal assessment

Advantages of AI Presentations

  • Faster creation.
  • Better visual suggestions for beginners.
  • Content summarization.
  • Automatic layouts.
  • Time saving.
  • Helpful for rapid prototyping.

Limitations of AI Presentations

  • Hallucinated or incorrect information.
  • Generic and repetitive content.
  • Repetitive design patterns.
  • Limited domain expertise.
  • Confidentiality risks if sensitive data is pasted.
  • Weak audience-specific understanding.
  • Weak storytelling without human editing.
  • Lack of personal experience and authenticity.

Important Lesson

AI assists presenters. AI does not replace presentation skills.

15. Prompt Engineering for Presentation Creation

Prompt quality directly affects AI output quality.

Poor Prompt

Create presentation on AI

Problem: too vague; no audience, slide count, structure, or purpose.

Better Prompt

Create a 10-slide presentation on Artificial Intelligence for DCA students.
Include introduction, definition, applications, benefits, risks, career opportunities, and conclusion.
Add short speaker notes for each slide.
Use simple language suitable for beginner computer students.

Strong Prompt Checklist

  • Topic.
  • Audience.
  • Number of slides.
  • Required sections.
  • Tone/language level.
  • Need for speaker notes.
  • Visual suggestions if required.
  • Exam/business purpose.

Practice Exercise

Write one poor prompt and one improved prompt for a presentation on Cyber Security Awareness.

16. Business and Academic Applications

Education

Teachers and students use PowerPoint for lessons, seminars, project presentations, and online classes.

Business Meetings

Managers present reports, plans, and performance summaries.

Sales Presentations

Sales teams present product features, pricing, and benefits to customers.

Project Proposals

Teams explain project scope, timeline, budget, and outcomes.

Government Awareness Programs

Digital India, cyber safety, health campaigns, and welfare scheme awareness.

Training Sessions

Trainers use slides for step-by-step instructions and demonstrations.

Research Presentations

Students and researchers present findings with charts and summary slides.

Startup Pitch Decks

Founders present problem, solution, market, business model, and funding need.

Investor Presentations

Startups and businesses present growth plans and financial projections.

17. Presentation Skills

Creating slides is only part of success. Delivery matters equally.

Public Speaking

Speak clearly, confidently, and at a comfortable pace.

Eye Contact

Look at the audience, not only at the screen.

Voice Modulation

Change tone and emphasis to maintain interest.

Slide Navigation

Practice moving forward and backward smoothly.

Audience Engagement

Ask questions, use examples, and relate content to audience needs.

Handling Questions

Listen carefully, answer briefly, and admit if you need to check facts.

Presentation Confidence

Comes from preparation, rehearsal, and knowing your content.

Best Delivery Tips

  • Rehearse at least once.
  • Do not read every word from slides.
  • Keep slides visual and simple.
  • Use notes or Presenter View.
  • Keep backup PDF copy.
  • Arrive early to test projector/laptop.

18. Real-World Projects

Project 1: Digital India Presentation

8-10 slides covering introduction, services, benefits, examples, and conclusion.

Project 2: Cyber Security Awareness Presentation

Include phishing, malware, password safety, safe browsing, and reporting.

Project 3: AI in Education Presentation

Explain benefits, risks, tools, and responsible use.

Project 4: NITA Academy Course Presentation

Present course modules, duration, skills, and career benefits.

Project 5: Business Startup Pitch Deck

Include problem, solution, market, product, revenue model, team, and ask.

19. AI-Powered Practical Exercises

Exercise 1: ChatGPT Content Draft

Use ChatGPT to generate a 8-slide outline on Digital India. Copy content into PowerPoint and redesign manually.

Exercise 2: Canva AI Presentation

Generate a visual presentation in Canva AI and compare design with PowerPoint.

Exercise 3: Gamma Prompt Presentation

Create a web-based presentation in Gamma using a detailed prompt.

Exercise 4: Compare AI vs Manual

Create one slide set manually in PowerPoint and one using AI. Compare speed, accuracy, design, and control.

Exercise 5: Improve AI Output in PowerPoint

Take AI-generated content and improve it using 6×6 rule, theme, contrast, and better images.

20. Practical Lab Work

Lab 1: PowerPoint Basics

  • Guided Exercise: Create blank presentation and identify views.
  • Independent Exercise: Create 5 slides with different layouts.
  • Classroom Activity: Reorder slides in Slide Sorter view.
  • Home Assignment: Create topic outline for 8-slide presentation.
  • Mini Project: 8-slide Digital India deck.

Lab 2: Design and Themes

  • Guided Exercise: Apply one theme to all slides.
  • Independent Exercise: Improve contrast and font consistency.
  • Classroom Activity: Review good vs bad slide examples.
  • Home Assignment: Redesign a crowded slide using 6×6 rule.
  • Mini Project: Professional institute awareness deck.

Lab 3: Objects and SmartArt

  • Guided Exercise: Insert picture and shape.
  • Independent Exercise: Insert SmartArt process diagram.
  • Classroom Activity: Compare chart vs table use.
  • Home Assignment: Add one chart slide.
  • Mini Project: Cyber Security Awareness deck with image and SmartArt.

Lab 4: Transitions and Animations

  • Guided Exercise: Apply Fade transition to all slides.
  • Independent Exercise: Animate bullet list one-by-one.
  • Classroom Activity: Identify overused animation examples.
  • Home Assignment: Remove unnecessary animations from a sample deck.
  • Mini Project: Training session deck with simple animation.

Lab 5: Delivery and Export

  • Guided Exercise: Run slideshow with F5.
  • Independent Exercise: Use Presenter View and notes.
  • Classroom Activity: Practice B, W, Esc, and arrow keys.
  • Home Assignment: Export PDF and handout layout.
  • Mini Project: Final presentation ready for classroom delivery.

Lab 6: AI Workflow

  • Guided Exercise: Write a strong AI prompt.
  • Independent Exercise: Generate outline using ChatGPT or Gemini.
  • Classroom Activity: Fact-check AI content.
  • Home Assignment: Improve AI draft in PowerPoint.
  • Mini Project: AI-assisted then manually refined presentation.

21. Career Awareness

Presentation skills are valuable in many careers.

Career Why Presentation Skills Matter
Teacher Explain lessons clearly with visuals
Trainer Conduct workshops and demonstrations
Corporate Executive Present reports and strategies
Sales Professional Pitch products and close deals
Business Analyst Present findings and recommendations
Data Analyst Explain charts and insights
Project Manager Share timelines, risks, and progress
Startup Founder Pitch ideas to customers and investors
AI Consultant Explain tools, benefits, and limitations

22. Interview and Viva Questions

Basic Questions

  1. What is MS PowerPoint?
  2. What is a slide?
  3. What is the difference between transition and animation?
  4. What is a theme?
  5. What is Presenter View?

Practical Questions

  1. How do you insert a new slide?
  2. How do you start slideshow from beginning?
  3. How do you export presentation as PDF?
  4. How do you apply animation to bullet points?
  5. How do you insert a chart or SmartArt?

AI-Related Questions

  1. What are advantages of AI presentation tools?
  2. What are limitations of AI-generated slides?
  3. Why should AI content be reviewed?
  4. What makes a good AI prompt?
  5. Can AI replace a skilled presenter?

Model Answer Example

MS PowerPoint is presentation software used to create slide-based visual presentations for teaching, business, training, and seminars. Transition affects movement between slides, while animation affects objects within a slide.

23. Quick Revision Sheet

Most Important PowerPoint Features

  • New slide, duplicate slide, slide layouts.
  • Themes and slide master.
  • Insert picture, shape, SmartArt, chart, table.
  • Transitions and animations.
  • Presenter View and notes.
  • Print, PDF export, handouts.

Presentation Design Rules

  • 6×6 rule.
  • One main idea per slide.
  • Consistent theme and fonts.
  • High contrast.
  • Minimal text.
  • Simple transitions.

Essential Shortcuts

F5, Shift + F5, Esc, B, W, Ctrl + M, Ctrl + D, Ctrl + S, Ctrl + P, Ctrl + B, Ctrl + I, Ctrl + U.

AI Presentation Tools Summary

ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, Claude, Canva AI, Gamma, Tome, Beautiful.ai, SlidesAI, Presentations.ai.

AI Advantages and Limitations

Fast drafts and outline support, but requires fact-checking, manual design refinement, and responsible use.

Exam Tips

  • Transition = between slides.
  • Animation = within slide.
  • F5 = slideshow from start.
  • Shift + F5 = from current slide.
  • .pptx = PowerPoint file.
  • .odp = Impress file.
  • 6×6 rule keeps slides readable.

24. Assessment Enhancement

A. Multiple Choice Questions (30)

1. MS PowerPoint is mainly used for:

  • a) Presentations
  • b) Spreadsheets
  • c) Databases
  • d) Video editing only

2. One screen in a presentation is called a:

  • a) Workbook
  • b) Slide
  • c) Cell
  • d) Folder

3. Default PowerPoint file extension is:

  • a) .docx
  • b) .pptx
  • c) .xlsx
  • d) .pdf

4. Shortcut to start slideshow from beginning:

  • a) F5
  • b) F2
  • c) Ctrl + S
  • d) Ctrl + P

5. Shift + F5 starts slideshow:

  • a) From current slide
  • b) From first slide always
  • c) In notes view only
  • d) Without keyboard

6. Transition applies to:

  • a) Movement between slides
  • b) Text color only
  • c) Printer setup
  • d) File saving

7. Animation applies to:

  • a) Objects on a slide
  • b) Only first slide
  • c) Operating system
  • d) Spreadsheet cells

8. Shortcut for new slide:

  • a) Ctrl + M
  • b) Ctrl + N
  • c) Ctrl + P
  • d) F7

9. Shortcut for duplicate slide:

  • a) Ctrl + D
  • b) Ctrl + B
  • c) Ctrl + F
  • d) Alt + D

10. Presenter View is used to:

  • a) Show notes and next slide to presenter
  • b) Delete slides
  • c) Install printer
  • d) Edit Excel data

11. 6×6 rule suggests:

  • a) About 6 short bullets per slide
  • b) 6 fonts per slide
  • c) 6 hours per slide
  • d) 6 themes per slide

12. SmartArt is used for:

  • a) Diagrams and process visuals
  • b) Antivirus scanning
  • c) Booting computer
  • d) Database queries

13. Handout printing means:

  • a) Multiple slides per printed page
  • b) Printing only animations
  • c) Printing BIOS
  • d) Printing one cell

14. .ppsx file:

  • a) Opens directly in slideshow mode
  • b) Is a Word file
  • c) Is an Excel macro
  • d) Is a Linux command

15. LibreOffice presentation software is:

  • a) Impress
  • b) Writer
  • c) Calc
  • d) Base only

16. Impress default extension is:

  • a) .odp
  • b) .pptx
  • c) .ods
  • d) .odt

17. Esc during slideshow:

  • a) Ends slideshow
  • b) Saves file
  • c) Inserts slide
  • d) Opens chart

18. B during slideshow:

  • a) Black screen
  • b) Bold text
  • c) Backup file
  • d) Bullet list

19. Notes pane is used for:

  • a) Speaker notes
  • b) Printer settings
  • c) CPU details
  • d) File extensions

20. Slide Master controls:

  • a) Consistent design across slides
  • b) Internet speed
  • c) Keyboard layout
  • d) Antivirus updates

21. AI presentation tools are mainly useful for:

  • a) Faster draft creation
  • b) Replacing all presentation skills permanently
  • c) Removing need for rehearsal
  • d) Eliminating fact checking

22. Main limitation of AI-generated slides:

  • a) May contain incorrect information
  • b) Cannot create any text
  • c) Cannot be edited
  • d) Cannot be exported

23. Microsoft Copilot is especially useful with:

  • a) Microsoft 365 ecosystem
  • b) Only Linux terminal
  • c) Only BIOS setup
  • d) Only printer drivers

24. Canva AI is known for:

  • a) Beginner-friendly visual design
  • b) Spreadsheet formulas
  • c) Database tables
  • d) Operating system booting

25. Gamma is mainly:

  • a) Web-based AI presentation generator
  • b) Word processor
  • c) Antivirus
  • d) File manager

26. Better AI prompt should include:

  • a) Audience and slide structure
  • b) Only one word topic
  • c) No purpose
  • d) No slide count

27. PDF export is useful because:

  • a) Layout is preserved for sharing
  • b) It deletes animations automatically always
  • c) It converts slides into spreadsheet
  • d) It removes images

28. Best formal transition effect is usually:

  • a) Simple fade or push
  • b) Excessive spinning
  • c) Random sound only
  • d) No title slide

29. A good presentation slide should:

  • a) Support the speaker with clear visuals
  • b) Contain full speech word-for-word always
  • c) Use 10 fonts minimum
  • d) Avoid all images

30. AI assists presenters but:

  • a) Does not replace presentation skills
  • b) Removes need for audience engagement
  • c) Makes rehearsal unnecessary always
  • d) Guarantees perfect facts always

B. True/False (20)

  1. MS PowerPoint is presentation software.
  2. A slide is one screen of a presentation.
  3. Transition applies between slides.
  4. Animation applies to objects within a slide.
  5. F5 starts slideshow from beginning.
  6. Shift + F5 starts from current slide.
  7. Ctrl + M inserts a new slide.
  8. Presenter View helps the speaker see notes.
  9. 6×6 rule means long paragraphs on every slide.
  10. Themes provide consistent design.
  11. SmartArt helps create diagrams.
  12. AI-generated content should always be trusted without review.
  13. ChatGPT can help generate presentation outlines.
  14. Copilot can assist inside Microsoft PowerPoint.
  15. Canva AI is useful for visual presentations.
  16. Exporting PDF helps share final presentations professionally.
  17. Reading every word from slides is a good presentation habit.
  18. Impress is an open-source alternative to PowerPoint.
  19. Good prompts produce better AI presentation drafts.
  20. Presentation skills are useful in business and education.

C. Fill in the Blanks (15)

  1. MS PowerPoint is used to create __________.
  2. One screen in a presentation is called a __________.
  3. Default PowerPoint extension is __________.
  4. Shortcut to start slideshow from beginning is __________.
  5. Shortcut to start from current slide is __________.
  6. Shortcut for new slide is __________.
  7. Transition applies __________ slides.
  8. Animation applies to objects __________ a slide.
  9. Presenter View shows __________ to the speaker.
  10. 6×6 rule suggests short __________.
  11. SmartArt is used for __________.
  12. LibreOffice presentation software is __________.
  13. Impress file extension is __________.
  14. AI tools can generate presentation __________ quickly.
  15. AI content must be __________ before final use.

D. Match the Following (10)

Column A Column B
1. F5 a. New slide
2. Shift + F5 b. Slideshow from beginning
3. Ctrl + M c. Slideshow from current slide
4. Transition d. Between slides
5. Animation e. Within slide objects
6. Presenter View f. Speaker notes support
7. SmartArt g. Diagrams
8. Theme h. Consistent design
9. ChatGPT i. Content/outline generation
10. PDF Export j. Fixed-layout sharing

E. Viva Questions (15)

  1. What is MS PowerPoint?
  2. What is a slide?
  3. What is the difference between transition and animation?
  4. What is a theme?
  5. What is Presenter View?
  6. What is the 6×6 rule?
  7. What is SmartArt?
  8. How do you export a presentation as PDF?
  9. What is Slide Master?
  10. What is LibreOffice Impress?
  11. Name two AI presentation tools.
  12. What is an advantage of AI presentations?
  13. What is a limitation of AI presentations?
  14. What makes a good AI prompt?
  15. Why are presentation delivery skills important?

F. Practical Questions (10)

  1. Create an 8-slide presentation on Digital India.
  2. Apply one theme consistently to all slides.
  3. Insert one picture and one SmartArt diagram.
  4. Apply simple transition to all slides.
  5. Animate bullet points one by one.
  6. Add speaker notes to three slides.
  7. Run slideshow using F5 and practice Esc, B, and arrow keys.
  8. Export presentation as PDF.
  9. Create a handout layout with 6 slides per page.
  10. Use AI to generate outline, then improve slides manually in PowerPoint.

G. Case Studies (5)

Case Study 1: Classroom Seminar

A student must present a 10-minute seminar on Cyber Security Awareness.

Questions:

  1. How many slides are ideal?
  2. Which design rules should be followed?
  3. Which PowerPoint features should be used?
  4. Should AI be used? How?

Case Study 2: Business Pitch

A startup founder needs a pitch deck for investors.

Questions:

  1. What slides are essential?
  2. Why is visual hierarchy important?
  3. Why export PDF backup?
  4. Why not rely only on AI?

Case Study 3: Government Awareness Camp

A CSC operator must explain Digital India services visually.

Questions:

  1. Which slide structure is suitable?
  2. Why use images and icons?
  3. Why keep text minimal?
  4. Why use simple transitions?

Case Study 4: AI Draft Review

A student generates a presentation using ChatGPT but finds some facts doubtful.

Questions:

  1. What should the student do?
  2. Why verify facts?
  3. How can PowerPoint improve the draft?
  4. What is responsible AI use?

Case Study 5: Projector Failure

A presenter reaches the venue and the projector fails, but a PDF backup exists.

Questions:

  1. Why was PDF backup useful?
  2. What other backup options exist?
  3. Why rehearse without reading slides?
  4. Why are speaker notes helpful?

H. Presentation Projects (5)

  1. Digital India Presentation.
  2. Cyber Security Awareness Presentation.
  3. AI in Education Presentation.
  4. NITA Academy Course Presentation.
  5. Business Startup Pitch Deck.

Answer Keys

MCQ Answers

1-a | 2-b | 3-b | 4-a | 5-a | 6-a | 7-a | 8-a | 9-a | 10-a | 11-a | 12-a | 13-a | 14-a | 15-a | 16-a | 17-a | 18-a | 19-a | 20-a | 21-a | 22-a | 23-a | 24-a | 25-a | 26-a | 27-a | 28-a | 29-a | 30-a

True/False Answers

1-True | 2-True | 3-True | 4-True | 5-True | 6-True | 7-True | 8-True | 9-False | 10-True | 11-True | 12-False | 13-True | 14-True | 15-True | 16-True | 17-False | 18-True | 19-True | 20-True

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. presentations
  2. slide
  3. .pptx
  4. F5
  5. Shift + F5
  6. Ctrl + M
  7. between
  8. within/on
  9. notes/next slide preview
  10. bullet points
  11. diagrams/process visuals
  12. Impress
  13. .odp
  14. outlines/content
  15. reviewed/fact-checked

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-c | 3-a | 4-d | 5-e | 6-f | 7-g | 8-h | 9-i | 10-j

Case Study Model Answers

  1. About 8-10 slides; follow 6×6 rule and simple theme; use images, SmartArt, transitions, notes; AI may help outline but content must be reviewed.
  2. Problem, solution, market, product, business model, team, ask; hierarchy guides investor attention; PDF backup ensures sharing; AI draft still needs founder control and facts.
  3. Title, intro, services, benefits, examples, summary; images/icons improve understanding; minimal text helps audience listen; simple transitions look professional.
  4. Verify facts from trusted sources; AI may hallucinate; PowerPoint improves design and structure; responsible AI means review, edit, and personalize.
  5. PDF can be shared/displayed alternatively; keep PPT/PDF/USB/cloud backup; rehearsal helps without projector; notes support speaker confidence.

Final Self-Check

  • I can create and manage slides in MS PowerPoint.
  • I can apply themes, layouts, and slide master.
  • I can insert images, shapes, SmartArt, charts, and tables.
  • I can use transitions and animations appropriately.
  • I can run slideshow with keyboard shortcuts and Presenter View.
  • I can export PDF, handouts, and optional video/ppsx.
  • I understand AI presentation tools, advantages, and limitations.
  • I can write better prompts and review AI content responsibly.
  • I can deliver presentations with confidence and audience focus.
  • I can answer PowerPoint practical, viva, interview, and exam questions confidently.

chapters.title: Practice MCQs & Labs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs & Labs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 05: Presentation

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt

  2. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel

  3. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print

  4. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview

  5. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7

  6. Transition applies to? a) Between slides b) Objects only c) Notes d) Handouts only

  7. Animation applies to? a) Objects on slide b) Between slides c) Printer d) BIOS

  8. Title slide layout has? a) Title + subtitle placeholders b) Blank only c) Excel table d) Database

  9. Theme changes? a) Colors fonts effects b) CPU speed c) RAM d) Internet

  10. Widescreen slide ratio often? a) 16:9 b) 4:3 only c) 1:1 only d) None

  11. Duplicate slide? a) Right-click Duplicate b) F5 c) Delete d) Esc

  12. Presenter View shows? a) Notes and timer b) BIOS c) Excel d) Email only

  13. B key during show? a) Black screen b) Blue screen c) Boot d) Bold text

  14. Esc ends? a) Slide show b) Windows c) Excel d) Internet

  15. Handouts print? a) Multiple slides per page b) One slide only always c) Cannot print d) Email only

  16. .ppsx opens in? a) Slideshow directly b) Word c) Access d) Notepad

  17. Animation Pane controls? a) Order and timing b) Printer c) Virus d) RAM

  18. SmartArt used for? a) Diagrams b) Spreadsheets c) Database d) Email server

  19. Slide Sorter view for? a) Reorder slides b) Edit BIOS c) Format C: d) Scan

  20. 6×6 rule means? a) Readable bullets ~6 words b) Excel rows c) RAM GB d) Print margin mm

  21. Insert video from? a) Insert tab b) File Close c) BIOS d) Task Manager

  22. Apply transition to all? a) Transitions → Apply To All b) Delete slides c) Save only d) Scan

  23. Export presentation PDF? a) Save As PDF b) Cannot c) Print paper only d) Insert PDF

  24. Slide Master edits? a) Master layout for all slides b) Notepad c) Calculator d) Paint only

  25. Reading View? a) Full screen read slides b) Edit CPU c) Print only d) Email

  26. Action button for? a) Hyperlink/navigation b) Virus scan c) RAM upgrade d) OS install

  27. Best practice slides? a) Short bullets clear visuals b) Full essay paragraphs c) No title d) 50 animations each

  28. Entrance animation? a) Object appears b) Object exits c) Slide changes d) Print starts

  29. Design Ideas suggests? a) Layouts (365) b) CPU upgrades c) RAM d) Printer ink

  30. Notes pane for? a) Speaker notes b) Student grades c) Database d) Email

  31. Laser pointer in show? a) Ctrl+L in some versions / pen tool b) Delete slide c) Format disk d) Uninstall

  32. Loop slideshow option? a) Repeats until Esc b) Never repeats c) Deletes file d) Emails slides

  33. Hide slide? a) Skip in show but keep b) Delete permanently always c) Print only d) Email only

  34. Rehearse timings records? a) Slide duration b) CPU temp c) RAM d) Ink level

chapters.title: Practical Lab Pack — PowerPoint
chapters.heading: Practical Lab Pack — PowerPoint
chapters.contentHtml:
Presentation — Practical Lab Pack (CCC)

Software: MS PowerPoint or LibreOffice Impress
Total labs: 4 · Time: ~8 hours practical

Lab 1: 5-Slide "About Yourself" (3 hrs)

Create presentation with exactly 5 slides:

Slide Content
1 Title: Your Name — CCC Student (Title Slide layout)
2 About Yourself — photo + 3 bullet points
3 Education — school/college, course
4 Skills — computer skills list
5 Career Goal + Thank You

Apply one theme to all slides.

Lab 2: Transitions & Animation (2 hrs)

  1. Same presentation — add same transition to all slides
  2. Animate bullet points on slide 2 — appear one by one
  3. Run slideshow F5; practice Esc to exit

Lab 3: SmartArt / Image (1.5 hrs)

  1. New slide with SmartArt process diagram (3 steps: Learn → Practice → Pass CCC)
  2. Insert image from device with caption
  3. Save as PDF

Lab 4: Handouts & Export (1.5 hrs)

  1. Print preview — 6 slides per page handout
  2. Save as .ppsx (or Impress slideshow export)
  3. Export video/mp4 if software supports

Trainer Sign-off Sheet

Lab Student Name Date Initial
1–4

chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 05: Presentation

MCQ Answer Key

  1. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt Answer: a) .pptx Explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  2. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  3. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print Answer: a) Beginning Explanation: F5 starts the PowerPoint/Impress slideshow from the first slide. Shift+F5 starts from the current slide.

  4. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview Answer: a) Current slide Explanation: Use Current slide for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  5. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7 Answer: a) Ctrl+M Explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

  6. Transition applies to? a) Between slides b) Objects only c) Notes d) Handouts only Answer: a) Between slides Explanation: Between slides — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  7. Animation applies to? a) Objects on slide b) Between slides c) Printer d) BIOS Answer: a) Objects on slide Explanation: Objects on slide — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  8. Title slide layout has? a) Title + subtitle placeholders b) Blank only c) Excel table d) Database Answer: a) Title + subtitle placeholders Explanation: Title + subtitle placeholders is the correct answer. Blank only and Excel table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Theme changes? a) Colors fonts effects b) CPU speed c) RAM d) Internet Answer: a) Colors fonts effects Explanation: Colors fonts effects is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  10. Widescreen slide ratio often? a) 16:9 b) 4:3 only c) 1:1 only d) None Answer: a) 16:9 Explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  11. Duplicate slide? a) Right-click Duplicate b) F5 c) Delete d) Esc Answer: a) Right-click Duplicate Explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  12. Presenter View shows? a) Notes and timer b) BIOS c) Excel d) Email only Answer: a) Notes and timer Explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  13. B key during show? a) Black screen b) Blue screen c) Boot d) Bold text Answer: a) Black screen Explanation: Black screen is the correct answer. Blue screen and Boot are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  14. Esc ends? a) Slide show b) Windows c) Excel d) Internet Answer: a) Slide show Explanation: Slide show is the correct answer. Windows and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. Handouts print? a) Multiple slides per page b) One slide only always c) Cannot print d) Email only Answer: a) Multiple slides per page Explanation: Multiple slides per page is the correct answer. One slide only always and Cannot print are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. .ppsx opens in? a) Slideshow directly b) Word c) Access d) Notepad Answer: a) Slideshow directly Explanation: Slideshow directly is the correct answer. Word and Access are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Animation Pane controls? a) Order and timing b) Printer c) Virus d) RAM Answer: a) Order and timing Explanation: Order and timing is the correct answer. Printer and Virus are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  18. SmartArt used for? a) Diagrams b) Spreadsheets c) Database d) Email server Answer: a) Diagrams Explanation: Diagrams is the main purpose or function described in the question. Spreadsheets and Database are not the right choices here.

  19. Slide Sorter view for? a) Reorder slides b) Edit BIOS c) Format C: d) Scan Answer: a) Reorder slides Explanation: Reorder slides is the correct answer. Edit BIOS and Format C: are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. 6×6 rule means? a) Readable bullets ~6 words b) Excel rows c) RAM GB d) Print margin mm Answer: a) Readable bullets ~6 words Explanation: Readable bullets ~6 words is the correct answer. Excel rows and RAM GB are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

Delete slides and Save only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  23. Export presentation PDF? a) Save As PDF b) Cannot c) Print paper only d) Insert PDF Answer: a) Save As PDF Explanation: Save As PDF is the correct answer. Cannot and Print paper only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

Slide Master edits? a) Master layout for all slides b) Notepad c) Calculator d) Paint only Answer: a) Master layout for all slides Explanation: Master layout for all slides is the correct answer. Notepad and Calculator are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. Notes pane for? a) Speaker notes b) Student grades c) Database d) Email Answer: a) Speaker notes Explanation: Speaker notes is the correct answer. Student grades and Database are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  31. Laser pointer in show? a) Ctrl+L in some versions / pen tool b) Delete slide c) Format disk d) Uninstall Answer: a) Ctrl+L in some versions / pen tool Explanation: Ctrl+L in some versions / pen tool is the correct answer. Delete slide and Format disk are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Loop slideshow option? a) Repeats until Esc b) Never repeats c) Deletes file d) Emails slides Answer: a) Repeats until Esc Explanation: Repeats until Esc is the correct answer. Never repeats and Deletes file are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Hide slide? a) Skip in show but keep b) Delete permanently always c) Print only d) Email only Answer: a) Skip in show but keep Explanation: Skip in show but keep is the correct answer. Delete permanently always and Print only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  34. Rehearse timings records? a) Slide duration b) CPU temp c) RAM d) Ink level Answer: a) Slide duration Explanation: Slide duration is the correct answer. CPU temp and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.
