  37. Normal view is? a) Default edit view b) Print only c) Slideshow d) BIOS Answer: a) Default edit view Explanation: Default edit view is the correct answer. Print only and Slideshow are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. Footer on slides can show? a) Date slide number b) CPU serial c) RAM only d) Password Answer: a) Date slide number Explanation: Date slide number is the correct answer. CPU serial and RAM only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  39. Group objects? a) Right-click Group b) F5 c) Esc d) Delete OS Answer: a) Right-click Group Explanation: Right-click Group is the correct answer. F5 and Esc are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  40. Impress file extension often? a) .odp b) .pptx only c) .doc d) .xls Answer: a) .odp Explanation: .odp is the correct answer. .pptx only and .doc are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Transition duration controls? a) Speed between slides b) CPU c) Internet d) Email size Answer: a) Speed between slides Explanation: Speed between slides is the correct answer. CPU and Internet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Widescreen slide ratio often?
questions.explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Duplicate slide?
questions.explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Presenter View shows?
questions.explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: B key during show?
questions.explanation: Black screen is the correct answer. Blue screen and Boot are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Esc ends?
questions.explanation: Slide show is the correct answer. Windows and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Handouts print?
questions.explanation: Multiple slides per page is the correct answer. One slide only always and Cannot print are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: .ppsx opens in?
questions.explanation: Slideshow directly is the correct answer. Word and Access are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Animation Pane controls?
questions.explanation: Order and timing is the correct answer. Printer and Virus are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: SmartArt used for?
questions.explanation: Diagrams is the main purpose or function described in the question. Spreadsheets and Database are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Slide Sorter view for?
questions.explanation: Reorder slides is the correct answer. Edit BIOS and Format C: are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: 6×6 rule means?
questions.explanation: Readable bullets ~6 words is the correct answer. Excel rows and RAM GB are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Insert video from?
questions.explanation: Insert tab is the correct answer. File Close and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Apply transition to all?
questions.explanation: Transitions → Apply To All is the correct answer. Delete slides and Save only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Export presentation PDF?
questions.explanation: Save As PDF is the correct answer. Cannot and Print paper only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Slide Master edits?
questions.explanation: Master layout for all slides is the correct answer. Notepad and Calculator are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Reading View?
questions.explanation: Full screen read slides is the correct answer. Edit CPU and Print only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Action button for?
questions.explanation: Hyperlink/navigation is the correct answer. Virus scan and RAM upgrade are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Best practice slides?
questions.explanation: Short bullets clear visuals is the correct answer. Full essay paragraphs and No title are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Entrance animation?
questions.explanation: Object appears is the correct answer. Object exits and Slide changes are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Design Ideas suggests?
questions.explanation: Layouts (365) is the correct answer. CPU upgrades and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Notes pane for?
questions.explanation: Speaker notes is the correct answer. Student grades and Database are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Laser pointer in show?

options.text: Ctrl+L in some versions / pen tool

questions.explanation: Ctrl+L in some versions / pen tool is the correct answer. Delete slide and Format disk are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Loop slideshow option?
questions.explanation: Repeats until Esc is the correct answer. Never repeats and Deletes file are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Hide slide?
questions.explanation: Skip in show but keep is the correct answer. Delete permanently always and Print only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Rehearse timings records?
questions.explanation: Slide duration is the correct answer. CPU temp and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Chart on slide data from?
questions.explanation: Excel often is the correct answer. BIOS and Registry only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Compress media reduces?
questions.explanation: File size is the correct answer. Slide count and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Normal view is?
questions.explanation: Default edit view is the correct answer. Print only and Slideshow are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Footer on slides can show?
questions.explanation: Date slide number is the correct answer. CPU serial and RAM only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Group objects?
questions.explanation: Right-click Group is the correct answer. F5 and Esc are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Impress file extension often?
questions.explanation: .odp is the correct answer. .pptx only and .doc are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Transition duration controls?
questions.explanation: Speed between slides is the correct answer. CPU and Internet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Fly In is type of?
questions.explanation: Entrance animation is the correct answer. Exit only and Transition are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Fade transition?
questions.explanation: Smooth appear between slides is the correct answer. Delete slide and Save PDF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Thank you slide typically?
questions.explanation: Last slide is the correct answer. First only and Hidden always are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: About Yourself presentation has slides on?
questions.explanation: Intro education skills goal is the correct answer. Only Excel and Only Access are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ctrl+M in PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

questions.question: Slide layout Title and Content for?
questions.explanation: Bullets and title is the correct answer. Blank only and Database are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Export video mp4?
questions.explanation: File Export Create Video is the correct answer. Insert and Home are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: PowerPoint extension?
questions.explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Impress is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Module 6: Internet, Web Browsing, and AI-Powered Search (8 Hours)

Course: CCC / DCA / Digital Literacy | Module duration: 8 hours

Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Explain how the internet, WWW, websites, browsers, servers, clients, DNS, IP addresses, and protocols work.
  • Understand the evolution of the internet from Web 1.0 to Web 2.0, Web 3.0 concepts, and AI-powered internet tools.
  • Use traditional browsers such as Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari safely and efficiently.
  • Understand AI-enhanced browsers such as Microsoft Edge with Copilot, Arc Browser, and Opera AI features.
  • Use essential browser shortcuts for tabs, navigation, downloads, history, bookmarks, and search.
  • Search effectively using Google, Bing, Yahoo, DuckDuckGo, and advanced search operators.
  • Use AI search and assistant tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, Claude, and Perplexity responsibly.
  • Compare traditional search and AI search, and verify information before using it.
  • Use government digital services such as DigiLocker, UMANG, Aadhaar services, PAN services, passport services, e-Shram, and scholarship portals safely.
  • Use cloud storage and collaboration tools such as Google Drive, OneDrive, Google Docs, Microsoft 365 Online, Zoom, Meet, and Teams.
  • Identify phishing, smishing, vishing, malware, ransomware, spyware, fake job scams, UPI frauds, QR code scams, misinformation, deepfakes, and fake news.
  • Follow privacy, cybersecurity, social media, and responsible AI safety rules.
  • Connect internet skills with careers such as digital marketing, content creation, web design, SEO, virtual assistance, data analysis, AI research, and cybersecurity.

1. Introduction to the Internet

What It Is

The internet is a global network of connected computers, mobile phones, servers, routers, data centers, and communication cables. It allows people and organizations to exchange information across the world.

Why It Is Used

The internet is used for communication, education, banking, shopping, entertainment, government services, research, business, cloud storage, online classes, and artificial intelligence tools.

Real-World Analogy

Think of the internet as a huge road network:

Internet Concept Road Analogy
Internet Road network connecting cities
Website Building or office
URL Full address of the building
Server Office that stores information
Client Visitor requesting information
Router Traffic junction
DNS Phonebook/address directory
ISP Road service provider

How the Internet Works

Your Device -> Browser -> ISP -> DNS -> Web Server -> Website Response -> Browser Display
  1. You type a website address.
  2. Browser asks DNS to find the server IP address.
  3. Request travels through your ISP and internet network.
  4. Server sends back web page data.
  5. Browser displays the page.

Practice Exercise

Open a browser and visit https://www.digitalindia.gov.in. Identify the protocol, domain name, and padlock icon.

2. Evolution of the Internet

Web 1.0: Read-Only Web

Web 1.0 websites were mostly static. Users could read information but had limited interaction. Examples included early company pages, basic news sites, and static educational websites.

Web 2.0: Social and User-Generated Web

Web 2.0 made the internet interactive. Users can create, upload, comment, share, like, and collaborate.

Examples:

  • Facebook
  • YouTube
  • Wikipedia
  • Instagram
  • Blogs
  • Online forums

Web 3.0: Introduction

Web 3.0 is an emerging idea focused on decentralization, user ownership, blockchain-based identity, and peer-to-peer systems. It is still developing and should be understood as a concept, not as a fully mature replacement for the current internet.

AI-Powered Internet Era

Modern internet usage now includes AI assistants and AI search tools. Instead of only opening links, students can ask questions and receive summaries, explanations, examples, and structured answers.

Examples:

  • ChatGPT
  • Google Gemini
  • Microsoft Copilot
  • Claude
  • Perplexity AI

Important Lesson

Internet usage has evolved from reading pages, to creating content, to asking AI-powered systems for direct answers. However, students must verify important information from trusted sources.

3. Internet Fundamentals

Internet

A worldwide network that connects devices and allows communication and data sharing.

WWW

WWW (World Wide Web) is a system of linked web pages accessed using browsers. The web is one service on the internet. Email, file transfer, video calls, and online gaming are also internet services.

Website

A collection of related web pages under one domain name. Example: digitalindia.gov.in.

Web Page

A single page displayed in a browser. Example: a login page, article page, or contact page.

Domain Name

Human-friendly website name such as google.com or uidai.gov.in.

Hosting

Hosting means storing website files on a server so people can access them through the internet.

DNS

DNS (Domain Name System) converts domain names into IP addresses. It works like a phonebook for the internet.

IP Address

An IP address is a numerical address of a device or server on a network, like a house number.

Server

A server stores websites, files, databases, videos, or applications and sends them to users when requested.

Client

A client is the user's device or software requesting information, such as your phone, laptop, or browser.

Protocol

A protocol is a set of rules for communication. HTTP and HTTPS are web protocols.

HTTP

HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol) transfers web pages but is not encrypted.

HTTPS

HTTPS (HTTP Secure) encrypts communication between browser and website. It is essential for login, banking, payments, and forms.

URL Structure

https://www.example.gov.in:443/services/index.html?page=1#top
|----|   |-----------------| |--| |----------------| |----| |---|
protocol       domain       port       path          query fragment

HTTP vs HTTPS

Feature HTTP HTTPS
Security Not encrypted Encrypted
Padlock Usually absent Usually present
Use General reading only Login, payments, forms
Risk Data can be intercepted Safer for sensitive data

Practice Exercise

Write the parts of this URL: https://scholarships.gov.in/student/login.

4. Internet Connection and ISP

ISP

An Internet Service Provider gives internet access. Examples: Jio, Airtel, BSNL, local broadband providers.

Connection Types

Type Use
Fibre broadband Home, school, office, high-speed internet
Mobile data Phone internet using SIM
Wi-Fi Wireless local access through router
Hotspot Sharing mobile internet to laptop
Public Wi-Fi Railway station, library, cafe, campus

Data Terms

  • Bandwidth: data capacity per second, often Mbps.
  • Data limit: total data allowed in GB.
  • Latency: delay in response, important for video calls and gaming.
  • Signal strength: quality of Wi-Fi/mobile connection.

Safe Connection Practice

Avoid banking, UPI, or password entry on unknown public Wi-Fi. Use mobile data or trusted networks for sensitive work.

5. Modern Web Browsers

What It Is

A web browser is software used to open websites and web applications.

Traditional Browsers

  • Google Chrome
  • Microsoft Edge
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Safari

AI-Enhanced Browsers

Microsoft Edge with Copilot

Edge includes Copilot features for summarizing web pages, answering questions, and helping with productivity.

Arc Browser

Arc focuses on modern browsing workflows, spaces, and productivity-oriented organization.

Opera Browser AI Features

Opera includes AI-related sidebar tools and browsing assistance features in supported versions.

How Browsers Are Becoming AI-Enabled

Modern browsers are moving from simple page viewing to assistance-based browsing. They can summarize articles, answer questions about pages, help write content, compare information, and improve productivity.

Browser Window Parts

Part Use
Address bar Type URL or search query
Tabs Open multiple pages
Back/Forward Move through browsing history
Refresh Reload page
Bookmarks/Favorites Save useful websites
History Previously visited pages
Downloads Downloaded files list
Settings Privacy, cookies, search engine, homepage
Extensions Extra browser tools

Browser Settings Students Should Know

  • Set homepage.
  • Change default search engine.
  • Clear cache and cookies.
  • Enable safe browsing.
  • Block pop-ups.
  • Manage downloads location.
  • Review site permissions such as camera, microphone, and location.

6. Essential Browser Shortcuts

Tabs

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + T New tab
Ctrl + W Close current tab
Ctrl + Shift + T Reopen closed tab
Ctrl + Tab Move to next tab

Navigation

Shortcut Use
Alt + Left Arrow Back
Alt + Right Arrow Forward
F5 Refresh
Ctrl + R Refresh

Search

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + F Find text on page
Ctrl + L Select address bar

Downloads

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + J Downloads

History

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + H History

Bookmarks

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + D Bookmark current page
Ctrl + Shift + B Show/hide bookmarks bar

Shortcut Drills

  1. Open three tabs using Ctrl + T.
  2. Search NIELIT CCC exam pattern.
  3. Bookmark official result with Ctrl + D.
  4. Open Downloads with Ctrl + J.
  5. Reopen a closed tab with Ctrl + Shift + T.

7. Search Engines

Traditional Search Engines

  • Google
  • Bing
  • Yahoo
  • DuckDuckGo

Crawling

Search engines use automated programs called crawlers to discover web pages.

Indexing

Discovered pages are stored in a large searchable index.

Ranking

Search engines rank pages based on relevance, quality, freshness, popularity, location, and many other signals.

Search Results

Search results include links, snippets, images, videos, maps, ads, and sometimes direct answers.

Effective Search Techniques

Technique Example
Specific keywords NIELIT CCC syllabus 2026
Exact phrase \"Digital India mission\"
Government sites site:gov.in DigiLocker
PDF files filetype:pdf CCC study material
Exclude word python tutorial -snake
Define term define browser

Evaluating Search Results

  • Check date.
  • Check author or organization.
  • Prefer official domains such as .gov.in, .edu, .nic.in.
  • Avoid clickbait.
  • Cross-check important facts.

8. AI Search Engines and Assistants

ChatGPT

Best for:

  • Learning explanations.
  • Summaries.
  • Writing assistance.
  • Practice questions.
  • Study plans.

Limitations:

  • May provide incorrect information.
  • Does not always show sources.
  • Needs fact verification.

Google Gemini

Best for:

  • Google ecosystem support.
  • Research assistance.
  • Summaries and content drafting.

Microsoft Copilot

Best for:

  • Productivity tasks.
  • Microsoft integration.
  • Web-assisted answers in supported modes.

Claude

Best for:

  • Long document explanation.
  • Detailed writing.
  • Structured summaries.

Perplexity AI

Best for:

  • AI-powered web search.
  • Source-based answers.
  • Quick research with citations.

Traditional Search vs AI Search

Feature Traditional Search AI Search
Output Links Direct answers
Speed Fast Fast
Verification User reads sources User must verify AI answer
Research depth High if user studies sources Moderate to high depending on tool
Learning curve Low Low
Best for Official sources, exact facts Summaries, explanations, starting research
Risk Clickbait, SEO spam Hallucination, missing context

Important Lesson

AI answers should always be verified for critical decisions such as exams, health, banking, government forms, legal matters, and job applications.

9. Prompt Engineering for Internet Research

Poor Search

computer

Problem: too broad.

Better Search

computer applications in education examples

Poor AI Prompt

Tell me about AI

Better AI Prompt

Explain Artificial Intelligence for DCA students with simple examples,
career opportunities, risks, and 10 exam questions.
Use beginner-friendly language.

Good Prompt Checklist

  • Topic.
  • Audience.
  • Purpose.
  • Required format.
  • Examples needed.
  • Length.
  • Language level.
  • Need for sources or verification.

Practice Exercise

Write a traditional search query and an AI prompt for Digital India services for students.

10. Downloads, Uploads, Cookies, Cache, and Privacy

Download

Downloading means saving a file from the internet to your device.

Examples:

  • PDF study material.
  • Image.
  • Admit card.
  • App installer.

Upload

Uploading means sending a file from your device to the internet.

Examples:

  • Email attachment.
  • Google Drive upload.
  • Online admission form document upload.

Safe Download Steps

  1. Download from official/trusted websites.
  2. Check file extension.
  3. Avoid unknown .exe files.
  4. Scan with antivirus.
  5. Note save location.
  6. Avoid pirated software and cracked games.

Cookies

Cookies are small files saved by websites in the browser. They remember login sessions, language, cart items, and preferences. Advertising cookies can also track browsing behavior.

Cache

Cache stores temporary copies of website images and files to load pages faster. Clear cache if a site shows old content or behaves incorrectly.

Sessions

A session keeps you logged into a website temporarily. Always log out on shared devices.

Incognito / Private Browsing

Private mode does not save local browsing history, but your ISP, school network, websites, or employer may still see activity. It is not full anonymity.

Practice Exercise

Open browser settings and find options to clear browsing history, cache, and cookies.

11. Digital Government Services

DigiLocker

Stores digital documents such as Aadhaar, driving license, marksheets, and certificates.

UMANG

Provides access to many government services in one app.

Aadhaar Services

UIDAI services for Aadhaar download, update status, verification, and authentication.

PAN Services

Apply, update, or check PAN-related services through official portals.

Passport Services

Passport application, appointment, and status tracking.

Income Tax Portal

PAN linking, filing returns, form downloads, and tax services.

e-Shram

Registration for unorganized workers.

National Scholarship Portal

Scholarship applications and tracking for students.

Safe Usage Practices

  • Use official domains only.
  • Check HTTPS padlock.
  • Do not share OTP, password, Aadhaar OTP, UPI PIN, or PAN details casually.
  • Log out after use.
  • Avoid cyber cafe auto-save of passwords.
  • Keep downloaded documents in secure folders.

12. Cloud Services and Online Storage

What It Is

Cloud storage saves files on internet servers so they can be accessed from different devices.

Examples

  • Google Drive
  • OneDrive
  • Dropbox
  • iCloud

Upload

Sending files to cloud storage.

Download

Saving cloud files to your device.

Sharing

Creating a link or giving access to another user.

Collaboration

Multiple people editing or viewing the same document online.

Safe Sharing Tips

  • Share with specific people when possible.
  • Avoid public links for private documents.
  • Remove access after work is complete.
  • Do not upload sensitive documents to unknown platforms.

Practice Exercise

Create a cloud folder named CCC Study Material, upload one PDF, and share it with view-only access.

13. Online Collaboration Tools

Google Docs

Online word processor for collaborative writing.

Google Sheets

Online spreadsheet for shared data entry and analysis.

Google Slides

Online presentation tool.

Microsoft 365 Online

Online versions of Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneDrive, and Teams.

Zoom

Video meetings and webinars.

Google Meet

Online classes and meetings.

Microsoft Teams

Meetings, chat, files, classes, and workplace collaboration.

Real-World Use

  • Online classes.
  • Team project work.
  • Shared notes.
  • Remote office meetings.
  • Teacher-student assignment review.

14. Social Media Literacy

Responsible Usage

Use social media for learning, communication, and networking, not for harmful behavior.

Privacy Settings

Control who can see your profile, posts, photos, and contact details.

Digital Footprint

Everything posted online can affect your reputation, education, and job opportunities.

Online Reputation

Employers and colleges may check public online behavior.

Cyberbullying Awareness

Do not insult, threaten, harass, or share private photos. Report abuse and inform guardians/teachers.

Screen-Time Management

Use time limits and avoid endless scrolling that affects study and health.

15. Cybersecurity Awareness

Phishing

Fake websites or emails that steal login details.

Example: fake bank login page claiming account will be blocked.

Smishing

SMS-based phishing.

Example: Your KYC expired. Click this link.

Vishing

Voice-call fraud.

Example: caller pretends to be bank officer and asks OTP.

Malware

Harmful software that damages or steals data.

Ransomware

Locks files and demands payment.

Spyware

Secretly monitors user activity.

Social Engineering

Manipulating people to reveal confidential information.

Fake Job Scams

Fraudsters ask registration fees for fake jobs.

UPI Frauds

Scammers ask users to enter UPI PIN to receive money. UPI PIN is needed to send money, not receive money.

QR Code Scams

Scanning unknown QR codes may lead to payment requests or fake pages.

Indian Safety Examples

  • Fake courier KYC messages.
  • Fake electricity bill disconnection links.
  • Fake loan apps demanding contacts/photos access.
  • Fake scholarship links asking processing fees.
  • Fake customer care numbers found through random search results.

Safe Browsing Checklist

  • Use HTTPS for sensitive sites.
  • Check domain carefully.
  • Do not share OTP, PIN, passwords.
  • Install updates.
  • Use antivirus and firewall.
  • Download from official sources.
  • Report cybercrime at cybercrime.gov.in.

16. Misinformation and Deepfakes

Fake News

False or misleading information spread as news.

Deepfake Videos

AI-generated or altered videos that make people appear to say or do things they did not.

AI-Generated Images

Images created by AI that may look realistic but are not real.

Verification Techniques

  • Check official sources.
  • Search same news on trusted websites.
  • Check date and context.
  • Use reverse image search.
  • Look for unnatural faces, hands, shadows, or audio mismatch.
  • Avoid forwarding before verifying.

Fact-Checking Websites

  • PIB Fact Check
  • Alt News
  • Boom Live
  • Factly

Important Skill

Do not believe everything seen online.

17. AI and Internet Safety

Risks of Sharing Sensitive Information with AI

Do not paste passwords, OTPs, Aadhaar numbers, bank details, confidential documents, private photos, or employer data into AI tools.

Privacy Considerations

AI tools may store or process user prompts depending on settings and policy.

Data Usage

Read privacy settings before uploading personal or business data.

Responsible AI Usage

  • Use AI for drafts, summaries, and learning support.
  • Verify facts.
  • Do not copy AI answers blindly.
  • Add personal understanding.
  • Avoid academic dishonesty.

Verifying AI Responses

Use official websites, textbooks, teachers, and trusted sources.

18. Online Learning Resources

NPTEL

Government-supported online courses, especially technical and higher education.

SWAYAM

Indian online learning platform for school, college, and skill courses.

Coursera

Global courses from universities and companies. Some are paid.

Udemy

Practical skill courses. Quality varies by instructor.

Khan Academy

Free learning resources for school-level subjects.

YouTube Learning Channels

Useful for tutorials, but students must verify quality and avoid distractions.

Benefits

  • Learn anytime.
  • Repeat lessons.
  • Access free/low-cost material.
  • Build skills beyond classroom.

Limitations

  • Misinformation possible.
  • Distraction risk.
  • Certificates may vary in value.
  • Requires self-discipline.

19. Industry Applications

Education

Online classes, LMS, digital exams, e-books, research, cloud assignments.

Banking

Net banking, UPI, fraud monitoring, online statements, customer service.

Healthcare

Telemedicine, online reports, appointment booking, hospital portals.

Government

Digital India, certificates, schemes, identity services, tax portals.

Retail

E-commerce, online payments, delivery tracking, customer support.

Logistics

Shipment tracking, route planning, warehouse updates.

Manufacturing

Supplier communication, inventory systems, IoT monitoring.

IT Services

Cloud servers, remote support, software development, cybersecurity.

Artificial Intelligence

AI tools require internet access for cloud-based models, APIs, datasets, and deployment.

20. Career Awareness

Career Internet Skill Needed
Digital Marketing SEO, social media, analytics, ads
Content Creator Research, publishing, audience engagement
Web Designer Websites, hosting, domains, browsers
SEO Specialist Search ranking, keywords, analytics
Social Media Manager Platform strategy, safety, scheduling
Virtual Assistant Email, cloud tools, online meetings
Data Analyst Online data, dashboards, research
AI Research Assistant Prompting, source checking, AI tools
Cybersecurity Analyst Threat detection, phishing awareness

21. Practical Activities

Traditional Activities

  1. Browser navigation: identify address bar, tabs, bookmarks, history, downloads.
  2. Connect to Wi-Fi/mobile hotspot and note ISP name.
  3. Visit an HTTPS website and identify padlock.
  4. Download a PDF from an official site.
  5. Upload a file to Google Drive or OneDrive.
  6. Bookmark trusted study websites.
  7. Clear cache and cookies under teacher guidance.

Modern Activities

  1. Use ChatGPT to explain Digital India.
  2. Compare Google Search vs AI Search for the same topic.
  3. Use Google Drive to create a shared study folder.
  4. Share a document online with view-only access.
  5. Verify a viral news claim using trusted fact-checking sources.

22. Real-World Projects

Project 1: Research Digital India Using Google Search

Use search operators, official websites, and bookmarks. Prepare a one-page summary.

Project 2: Research Digital India Using ChatGPT

Ask for a beginner-friendly explanation. Compare with official sources.

Project 3: Create a Cloud-Shared Study Folder

Upload PDFs and share with classmates safely.

Project 4: Identify Phishing Emails

Collect sample safe/fake email screenshots and list warning signs.

Project 5: Fact-Check Viral News

Choose a viral claim and verify through official or fact-checking sources.

23. Interview and Viva Questions

Internet Fundamentals

  1. What is the internet?
  2. What is the difference between internet and WWW?
  3. What is DNS?
  4. What is an IP address?
  5. What is HTTPS?

Search Engines

  1. What is crawling?
  2. What is indexing?
  3. What is ranking?
  4. What is site:gov.in used for?
  5. Why should search results be verified?

AI Search

  1. What is AI search?
  2. Name two AI search/assistant tools.
  3. Why can AI answers be risky?
  4. What is a good AI prompt?
  5. Why should AI answers be verified?

Browser Usage

  1. What is a browser?
  2. What is the shortcut for a new tab?
  3. What is bookmark?
  4. What is cache?
  5. What is incognito mode?

Cybersecurity

  1. What is phishing?
  2. What is ransomware?
  3. What is smishing?
  4. What is UPI fraud?
  5. Why should OTP not be shared?

Cloud and Digital Literacy

  1. What is cloud storage?
  2. What is Google Drive?
  3. What is online collaboration?
  4. What is digital footprint?
  5. What is responsible AI usage?

24. Quick Revision Sheet

Important Terms

Internet, WWW, browser, URL, ISP, DNS, IP address, server, client, HTTP, HTTPS, cookies, cache, incognito, phishing, malware, cloud storage, AI search.

Browser Shortcuts

Ctrl + T, Ctrl + W, Ctrl + Shift + T, Ctrl + Tab, Alt + Left, Alt + Right, F5, Ctrl + R, Ctrl + F, Ctrl + L, Ctrl + J, Ctrl + H, Ctrl + D, Ctrl + Shift + B.

Search Operators

\"exact phrase\", site:gov.in, filetype:pdf, -exclude, define:.

AI Search Tools

ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot, Claude, Perplexity.

Cyber Safety Rules

  • Check HTTPS and official domain.
  • Do not share OTP/PIN/password.
  • Avoid suspicious links.
  • Verify UPI requests.
  • Download only from trusted sources.
  • Report fraud at cybercrime.gov.in.

Government Portals

DigiLocker, UMANG, UIDAI/Aadhaar, PAN services, Passport Seva, Income Tax Portal, e-Shram, National Scholarship Portal.

Exam Tips

  • Internet is the network; WWW is web pages.
  • Browser opens websites.
  • URL is web address.
  • DNS converts domain to IP.
  • HTTPS is safer than HTTP.
  • Download means internet to device; upload means device to internet.
  • Cookies store small website data.
  • Cache improves loading speed.
  • AI answers need verification.

25. Assessment Enhancement

A. Multiple Choice Questions (30)

1. Internet is best described as:

  • b) A single computer

  • c) Wide Web Wire

3. Software used to open websites is:

  • b) Spreadsheet
  • c) Scanner
  • d) Compiler

4. URL means:

  • a) Web page address
  • b) RAM type
  • c) Printer command
  • d) Keyboard shortcut

5. ISP provides:

  • a) Internet service
  • b) CPU repair
  • c) MS Office license
  • d) Monitor stand

6. DNS converts:

  • a) Domain name to IP address
  • b) Printer to scanner
  • c) PDF to video
  • d) Password to OTP

7. HTTPS is:

  • a) Encrypted and safer than HTTP
  • b) Always fake
  • c) Only for games

  • d) Source code only

15. Download means:

  • b) Device to internet

16. Upload means:

  • a) Device to internet
  • b) Internet to device

  • a) Small files saved by websites

  • c) CPU parts
  • d) Antivirus only

18. Cache helps:

  • a) Load pages faster
  • b) Delete internet

19. Phishing tries to:

  • c) Watching public videos always
  • d) Opening calculator

  • a) Online record of activity/posts
  • b) Shoe size
  • c) Printer marks
  • d) Mouse pad

30. cybercrime.gov.in is used to:

  • a) Report cybercrime
  • b) Download movies
  • c) Create fake accounts
  • d) Buy lottery tickets

B. True/False (20)

  1. Internet is a global network.
  2. WWW and internet are exactly the same thing.
  3. Browser opens websites.
  4. DNS converts domain names to IP addresses.
  5. HTTPS is safer than HTTP for payments.
  6. Ctrl + T opens a new tab.
  7. Ctrl + Shift + T reopens a closed tab.
  8. Search results should never be verified.
  9. AI answers can be incorrect.
  10. ChatGPT can help create summaries.
  11. Cookies can remember website preferences.
  12. Incognito mode makes users completely invisible to everyone.
  13. Download means internet to device.
  14. Upload means device to internet.
  15. OTP should be shared with callers who claim to be bank officers.
  16. Phishing sites may look like real sites.
  17. Deepfakes can mislead people.
  18. Cloud storage allows files to be accessed online.
  19. Social media posts can affect reputation.
  20. Digital literacy includes safety and responsibility.

C. Fill in the Blanks (15)

  1. WWW stands for __________.
  2. ISP stands for __________.
  3. DNS stands for __________.
  4. HTTPS means HTTP __________.
  5. URL is the __________ of a web page.
  6. Ctrl + T opens a new __________.
  7. Ctrl + H opens browser __________.
  8. Ctrl + J opens __________.
  9. Download means internet to __________.
  10. Upload means device to __________.
  11. Cookies are small files saved by __________.
  12. DigiLocker stores digital __________.
  13. AI answers should be __________.
  14. Smishing uses __________ messages.
  15. Ransomware locks files and demands __________.

D. Match the Following (10)

Column A Column B
1. DNS a. Secure web protocol
2. HTTPS b. Converts domain to IP
3. Ctrl + T c. New tab
4. Ctrl + D d. Bookmark
5. Ctrl + J e. Downloads
6. DigiLocker f. Digital documents
7. Phishing g. Fake login/scam
8. Smishing h. SMS fraud
9. Cloud storage i. Online file storage
10. Deepfake j. AI-altered fake media

E. Viva Questions (15)

  1. What is the internet?
  2. What is the difference between internet and WWW?
  3. What is a browser?
  4. What is a URL?
  5. What is DNS?
  6. What is HTTPS?
  7. What is the difference between download and upload?
  8. What are cookies?
  9. What is cache?
  10. What is phishing?
  11. What is AI search?
  12. Name two AI assistant/search tools.
  13. Why should AI answers be verified?
  14. What is cloud storage?
  15. What is digital footprint?

F. Practical Questions (10)

  1. Open Chrome/Edge and identify address bar, tabs, history, downloads, and bookmarks.
  2. Search NIELIT CCC exam pattern and bookmark a trusted result.
  3. Use site:gov.in DigiLocker and open an official result.
  4. Download a PDF from an official site.
  5. Upload a file to Google Drive or OneDrive.
  6. Clear browser cache and cookies under supervision.
  7. Compare a Google Search result with an AI-generated answer.
  8. Identify five signs of a phishing website.
  9. Create a shared cloud folder for study materials.
  10. Fact-check a viral claim using trusted sources.

G. Case Studies (5)

Case Study 1: Fake Bank SMS

A student receives SMS: Your bank KYC expired. Click link and enter OTP.

Questions:

  1. What type of scam is this?
  2. What should the student do?
  3. Why should OTP not be shared?
  4. How can the bank site be verified?

Case Study 2: AI Research for Assignment

A learner asks ChatGPT for Digital India information and gets a detailed answer.

Questions:

  1. Can the answer be used directly?
  2. How should it be verified?
  3. What should be added by the learner?
  4. Which official sources can help?

Case Study 3: Public Wi-Fi Login

A user wants to do net banking on railway station Wi-Fi.

Questions:

  1. Is it safe?
  2. What should be used instead?
  3. Why is HTTPS still important?
  4. What other precautions are needed?

Case Study 4: Viral News Video

A video claims a government scholarship is ending today and asks for payment.

Questions:

  1. What risks are present?
  2. How can the news be verified?
  3. Which official portal should be checked?
  4. Why avoid forwarding immediately?

Case Study 5: Cloud Sharing Mistake

A student shares a Google Drive folder publicly containing Aadhaar PDF.

Questions:

  1. What is the privacy risk?
  2. How should sharing be changed?
  3. Why remove access after work?
  4. What data should not be publicly shared?

H. Projects (5)

  1. Research Digital India using Google Search and prepare a verified summary.
  2. Research Digital India using ChatGPT and compare with official sources.
  3. Create a cloud-shared study folder with safe permissions.
  4. Identify and explain five phishing email/SMS examples.
  5. Fact-check a viral news claim using official and fact-checking websites.

Answer Keys

MCQ Answers

1-a | 2-a | 3-a | 4-a | 5-a | 6-a | 7-a | 8-a | 9-a | 10-a | 11-a | 12-a | 13-a | 14-a | 15-a | 16-a | 17-a | 18-a | 19-a | 20-a | 21-a | 22-a | 23-a | 24-a | 25-a | 26-a | 27-a | 28-a | 29-a | 30-a

True/False Answers

1-True | 2-False | 3-True | 4-True | 5-True | 6-True | 7-True | 8-False | 9-True | 10-True | 11-True | 12-False | 13-True | 14-True | 15-False | 16-True | 17-True | 18-True | 19-True | 20-True

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. World Wide Web
  2. Internet Service Provider
  3. Domain Name System
  4. Secure
  5. address
  6. tab
  7. history
  8. downloads
  9. device
  10. internet/cloud
  11. websites/browser
  12. documents
  13. verified
  14. SMS/text
  15. payment/ransom

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-a | 3-c | 4-d | 5-e | 6-f | 7-g | 8-h | 9-i | 10-j

Case Study Model Answers

  1. Smishing/phishing; do not click, report/block, contact bank through official number; OTP gives account access; verify official domain/app.
  2. Do not use blindly; verify with official websites and trusted sources; add learner's own understanding; use Digital India/government portals.
  3. Not safe for banking; use trusted mobile data; HTTPS encrypts connection; avoid saving passwords and log out.
  4. Risks include fake news, phishing, payment fraud; verify through official scholarship portal and fact-checking sites; do not forward unverified claims.
  5. Aadhaar/public document exposure risk; share with specific people only; remove access after use; avoid public sharing of Aadhaar, PAN, bank details, certificates.

Final Self-Check

  • I can explain internet, WWW, browser, URL, ISP, DNS, IP address, server, client, HTTP, and HTTPS.
  • I can use browser tabs, bookmarks, history, downloads, and shortcuts.
  • I can search effectively using Google/Bing and search operators.
  • I can compare traditional search with AI search.
  • I can use AI assistants responsibly and verify answers.
  • I can download, upload, and share cloud files safely.
  • I can use DigiLocker, UMANG, and government portals with safety checks.
  • I can identify phishing, smishing, vishing, malware, ransomware, UPI fraud, QR scams, fake news, and deepfakes.
  • I can protect privacy and manage digital footprint.
  • I can answer internet browsing practical, viva, and exam questions confidently.

Subject 6: Internet & Web Browsing (8 Hours)

Learning Objectives

  • Explain internet, WWW, browser, URL, and ISP
  • Connect to the internet using Wi-Fi and mobile data
  • Browse websites safely using HTTPS and bookmarks
  • Use search engines effectively for CCC exam topics
  • Download and upload files correctly
  • Understand cookies, cache, and browser privacy settings
  • Follow safe browsing habits to avoid scams and malware

1. Internet Basics

Internet = a global network (vishwa jaal) of millions of computers connected through cables, fibre, and wireless links.

WWW (World Wide Web) = a system of linked web pages on the internet, accessed through browsers using HTTP/HTTPS.

Term Meaning
Website Collection of related web pages on one server
Web page Single HTML document shown in browser
Home page First/main page of a website
Hyperlink Clickable link to another page or site
Server Computer that stores and sends web pages
Client Your computer or phone that requests pages

How you connect to the internet

  1. ISP (Internet Service Provider) — company that gives internet (Jio, Airtel, BSNL, local broadband)
  2. Modem — converts signal from ISP line to digital data
  3. Router — shares connection to many devices (often Wi-Fi)
  4. Wi-Fi — wireless connection within home/office
  5. Mobile data (4G/5G) — internet through SIM on phone

IP Address — unique number of a device on the internet (like house number).
Domain name — easy name like google.com instead of remembering IP numbers.
DNS (Domain Name System) — converts domain name to IP address.

2. Web Browser

A browser is software to open and view websites.

Popular browsers (CCC exam)

  • Google Chrome
  • Microsoft Edge
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Safari (Apple devices)

Parts of browser window

Part Use
Address bar Type URL or search keywords
Tabs Open many pages (Ctrl+T = new tab)
Back / Forward Visit previous/next page
Refresh (F5) Reload current page
Bookmarks / Favorites Save site for quick access (Ctrl+D)
History (Ctrl+H) List of pages you visited
Downloads (Ctrl+J) Files saved from internet
Settings Privacy, homepage, default search engine

Browser settings students should know

  • Set homepage (page that opens on start)
  • Clear cache and cookies when site behaves wrongly
  • Enable pop-up blocker for safer browsing
  • Turn on safe browsing warnings (Chrome/Edge)

3. URL — Uniform Resource Locator

URL = full web address of a page or file.

Structure of a URL

https://www.example.gov.in:443/courses/index.html?page=1#top
└─┬─┘   └──────┬──────┘└┬┘ └──────┬──────┘ └────┬────┘ └┬┘
protocol    domain    port    path        query   fragment
Part Example Meaning
Protocol https:// Rules for connection
Domain www.example.gov.in Website name
Path /courses/index.html Location of page on server
Query ?page=1 Extra parameters

HTTP vs HTTPS

Protocol Security Use
HTTP Not encrypted General reading only
HTTPS Encrypted (SSL/TLS) Login, banking, payments

HTTPS shows a padlock icon in address bar. Always use HTTPS for passwords and online payment.

Common top-level domains (TLD)

  • .com — commercial
  • .org — organizations
  • .edu — educational
  • .gov.in — Indian government (official)
  • .nic.in — National Informatics Centre (government)

4. ISP and Connection Types

ISP provides internet access for a fee (monthly plan / prepaid recharge).

Connection type Speed Typical use
Broadband (DSL/Fibre) High Home, office
Mobile data Medium–high Phone, hotspot
Dial-up (old) Very slow Rare today

Data usage terms

  • Bandwidth — amount of data that can flow per second (Mbps)
  • Data limit — GB allowed per month in mobile plan
  • Hotspot — share phone internet to laptop via Wi-Fi

5. Search Engines

Search engine finds web pages matching your keywords.

Popular search engines

Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Yahoo

Effective search tips (CCC useful)

  • Use specific keywords: NIELIT CCC syllabus 2025 not just computer
  • Quotes for exact phrase: "Digital India mission"
  • site: filter: site:gov.in UMANG app
  • filetype: filter: filetype:pdf CCC study material
  • Open results from trusted sources (.gov.in, .edu, official institute sites)

Evaluating search results

  • Check date of article — old news may be wrong
  • Avoid clickbait titles
  • Cross-check important facts on official website

6. Download and Upload

Action Direction Example
Download Internet → your device Save PDF, image, app
Upload Your device → internet Attach file to email, cloud upload

Safe download steps

  1. Download only from official or trusted websites
  2. Check file extension (.pdf, .docx, .jpg) — avoid unknown .exe unless sure
  3. Scan with antivirus after download
  4. Note save location (Downloads folder)
  5. Do not download pirated software or cracked games (virus risk)

Upload uses

  • Email attachments
  • Google Drive / OneDrive
  • Online form document upload (admission, job application)

7. Cookies, Cache, and Sessions

Cookies

Small text files stored by browser on your computer. Websites use cookies to:

  • Remember login (session cookie)
  • Save preferences (language, theme)
  • Track usage (advertising cookies)

Risk: Third-party cookies can track browsing. Clear cookies or use private/incognito mode on shared PC.

Cache

Temporary copies of images and pages stored locally for faster loading.
If a website shows old content, clear cache and refresh.

Incognito / Private browsing

  • Does not save history on local PC (still visible to ISP/school network admin)
  • Useful on cyber café or library computer
  • Always log out after banking/email on shared PC

8. Internet Safety While Browsing

Common online risks

Risk What happens Protection
Phishing site Fake login page steals password Check URL, padlock, official domain
Malware download Virus in fake software Official app stores only
Fake offers "Free iPhone" scam links Do not click suspicious ads
Public Wi-Fi sniffing Data intercepted Avoid banking on open Wi-Fi

Safe browsing checklist

  • Use HTTPS for sensitive sites
  • Keep browser and Windows updated
  • Install antivirus and enable firewall
  • Do not share OTP, PIN, password on call or chat
  • Verify .gov.in for government schemes
  • Report fraud at cybercrime.gov.in

Parental and student discipline

  • Limit screen time (samay prabandhan)
  • Do not meet strangers from chat without guardian
  • Think before posting personal photos (digital footprint)

9. Important CCC Exam Terms

Term Full form / meaning
WWW World Wide Web
URL Uniform Resource Locator
ISP Internet Service Provider
HTTP HyperText Transfer Protocol
HTTPS HTTP Secure
DNS Domain Name System
IP Internet Protocol address
Wi-Fi Wireless Fidelity
HTML HyperText Markup Language (web page language)

Lab Tasks

  1. Connect: Connect laptop/phone to Wi-Fi or mobile hotspot; note ISP name and signal strength.
  2. Browser tour: Open Chrome or Edge — identify address bar, tabs, bookmarks, history, downloads.
  3. URL practice: Visit https://www.digitalindia.gov.in — note protocol, domain, padlock icon.
  4. Search: Search NIELIT CCC exam pattern — open one official/trusted result; bookmark it (Ctrl+D).
  5. Advanced search: Use site:gov.in DigiLocker and open official page.
  6. Download: Download a PDF from a government site; save to Documents\CCC folder.
  7. Upload: Upload the same PDF to Google Drive (or institute cloud); note share link steps.
  8. Privacy: Open browser settings — find option to clear cookies and cache; list 3 cookie uses.
  9. HTTPS check: Compare HTTP vs HTTPS site — observe padlock difference.
  10. Safety list: Write 5 signs of a fake/phishing website.

Summary Checklist

  • I can explain internet, WWW, browser, URL, and ISP
  • I know parts of browser and shortcuts (Ctrl+T, Ctrl+D, Ctrl+H, Ctrl+J)
  • I can read URL parts and difference between HTTP and HTTPS
  • I can search effectively using keywords and site: filter
  • I can download and upload files safely
  • I understand cookies, cache, and private browsing
  • I follow safe browsing rules for CCC and daily life

Quick-Check MCQs (Questions Only)

  1. Internet is best described as (a) a global network of connected computers (b) a single supercomputer in Delhi (c) only email system (d) a type of printer

  2. WWW stands for (a) World Wide Web (b) World Windows Work (c) Wide Web Wire (d) Web World Write

  3. Software used to open websites is called (a) browser (b) spreadsheet (c) compiler (d) scanner driver

  4. URL is (a) the address of a web page (b) a type of RAM (c) printer command (d) keyboard layout

  5. ISP provides (a) internet connection service (b) free MS Office license (c) CPU replacement (d) monitor repair

  6. HTTPS compared to HTTP is (a) more secure with encryption (b) slower and never used (c) only for games (d) without any difference

  7. Download means (a) saving a file from internet to your device (b) sending file from device to internet (c) deleting browser history (d) shutting down the PC

  8. Cookies in a browser are (a) small files websites store on your computer (b) physical biscuits for lab (c) antivirus programs (d) types of CPU

  9. To search only government India sites, useful operator is (a) site:gov.in (b) print:gov (c) delete:all (d) virus:scan

  10. Padlock icon in address bar usually indicates (a) secure HTTPS connection (b) website is always free (c) no images on page (d) computer has virus

chapters.heading: Practice MCQs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 06: Internet & Web Browsing

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard

  2. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide

  3. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access

  4. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator

  5. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU

  6. HTTPS indicates? a) Secure connection b) Slow site c) No images d) Free Wi-Fi

  7. HTTP stands for? a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol b) High Transfer Text Program c) Hyper Tool Transfer d) Home Text Protocol

  8. Download means? a) Save from internet to PC b) Upload c) Delete d) Print

  9. Upload means? a) Send from PC to internet b) Download c) Shutdown d) Format

  10. Google is a? a) Search engine b) Operating system c) Word processor d) Printer

  11. Bookmark saves? a) Favorite website b) Deletes site c) Formats PC d) Installs OS

  12. Ctrl+T in browser? a) New tab b) Close tab c) Print d) Save page

  13. History shows? a) Past visited pages b) RAM usage c) CPU model d) Printer queue

  14. Home page is? a) First page of website b) Last page c) Error page d) BIOS

  15. Web page is? a) Single HTML document b) Entire website always c) Email d) Database table

  16. Modem/router connects? a) Home to ISP b) Keyboard to mouse c) Printer to scanner d) CPU to RAM only

  17. Wi-Fi is? a) Wireless network b) Wired cable only c) Printer type d) Virus

  18. Search with exact phrase use? a) Quotes "phrase" b) Brackets only c) Delete key d) F5 only

  19. site:gov.in search limits to? a) Government sites b) All games c) All shops d) Printers

  20. Pop-up blocker helps? a) Block unwanted windows b) Speed CPU c) Add RAM d) Print faster

  21. Cookies store? a) Site preferences/login info b) Hardware drivers c) CPU firmware d) Printer ink

  22. Refresh page? a) F5 or reload button b) Delete OS c) Format disk d) Uninstall browser

  23. Address bar used to? a) Type URL or search b) Print c) Scan d) Save Excel

  24. Browser tabs allow? a) Multiple pages open b) One page only c) No internet d) BIOS edit

  25. Downloading from official source is? a) Safer practice b) Always virus c) Illegal always d) Impossible

  26. Broadband is? a) High-speed internet b) Slow dial-up only c) Printer cable d) Keyboard type

  27. Mobile data connects phone via? a) Cellular network b) Printer only c) Scanner only d) HDMI only

  28. Hyperlink connects to? a) Another page/resource b) CPU only c) RAM only d) Printer ink

  29. Domain .edu often? a) Educational institution b) Government India only c) Games only d) Printer

  30. Phishing often via? a) Fake links/emails b) CPU upgrade c) RAM stick d) Mouse

  31. Incognito/private mode? a) Less local history saved b) Fully anonymous always guaranteed c) Free internet d) No HTTPS

  32. Search engine besides Google? a) Bing, DuckDuckGo b) Word, Excel c) Paint, Notepad d) None

  33. Web server stores? a) Website files b) Printer ink c) Keyboard keys d) Mouse ball

  34. HTML is? a) Markup language for web pages b) Spreadsheet c) Database d) OS

  35. Downloading PDF from trusted site? a) Check HTTPS and domain b) Click any popup c) Share password d) Disable antivirus

  36. Browser cache stores? a) Temporary page files b) CPU programs c) BIOS d) Printer drivers only

  37. Clear cache can fix? a) Loading/display issues b) Broken keyboard c) No monitor d) No CPU

  38. Default search engine set in? a) Browser settings b) BIOS only c) Word options d) Excel

  39. FTP used for? a) File transfer b) Email only c) Word processing d) Presentation

  40. Online form submit after? a) Reading instructions carefully b) Random clicks c) Sharing OTP d) Disabling HTTPS

  41. Digital footprint means? a) Trail of online activity b) Physical shoe size c) Printer mark d) CPU serial

  42. Web browser not same as? a) Search engine only b) Internet itself c) Website server d) All same always wrong

  43. LAN connects? a) Local area computers b) Only printers globally c) Only phones satellite d) BIOS chips

  44. DNS translates? a) Domain name to IP address b) Word to PDF c) Excel to PPT d) Email to print

  45. IPv4 address looks like? a) 192.168.1.1 b) www.google.com c) user@gmail.com d) .docx

  46. Streaming plays media? a) Without full download first b) Only after printing c) Only on paper d) Never online

  47. Browser extension adds? a) Extra features b) CPU hardware c) RAM chips d) Printer toner

  48. Safe download check? a) Verify publisher and HTTPS b) Double every popup c) Turn off firewall d) Share Aadhaar

  49. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard

  50. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide

questions.question: WWW stands for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

questions.explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

questions.question: HTTP stands for?

chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 06: Internet & Web Browsing

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard Answer: a) Global network of computers Explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

  2. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide Answer: a) World Wide Web Explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

  3. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access Answer: a) Google Chrome Explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  4. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator Answer: a) Uniform Resource Locator Explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

  5. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU Answer: a) Internet connection Explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  6. HTTPS indicates? a) Secure connection b) Slow site c) No images d) Free Wi-Fi Answer: a) Secure connection Explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

  7. HTTP stands for? a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol b) High Transfer Text Program c) Hyper Tool Transfer d) Home Text Protocol Answer: a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol Explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

  8. Download means? a) Save from internet to PC b) Upload c) Delete d) Print Answer: a) Save from internet to PC Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

  9. Upload means? a) Send from PC to internet b) Download c) Shutdown d) Format Answer: a) Send from PC to internet Explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

  10. Google is a? a) Search engine b) Operating system c) Word processor d) Printer Answer: a) Search engine Explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

  11. Bookmark saves? a) Favorite website b) Deletes site c) Formats PC d) Installs OS Answer: a) Favorite website Explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  12. Ctrl+T in browser? a) New tab b) Close tab c) Print d) Save page Answer: a) New tab Explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  13. History shows? a) Past visited pages b) RAM usage c) CPU model d) Printer queue Answer: a) Past visited pages Explanation: Past visited pages — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  14. Home page is? a) First page of website b) Last page c) Error page d) BIOS Answer: a) First page of website Explanation: First page of website is the correct answer. Last page and Error page are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. Web page is? a) Single HTML document b) Entire website always c) Email d) Database table Answer: a) Single HTML document Explanation: Single HTML document is the correct answer. Entire website always and Email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Modem/router connects? a) Home to ISP b) Keyboard to mouse c) Printer to scanner d) CPU to RAM only Answer: a) Home to ISP Explanation: Home to ISP is the correct answer. Keyboard to mouse and Printer to scanner are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Wi-Fi is? a) Wireless network b) Wired cable only c) Printer type d) Virus Answer: a) Wireless network Explanation: Wireless network — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  18. Search with exact phrase use? a) Quotes "phrase" b) Brackets only c) Delete key d) F5 only Answer: a) Quotes "phrase" Explanation: Quotes "phrase" is the correct answer. Brackets only and Delete key are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  19. site:gov.in search limits to? a) Government sites b) All games c) All shops d) Printers Answer: a) Government sites Explanation: Government sites is the correct answer. All games and All shops are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. Pop-up blocker helps? a) Block unwanted windows b) Speed CPU c) Add RAM d) Print faster Answer: a) Block unwanted windows Explanation: Block unwanted windows — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  21. Cookies store? a) Site preferences/login info b) Hardware drivers c) CPU firmware d) Printer ink Answer: a) Site preferences/login info Explanation: Site preferences/login info — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  22. Refresh page? a) F5 or reload button b) Delete OS c) Format disk d) Uninstall browser Answer: a) F5 or reload button Explanation: F5 or reload button is the correct answer. Delete OS and Format disk are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  23. Address bar used to? a) Type URL or search b) Print c) Scan d) Save Excel Answer: a) Type URL or search Explanation: Type URL or search is the main purpose or function described in the question. Print and Scan are not the right choices here.

  24. Browser tabs allow? a) Multiple pages open b) One page only c) No internet d) BIOS edit Answer: a) Multiple pages open Explanation: Multiple pages open is the correct answer. One page only and No internet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  25. Downloading from official source is? a) Safer practice b) Always virus c) Illegal always d) Impossible Answer: a) Safer practice Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

  26. Broadband is? a) High-speed internet b) Slow dial-up only c) Printer cable d) Keyboard type Answer: a) High-speed internet Explanation: High-speed internet — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  27. Mobile data connects phone via? a) Cellular network b) Printer only c) Scanner only d) HDMI only Answer: a) Cellular network Explanation: Cellular network is the correct answer. Printer only and Scanner only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. Hyperlink connects to? a) Another page/resource b) CPU only c) RAM only d) Printer ink Answer: a) Another page/resource Explanation: Another page/resource is the correct answer. CPU only and RAM only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Domain .edu often? a) Educational institution b) Government India only c) Games only d) Printer Answer: a) Educational institution Explanation: Educational institution is the correct answer. Government India only and Games only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. Phishing often via? a) Fake links/emails b) CPU upgrade c) RAM stick d) Mouse Answer: a) Fake links/emails Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  31. Incognito/private mode? a) Less local history saved b) Fully anonymous always guaranteed c) Free internet d) No HTTPS Answer: a) Less local history saved Explanation: Less local history saved is the correct answer. Fully anonymous always guaranteed and Free internet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Search engine besides Google? a) Bing, DuckDuckGo b) Word, Excel c) Paint, Notepad d) None Answer: a) Bing, DuckDuckGo Explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

  33. Web server stores? a) Website files b) Printer ink c) Keyboard keys d) Mouse ball Answer: a) Website files Explanation: Website files is the correct answer. Printer ink and Keyboard keys are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  34. HTML is? a) Markup language for web pages b) Spreadsheet c) Database d) OS Answer: a) Markup language for web pages Explanation: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the code used to structure web pages displayed in browsers.

  35. Downloading PDF from trusted site? a) Check HTTPS and domain b) Click any popup c) Share password d) Disable antivirus Answer: a) Check HTTPS and domain Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

  36. Browser cache stores? a) Temporary page files b) CPU programs c) BIOS d) Printer drivers only Answer: a) Temporary page files Explanation: Temporary page files is the correct answer. CPU programs and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  37. Clear cache can fix? a) Loading/display issues b) Broken keyboard c) No monitor d) No CPU Answer: a) Loading/display issues Explanation: Loading/display issues is the correct answer. Broken keyboard and No monitor are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. Default search engine set in? a) Browser settings b) BIOS only c) Word options d) Excel Answer: a) Browser settings Explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

  39. FTP used for? a) File transfer b) Email only c) Word processing d) Presentation Answer: a) File transfer Explanation: File transfer is the main purpose or function described in the question. Email only and Word processing are not the right choices here.

  40. Online form submit after? a) Reading instructions carefully b) Random clicks c) Sharing OTP d) Disabling HTTPS Answer: a) Reading instructions carefully Explanation: Reading instructions carefully is the correct answer. Random clicks and Sharing OTP are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Digital footprint means? a) Trail of online activity b) Physical shoe size c) Printer mark d) CPU serial Answer: a) Trail of online activity Explanation: Trail of online activity is the correct answer. Physical shoe size and Printer mark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  42. Web browser not same as? a) Search engine only b) Internet itself c) Website server d) All same always wrong Answer: a) Search engine only Explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

  43. LAN connects? a) Local area computers b) Only printers globally c) Only phones satellite d) BIOS chips Answer: a) Local area computers Explanation: Local area computers — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  44. DNS translates? a) Domain name to IP address b) Word to PDF c) Excel to PPT d) Email to print Answer: a) Domain name to IP address Explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) converts website names like google.com into numeric IP addresses computers use.

  45. IPv4 address looks like? a) 192.168.1.1 b) www.google.com c) user@gmail.com d) .docx Answer: a) 192.168.1.1 Explanation: 192.168.1.1 is the correct answer. www.google.com and user@gmail.com are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  46. Streaming plays media? a) Without full download first b) Only after printing c) Only on paper d) Never online Answer: a) Without full download first Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

  47. Browser extension adds? a) Extra features b) CPU hardware c) RAM chips d) Printer toner Answer: a) Extra features Explanation: Extra features is the correct answer. CPU hardware and RAM chips are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  48. Safe download check? a) Verify publisher and HTTPS b) Double every popup c) Turn off firewall d) Share Aadhaar Answer: a) Verify publisher and HTTPS Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

  49. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard Answer: a) Global network of computers Explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

  50. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide Answer: a) World Wide Web Explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

Short Answer Key

  1. Define key concept in Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  2. List five important terms from Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  3. Name three practical tasks for Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  4. Why is Internet & Web Browsing important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  5. Give two exam tips for Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  6. Define key concept in Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  7. List five important terms from Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  8. Name three practical tasks for Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  9. Why is Internet & Web Browsing important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  10. Give two exam tips for Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  11. Define key concept in Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  12. List five important terms from Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  13. Name three practical tasks for Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  14. Why is Internet & Web Browsing important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  15. Give two exam tips for Internet & Web Browsing. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

Practical Checklist Key

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Internet & Web Browsing study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  2. Complete lab task 2 from Internet & Web Browsing study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  6. Peer review exercise in lab. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  8. Document steps in notebook. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.
