questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

questions.question: Browser cache stores?
questions.explanation: Temporary page files is the correct answer. CPU programs and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Clear cache can fix?
questions.explanation: Loading/display issues is the correct answer. Broken keyboard and No monitor are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Default search engine set in?
questions.explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

questions.question: FTP used for?
questions.explanation: File transfer is the main purpose or function described in the question. Email only and Word processing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Online form submit after?
questions.explanation: Reading instructions carefully is the correct answer. Random clicks and Sharing OTP are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Digital footprint means?
questions.explanation: Trail of online activity is the correct answer. Physical shoe size and Printer mark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Web browser not same as?
questions.explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

questions.question: LAN connects?
questions.explanation: Local area computers — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: DNS translates?
questions.explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) converts website names like google.com into numeric IP addresses computers use.

questions.question: IPv4 address looks like?
questions.explanation: 192.168.1.1 is the correct answer. www.google.com and user@gmail.com are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Streaming plays media?
questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

questions.question: Browser extension adds?
questions.explanation: Extra features is the correct answer. CPU hardware and RAM chips are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Safe download check?
questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

questions.question: Internet is?
questions.explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

questions.question: WWW stands for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Subject 7: Communication & Collaboration (8 Hours)

Learning Objectives

  • Create and use email for study and official communication
  • Understand To, CC, BCC, subject, body, and attachments
  • Use Gmail and basic Outlook webmail features
  • Join and participate in online meetings (Google Meet, Zoom)
  • Store and share files using cloud Drive services
  • Understand social media basics and responsible use
  • Follow email and online netiquette (sahi vyavahar)

1. Electronic Mail (Email)

Email = sending messages over the internet. Fast, cheap, and used for study, jobs, and government services.

Email address format

username@domain.com
   │        │
   │        └── Domain (Gmail, Outlook, company, college)
   └── Your chosen name (unique on that domain)

Examples: student@gmail.com, name@college.edu.in

Webmail vs Email client

Type Access Examples
Webmail Browser Gmail.com, Outlook.com
Email client Desktop/mobile app Outlook app, Thunderbird

CCC focus: Webmail (Gmail) is most common in exam and daily use.

2. Email Components

Field Purpose
To Main recipient(s) — must have at least one
CC (Carbon Copy) Extra recipients — everyone can see all CC names
BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) Hidden copy — other recipients cannot see BCC list
Subject Short title — must be clear and relevant
Body Main message text
Attachment File sent with email (PDF, photo, Word)
Signature Name, phone, designation at bottom (optional)

When to use CC vs BCC

  • CC: Inform teacher and class rep together — transparency
  • BCC: Send newsletter to many people without exposing everyone's email (privacy)

Attachment rules

  • Click paperclip icon to attach file
  • Gmail limit ~25 MB per email (large files → use Drive link)
  • Scan attachments with antivirus if from unknown sender
  • Common formats: .pdf, .docx, .jpg, .png

3. Gmail — Practical Guide

Gmail = Google's free email service (Google account required).

Creating Gmail account

  1. Visit gmail.com → Create account
  2. Enter name, choose username, strong password
  3. Add recovery phone/email for account recovery
  4. Enable 2-Step Verification (recommended)

Main Gmail actions

Action Steps
Compose Click Compose → fill To, Subject, Body → Send
Reply Answer only the sender
Reply All Answer sender + all To/CC recipients
Forward Send received mail to another person
Star / Mark important Flag mail for follow-up
Archive Remove from Inbox but keep mail
Trash / Delete Move to bin
Search Use search bar — from:teacher subject:CCC
Labels / Folders Organize mail by topic

Gmail safety settings

  • Check Last account activity (bottom of Gmail) on shared PC
  • Review Filters for auto-sorting institute mail
  • Never share Gmail password; log out on cyber café

4. Email Writing and Netiquette

Netiquette = polite and correct behaviour on the internet (Internet + etiquette).

Professional email structure

  1. Subject: Clear — CCC Assignment Unit 7 — Ravi Kumar
  2. Greeting: Respected Sir/Madam or Dear [Name]
  3. Body: Short paragraphs; purpose in first lines
  4. Closing: Thank you, Regards, your full name
  5. Signature: Class, roll number, phone (if official)

Do's and Don'ts

Do Don't
Use proper grammar Write ALL CAPS (jorse chillana — looks rude)
Reply within reasonable time Send virus attachments
Proofread before Send Share passwords or OTP in email
Use BCC for bulk mail Forward chain mails without checking
Keep official tone Use slang in job/college mail

Spam and phishing in inbox

  • Spam = unwanted bulk mail — mark as Spam, do not reply
  • Phishing email = fake bank/Gmail warning — check sender address, do not click suspicious links

5. Online Meetings — Google Meet and Zoom

Online classes and interviews use video conferencing tools.

Google Meet

  • Join via link or meeting code from teacher
  • Needs Google account or guest name entry
  • Features: camera, mic, chat, screen share, raise hand
  • Mute when not speaking (background noise kam)

Zoom

  • Join via link or Meeting ID + Passcode
  • Host can control mute, waiting room, recording
  • Chat panel for questions

Meeting etiquette (CCC lab useful)

Rule Reason
Join 2–5 minutes early Test mic and camera
Dress decently Professional appearance
Stable internet Avoid disconnect
Quiet place Clear audio
Camera on when asked Better engagement
Use chat for doubts Do not interrupt speaker

Screen sharing

Teacher shares PPT; student may share assignment for demo. Only share intended window — close personal tabs first.

6. Cloud Storage and Collaboration

Cloud = files stored on internet servers, accessible from any device.

Google Drive

  • Free storage (15 GB with Google account)
  • Upload files/folders from PC
  • Share with link — permission: Viewer / Commenter / Editor
  • Google Docs, Sheets, Slides — real-time collaboration (sah-karya)

Microsoft OneDrive

  • Linked to Microsoft account
  • Integrates with Word, Excel online
  • Share and co-edit documents

Collaboration features

  • Version history — see who changed what
  • Comments — feedback without editing main text
  • Shared folder — group project files in one place

CCC exam points

  • Cloud = backup + sharing + teamwork
  • Link sharing safer than huge email attachments
  • Set permission to View only when sending notes publicly

7. Social Media Basics

Social media = platforms to connect, share, and learn.

Platform Main use
WhatsApp Messaging, groups, voice/video call
Facebook Community, pages, events
Instagram Photos, reels, short video
LinkedIn Professional network, jobs
YouTube Learning videos, tutorials
X (Twitter) News, short updates

Responsible social media use

  • Do not share Aadhaar, OTP, bank details in chat
  • Verify news before forwarding (fake news / galat khabar)
  • Respect privacy — ask before posting others' photos
  • Report abuse using platform Report button
  • Follow age rules and parental guidance

WhatsApp for study groups

  • Mute noisy groups during study time
  • Use broadcast lists for one-way announcements
  • Pin important messages (exam date, link)

8. Other Collaboration Tools (Awareness)

Tool Use
Microsoft Teams College/office classes and chat
Slack Workplace team channels
Telegram Large channels, file sharing
Google Classroom Assignments and grading

CCC may ask concept — "tool for online class collaboration" → Meet, Zoom, Teams.

9. Important CCC Exam Terms

Term Meaning
Email Electronic mail
CC Carbon Copy — visible to all
BCC Blind Carbon Copy — hidden
Attachment File with email
Webmail Email through browser
Cloud storage Online file storage
Netiquette Internet manners
Phishing Fake messages to steal data
Screen share Show your screen in meeting
2FA / 2-Step Extra login security

Lab Tasks

  1. Gmail setup: Create or open Gmail — complete profile and recovery phone.
  2. Compose: Send email to trainer with subject CCC Unit 7 — [Your Name] and short introduction.
  3. CC/BCC: Send one mail with CC to classmate; another with BCC to two emails — observe visibility difference.
  4. Attachment: Attach a Word/PDF assignment; note size limit message if file is large.
  5. Reply chain: Reply and Reply All to a sample thread — note who receives mail.
  6. Forward: Forward an institute notice to your own alternate email.
  7. Drive: Upload assignment to Google Drive; share view-only link in email instead of attachment.
  8. Meet/Zoom: Join a practice meeting — test mic, camera, chat, and mute.
  9. Netiquette: Write one formal and one informal email on same topic — compare tone.
  10. Social media: List 3 privacy settings you should enable on any one platform you use.

Summary Checklist

  • I can compose, reply, forward, and attach files in Gmail
  • I know difference between To, CC, and BCC
  • I follow netiquette for study and official mail
  • I can join Meet/Zoom and use mute, chat, screen share
  • I can upload and share files on Google Drive with correct permission
  • I understand safe and responsible social media use
  • I can identify spam and phishing emails

Quick-Check MCQs (Questions Only)

  1. Correct email format is (a) user@domain.com (b) user.domain only (c) @domain without user (d) http://user.com

  2. CC in email means (a) visible copy to additional recipients (b) hidden copy (c) delete copy (d) print command

  3. BCC is used when (a) recipients should not see each other's addresses (b) mail must be printed (c) attachment is removed (d) subject is blank

  4. Paperclip icon in email is for (a) attachment (b) delete mail (c) change font (d) shutdown PC

  5. Gmail is an example of (a) webmail service (b) operating system (c) spreadsheet (d) printer driver

  6. Google Meet is mainly used for (a) online video meetings (b) virus scanning (c) disk formatting (d) BIOS setup

  7. Cloud storage like Google Drive stores files (a) on internet servers accessible online (b) only inside CPU (c) on printer drum (d) in keyboard

  8. Netiquette refers to (a) proper behaviour on the internet (b) network cable colour (c) antivirus brand (d) type of RAM

  9. Sharing a 50 MB video in Gmail is better done by (a) Drive link instead of direct attachment (b) sending 50 separate emails (c) posting password in subject (d) disabling internet

  10. Phishing email often tries to (a) steal login or bank details through fake messages (b) improve computer speed (c) install legal updates only (d) teach typing skills

chapters.title: Practice MCQs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 07: Communication & Collaboration

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail

  2. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only

  3. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home

  4. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy

  5. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy

  6. Reply sends to? a) Original sender b) Everyone BCC hidden c) Nobody d) Printer

  7. Forward sends? a) Copy to new recipient b) Deletes original c) Formats PC d) Installs OS

  8. Gmail provided by? a) Google b) Microsoft only c) Apple only d) Government only

  9. Outlook email by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Linux kernel

  10. Compose email needs? a) To, Subject, Body b) CPU serial only c) Printer model d) BIOS version

  11. Email etiquette includes? a) Clear subject line b) ALL CAPS shouting OK always c) Share passwords d) No greeting ever required wrong

  12. Cloud storage example? a) Google Drive b) CPU cache c) RAM stick d) Printer tray

  13. Google Meet used for? a) Video meetings b) Word processing c) Database d) Antivirus

  14. Zoom is? a) Video conferencing tool b) Spreadsheet c) OS d) Printer driver

  15. Microsoft Teams for? a) Collaboration and meetings b) Only games c) Only photos d) BIOS

  16. WhatsApp Web requires? a) Phone linked scan QR b) Only printer c) Only scanner d) BIOS password

  17. Share Drive file link sets? a) View or edit permission b) Deletes file c) Formats PC d) Removes OS

  18. Spam is? a) Unwanted bulk email b) Helpful newsletter always c) OS update d) CPU

  19. Mark as spam should? a) Not reply to suspicious b) Reply with password c) Forward to all d) Click all links

  20. Video call needs usually? a) Camera mic internet b) Printer only c) Scanner only d) Floppy disk

  21. Mute in meeting? a) Others cannot hear you b) You cannot hear c) Meeting ends d) PC shuts down

  22. Screen share shows? a) Your screen to participants b) CPU chip c) RAM label d) Printer ink

  23. Collaboration doc editing? a) Multiple users same file online b) One user paper only c) No internet ever d) BIOS edit

  24. Social media examples? a) Facebook, LinkedIn b) Excel, Word only c) BIOS, CPU only d) None

  25. Professional network often? a) LinkedIn b) Only games c) Only music d) Printer manual

  26. Do not share in email? a) Passwords and OTP b) Assignment files allowed sometimes c) Resume when applying OK d) Project docs when needed

  27. Email signature includes? a) Name contact info b) Password c) OTP d) Bank PIN

  28. Large file share better via? a) Cloud link not huge attachment b) 10 GB attachment always c) Floppy d) BIOS

  29. Calendar invite for? a) Scheduling meetings b) Virus scan c) Format disk d) Print only

  30. Instant messaging is? a) Real-time text chat b) Postal mail c) Fax only d) Printer queue

  31. Telegram/WhatsApp are? a) Messaging apps b) Spreadsheets c) Databases d) OS kernels

  32. OneDrive cloud by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Intel

  33. Dropbox is? a) Cloud file storage b) Antivirus c) Word processor d) CPU

  34. Reply All sends to? a) All original recipients b) Sender only c) Nobody d) Printer

  35. Draft email saved? a) Incomplete message saved b) Sent automatically c) Deleted always d) Printed

  36. Out of office auto-reply? a) Automatic response when away b) Virus c) Spam filter off d) BIOS

  37. Netiquette means? a) Online behavior etiquette b) Network cable type c) Printer setting d) CPU speed

  38. Phishing email may ask? a) Password or OTP wrongly b) Nothing suspicious ever c) Only hello d) Only weather

  39. Verify sender email by? a) Checking address carefully b) Clicking all links c) Downloading exe d) Sharing Aadhaar

  40. Collaborative whiteboard tools? a) Jamboard, Miro examples b) Only Notepad c) Only Calculator d) BIOS setup

  41. Breakout rooms in meetings? a) Small group sessions b) Delete participants c) Format PC d) Print slides

  42. Recording meeting needs? a) Consent and policy b) Never inform anyone wrong c) Always illegal wrong blanket d) Printer permission

  43. Sync cloud folder means? a) Files updated across devices b) Delete local copies always wrong c) Remove internet d) BIOS flash

  44. Email phishing red flag? a) Urgent threats and bad grammar b) Official logo always means safe wrong c) HTTPS link always safe wrong alone d) Known contact always safe wrong

  45. Chat emoji in formal job email? a) Use sparingly professionally b) Required 100 emojis c) Replaces subject line d) Replaces signature bank PIN

  46. Shared document comment feature? a) Feedback without changing main text optionally b) Deletes file c) Formats C: d) Removes OS

  47. Webmail accessed via? a) Browser b) Only postal mail c) Only fax d) Printer panel

  48. Email client example? a) Outlook app Thunderbird b) Chrome browser only always wrong both can c) Calculator d) Paint

  49. 2FA on email adds? a) Extra login security b) Second printer c) More spam d) Removes password

  50. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Communication & Collaboration.
  2. List five important terms from Communication & Collaboration.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Communication & Collaboration.
  4. Why is Communication & Collaboration important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Communication & Collaboration.
  6. Define key concept in Communication & Collaboration.
  7. List five important terms from Communication & Collaboration.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Communication & Collaboration.
  9. Why is Communication & Collaboration important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Communication & Collaboration.
  11. Define key concept in Communication & Collaboration.
  12. List five important terms from Communication & Collaboration.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Communication & Collaboration.
  14. Why is Communication & Collaboration important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Communication & Collaboration.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Communication & Collaboration study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Communication & Collaboration study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

chapters.title: Answer Key (unlock after practice)
chapters.heading: Answer Key (unlock after practice)
chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 07: Communication & Collaboration

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail Answer: a) Electronic Mail Explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

  2. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only Answer: a) user@domain.com Explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

  3. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home Answer: a) Paperclip Explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  4. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy Answer: a) Carbon copy visible Explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

  5. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy Answer: a) Blind carbon copy hidden Explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

  6. Reply sends to? a) Original sender b) Everyone BCC hidden c) Nobody d) Printer Answer: a) Original sender Explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  7. Forward sends? a) Copy to new recipient b) Deletes original c) Formats PC d) Installs OS Answer: a) Copy to new recipient Explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  8. Gmail provided by? a) Google b) Microsoft only c) Apple only d) Government only Answer: a) Google Explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Outlook email by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Linux kernel Answer: a) Microsoft Explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  10. Compose email needs? a) To, Subject, Body b) CPU serial only c) Printer model d) BIOS version Answer: a) To, Subject, Body Explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  11. Email etiquette includes? a) Clear subject line b) ALL CAPS shouting OK always c) Share passwords d) No greeting ever required wrong Answer: a) Clear subject line Explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  12. Cloud storage example? a) Google Drive b) CPU cache c) RAM stick d) Printer tray Answer: a) Google Drive Explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

  13. Google Meet used for? a) Video meetings b) Word processing c) Database d) Antivirus Answer: a) Video meetings Explanation: Tools like Google Meet, Zoom, and Teams let people join video calls and share screens for online classes and meetings.

  14. Zoom is? a) Video conferencing tool b) Spreadsheet c) OS d) Printer driver Answer: a) Video conferencing tool Explanation: Tools like Google Meet, Zoom, and Teams let people join video calls and share screens for online classes and meetings.

  15. Microsoft Teams for? a) Collaboration and meetings b) Only games c) Only photos d) BIOS Answer: a) Collaboration and meetings Explanation: Tools like Google Meet, Zoom, and Teams let people join video calls and share screens for online classes and meetings.

  16. WhatsApp Web requires? a) Phone linked scan QR b) Only printer c) Only scanner d) BIOS password Answer: a) Phone linked scan QR Explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

  17. Share Drive file link sets? a) View or edit permission b) Deletes file c) Formats PC d) Removes OS Answer: a) View or edit permission Explanation: View or edit permission is the correct answer. Deletes file and Formats PC are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  18. Spam is? a) Unwanted bulk email b) Helpful newsletter always c) OS update d) CPU Answer: a) Unwanted bulk email Explanation: Unwanted bulk email — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  19. Mark as spam should? a) Not reply to suspicious b) Reply with password c) Forward to all d) Click all links Answer: a) Not reply to suspicious Explanation: Not reply to suspicious is the correct answer. Reply with password and Forward to all are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. Video call needs usually? a) Camera mic internet b) Printer only c) Scanner only d) Floppy disk Answer: a) Camera mic internet Explanation: Camera mic internet is the correct answer. Printer only and Scanner only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  21. Mute in meeting? a) Others cannot hear you b) You cannot hear c) Meeting ends d) PC shuts down Answer: a) Others cannot hear you Explanation: Others cannot hear you — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  22. Screen share shows? a) Your screen to participants b) CPU chip c) RAM label d) Printer ink Answer: a) Your screen to participants Explanation: Your screen to participants — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  23. Collaboration doc editing? a) Multiple users same file online b) One user paper only c) No internet ever d) BIOS edit Answer: a) Multiple users same file online Explanation: Multiple users same file online is the correct answer. One user paper only and No internet ever are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  24. Social media examples? a) Facebook, LinkedIn b) Excel, Word only c) BIOS, CPU only d) None Answer: a) Facebook, LinkedIn Explanation: Facebook, LinkedIn is the correct answer. Excel, Word only and BIOS, CPU only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  25. Professional network often? a) LinkedIn b) Only games c) Only music d) Printer manual Answer: a) LinkedIn Explanation: LinkedIn is the correct answer. Only games and Only music are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. Do not share in email? a) Passwords and OTP b) Assignment files allowed sometimes c) Resume when applying OK d) Project docs when needed Answer: a) Passwords and OTP Explanation: Passwords and OTP is the correct answer. Assignment files allowed sometimes and Resume when applying OK are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  27. Email signature includes? a) Name contact info b) Password c) OTP d) Bank PIN Answer: a) Name contact info Explanation: Name contact info is the correct answer. Password and OTP are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. Large file share better via? a) Cloud link not huge attachment b) 10 GB attachment always c) Floppy d) BIOS Answer: a) Cloud link not huge attachment Explanation: Cloud link not huge attachment is the correct answer. 10 GB attachment always and Floppy are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Calendar invite for? a) Scheduling meetings b) Virus scan c) Format disk d) Print only Answer: a) Scheduling meetings Explanation: Scheduling meetings is the correct answer. Virus scan and Format disk are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. Instant messaging is? a) Real-time text chat b) Postal mail c) Fax only d) Printer queue Answer: a) Real-time text chat Explanation: Real-time text chat is the correct answer. Postal mail and Fax only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  31. Telegram/WhatsApp are? a) Messaging apps b) Spreadsheets c) Databases d) OS kernels Answer: a) Messaging apps Explanation: Messaging apps is the correct answer. Spreadsheets and Databases are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. OneDrive cloud by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Intel Answer: a) Microsoft Explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Dropbox is? a) Cloud file storage b) Antivirus c) Word processor d) CPU Answer: a) Cloud file storage Explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

  34. Reply All sends to? a) All original recipients b) Sender only c) Nobody d) Printer Answer: a) All original recipients Explanation: All original recipients is the correct answer. Sender only and Nobody are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  35. Draft email saved? a) Incomplete message saved b) Sent automatically c) Deleted always d) Printed Answer: a) Incomplete message saved Explanation: Incomplete message saved is the correct answer. Sent automatically and Deleted always are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  36. Out of office auto-reply? a) Automatic response when away b) Virus c) Spam filter off d) BIOS Answer: a) Automatic response when away Explanation: Automatic response when away is the correct answer. Virus and Spam filter off are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  37. Netiquette means? a) Online behavior etiquette b) Network cable type c) Printer setting d) CPU speed Answer: a) Online behavior etiquette Explanation: Online behavior etiquette — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  38. Phishing email may ask? a) Password or OTP wrongly b) Nothing suspicious ever c) Only hello d) Only weather Answer: a) Password or OTP wrongly Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  39. Verify sender email by? a) Checking address carefully b) Clicking all links c) Downloading exe d) Sharing Aadhaar Answer: a) Checking address carefully Explanation: Checking address carefully is the correct answer. Clicking all links and Downloading exe are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  40. Collaborative whiteboard tools? a) Jamboard, Miro examples b) Only Notepad c) Only Calculator d) BIOS setup Answer: a) Jamboard, Miro examples Explanation: Jamboard, Miro examples is the correct answer. Only Notepad and Only Calculator are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Breakout rooms in meetings? a) Small group sessions b) Delete participants c) Format PC d) Print slides Answer: a) Small group sessions Explanation: Small group sessions is the correct answer. Delete participants and Format PC are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  42. Recording meeting needs? a) Consent and policy b) Never inform anyone wrong c) Always illegal wrong blanket d) Printer permission Answer: a) Consent and policy Explanation: Consent and policy is the correct answer. Never inform anyone wrong and Always illegal wrong blanket are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  43. Sync cloud folder means? a) Files updated across devices b) Delete local copies always wrong c) Remove internet d) BIOS flash Answer: a) Files updated across devices Explanation: Files updated across devices is the correct answer. Delete local copies always wrong and Remove internet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  44. Email phishing red flag? a) Urgent threats and bad grammar b) Official logo always means safe wrong c) HTTPS link always safe wrong alone d) Known contact always safe wrong Answer: a) Urgent threats and bad grammar Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  45. Chat emoji in formal job email? a) Use sparingly professionally b) Required 100 emojis c) Replaces subject line d) Replaces signature bank PIN Answer: a) Use sparingly professionally Explanation: Use sparingly professionally is the correct answer. Required 100 emojis and Replaces subject line are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  46. Shared document comment feature? a) Feedback without changing main text optionally b) Deletes file c) Formats C: d) Removes OS Answer: a) Feedback without changing main text optionally Explanation: Feedback without changing main text optionally is the correct answer. Deletes file and Formats C: are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  47. Webmail accessed via? a) Browser b) Only postal mail c) Only fax d) Printer panel Answer: a) Browser Explanation: Browser is the correct answer. Only postal mail and Only fax are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  48. Email client example? a) Outlook app Thunderbird b) Chrome browser only always wrong both can c) Calculator d) Paint Answer: a) Outlook app Thunderbird Explanation: Outlook app Thunderbird is a correct example for what the question asks about. Chrome browser only always wrong both can and Calculator are not the right choices here.

  49. 2FA on email adds? a) Extra login security b) Second printer c) More spam d) Removes password Answer: a) Extra login security Explanation: Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds a second step (OTP, app code, fingerprint) after your password for stronger security.

  50. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail Answer: a) Electronic Mail Explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

Short Answer Key

  1. Define key concept in Communication & Collaboration. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  2. List five important terms from Communication & Collaboration. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  3. Name three practical tasks for Communication & Collaboration. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  4. Why is Communication & Collaboration important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  5. Give two exam tips for Communication & Collaboration. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  6. Define key concept in Communication & Collaboration. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  7. List five important terms from Communication & Collaboration. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  8. Name three practical tasks for Communication & Collaboration. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  9. Why is Communication & Collaboration important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  10. Give two exam tips for Communication & Collaboration. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  11. Define key concept in Communication & Collaboration. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  12. List five important terms from Communication & Collaboration. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  13. Name three practical tasks for Communication & Collaboration. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  14. Why is Communication & Collaboration important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  15. Give two exam tips for Communication & Collaboration. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

Practical Checklist Key

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Communication & Collaboration study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  2. Complete lab task 2 from Communication & Collaboration study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  6. Peer review exercise in lab. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  8. Document steps in notebook. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Subject 8: Digital Financial Services (6 Hours)

Learning Objectives

  • Understand digital payment systems used in India (UPI, wallets, net banking)
  • Explain NPCI, BHIM, and AEPS in simple terms
  • Use QR codes and mobile banking safely
  • Know IMPS, NEFT, and RTGS for money transfer
  • Follow OTP, PIN, and password safety for financial apps
  • Identify common digital payment frauds and protections

1. Digital India and Cashless Economy

Digital financial services = banking and payments through mobile, internet, and cards instead of only cash (nakad).

Benefits

  • Fast transfer anytime (24×7 for many services)
  • Less need to carry cash
  • Easy record of transactions (ledger / lekhha)
  • Government schemes and subsidies direct to bank (DBT — Direct Benefit Transfer)

RBI and NPCI role

  • RBI (Reserve Bank of India) — central bank; regulates banking and payment rules
  • NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India) — builds and runs retail payment systems (UPI, RuPay, AePS)

2. UPI — Unified Payments Interface

UPI = instant bank-to-bank payment using mobile app and UPI ID or QR code.

How UPI works (simple steps)

  1. Install UPI app (PhonePe, Google Pay, Paytm, BHIM, bank app)
  2. Link bank account and verify mobile number registered with bank
  3. Create UPI PIN (4 or 6 digit — different from app login password)
  4. Pay using UPI ID, QR scan, or mobile number

UPI terms

Term Meaning
UPI ID / VPA Virtual address like name@oksbi
UPI PIN Secret PIN to authorize payment from bank
QR Code Scannable code for merchant payment
Request money Ask someone to pay you via UPI
Transaction ID Reference number after payment — save screenshot

UPI limits (general awareness)

  • Per transaction and per day limits set by bank and app
  • For high amounts, bank may ask extra verification

3. BHIM App

BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) = government-promoted UPI app by NPCI.

Features

  • Send/request money via UPI
  • Scan QR and pay
  • Check balance (with UPI PIN)
  • Works across participating banks
  • Available in multiple Indian languages

Exam tip: BHIM is based on UPI platform developed by NPCI.

4. Mobile Wallets

Wallet = stored prepaid balance in app for quick small payments.

Wallet type Example Note
Semi-closed Paytm wallet, PhonePe wallet Pay approved merchants
Bank-linked UPI Google Pay, BHIM Direct from bank account
Open wallet Some bank wallets Broader use with KYC

Wallet vs UPI

  • Wallet: Money loaded in advance in app
  • UPI: Money debited directly from linked bank account

KYC (Know Your Customer)

Identity verification (Aadhaar, PAN) for higher limits and full features — required by RBI rules.

5. Net Banking (Internet Banking)

Net banking = access bank account through bank website or app.

Common services

  • Check balance and mini statement
  • Fund transfer (NEFT, IMPS, RTGS, within bank)
  • Pay bills (electricity, mobile, tax)
  • Open FD/RD, request cheque book
  • Block lost debit card

Login security

  • User ID + Password + often OTP to registered mobile
  • Use official bank URL — type yourself, do not click email links
  • Enable transaction alerts on SMS/email

6. AePS — Aadhaar Enabled Payment System

AePS = withdraw cash, check balance, or deposit using Aadhaar number and fingerprint at bank mitra / micro ATM.

Uses in rural India

  • Banking without smartphone
  • Government benefit withdrawal
  • NPCI infrastructure through bank BC (Business Correspondent) points

Exam point: AePS uses Aadhaar authentication + bank link — operated under NPCI.

7. QR Code Payments

QR (Quick Response) code = square barcode scanned by phone camera.

Types

  • Bharat QR — standard for Indian merchants
  • UPI QR — pay to merchant UPI ID
  • Static QR — same code for all amounts (shop display)
  • Dynamic QR — amount embedded for each bill

Safe QR scanning

  • Check merchant name shown in app before entering UPI PIN
  • Do not scan unknown QR stickers in public places (fraud trap)
  • Verify amount on screen matches bill

8. OTP Safety and Payment Security

OTP (One Time Password) = short code sent by bank/app for one transaction or login.

Golden rules (CCC important)

Rule Why
Never share OTP with anyone Bank never asks OTP on phone call
Do not share UPI PIN PIN = full account access
Read SMS carefully Check amount and payee name
Use official apps only Fake apps steal credentials
Log out after use on shared phone Protect account
Enable app lock / biometric Extra layer

Common frauds (jaal / dhokha)

  • Fake customer care number on Google — calls and asks OTP
  • Screen sharing request during "refund"
  • Wrong QR pasted over real merchant QR
  • Phishing links — bank-verify.fake.com

Report: National Cyber Crime Portal — cybercrime.gov.in; bank helpline immediately.

9. Fund Transfer Methods — IMPS, NEFT, RTGS

System Speed Hours Typical use
IMPS Instant (minutes) 24×7 Urgent small/medium transfer
NEFT Batch — may take hours Scheduled slots (now mostly 24×7) Regular transfers
RTGS Real-time large transfer Banking hours (high value) Big business payments

Details students should know

  • IMPS — Immediate Payment Service — mobile/internet/net banking
  • NEFT — National Electronic Funds Transfer
  • RTGS — Real Time Gross Settlement — usually minimum amount limit (high)
  • All need correct account number and IFSC code

IFSC = Indian Financial System Code — identifies bank branch (11 characters).

10. Cards and Other Digital Tools (Overview)

Tool Use
Debit card Pay from savings account; ATM cash
Credit card Borrow limit from bank — repay bill
RuPay card Indian card network by NPCI
USSD *99# Basic banking on simple mobile without internet
BBPS Bharat Bill Payment System — unified bill pay

Lab Tasks

  1. UPI app: Install BHIM or Google Pay — link bank in training/demo mode; note steps.
  2. UPI ID: Find your UPI ID in app settings; write format name@bankhandle.
  3. QR scan: Scan a sample merchant QR (trainer/demo) — observe name and amount screen before PIN.
  4. Net banking tour: Login to bank demo site — locate balance, mini statement, fund transfer menu.
  5. Transfer types: List when you would choose IMPS vs NEFT from bank help page.
  6. IFSC: Find IFSC of your local branch from bank website or cheque leaf.
  7. OTP rules: Write 5 situations when you must NOT share OTP.
  8. Fraud case study: Read one RBI/NPCI awareness poster on UPI fraud — summarize 3 tips.
  9. AePS: Watch demo video of Aadhaar fingerprint banking — list required items.
  10. Receipt habit: After any demo payment, note transaction ID and screenshot practice.

Summary Checklist

  • I can explain UPI, BHIM, NPCI, and AePS
  • I know difference between wallet and UPI bank transfer
  • I can describe safe QR and UPI PIN practices
  • I never share OTP or PIN with anyone on call or chat
  • I know IMPS, NEFT, RTGS and IFSC purpose
  • I use only official bank/app links for net banking
  • I know where to report digital payment fraud

Quick-Check MCQs (Questions Only)

  1. UPI full form is (a) Unified Payments Interface (b) Universal Payment Internet (c) United Pay India (d) User Personal ID

  2. NPCI develops (a) Indian retail payment systems like UPI (b) computer monitors (c) MS Word (d) printer drivers

  3. BHIM app is based on (a) UPI platform (b) only email (c) floppy disk format (d) BIOS settings

  4. UPI PIN is used to (a) authorize payment from linked bank account (b) unlock computer case (c) format hard disk (d) change monitor resolution

  5. OTP should be (a) kept secret and never shared (b) posted on social media (c) shared with caller claiming to be bank (d) written on ATM wall

  6. AePS uses (a) Aadhaar authentication for banking services (b) only email password (c) floppy disk (d) scanner calibration

  7. IMPS transfer is generally (a) immediate and available 24×7 (b) only by post office (c) without any bank (d) only for foreign currency

  8. IFSC code identifies (a) bank branch in electronic transfer (b) internet speed (c) printer model (d) keyboard layout

  9. Scanning unknown QR code in public may lead to (a) payment fraud or wrong payee (b) free government laptop guarantee (c) automatic antivirus upgrade (d) faster CPU clock

  10. Net banking should be accessed through (a) official bank website or app typed manually (b) random link in unknown SMS (c) public shared password (d) cyber café without logout

chapters.title: Practice MCQs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 08: Digital Financial Services

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index

  2. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only

  3. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always

  4. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator

  5. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart

  6. QR code payment scans? a) Merchant QR to pay b) CPU barcode c) Printer test page d) BIOS screen

  7. OTP in banking means? a) One Time Password b) Open Transfer Protocol c) Online Text Payment d) Only Total Price

  8. Never share OTP with? a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent b) Your own memory c) Bank official phone asking wrong scam d) Secure app entry yourself OK

  9. IMPS is? a) Immediate payment service b) Internet mail print c) Image media file d) Internal memory

  10. NEFT is? a) Electronic fund transfer b) Network email file c) New excel format d) National education test

  11. RTGS for? a) Large value real-time transfer b) Email attachment c) Word mail merge d) PPT animation

  12. Debit card deducts from? a) Your bank account b) Credit loan always wrong for debit c) Email account d) Cloud drive

  13. Credit card spends? a) Borrowed credit line pay later b) Always savings only wrong c) Cash automatic free wrong d) Email storage

  14. UPI PIN is? a) Secret for authorizing UPI pay b) Email password same wrong c) Public share OK wrong d) Printer code

  15. Balance enquiry via UPI app? a) Check linked account balance b) Delete account c) Format phone d) Install virus

  16. Scan & Pay uses? a) Camera scan QR b) Printer scanner document only different context c) Keyboard only d) Mouse only

  17. Cashless economy means? a) Digital payments preferred b) No banks c) No internet d) Only coins

  18. NPCI role in India? a) Payment infrastructure UPI etc b) Word processing c) OS development d) Hardware manufacturing only

  19. Mobile banking app from? a) Your bank official app b) Random link SMS c) Unknown email attachment d) Pop-up ad exe

  20. Phishing for banking? a) Fake sites steal credentials b) Legitimate bank always SMS link wrong caution c) CPU upgrade d) RAM sale

  21. Virtual payment address VPA? a) username@upi handle b) email only always c) printer name d) BIOS

  22. Transaction SMS alert helps? a) Monitor account activity b) Spam you always wrong c) Delete balance d) Format card

  23. Secure banking use? a) Official app/HTTPS site b) Public cafe logged forever wrong caution c) Share PIN helper wrong d) Screenshot PIN wrong

  24. Wallet KYC required for? a) Regulatory limits and security b) Paint color c) Word font d) PPT theme

  25. Contactless card tap uses? a) NFC radio short range b) Internet download c) Email attach d) Word table

  26. UPI limit set by? a) Bank and NPCI rules b) Microsoft Word c) Excel row count d) PPT slide number

  27. Refund to UPI goes? a) Back to source account usually b) To random email c) Deleted d) Printer

  28. Bill pay via app? a) Electricity mobile etc b) Only games c) Only music d) BIOS update

  29. Auto-debit mandate? a) Scheduled automatic payment b) Virus scan c) Word macro always wrong context d) Print duplex

  30. GST invoice digital? a) Electronic tax invoice b) Email spam c) CPU invoice d) RAM receipt only paper

  31. Digital rupee CBDC pilot? a) Central bank digital currency initiative b) Bitcoin same wrong c) Word coin d) Excel token only joke

  32. Secure Wi-Fi for banking? a) Trusted network VPN caution public b) Any free open always safe wrong c) Share password cafe wrong d) Disable lock screen wrong

  33. Check passbook online via? a) Net banking statement b) Random PDF email c) Unknown APK d) Pop-up

  34. UPI 123Pay includes? a) IVR / feature phone UPI b) Only supercomputer c) Only mainframe d) Only typewriter

  35. Pay to mobile number UPI needs? a) Registered on UPI b) Only fax c) Only postal d) Printer IP

  36. Merchant UPI QR contains? a) Payment address info b) Your PIN never c) Password never d) OTP never in QR

  37. Failed transaction check? a) App history and bank statement b) Ignore always wrong c) Share OTP support scam wrong d) Uninstall OS

  38. Link bank to UPI app steps? a) Official app verify OTP b) SMS link unknown c) Email EXE d) Facebook message link

  39. Digital literacy in finance? a) Safe use of digital payment tools b) Avoid all technology wrong c) Share all passwords wrong d) Only cash forever wrong policy

  40. Insurance premium online pay? a) Insurer portal/app b) Unknown WhatsApp link only wrong c) Random APK d) Email EXE always wrong

  41. Loan EMI pay digitally via? a) Bank app UPI net banking b) Word mail merge c) Excel chart only d) PPT slide

  42. Tax payment online portal? a) Income tax e-filing etc b) Random blog c) Pop-up casino d) Unknown Telegram

  43. e-RUPI voucher is? a) Purpose-specific digital voucher b) Email virus c) Word template d) Printer driver

  44. Secure UPI PIN creation? a) Unique not birthday 1234 b) Share with friend c) Write on public wall d) Same as email always

  45. Bank helpline verify from? a) Official website/card back b) SMS caller unknown c) Email link d) Pop-up

  46. Transaction dispute raise via? a) Bank app/customer care b) Delete app only wrong c) Share PIN d) Format phone first wrong

  47. UPI international expansion means? a) Pay abroad select countries merchants b) Free money wrong c) No verification wrong d) Replaces passport wrong

  48. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index

  49. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only

  50. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Digital Financial Services.
  2. List five important terms from Digital Financial Services.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Digital Financial Services.
  4. Why is Digital Financial Services important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Digital Financial Services.
  6. Define key concept in Digital Financial Services.
  7. List five important terms from Digital Financial Services.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Digital Financial Services.
  9. Why is Digital Financial Services important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Digital Financial Services.
  11. Define key concept in Digital Financial Services.
  12. List five important terms from Digital Financial Services.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Digital Financial Services.
  14. Why is Digital Financial Services important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Digital Financial Services.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Digital Financial Services study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Digital Financial Services study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

questions.question: UPI stands for?
questions.explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

questions.question: BHIM app developed by?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

questions.question: Net banking allows?

options.text: Only phone call branch only always

questions.explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: Digital wallet example?
questions.explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

questions.question: AEPS uses?
questions.explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

questions.question: QR code payment scans?
questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.question: OTP in banking means?
questions.explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

questions.question: Never share OTP with?

options.text: Bank official phone asking wrong scam

questions.explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

questions.question: IMPS is?
questions.explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

questions.question: NEFT is?
questions.explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

questions.question: RTGS for?
questions.explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

questions.question: Debit card deducts from?

options.text: Credit loan always wrong for debit

questions.explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

questions.question: Credit card spends?
questions.explanation: A credit card lets you spend on borrowed credit from the bank — you pay the bill later with interest if delayed.

questions.question: UPI PIN is?
questions.explanation: Secret for authorizing UPI pay is the correct answer. Email password same wrong and Public share OK wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Balance enquiry via UPI app?
questions.explanation: Check linked account balance is the correct answer. Delete account and Format phone are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.explanation: Your bank official app is the correct answer. Random link SMS and Unknown email attachment are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 08: Digital Financial Services

MCQ Answer Key

  1. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index Answer: a) Unified Payments Interface Explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

  2. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only Answer: a) NPCI / Government initiative Explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

  3. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always Answer: a) Banking via internet Explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

  4. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator Answer: a) Paytm, PhonePe Explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

  5. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart Answer: a) Aadhaar authentication for payments Explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

  6. QR code payment scans? a) Merchant QR to pay b) CPU barcode c) Printer test page d) BIOS screen Answer: a) Merchant QR to pay Explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

  7. OTP in banking means? a) One Time Password b) Open Transfer Protocol c) Online Text Payment d) Only Total Price Answer: a) One Time Password Explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

  8. Never share OTP with? a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent b) Your own memory c) Bank official phone asking wrong scam d) Secure app entry yourself OK Answer: a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent Explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  9. IMPS is? a) Immediate payment service b) Internet mail print c) Image media file d) Internal memory Answer: a) Immediate payment service Explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

  10. NEFT is? a) Electronic fund transfer b) Network email file c) New excel format d) National education test Answer: a) Electronic fund transfer Explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

  11. RTGS for? a) Large value real-time transfer b) Email attachment c) Word mail merge d) PPT animation Answer: a) Large value real-time transfer Explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

  12. Debit card deducts from? a) Your bank account b) Credit loan always wrong for debit c) Email account d) Cloud drive Answer: a) Your bank account Explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

  13. Credit card spends? a) Borrowed credit line pay later b) Always savings only wrong c) Cash automatic free wrong d) Email storage Answer: a) Borrowed credit line pay later Explanation: A credit card lets you spend on borrowed credit from the bank — you pay the bill later with interest if delayed.

  14. UPI PIN is? a) Secret for authorizing UPI pay b) Email password same wrong c) Public share OK wrong d) Printer code Answer: a) Secret for authorizing UPI pay Explanation: Secret for authorizing UPI pay is the correct answer. Email password same wrong and Public share OK wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. Balance enquiry via UPI app? a) Check linked account balance b) Delete account c) Format phone d) Install virus Answer: a) Check linked account balance Explanation: Check linked account balance is the correct answer. Delete account and Format phone are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Scan & Pay uses? a) Camera scan QR b) Printer scanner document only different context c) Keyboard only d) Mouse only Answer: a) Camera scan QR Explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

  17. Cashless economy means? a) Digital payments preferred b) No banks c) No internet d) Only coins Answer: a) Digital payments preferred Explanation: Digital payments preferred — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  18. NPCI role in India? a) Payment infrastructure UPI etc b) Word processing c) OS development d) Hardware manufacturing only Answer: a) Payment infrastructure UPI etc Explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

  19. Mobile banking app from? a) Your bank official app b) Random link SMS c) Unknown email attachment d) Pop-up ad exe Answer: a) Your bank official app Explanation: Your bank official app is the correct answer. Random link SMS and Unknown email attachment are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. Phishing for banking? a) Fake sites steal credentials b) Legitimate bank always SMS link wrong caution c) CPU upgrade d) RAM sale Answer: a) Fake sites steal credentials Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  21. Virtual payment address VPA? a) username@upi handle b) email only always c) printer name d) BIOS Answer: a) username@upi handle Explanation: username@upi handle is the correct answer. email only always and printer name are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  22. Transaction SMS alert helps? a) Monitor account activity b) Spam you always wrong c) Delete balance d) Format card Answer: a) Monitor account activity Explanation: Monitor account activity is the correct answer. Spam you always wrong and Delete balance are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  23. Secure banking use? a) Official app/HTTPS site b) Public cafe logged forever wrong caution c) Share PIN helper wrong d) Screenshot PIN wrong Answer: a) Official app/HTTPS site Explanation: Official app/HTTPS site — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  24. Wallet KYC required for? a) Regulatory limits and security b) Paint color c) Word font d) PPT theme Answer: a) Regulatory limits and security Explanation: Regulatory limits and security — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  25. Contactless card tap uses? a) NFC radio short range b) Internet download c) Email attach d) Word table Answer: a) NFC radio short range Explanation: NFC radio short range is the correct answer. Internet download and Email attach are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. UPI limit set by? a) Bank and NPCI rules b) Microsoft Word c) Excel row count d) PPT slide number Answer: a) Bank and NPCI rules Explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

  27. Refund to UPI goes? a) Back to source account usually b) To random email c) Deleted d) Printer Answer: a) Back to source account usually Explanation: Back to source account usually is the correct answer. To random email and Deleted are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. Bill pay via app? a) Electricity mobile etc b) Only games c) Only music d) BIOS update Answer: a) Electricity mobile etc Explanation: Electricity mobile etc is the correct answer. Only games and Only music are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Auto-debit mandate? a) Scheduled automatic payment b) Virus scan c) Word macro always wrong context d) Print duplex Answer: a) Scheduled automatic payment Explanation: Scheduled automatic payment is the correct answer. Virus scan and Word macro always wrong context are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. GST invoice digital? a) Electronic tax invoice b) Email spam c) CPU invoice d) RAM receipt only paper Answer: a) Electronic tax invoice Explanation: Electronic tax invoice is the correct answer. Email spam and CPU invoice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  31. Digital rupee CBDC pilot? a) Central bank digital currency initiative b) Bitcoin same wrong c) Word coin d) Excel token only joke Answer: a) Central bank digital currency initiative Explanation: Central bank digital currency initiative is the correct answer. Bitcoin same wrong and Word coin are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Secure Wi-Fi for banking? a) Trusted network VPN caution public b) Any free open always safe wrong c) Share password cafe wrong d) Disable lock screen wrong Answer: a) Trusted network VPN caution public Explanation: Trusted network VPN caution public is the correct answer. Any free open always safe wrong and Share password cafe wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Check passbook online via? a) Net banking statement b) Random PDF email c) Unknown APK d) Pop-up Answer: a) Net banking statement Explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

  34. UPI 123Pay includes? a) IVR / feature phone UPI b) Only supercomputer c) Only mainframe d) Only typewriter Answer: a) IVR / feature phone UPI Explanation: IVR / feature phone UPI is the correct answer. Only supercomputer and Only mainframe are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  36. Merchant UPI QR contains? a) Payment address info b) Your PIN never c) Password never d) OTP never in QR Answer: a) Payment address info Explanation: Payment address info is the correct answer. Your PIN never and Password never are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

Unknown WhatsApp link only wrong and Random APK are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Loan EMI pay digitally via? a) Bank app UPI net banking b) Word mail merge c) Excel chart only d) PPT slide Answer: a) Bank app UPI net banking Explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

  42. Tax payment online portal? a) Income tax e-filing etc b) Random blog c) Pop-up casino d) Unknown Telegram Answer: a) Income tax e-filing etc Explanation: Income tax e-filing etc is the correct answer. Random blog and Pop-up casino are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  43. e-RUPI voucher is? a) Purpose-specific digital voucher b) Email virus c) Word template d) Printer driver Answer: a) Purpose-specific digital voucher Explanation: Purpose-specific digital voucher is the correct answer. Email virus and Word template are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  44. Secure UPI PIN creation? a) Unique not birthday 1234 b) Share with friend c) Write on public wall d) Same as email always Answer: a) Unique not birthday 1234 Explanation: Unique not birthday 1234 — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  45. Bank helpline verify from? a) Official website/card back b) SMS caller unknown c) Email link d) Pop-up Answer: a) Official website/card back Explanation: Official website/card back — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  46. Transaction dispute raise via? a) Bank app/customer care b) Delete app only wrong c) Share PIN d) Format phone first wrong Answer: a) Bank app/customer care Explanation: Bank app/customer care — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  47. UPI international expansion means? a) Pay abroad select countries merchants b) Free money wrong c) No verification wrong d) Replaces passport wrong Answer: a) Pay abroad select countries merchants Explanation: Pay abroad select countries merchants is the correct answer. Free money wrong and No verification wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  48. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index Answer: a) Unified Payments Interface Explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

  49. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only Answer: a) NPCI / Government initiative Explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

  50. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always Answer: a) Banking via internet Explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: UPI stands for?
questions.explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

questions.question: BHIM app developed by?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

questions.question: Net banking allows?

options.text: Only phone call branch only always

questions.explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: Digital wallet example?
questions.explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

questions.question: AEPS uses?
questions.explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

questions.question: QR code payment scans?
questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.question: OTP in banking means?
questions.explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

options.text: Bank official phone asking wrong scam

questions.explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

questions.explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

questions.question: NEFT is?
questions.explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

questions.question: RTGS for?
questions.explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

questions.question: Debit card deducts from?

options.text: Credit loan always wrong for debit

questions.explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

questions.question: Credit card spends?
questions.explanation: A credit card lets you spend on borrowed credit from the bank — you pay the bill later with interest if delayed.
