chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Subject 9: e-Governance Services (6 Hours)

Learning Objectives

  • Explain e-governance and Digital India mission
  • Use DigiLocker and UMANG for government services
  • Understand online application process for PAN, passport, voter ID
  • Verify official government websites (.gov.in, .nic.in)
  • Fill online forms safely and save acknowledgements
  • Know benefits of digital services for citizens (nagrik seva)

1. What is e-Governance?

e-Governance (electronic governance) = government services delivered through internet and digital platforms — fast, transparent, less paperwork (kam kagaz-kachhra).

Traditional vs e-Governance

Traditional e-Governance
Long queues at office Apply from home/mobile
Paper files Digital records
Repeated visits Track status online
Middlemen risk Direct citizen–government link

Benefits for citizens

  • Transparency — status tracking, less corruption opportunity
  • Speed — faster processing when system works well
  • Accessibility — rural areas via CSC, mobile apps
  • Cost saving — travel and time (samay aur paise bachat)

2. Digital India Mission

Digital India = flagship programme to transform India into digitally empowered society.

Nine pillars (awareness level)

  1. Broadband Highways
  2. Universal Access to Mobile Connectivity
  3. Public Internet Access Programme
  4. e-Governance — Reforming Government through Technology
  5. e-Kranti — Electronic delivery of services
  6. Information for All
  7. Electronics Manufacturing
  8. IT for Jobs
  9. Early Harvest Programmes

Key initiatives students should know

Initiative Purpose
Digital India Overall digital empowerment
DigiLocker Store official documents digitally
UMANG One app for many government services
MyGov Citizen participation platform
CSC (Common Service Centre) Village-level digital seva kendra
UPI / DBT Direct benefit to bank accounts
Aadhaar Unique identity for many services

3. DigiLocker

DigiLocker = secure cloud locker from Government of India to store and share verified documents.

Website: https://www.digilocker.gov.in

Features

  • Link with Aadhaar and mobile OTP
  • Pull certificates from issuing boards (CBSE, etc.) if integrated
  • Upload PDF documents (size limits apply)
  • eSign documents digitally
  • Share document link to employer/college with consent

Documents commonly stored

  • Marksheets, certificates
  • Driving licence (if issued digitally)
  • Vehicle registration (where available)
  • PAN verification record

Benefits

  • No need to carry original everywhere
  • Reduces fake document use when verified from issuer
  • Available 24×7 on mobile app and web

4. UMANG App

UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) = single mobile app for multiple government department services.

Features

  • One login for many services
  • Available in Indian languages
  • Bill pay, EPFO, PAN, passport enquiry, CBSE results (service list changes — check app)
  • Push notifications for application status

Exam tip: UMANG is integrated platform — not replacing each department database but providing unified access.

5. Verifying Official Government Websites

Fake websites steal data and fees. Always verify before pay or login.

Trusted domain patterns (India)

Domain Usually indicates
.gov.in Government of India / state government
.nic.in NIC-hosted government sites
Official department URL Listed on India.gov.in or ministry site

Verification checklist

  1. HTTPS padlock in browser
  2. Domain spelling — gov.in not gov.com or g0v.in
  3. Cross-check link from official pamphlet or India.gov.in
  4. Never pay on site reached only from random WhatsApp forward
  5. Look for copyright / ministry name and contact details

Examples of official portals (awareness)

  • Income Tax — incometax.gov.in
  • Passport — portal2.passportindia.gov.in
  • Voter — voters.eci.gov.in
  • MCA / company — mca.gov.in
  • DigiLocker — digilocker.gov.in

6. PAN Card — Online Services

PAN (Permanent Account Number) = tax identity issued by Income Tax Department.

Online services (through NSDL or UTIITSL portals linked from incometax.gov.in)

  • Apply for new PAN
  • Correction / reprint
  • Track application status
  • Link Aadhaar with PAN (mandatory for many cases)

Safe application steps

  1. Open site only from incometax.gov.in links
  2. Keep Aadhaar, photo, signature scan ready
  3. Pay fee only on official payment gateway
  4. Save acknowledgement number and receipt PDF
  5. Track status using acknowledgement

7. Passport — Online Application

Passport Seva — online appointment and form system under Ministry of External Affairs.

Process overview

  1. Register on official Passport Seva portal
  2. Fill online application form — choose fresh / reissue
  3. Pay fee online
  4. Book Police Station / PSK appointment
  5. Visit centre with original documents
  6. Track application status online

Documents (typical — verify latest rules)

  • Aadhaar, birth proof, address proof, photos
  • Old passport if reissue

Important: Only use passportindia.gov.in official links — agents may charge extra unnecessarily.

8. Voter ID — Online Services

Election Commission of India (ECI) provides online voter services.

Portal: voters.eci.gov.in (verify current URL on ECI site)

Services include

  • New voter registration (Form 6)
  • Correction of details (Form 8)
  • Migration / address change
  • Download e-EPIC (digital voter card where available)
  • Search name in electoral roll

Steps awareness

  • Fill Form online with mobile OTP
  • Upload supporting documents if required
  • Note reference number
  • Track application status

Voting is both right and duty (matadhikar aur kartavya) — keep voter details updated.

9. Other Common e-Governance Services

Service Portal / App Use
Aadhaar update uidai.gov.in Address/mobile update, download
Driving licence Parivahan / state transport Apply, renewal
Birth/death certificate State municipal portals Civil registration
PM schemes respective ministry sites Scholarship, pension enquiry
GST (business) gst.gov.in Business taxpayers
FASTag / NHAI nhai.gov.in Toll payments

CSC — Common Service Centre

Village entrepreneur runs CSC with computer — helps citizens who cannot use internet alone. Part of Digital India last-mile delivery.

10. Online Form Best Practices

Before filling

  • Read instructions PDF completely
  • Keep scanned documents correct size (KB/MB limit)
  • Stable internet and power backup

While filling

  • Match spelling with Aadhaar and certificates
  • Save draft if option available
  • Use valid email and mobile — OTP will come there

After submit

  • Save acknowledgement / application ID screenshot
  • Print or PDF confirmation page
  • Note helpline number from official site only

Lab Tasks

  1. Digital India: Visit digitalindia.gov.in — list 3 objectives of the mission.
  2. DigiLocker: Create account (or demo walkthrough) — explore issued documents and upload sections.
  3. UMANG: Install UMANG — browse 5 available services in your state.
  4. Domain check: Compare real .gov.in site vs a fake-looking URL — write 4 differences.
  5. PAN awareness: Open incometax.gov.in — locate PAN services link (do not submit fake data).
  6. Passport: Explore Passport Seva site — list documents needed for fresh passport (from official FAQ).
  7. Voter: Open ECI voter portal — find "search in electoral roll" or registration form name.
  8. Form practice: Fill a sample HTML practice form with validation (trainer provided).
  9. Acknowledgement: Document what to save after submitting any government online form.
  10. CSC: Find nearest CSC on locator (if available) — note services offered.

Summary Checklist

  • I can explain e-governance and Digital India benefits
  • I know DigiLocker and UMANG main purpose
  • I can verify official .gov.in / .nic.in websites
  • I understand online PAN, passport, voter application flow
  • I save acknowledgement and track application status
  • I avoid fake government websites and phishing
  • I know CSC helps rural digital access

Quick-Check MCQs (Questions Only)

  1. e-Governance means (a) delivering government services through digital means (b) removing all government offices (c) only playing online games (d) banning internet in villages

  2. DigiLocker is used to (a) store and share verified digital documents (b) download movies only (c) format computer (d) repair printer

  3. UMANG app provides (a) unified access to multiple government services (b) only social media chat (c) hardware drivers (d) BIOS update

  4. Official Indian government sites often use domain (a) .gov.in (b) .com.only.fake (c) .freegames (d) .personal.blog

  5. Digital India mission aims to (a) digitally empower citizens and improve governance (b) stop all cash use overnight without technology (c) remove mobile phones (d) close all schools

  6. PAN card is related to (a) Income Tax identity (b) driving licence only (c) voter list only (d) passport immigration only

  7. Passport Seva online portal helps in (a) application and appointment booking (b) CPU upgrade (c) monitor repair (d) keyboard cleaning

  8. Voter registration online in India is managed under (a) Election Commission of India (b) private shopping mall (c) game company (d) local cable operator only

  9. Before paying on a government website you should (a) verify official URL and HTTPS (b) trust any WhatsApp link (c) share OTP with helper (d) ignore spelling of domain

  10. CSC centres in villages mainly provide (a) assisted digital government and business services (b) only coffee shop (c) computer hardware manufacturing (d) antivirus CD sales only

chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 09: E-Governance Services

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips

  2. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor

  3. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only

  4. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme

  5. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus

  6. Online PAN apply via? a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals b) Random WhatsApp c) Unknown email EXE d) Pop-up ad

  7. Passport application online? a) Passport Seva portal b) Facebook form c) Instagram DM d) Random APK

  8. Voter ID services online? a) NVSP portal b) Unknown blog c) Pirate site d) Spam email link

  9. Income tax e-filing? a) Official incometax.gov.in b) Random .com similar phishing caution c) Word doc macro d) Excel only offline always wrong

  10. GST registration online? a) gst.gov.in b) Unknown mirror site c) Telegram bot only wrong d) Email attachment exe

  11. EPFO member portal for? a) Provident fund services b) Games c) Music streaming d) PPT templates

  12. Ayushman Bharat digital mission? a) Health records ecosystem b) Word spell check c) Excel pivot only d) Printer driver

  13. Digital India initiative aims? a) Digital empowerment b) Remove all computers wrong c) Ban internet wrong d) Only imports hardware wrong

  14. MyGov platform for? a) Citizen engagement b) Private banking only c) Social games only d) Antivirus sales only

  15. eDistrict services include? a) Certificates licenses local b) Only international flights c) Only gaming d) BIOS update

  16. Birth certificate online some states? a) State service portal b) Random PDF email c) Unknown APK d) Pop-up

  17. Caste/income certificate apply? a) State e-District/Seva portal b) WhatsApp stranger c) Email EXE d) Fake FB page

  18. Driving license parivahan? a) Parivahan Sewa portal b) Unknown blog APK c) Instagram only wrong d) Word template only

  19. Railway ticket IRCTC? a) Official irctc.co.in b) Similar phishing domain caution c) Unknown app d) SMS link only wrong

  20. PM-KISAN infom portal? a) Farmer benefit scheme info b) Game cheat c) Movie torrent d) Printer ink

  21. Open Government Data? a) data.gov.in datasets b) Private password list wrong c) Pirate movies d) Spam

  22. Single Sign On some portals? a) One login multiple services b) Share password publicly wrong c) No authentication wrong d) BIOS password

  23. OTP on gov portal login? a) Verification security b) Share with agent scam wrong c) Post on Facebook wrong d) Email everyone wrong

  24. Digital signature certificate? a) Legal e-sign identity b) Word bold italic c) Excel chart d) PPT animation

  25. eSanad for? a) Apostille attestation online b) Games c) Music d) Printer test

  26. National Scholarship Portal? a) Student scholarships apply b) Random WhatsApp fee wrong c) Email EXE scholarship wrong d) Pop-up lottery

  27. CoWIN was for? a) Vaccination registration info b) Word merge c) Excel only d) PPT only

  28. ePathshala provides? a) NCERT digital books b) Pirate movies c) Bank loans only d) Printer drivers

  29. Bharat BillPay system? a) Unified bill payments b) Email virus name c) Word feature d) CPU feature

  30. GeM Government e Marketplace? a) Gov procurement platform b) Personal shopping Amazon same wrong c) Game store only d) Social media

  31. eOffice paperless? a) Digital file workflow gov offices b) Email delete all wrong c) No records wrong d) Printer only office wrong

  32. Cyber crime report portal? a) cybercrime.gov.in b) Random blog c) Unknown APK d) Pop-up

  33. Verify gov website check? a) .gov.in domain HTTPS b) Any .com OK wrong c) HTTP enough wrong sensitive d) Email link only wrong

  34. Aadhaar eKYC in banking? a) Online identity verify b) Share Aadhaar OTP scam wrong c) Public post Aadhaar wrong d) Word doc only

  35. State service portal examples? a) Odisha / state specific b) Only foreign sites c) Only games d) Printer manual

  36. Online grievance redressal? a) CPGRAMS etc b) Instagram story only wrong c) Delete email wrong d) Share PIN

  37. Digital certificate in DigiLocker from? a) Issuing authority linked b) Random WhatsApp PDF c) Unknown email d) Pop-up

  38. eSign on documents? a) Aadhaar OTP based sign b) Word underline c) Excel bold d) PPT transition

  39. RTI online filing some states? a) State portal b) Unknown fee WhatsApp wrong c) Email EXE d) Fake helpline

  40. Land records online some states? a) Bhulekh etc b) Random APK c) Pirate site d) Spam link

  41. eCourts services? a) Case status online b) Games c) Movies d) Printer queue

  42. National Career Service? a) Job seeker employer portal b) Only social chat c) Only games d) BIOS

  43. SWAYAM MOOCs? a) Free online courses b) Pirate software c) Spam email d) Unknown miner APK

  44. eHospital HMIS? a) Hospital management info systems b) Word only clinic wrong c) Excel only wrong blanket d) PPT only

chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 09: E-Governance Services

MCQ Answer Key

  1. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips Answer: a) Digital certificates documents Explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

  2. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor Answer: a) Government services unified Explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

  3. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only Answer: a) Government services via ICT Explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

  4. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme Answer: a) Identity authentication Explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

  5. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus Answer: a) National portal Explanation: India.gov.in is the official national portal linking to government websites and services.

  6. Online PAN apply via? a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals b) Random WhatsApp c) Unknown email EXE d) Pop-up ad Answer: a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals Explanation: NSDL/UTIITSL portals is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp and Unknown email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7. Passport application online? a) Passport Seva portal b) Facebook form c) Instagram DM d) Random APK Answer: a) Passport Seva portal Explanation: Passport Seva portal is the correct answer. Facebook form and Instagram DM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  8. Voter ID services online? a) NVSP portal b) Unknown blog c) Pirate site d) Spam email link Answer: a) NVSP portal Explanation: NVSP portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Income tax e-filing? a) Official incometax.gov.in b) Random .com similar phishing caution c) Word doc macro d) Excel only offline always wrong Answer: a) Official incometax.gov.in Explanation: Official incometax.gov.in is the correct answer. Random .com similar phishing caution and Word doc macro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  10. GST registration online? a) gst.gov.in b) Unknown mirror site c) Telegram bot only wrong d) Email attachment exe Answer: a) gst.gov.in Explanation: gst.gov.in is the correct answer. Unknown mirror site and Telegram bot only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  11. EPFO member portal for? a) Provident fund services b) Games c) Music streaming d) PPT templates Answer: a) Provident fund services Explanation: Provident fund services is the correct answer. Games and Music streaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  12. Ayushman Bharat digital mission? a) Health records ecosystem b) Word spell check c) Excel pivot only d) Printer driver Answer: a) Health records ecosystem Explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  13. Digital India initiative aims? a) Digital empowerment b) Remove all computers wrong c) Ban internet wrong d) Only imports hardware wrong Answer: a) Digital empowerment Explanation: Digital India aims to make citizens digitally empowered through online services, connectivity, and digital literacy.

  14. MyGov platform for? a) Citizen engagement b) Private banking only c) Social games only d) Antivirus sales only Answer: a) Citizen engagement Explanation: Citizen engagement is the correct answer. Private banking only and Social games only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. eDistrict services include? a) Certificates licenses local b) Only international flights c) Only gaming d) BIOS update Answer: a) Certificates licenses local Explanation: Certificates licenses local is the correct answer. Only international flights and Only gaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Birth certificate online some states? a) State service portal b) Random PDF email c) Unknown APK d) Pop-up Answer: a) State service portal Explanation: State service portal is the correct answer. Random PDF email and Unknown APK are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Caste/income certificate apply? a) State e-District/Seva portal b) WhatsApp stranger c) Email EXE d) Fake FB page Answer: a) State e-District/Seva portal Explanation: State e-District/Seva portal is the correct answer. WhatsApp stranger and Email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  18. Driving license parivahan? a) Parivahan Sewa portal b) Unknown blog APK c) Instagram only wrong d) Word template only Answer: a) Parivahan Sewa portal Explanation: Parivahan Sewa portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog APK and Instagram only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  19. Railway ticket IRCTC? a) Official irctc.co.in b) Similar phishing domain caution c) Unknown app d) SMS link only wrong Answer: a) Official irctc.co.in Explanation: Official irctc.co.in is the correct answer. Similar phishing domain caution and Unknown app are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. PM-KISAN infom portal? a) Farmer benefit scheme info b) Game cheat c) Movie torrent d) Printer ink Answer: a) Farmer benefit scheme info Explanation: Farmer benefit scheme info is the correct answer. Game cheat and Movie torrent are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  21. Open Government Data? a) data.gov.in datasets b) Private password list wrong c) Pirate movies d) Spam Answer: a) data.gov.in datasets Explanation: data.gov.in datasets is the correct answer. Private password list wrong and Pirate movies are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  22. Single Sign On some portals? a) One login multiple services b) Share password publicly wrong c) No authentication wrong d) BIOS password Answer: a) One login multiple services Explanation: One login multiple services is the correct answer. Share password publicly wrong and No authentication wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  23. OTP on gov portal login? a) Verification security b) Share with agent scam wrong c) Post on Facebook wrong d) Email everyone wrong Answer: a) Verification security Explanation: Verification security is the correct answer. Share with agent scam wrong and Post on Facebook wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  24. Digital signature certificate? a) Legal e-sign identity b) Word bold italic c) Excel chart d) PPT animation Answer: a) Legal e-sign identity Explanation: Legal e-sign identity is the correct answer. Word bold italic and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  25. eSanad for? a) Apostille attestation online b) Games c) Music d) Printer test Answer: a) Apostille attestation online Explanation: Apostille attestation online is the correct answer. Games and Music are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. National Scholarship Portal? a) Student scholarships apply b) Random WhatsApp fee wrong c) Email EXE scholarship wrong d) Pop-up lottery Answer: a) Student scholarships apply Explanation: Student scholarships apply is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp fee wrong and Email EXE scholarship wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  27. CoWIN was for? a) Vaccination registration info b) Word merge c) Excel only d) PPT only Answer: a) Vaccination registration info Explanation: Vaccination registration info is the correct answer. Word merge and Excel only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. ePathshala provides? a) NCERT digital books b) Pirate movies c) Bank loans only d) Printer drivers Answer: a) NCERT digital books Explanation: NCERT digital books is the correct answer. Pirate movies and Bank loans only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Bharat BillPay system? a) Unified bill payments b) Email virus name c) Word feature d) CPU feature Answer: a) Unified bill payments Explanation: Unified bill payments is the correct answer. Email virus name and Word feature are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. GeM Government e Marketplace? a) Gov procurement platform b) Personal shopping Amazon same wrong c) Game store only d) Social media Answer: a) Gov procurement platform Explanation: Gov procurement platform is the correct answer. Personal shopping Amazon same wrong and Game store only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  31. eOffice paperless? a) Digital file workflow gov offices b) Email delete all wrong c) No records wrong d) Printer only office wrong Answer: a) Digital file workflow gov offices Explanation: Digital file workflow gov offices is the correct answer. Email delete all wrong and No records wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Cyber crime report portal? a) cybercrime.gov.in b) Random blog c) Unknown APK d) Pop-up Answer: a) cybercrime.gov.in Explanation: cybercrime.gov.in is the correct answer. Random blog and Unknown APK are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Verify gov website check? a) .gov.in domain HTTPS b) Any .com OK wrong c) HTTP enough wrong sensitive d) Email link only wrong Answer: a) .gov.in domain HTTPS Explanation: Official Indian government sites use .gov.in — always verify the domain and HTTPS before entering personal data.

  34. Aadhaar eKYC in banking? a) Online identity verify b) Share Aadhaar OTP scam wrong c) Public post Aadhaar wrong d) Word doc only Answer: a) Online identity verify Explanation: Online identity verify is the correct answer. Share Aadhaar OTP scam wrong and Public post Aadhaar wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  35. State service portal examples? a) Odisha / state specific b) Only foreign sites c) Only games d) Printer manual Answer: a) Odisha / state specific Explanation: Odisha / state specific is the correct answer. Only foreign sites and Only games are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  36. Online grievance redressal? a) CPGRAMS etc b) Instagram story only wrong c) Delete email wrong d) Share PIN Answer: a) CPGRAMS etc Explanation: CPGRAMS etc is the correct answer. Instagram story only wrong and Delete email wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  37. Digital certificate in DigiLocker from? a) Issuing authority linked b) Random WhatsApp PDF c) Unknown email d) Pop-up Answer: a) Issuing authority linked Explanation: Issuing authority linked is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp PDF and Unknown email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. eSign on documents? a) Aadhaar OTP based sign b) Word underline c) Excel bold d) PPT transition Answer: a) Aadhaar OTP based sign Explanation: Aadhaar OTP based sign is the correct answer. Word underline and Excel bold are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  39. RTI online filing some states? a) State portal b) Unknown fee WhatsApp wrong c) Email EXE d) Fake helpline Answer: a) State portal Explanation: State portal is the correct answer. Unknown fee WhatsApp wrong and Email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  40. Land records online some states? a) Bhulekh etc b) Random APK c) Pirate site d) Spam link Answer: a) Bhulekh etc Explanation: Bhulekh etc is the correct answer. Random APK and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. eCourts services? a) Case status online b) Games c) Movies d) Printer queue Answer: a) Case status online Explanation: Case status online is the correct answer. Games and Movies are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  42.   43. SWAYAM MOOCs? a) Free online courses b) Pirate software c) Spam email d) Unknown miner APK Answer: a) Free online courses Explanation: Free online courses is the correct answer. Pirate software and Spam email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  45.   47. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips Answer: a) Digital certificates documents Explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

  48. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor Answer: a) Government services unified Explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Subject 10: Cyber Security (6 Hours)

Learning Objectives

  • Define virus, malware, phishing, ransomware, and spyware
  • Create and manage strong passwords
  • Understand Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
  • Explain firewall, antivirus, and safe browsing
  • Recognize social engineering and online fraud
  • Know how to report cybercrime at cybercrime.gov.in
  • Build daily habits for personal and device security

1. What is Cyber Security?

Cyber security = protecting computers, networks, data, and accounts from digital attacks (cyber hamle se suraksha).

Why it matters for CCC students

  • Daily use of Gmail, UPI, social media
  • Exams and jobs online
  • Personal photos and documents on phone
  • One mistake can cause money loss or identity theft (pehchaan chori)

CIA triad (basic concept)

Principle Meaning
Confidentiality Only authorized person sees data
Integrity Data is not changed wrongly
Availability System works when needed

2. Malware — Malicious Software

Malware = any software designed to harm or steal.

Common types

Type What it does How it spreads
Virus Attaches to file/program; spreads when opened USB, email attachment, infected download
Worm Spreads alone over network Weak passwords, unpatched systems
Trojan Looks like useful software; hidden harm Fake game, cracked app
Spyware Secretly watches activity, keystrokes Bundled with free software
Ransomware Locks files; demands money to unlock Phishing links, bad attachments
Adware Unwanted ads; may track browsing Free toolbars, shady installers

Signs of infection

  • PC very slow suddenly
  • Unknown programs start automatically
  • Files missing or extension changed (.locked)
  • Antivirus disabled without your action
  • Unexpected pop-ups and browser homepage change

3. Phishing and Social Engineering

Phishing = fake message or website pretending to be bank, Gmail, or government to steal password/OTP.

Phishing channels

  • Email — "Your account will close, click here"
  • SMS / WhatsApp — fake KYC, lottery win, parcel link
  • Phone call — "I am from bank, tell OTP"
  • Fake websites — copy of SBI/Gmail login page

Social engineering

Tricking human mind (manav ko dhokha) instead of breaking code:

  • Urgency — "Act in 10 minutes"
  • Fear — "IT raid, pay now"
  • Greed — "You won ₹50 lakh"
  • Authority — "I am police / RBI officer"

How to avoid

  • Never share OTP, UPI PIN, password, CVV
  • Type bank URL yourself
  • Check sender email address carefully
  • Call official helpline from bank card — not from SMS number

4. Ransomware Awareness

Ransomware encrypts (locks) your files and criminals ask ransom (firauti) in crypto or UPI.

Protection

  • Backup important files to external drive or cloud regularly
  • Do not open unknown attachments — especially .zip, .exe
  • Keep Windows and apps updated
  • Use standard user account — not always Administrator

If attacked

  • Do not pay immediately — payment does not guarantee unlock
  • Disconnect from network
  • Report to police / cybercrime.gov.in
  • Restore from backup if available

5. Strong Passwords

Password = first lock on your digital life.

Weak passwords (never use)

  • 123456, password, your name, birth date, qwerty
  • Same password on Gmail and bank

Strong password rules

  • 8+ characters (12+ better)
  • Mix uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols
  • Unique for each important account
  • Not guessable from Facebook info

Example technique — passphrase

Mera@CCC2025$Safe! — long phrase with symbols (do not use this exact one publicly)

Password managers (awareness)

Apps like built-in browser manager or Bitwarden store complex passwords securely.

If password leaked

Change immediately; enable 2FA; check bank transactions.

6. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)

2FA / Two-Step Verification = two proofs needed to login:

  1. Something you know — password
  2. Something you have — OTP on phone, authenticator app, or security key

Where to enable (must for students)

  • Gmail / Google account
  • Banking and UPI apps
  • Social media (Instagram, Facebook)
  • DigiLocker, income tax portal

OTP types

  • SMS OTP (common; SIM swap risk — keep SIM safe)
  • Email OTP
  • Authenticator app (Google Authenticator) — works without SMS

Exam point: 2FA improves security even if password is stolen.

7. Firewall

Firewall = barrier controlling incoming and outgoing network traffic.

Types

  • Windows Firewall — built into Windows; block unauthorized access
  • Hardware firewall — in router
  • Corporate firewall — college/office network

What it does

  • Blocks suspicious connection requests
  • Allows safe browsing when configured properly
  • Not a substitute for antivirus — use both

8. Antivirus and Anti-malware

Antivirus = software detecting and removing malware.

Popular examples

Windows Security (Defender), Avast, Kaspersky, Bitdefender, Norton

Good practices

  • Keep virus definitions updated
  • Run full scan weekly on home PC
  • Scan USB before opening files
  • Download only from official sites

Limitations

  • Cannot stop you from giving OTP to scammer
  • May not catch newest threats — behaviour matters

9. Safe Browsing Habits

Habit Benefit
Use HTTPS on login/payment Encrypted connection
Log out on shared PC Others cannot open your account
Update browser and OS Security patches
Avoid public Wi-Fi for banking Reduce interception risk
Check app permissions on phone Stop unnecessary access to contacts/camera
Review privacy settings on social media Limit public data
Think before click Stop phishing

Physical security

  • Lock phone with PIN/fingerprint
  • Do not leave laptop unlocked in lab
  • Shred or secure papers with account numbers

10. Reporting Cybercrime in India

National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal: https://www.cybercrime.gov.in

What you can report

  • Online financial fraud
  • Social media harassment
  • Hacking, data theft
  • Child-related offences online (priority)
  • Ransomware, sextortion

How to report (overview)

  1. Visit cybercrime.gov.in
  2. File complaint — Cyber Crime Reporting section
  3. Provide evidence — screenshots, URLs, numbers, transaction IDs
  4. Note complaint reference number
  5. Also inform bank if money lost — block transaction quickly

Helpline

1930 — Cyber fraud financial helpline (many states — verify local availability)

Evidence to preserve

  • SMS, email headers
  • UPI transaction screenshot
  • Caller number and time
  • WhatsApp chat export if needed

11. CCC Exam Quick Threat Summary

Threat One-line definition
Virus Malware that spreads via files
Phishing Fake messages to steal secrets
Spam Unwanted bulk messages
Spyware Hidden monitoring software
Ransomware Locks data for money
Trojan Disguised harmful program
Firewall Traffic filter for network
2FA Two-step login security
Identity theft Using your identity fraudulently

Lab Tasks

  1. Password audit: Check if you reuse passwords — create 3 strong unique passphrases on paper (private, not shared).
  2. 2FA: Enable 2-Step Verification on Google account — note backup codes storage.
  3. Windows Security: Open Windows Security — run quick scan; check firewall is ON.
  4. Phishing examples: Trainer shows 3 sample fake SMS/emails — identify red flags.
  5. URL check: Compare real vs fake login URL — list 5 differences.
  6. Browser: Review privacy settings — cookies, safe browsing, password save option.
  7. USB caution: Discuss why autorun USB from unknown person is risky.
  8. Backup: Copy important folder to cloud or pen drive — document backup date.
  9. Report portal: Open cybercrime.gov.in — explore complaint categories (do not file test fake complaint).
  10. Security pledge: Write personal 10-point cyber safety rules for home and lab.

Summary Checklist

  • I can define virus, malware, phishing, ransomware, spyware
  • I create strong unique passwords and enable 2FA
  • I understand firewall and antivirus roles
  • I follow safe browsing and phone lock habits
  • I never share OTP, PIN, or password with anyone
  • I know how to report at cybercrime.gov.in and helpline 1930
  • I keep backups and Windows/apps updated

Quick-Check MCQs (Questions Only)

  1. Virus is a type of (a) harmful software that can spread (b) hardware printer part (c) network cable (d) keyboard cleaning liquid

  2. Phishing attack mainly tries to (a) trick user into revealing sensitive information (b) increase internet speed (c) format monitor (d) improve typing accuracy

  3. Ransomware typically (a) locks files and demands payment (b) gives free antivirus (c) increases RAM automatically (d) teaches coding

  4. Strong password should (a) mix letters, numbers, symbols and be 8+ characters (b) be your name only (c) be 123456 for easy recall (d) same for all websites

  5. 2FA means (a) two different steps to verify login (b) two printers connected (c) two monitors only (d) second email account for spam

  6. Firewall is used to (a) monitor and control network traffic (b) clean monitor screen (c) charge mobile battery (d) print documents faster

  7. Antivirus software helps to (a) detect and remove malware (b) create viruses (c) slow CPU intentionally (d) replace internet connection

  8. OTP shared with unknown caller may lead to (a) financial fraud and account takeover (b) free government laptop (c) automatic scholarship (d) faster broadband

  9. Cybercrime in India can be reported at (a) cybercrime.gov.in portal (b) random WhatsApp group (c) unknown .com lottery site (d) pirated software forum

  10. Regular data backup protects against (a) ransomware and accidental file loss (b) slow mouse only (c) keyboard layout change (d) monitor brightness issues

chapters.title: Practice MCQs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 10: Cyber Security

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature

  2. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong

  3. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache

  4. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam

  5. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only

  6. OTP should be? a) Kept secret one-time use b) Shared on Facebook c) Emailed to stranger d) Posted public

  7. Firewall helps? a) Block unauthorized access b) Print documents c) Edit Word d) Make charts

  8. HTTPS for banking? a) Encrypted connection prefer b) Optional never matters wrong c) Slower so avoid wrong d) Replaces password wrong

  9. Ransomware? a) Encrypts data demands payment b) Free backup tool c) Word feature d) Excel sort

  10. Spyware? a) Secretly monitors user b) Official antivirus c) Browser bookmark d) Email signature

  11. Trojan horse? a) Disguised malicious program b) Greek history only no malware wrong c) Hardware GPU d) Printer font

  12. Worm spreads? a) Self across network b) Only USB always wrong only one way c) Never copies wrong d) Word table

  13. Social engineering? a) Manipulate people not code only b) New OS feature c) Excel formula d) PPT theme

  14. Two-factor authentication? a) Password plus second verify b) Two passwords same wrong c) No password wrong d) Printer code

  15. Password manager helps? a) Store strong unique passwords b) Share all passwords wrong c) Post online wrong d) Remove need login wrong

  16. Update OS and apps? a) Patch security holes b) Slow PC purpose only wrong c) Delete files goal wrong d) Remove antivirus wrong

  17. Public Wi-Fi risk? a) Eavesdropping MITM caution b) Always safe wrong c) Faster so secure wrong d) Replaces VPN always wrong

  18. VPN provides? a) Encrypted tunnel privacy b) Free money c) Unlimited bank balance d) Removes need password

  19. Backup protects from? a) Ransomware data loss b) Slow CPU c) Low ink d) Bad grammar

  20. Do not click unknown? a) Email SMS links attachments b) Official typed URL OK cautious c) Verify sender d) Random APK

  21. USB unknown risk? a) May contain malware autorun caution b) Always safe wrong c) Charges phone only never data wrong d) Formats PC automatic wrong

  22. Screen lock PIN? a) Prevents casual access b) Share with all c) Write on monitor d) Disable always wrong

  23. Biometric login? a) Fingerprint face verify b) Password published c) OTP public d) Email all

  24. Cyber bullying? a) Online harassment b) Antivirus scan c) Excel chart d) Mail merge

  25. Report cyber crime India? a) cybercrime.gov.in helpline 1930 b) Ignore always wrong c) Post password help wrong d) Pay stranger wrong

  26. Identity theft? a) Steal personal info impersonate b) Word spell check c) Excel filter d) PPT slide

  27. Keylogger records? a) Keystrokes secretly b) Printer pages c) Monitor brightness d) CPU temp only

  28. Spam email handle? a) Mark spam do not reply b) Reply with bank details wrong c) Forward OTP wrong d) Click all links wrong

  29. Software pirated risk? a) Malware legal issues b) Always safer wrong c) Official update guaranteed wrong d) Free always clean wrong

  30. Browser saved password risk shared PC? a) Others may access b) Fully safe always wrong c) Encrypted always user proof wrong blanket d) BIOS only

  31. Logout shared computer? a) Protect account b) Optional never wrong c) Delete internet wrong d) Format disk wrong

  32. Children online safety? a) Supervision privacy settings b) Share location public always wrong c) Accept all friend requests wrong d) Post school address wrong

  33. Digital footprint permanent? a) Can persist be careful b) Disappears instantly always wrong c) Only paper matters wrong d) BIOS only

  34. Spear phishing? a) Targeted specific victim b) Random only always wrong distinction c) Antivirus name d) Printer test

  35. Whaling targets? a) Senior executives b) Fish animals only joke c) Keyboard whales wrong d) Excel whales wrong

  36. Patch Tuesday associated? a) Microsoft update release pattern b) Linux only wrong c) Word only wrong d) Games only

  37. Zero-day vulnerability? a) Unknown fix not yet b) Always patched day zero wrong c) Word bug grammar only wrong d) Printer paper

  38. Multi-factor not same as? a) Longer password only MFA needs second factor b) Two copies password wrong c) BIOS twice wrong d) Print twice

  39. Hashing passwords stored? a) One-way not plain text good practice b) Plain text best wrong c) Post online wrong d) Email admin wrong

  40. Cyber hygiene means? a) Regular safe digital habits b) Never wash hands joke wrong context c) Delete OS wrong d) Disable firewall wrong

  41. Secure browsing padlock? a) HTTPS indicator not alone enough but sign b) Virus free guarantee wrong c) Government site always wrong d) No phishing possible wrong

  42. App permissions review? a) Limit camera mic contacts b) Allow all always wrong c) Ignore updates wrong d) Install unknown APK wrong

  43. Smishing is? a) SMS phishing b) Email only phishing wrong type c) Word smudge d) Excel error

  44. Vishing is? a) Voice call phishing b) Video only wrong c) VPN name wrong d) Virus name wrong

  45. Cyber awareness month India? a) October observed b) Never observed wrong c) Word month wrong d) Excel month wrong

  46. Insider threat? a) Risk from within organization b) Only external hackers wrong only c) Antivirus insider brand wrong d) Printer jam

  47. Data encryption at rest? a) Stored data scrambled b) Delete data c) Print data d) Email plain always wrong

  48. Least privilege principle? a) Minimum access needed b) Admin for everyone wrong c) Share root password wrong d) Public write all files wrong

  49. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature

  50. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Cyber Security.
  2. List five important terms from Cyber Security.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Cyber Security.
  4. Why is Cyber Security important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Cyber Security.
  6. Define key concept in Cyber Security.
  7. List five important terms from Cyber Security.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Cyber Security.
  9. Why is Cyber Security important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Cyber Security.
  11. Define key concept in Cyber Security.
  12. List five important terms from Cyber Security.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Cyber Security.
  14. Why is Cyber Security important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Cyber Security.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Cyber Security study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Cyber Security study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Malware means?
questions.explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

questions.question: Phishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Antivirus protects from?
questions.explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Strong password example style?
questions.explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

questions.question: OTP should be?
questions.explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Firewall helps?
questions.explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

questions.question: HTTPS for banking?
questions.explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ransomware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Spyware?
questions.explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Trojan horse?

options.text: Greek history only no malware wrong

questions.explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.explanation: Manipulate people not code only is the correct answer. New OS feature and Excel formula are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Two-factor authentication?
questions.explanation: Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds a second step (OTP, app code, fingerprint) after your password for stronger security.

questions.question: Password manager helps?
questions.explanation: Store strong unique passwords is the correct answer. Share all passwords wrong and Post online wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Update OS and apps?

chapters.title: Answer Key (unlock after practice)
chapters.heading: Answer Key (unlock after practice)
chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 10: Cyber Security

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature Answer: a) Malicious software Explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

  2. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong Answer: a) Malicious software general Explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

  3. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache Answer: a) Fraudulent data theft attempt Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  4. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam Answer: a) Malware Explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  5. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only Answer: a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars Explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

  6. OTP should be? a) Kept secret one-time use b) Shared on Facebook c) Emailed to stranger d) Posted public Answer: a) Kept secret one-time use Explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7. Firewall helps? a) Block unauthorized access b) Print documents c) Edit Word d) Make charts Answer: a) Block unauthorized access Explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

  8. HTTPS for banking? a) Encrypted connection prefer b) Optional never matters wrong c) Slower so avoid wrong d) Replaces password wrong Answer: a) Encrypted connection prefer Explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Ransomware? a) Encrypts data demands payment b) Free backup tool c) Word feature d) Excel sort Answer: a) Encrypts data demands payment Explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

  10. Spyware? a) Secretly monitors user b) Official antivirus c) Browser bookmark d) Email signature Answer: a) Secretly monitors user Explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  11. Trojan horse? a) Disguised malicious program b) Greek history only no malware wrong c) Hardware GPU d) Printer font Answer: a) Disguised malicious program Explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  12. Worm spreads? a) Self across network b) Only USB always wrong only one way c) Never copies wrong d) Word table Answer: a) Self across network Explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  13. Social engineering? a) Manipulate people not code only b) New OS feature c) Excel formula d) PPT theme Answer: a) Manipulate people not code only Explanation: Manipulate people not code only is the correct answer. New OS feature and Excel formula are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  14. Two-factor authentication? a) Password plus second verify b) Two passwords same wrong c) No password wrong d) Printer code Answer: a) Password plus second verify Explanation: Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds a second step (OTP, app code, fingerprint) after your password for stronger security.

  15. Password manager helps? a) Store strong unique passwords b) Share all passwords wrong c) Post online wrong d) Remove need login wrong Answer: a) Store strong unique passwords Explanation: Store strong unique passwords is the correct answer. Share all passwords wrong and Post online wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Update OS and apps? a) Patch security holes b) Slow PC purpose only wrong c) Delete files goal wrong d) Remove antivirus wrong Answer: a) Patch security holes Explanation: Patch security holes is the correct answer. Slow PC purpose only wrong and Delete files goal wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Public Wi-Fi risk? a) Eavesdropping MITM caution b) Always safe wrong c) Faster so secure wrong d) Replaces VPN always wrong Answer: a) Eavesdropping MITM caution Explanation: Eavesdropping MITM caution is the correct answer. Always safe wrong and Faster so secure wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  18. VPN provides? a) Encrypted tunnel privacy b) Free money c) Unlimited bank balance d) Removes need password Answer: a) Encrypted tunnel privacy Explanation: Encrypted tunnel privacy is the correct answer. Free money and Unlimited bank balance are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  19. Backup protects from? a) Ransomware data loss b) Slow CPU c) Low ink d) Bad grammar Answer: a) Ransomware data loss Explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

  20. Do not click unknown? a) Email SMS links attachments b) Official typed URL OK cautious c) Verify sender d) Random APK Answer: a) Email SMS links attachments Explanation: Email SMS links attachments is the correct answer. Official typed URL OK cautious and Verify sender are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  21. USB unknown risk? a) May contain malware autorun caution b) Always safe wrong c) Charges phone only never data wrong d) Formats PC automatic wrong Answer: a) May contain malware autorun caution Explanation: May contain malware autorun caution is the correct answer. Always safe wrong and Charges phone only never data wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  22. Screen lock PIN? a) Prevents casual access b) Share with all c) Write on monitor d) Disable always wrong Answer: a) Prevents casual access Explanation: Prevents casual access is the correct answer. Share with all and Write on monitor are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  23. Biometric login? a) Fingerprint face verify b) Password published c) OTP public d) Email all Answer: a) Fingerprint face verify Explanation: Fingerprint face verify is the correct answer. Password published and OTP public are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  24. Cyber bullying? a) Online harassment b) Antivirus scan c) Excel chart d) Mail merge Answer: a) Online harassment Explanation: Online harassment is the correct answer. Antivirus scan and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  25. Report cyber crime India? a) cybercrime.gov.in helpline 1930 b) Ignore always wrong c) Post password help wrong d) Pay stranger wrong Answer: a) cybercrime.gov.in helpline 1930 Explanation: cybercrime.gov.in helpline 1930 is the correct answer. Ignore always wrong and Post password help wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. Identity theft? a) Steal personal info impersonate b) Word spell check c) Excel filter d) PPT slide Answer: a) Steal personal info impersonate Explanation: Steal personal info impersonate is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel filter are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  27. Keylogger records? a) Keystrokes secretly b) Printer pages c) Monitor brightness d) CPU temp only Answer: a) Keystrokes secretly Explanation: Keystrokes secretly is the correct answer. Printer pages and Monitor brightness are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. Spam email handle? a) Mark spam do not reply b) Reply with bank details wrong c) Forward OTP wrong d) Click all links wrong Answer: a) Mark spam do not reply Explanation: Mark spam do not reply is the correct answer. Reply with bank details wrong and Forward OTP wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Software pirated risk? a) Malware legal issues b) Always safer wrong c) Official update guaranteed wrong d) Free always clean wrong Answer: a) Malware legal issues Explanation: Malware legal issues is the correct answer. Always safer wrong and Official update guaranteed wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. Browser saved password risk shared PC? a) Others may access b) Fully safe always wrong c) Encrypted always user proof wrong blanket d) BIOS only Answer: a) Others may access Explanation: Others may access is the correct answer. Fully safe always wrong and Encrypted always user proof wrong blanket are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  31. Logout shared computer? a) Protect account b) Optional never wrong c) Delete internet wrong d) Format disk wrong Answer: a) Protect account Explanation: Protect account is the correct answer. Optional never wrong and Delete internet wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Children online safety? a) Supervision privacy settings b) Share location public always wrong c) Accept all friend requests wrong d) Post school address wrong Answer: a) Supervision privacy settings Explanation: Supervision privacy settings is the correct answer. Share location public always wrong and Accept all friend requests wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Digital footprint permanent? a) Can persist be careful b) Disappears instantly always wrong c) Only paper matters wrong d) BIOS only Answer: a) Can persist be careful Explanation: Can persist be careful is the correct answer. Disappears instantly always wrong and Only paper matters wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  34. Spear phishing? a) Targeted specific victim b) Random only always wrong distinction c) Antivirus name d) Printer test Answer: a) Targeted specific victim Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  35. Whaling targets? a) Senior executives b) Fish animals only joke c) Keyboard whales wrong d) Excel whales wrong Answer: a) Senior executives Explanation: Senior executives is the correct answer. Fish animals only joke and Keyboard whales wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  36. Patch Tuesday associated? a) Microsoft update release pattern b) Linux only wrong c) Word only wrong d) Games only Answer: a) Microsoft update release pattern Explanation: Microsoft update release pattern is the correct answer. Linux only wrong and Word only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  37. Zero-day vulnerability? a) Unknown fix not yet b) Always patched day zero wrong c) Word bug grammar only wrong d) Printer paper Answer: a) Unknown fix not yet Explanation: Unknown fix not yet is the correct answer. Always patched day zero wrong and Word bug grammar only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. Multi-factor not same as? a) Longer password only MFA needs second factor b) Two copies password wrong c) BIOS twice wrong d) Print twice Answer: a) Longer password only MFA needs second factor Explanation: Longer password only MFA needs second factor is the correct answer. Two copies password wrong and BIOS twice wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  39. Hashing passwords stored? a) One-way not plain text good practice b) Plain text best wrong c) Post online wrong d) Email admin wrong Answer: a) One-way not plain text good practice Explanation: One-way not plain text good practice is the correct answer. Plain text best wrong and Post online wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  40. Cyber hygiene means? a) Regular safe digital habits b) Never wash hands joke wrong context c) Delete OS wrong d) Disable firewall wrong Answer: a) Regular safe digital habits Explanation: Regular safe digital habits is the correct answer. Never wash hands joke wrong context and Delete OS wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Secure browsing padlock? a) HTTPS indicator not alone enough but sign b) Virus free guarantee wrong c) Government site always wrong d) No phishing possible wrong Answer: a) HTTPS indicator not alone enough but sign Explanation: HTTPS indicator not alone enough but sign is the correct answer. Virus free guarantee wrong and Government site always wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  42. App permissions review? a) Limit camera mic contacts b) Allow all always wrong c) Ignore updates wrong d) Install unknown APK wrong Answer: a) Limit camera mic contacts Explanation: Limit camera mic contacts is the correct answer. Allow all always wrong and Ignore updates wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  43. Smishing is? a) SMS phishing b) Email only phishing wrong type c) Word smudge d) Excel error Answer: a) SMS phishing Explanation: Phishing tricks users with fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  44. Vishing is? a) Voice call phishing b) Video only wrong c) VPN name wrong d) Virus name wrong Answer: a) Voice call phishing Explanation: Phishing tricks users with fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  45. Cyber awareness month India? a) October observed b) Never observed wrong c) Word month wrong d) Excel month wrong Answer: a) October observed Explanation: October observed is the correct answer. Never observed wrong and Word month wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  46. Insider threat? a) Risk from within organization b) Only external hackers wrong only c) Antivirus insider brand wrong d) Printer jam Answer: a) Risk from within organization Explanation: Risk from within organization is the correct answer. Only external hackers wrong only and Antivirus insider brand wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  47. Data encryption at rest? a) Stored data scrambled b) Delete data c) Print data d) Email plain always wrong Answer: a) Stored data scrambled Explanation: Stored data scrambled is the correct answer. Delete data and Print data are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  48. Least privilege principle? a) Minimum access needed b) Admin for everyone wrong c) Share root password wrong d) Public write all files wrong Answer: a) Minimum access needed Explanation: Minimum access needed is the correct answer. Admin for everyone wrong and Share root password wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  49. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature Answer: a) Malicious software Explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

  50. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong Answer: a) Malicious software general Explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

Short Answer Key

  1. Define key concept in Cyber Security. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  2. List five important terms from Cyber Security. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  3. Name three practical tasks for Cyber Security. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  4. Why is Cyber Security important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  5. Give two exam tips for Cyber Security. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  6. Define key concept in Cyber Security. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  7. List five important terms from Cyber Security. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  8. Name three practical tasks for Cyber Security. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  9. Why is Cyber Security important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  10. Give two exam tips for Cyber Security. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  11. Define key concept in Cyber Security. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  12. List five important terms from Cyber Security. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  13. Name three practical tasks for Cyber Security. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  14. Why is Cyber Security important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  15. Give two exam tips for Cyber Security. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

Practical Checklist Key

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Cyber Security study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  2. Complete lab task 2 from Cyber Security study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  6. Peer review exercise in lab. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  8. Document steps in notebook. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.
