questions.question: Cyber hygiene means?

options.text: Never wash hands joke wrong context

questions.explanation: Regular safe digital habits is the correct answer. Never wash hands joke wrong context and Delete OS wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Secure browsing padlock?

options.text: HTTPS indicator not alone enough but sign

questions.explanation: HTTPS indicator not alone enough but sign is the correct answer. Virus free guarantee wrong and Government site always wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: App permissions review?
questions.explanation: Limit camera mic contacts is the correct answer. Allow all always wrong and Ignore updates wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Smishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing tricks users with fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Vishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing tricks users with fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Cyber awareness month India?
questions.explanation: October observed is the correct answer. Never observed wrong and Word month wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Insider threat?
questions.explanation: Risk from within organization is the correct answer. Only external hackers wrong only and Antivirus insider brand wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Data encryption at rest?
questions.explanation: Stored data scrambled is the correct answer. Delete data and Print data are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Least privilege principle?
questions.explanation: Minimum access needed is the correct answer. Admin for everyone wrong and Share root password wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Malware means?
questions.explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.contentHtml:
Subject 11: Future Skills & Digital Literacy (4 Hours)

Learning Objectives

  • Understand basics of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML)
  • Explain IoT, cloud computing, and their daily-life examples
  • Define digital literacy and its importance for jobs
  • Get introductory awareness of coding and computational thinking
  • Discuss technology ethics, privacy, and responsible AI use
  • Develop mindset for lifelong learning (jeevan-anty siksha)

1. Digital Literacy in the 21st Century

Digital literacy = ability to use digital tools safely and effectively to learn, work, and participate in society (digital duniya mein safal rehna).

Components

Skill area Examples
Basic operations Smartphone, browser, email, UPI
Information literacy Search, verify fake news
Communication Email etiquette, online meetings
Safety Passwords, privacy, cyber awareness
Problem solving Try settings, help menu, restart
Creation Documents, presentations, simple posts

Why CCC connects to future skills

CCC builds foundation — without basics, advanced tools are harder. Certificate shows you can function in digital workplace.

2. Artificial Intelligence (AI) — Basics

AI (Artificial Intelligence) = computer systems that perform tasks needing human-like intelligence — understanding language, recognizing images, making suggestions.

AI in daily life (India examples)

  • Google Maps traffic route suggestion
  • YouTube recommended videos
  • Phone face unlock
  • Chatbots on bank and shopping sites
  • Voice assistants — Google Assistant, Alexa
  • UPI fraud detection patterns

AI vs normal program

Normal program AI system
Fixed step-by-step rules Learns patterns from data
Same output for same input usually May improve with more data
Calculator, MS Word spell simple rule Image recognition, language translation

Types of AI (awareness)

  • Narrow AI — one task (chess, spam filter) — all current practical AI
  • General AI — human-level all tasks — research stage, not common yet

Hindi note: AI = kritrim buddhi (machine showing smart behaviour).

3. Machine Learning (ML) — Introduction

Machine Learning = branch of AI where computer learns from data without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

Simple example

Email spam filter:

  • Trained on thousands of spam and good mails
  • Learns words and patterns
  • Classifies new mail as spam or not

Common ML uses

  • Recommendation (Netflix, Flipkart)
  • Medical image assist (doctor support, not replacement)
  • Crop disease detection apps for farmers
  • Speech-to-text in Indian languages

Data is fuel

More quality data → often better ML model.
Privacy concern: data must be collected with consent and protection.

4. Internet of Things (IoT)

IoT = everyday physical devices connected to internet, sending and receiving data.

Examples

Device Smart behaviour
Smart watch Heart rate, steps to phone
Smart bulb Control brightness from app
CCTV IP camera View home on mobile
Smart AC Schedule temperature
GPS tracker on vehicle Live location
Soil moisture sensor Farm irrigation alert

IoT structure (simple)

Sensor → Internet → App/Cloud → User action

Risks

  • Weak default passwords on cameras
  • Data leakage if company servers hacked
  • Always change default password; update firmware

5. Cloud Computing — Recap for Future Context

Cloud computing = using computing services (storage, software, servers) over internet on rent — pay as you use.

Service models (basic)

Model Meaning Example
SaaS Software as Service Gmail, Google Docs
PaaS Platform for developers Heroku, Google App Engine
IaaS Infrastructure (servers) AWS EC2, Azure VMs

Benefits

  • No heavy server in office
  • Scale up during festival sale for business
  • Access from anywhere

CCC already uses cloud — Drive, webmail. Future jobs may use cloud dashboards and online CRM tools.

6. Introduction to Coding

Coding (programming) = writing instructions in a language computers understand.

Why learn basics even if not developer

  • Automate boring tasks (Excel macros, simple scripts)
  • Understand app limitations and bugs
  • Better career in IT, data, testing, design, government tech posts
  • Logical thinking (logical soch)

Popular languages (awareness)

Language Common use
Python AI, data science, beginners
JavaScript Websites, apps
Java Android, enterprise
C / C++ System, competitive base
HTML/CSS Web page structure and style

Computational thinking

  1. Decomposition — break big problem into small parts
  2. Pattern recognition — find similar problems
  3. Abstraction — hide unnecessary detail
  4. Algorithm — step-by-step solution

Free learning paths

  • Code.org, Khan Academy, SWAYAM MOOC courses
  • YouTube Hindi/English beginner series
  • NIELIT / CSC skill courses

First program tradition: Print Hello World — just displays greeting on screen.

7. Technology Ethics and Responsible Use

Digital ethics = right and wrong use of technology respecting people and law.

Key topics

Topic Issue Responsible approach
Privacy Apps collect location, contacts Read permissions; minimal sharing
Fake news False information spreads fast Verify before forward
AI bias System unfair to some groups Demand fair testing; diverse data
Deepfake Fake video of real person Do not believe everything; check source
Copyright Copying videos, books illegally Use licensed or free content
E-waste Throwing old phones in trash Recycle at authorized centre
Screen addiction Health and study harm Time limits, breaks

AI in exams and jobs

  • Using ChatGPT to learn concepts — good if you verify
  • Copy-paste exam answers — cheating; harms your skill
  • Disclose AI use where institution requires

Indian framework awareness: NITI Aayog National Strategy for AI, Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP) — privacy law context.

8. Lifelong Learning (Continuous Upskilling)

Technology changes fast — today's tool may update next year.

Habits for lifelong learning

  • Learn one new tool each month (shortcut, app feature)
  • Follow official blogs — Google, Microsoft Learn
  • Practice English + Hindi technical terms for exams and jobs
  • Join free webinars — Skill India, NSDC, NIELIT
  • Build portfolio — certificates, small projects, volunteer digital seva

Future job areas (digital)

  • Data entry and office automation
  • Digital marketing assistant
  • Cyber security support
  • IoT technician
  • AI data labeling and testing
  • Government e-governance operator at CSC

Growth mindset (vriddhi soch)

Mistakes in lab are learning — ask teacher, search documentation, try again.

9. Emerging Trends (Short Awareness)

Trend One-line
5G Faster mobile internet for video and IoT
Blockchain Distributed record — used in some certificates and finance
AR/VR Augmented/Virtual reality for training and games
Robotics Machines doing physical tasks in factory and home
Green IT Energy-efficient data centres and e-waste control
BharatGPT / Indian language AI Local language technology growth

CCC exam may ask definition-level questions — not deep programming.

Lab Tasks

  1. AI hunt: List 5 AI features you already use on phone — explain each in one line.
  2. ML example: Discuss how YouTube or Flipkart recommendations might use past behaviour data.
  3. IoT home: Name 3 IoT devices you have seen in shop or home — what data they may send.
  4. Cloud check: Identify 3 SaaS apps you use daily (Gmail, WhatsApp Web, etc.).
  5. Code hello: Open code.org or Python online editor — run Hello World (trainer demo).
  6. Logic puzzle: Write step-by-step algorithm to make tea or login to Gmail — test with classmate.
  7. Fake news: Take one viral message — verify on fact-check site or official source.
  8. Permissions: Review mobile app permissions — disable one unnecessary access.
  9. Learn plan: Write 6-month skill plan — CCC, typing, Excel, one MOOC course.
  10. Ethics debate: Group discussion — "Should students use AI chatbots for homework?" — list pros and cons.

Summary Checklist

  • I can explain AI, ML, IoT, and cloud in simple words with examples
  • I understand digital literacy as broader than just using phone
  • I know basic coding purpose and computational thinking steps
  • I follow ethical and responsible technology use
  • I verify information before sharing online
  • I have a plan for continuous learning after CCC
  • I connect future skills to jobs and Digital India goals

Quick-Check MCQs (Questions Only)

  1. Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to (a) machines performing tasks needing human-like intelligence (b) only printing documents (c) cleaning hardware with water (d) typing speed only

  2. Machine Learning is (a) learning patterns from data by computer (b) reading machine manual only (c) repairing printer drum (d) BIOS password reset

  3. IoT means (a) everyday devices connected to internet sharing data (b) internet without any device (c) only email system (d) offline calculator

  4. Digital literacy includes (a) safe and effective use of digital technology (b) ignoring cyber safety (c) only playing games (d) avoiding all learning apps

  5. Cloud computing provides (a) online services like storage and software on demand (b) only rain water harvesting (c) physical-only floppy disks (d) CRT monitor repair

  6. Python is often used for (a) beginners, AI and data tasks (b) only washing machine repair (c) BIOS chip replacement (d) mouse ball cleaning

  7. Computational thinking includes (a) breaking problem into smaller steps (b) shouting at monitor (c) deleting all files (d) disabling all security

  8. Sharing unverified viral news may cause (a) spread of misinformation (b) automatic job promotion (c) free laptop from government always (d) faster CPU clock speed

  9. Lifelong learning means (a) continuously updating skills through life (b) stopping study after one exam (c) avoiding all new technology (d) only reading paper books forever

  10. Using AI chatbot to copy entire exam answer without understanding is (a) unethical and harmful to real learning (b) always officially encouraged in all exams (c) required by CCC syllabus practical (d) same as practical lab work

chapters.title: Practice MCQs
chapters.heading: Practice MCQs
chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 11: Future Skills

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink

  2. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol

  3. ChatGPT type tool is? a) Generative AI assistant b) Antivirus c) Spreadsheet d) Operating system

  4. IoT means? a) Internet of Things b) Internet of Text c) Input Output Tool d) Internal OS Test

  5. Cloud computing delivers? a) Services over internet b) Only rain weather joke c) Only local floppy d) Only printer

  6. Big data refers to? a) Large complex datasets b) Big monitor c) Big keyboard d) Big mouse

  7. Blockchain known for? a) Distributed ledger technology b) Word formatting c) Excel chart d) PPT animation

  8. Digital literacy means? a) Use technology effectively safely b) Avoid all tech wrong c) Only games wrong d) Only social media wrong narrow

  9. Coding teaches? a) Logical problem solving b) Only typing speed wrong only c) Only painting wrong d) Only printing wrong

  10. Python used for? a) Programming AI data scripts b) Word processing only wrong c) Printer driver only wrong d) BIOS only wrong

  11. 3D printing creates? a) Physical objects layer by layer b) Only PDF c) Only email d) Only music

  12. Virtual reality VR? a) Immersive simulated environment b) Email feature c) Word table d) Excel pivot only

  13. Augmented reality AR? a) Digital overlay on real world b) Only VR same wrong c) Only printer d) Only BIOS

  14. Robotics combines? a) Mechanical electronic software b) Only Word mail merge c) Only Excel d) Only Paint

  15. 5G network offers? a) Faster mobile broadband b) Free laptops wrong c) Replaces electricity wrong d) Word processor wrong

  16. Data privacy important because? a) Personal info protection b) Slow internet fix wrong c) Printer color wrong d) Font size wrong

  17. Ethical AI considers? a) Fairness transparency bias b) Only speed wrong c) Only logo wrong d) Only price wrong

  18. Automation at workplace? a) Tasks done by systems/scripts b) Always eliminates all jobs oversimplify wrong c) Only manual always wrong d) Only games wrong

  19. Remote work tools include? a) Video call cloud docs b) Only fax wrong c) Only typewriter wrong d) Only postal wrong

  20. Green computing aims? a) Energy efficient IT b) Paint PC green joke c) Delete internet wrong d) More paper wrong

  21. Open data government? a) Public datasets reuse b) Sell passwords wrong c) Pirate movies wrong d) Spam wrong

  22. Smart city uses IoT for? a) Traffic utilities monitoring b) Only games wrong c) Only movies wrong d) Remove citizens wrong

  23. Drone application? a) Delivery survey agriculture b) Word processing wrong c) Excel only wrong d) Email only wrong

  24. Cyber physical systems? a) Compute interacts physical world b) Word only wrong c) Only social media wrong d) Only fax wrong

  25. Digital twin? a) Virtual model of real system b) Two monitors wrong c) Two keyboards wrong d) Two emails wrong

  26. NFT controversial use? a) Digital ownership token art etc b) Word font wrong c) Excel row wrong d) Printer tray wrong

  27. Metaverse concept? a) Shared virtual spaces b) Email protocol wrong c) OS kernel wrong d) Printer language wrong

  28. Low code platforms? a) Build apps minimal coding b) No electricity wrong c) No internet wrong d) BIOS editor wrong

  29. API allows? a) Software systems communicate b) Printer ink type wrong c) Keyboard layout only wrong d) Mouse DPI only wrong

  30. Version control Git helps? a) Track code changes collaborate b) Print documents wrong c) Send fax wrong d) Mail merge wrong

  31. STEM skills include? a) Science Tech Engineering Math b) Only art wrong only c) Only sports wrong only d) Only music wrong only

  32. Soft skills with tech? a) Communication teamwork adaptability b) Only typing wrong only c) Only sleeping wrong d) Only eating wrong

  33. Lifelong learning means? a) Continuous skill update b) Stop after school wrong c) Never read wrong d) Avoid all courses wrong

  34. Online MOOC example? a) SWAYAM Coursera types b) Only piracy wrong c) Only spam wrong d) Only virus wrong

  35. Digital divide refers to? a) Gap in tech access skills b) Math division wrong joke c) Printer split wrong d) Excel divide error only joke

  36. Assistive technology helps? a) Disabilities access computing b) Hack banks wrong c) Spam wrong d) Pirate wrong

  37. E-waste recycling important? a) Environmental health b) Throw in river wrong c) Burn in open wrong d) Mix food wrong

  38. Quantum computing uses? a) Quantum physics for compute b) Word quantum spell wrong only c) Excel only wrong d) PPT only wrong

  39. Biometric future login? a) Face fingerprint iris b) Public password wrong c) Share OTP wrong d) Email PIN wrong

  40. AI bias can cause? a) Unfair decisions b) Faster printer wrong c) More RAM wrong d) Better virus wrong joke

  41. Human in the loop AI? a) Human oversight in decisions b) Robot only no human wrong c) Delete user wrong d) No review wrong sensitive

  42. Prompt engineering for? a) Effective AI instructions b) Printer prompts wrong c) BIOS prompts only wrong narrow d) Email subject only wrong

  43. Digital payment growth India driven by? a) UPI smartphone policy b) Only coins wrong c) Only barter wrong d) Remove banks wrong

  44. Future job skills CCC prepares? a) Digital office citizen skills b) Only rocket science wrong only c) Only surgery wrong only d) Only farming manual only wrong

  45. Responsible AI use student? a) Verify output cite sources b) Copy exam answers blindly wrong c) Share personal data with bot wrong d) Trust all hallucinations wrong

  46. Tech entrepreneurship example? a) Startup app solving problem b) Only spam email business wrong c) Only scam wrong d) Pirate software wrong

  47. Digital etiquette future workplace? a) Professional online behavior b) Caps lock always wrong c) Share secrets wrong d) Harass wrong

  48. Continuous upskilling platform NITA? a) Courses practice labs mocks b) Only games wrong c) Only movies wrong d) No learning wrong

  49. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink

  50. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Future Skills.
  2. List five important terms from Future Skills.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Future Skills.
  4. Why is Future Skills important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Future Skills.
  6. Define key concept in Future Skills.
  7. List five important terms from Future Skills.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Future Skills.
  9. Why is Future Skills important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Future Skills.
  11. Define key concept in Future Skills.
  12. List five important terms from Future Skills.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Future Skills.
  14. Why is Future Skills important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Future Skills.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Future Skills study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Future Skills study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

chapters.title: Answer Key (unlock after practice)
chapters.heading: Answer Key (unlock after practice)
chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 11: Future Skills

MCQ Answer Key

  1. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink Answer: a) Artificial Intelligence Explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

  2. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol Answer: a) Computers learn from data Explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

  3. ChatGPT type tool is? a) Generative AI assistant b) Antivirus c) Spreadsheet d) Operating system Answer: a) Generative AI assistant Explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

  4. IoT means? a) Internet of Things b) Internet of Text c) Input Output Tool d) Internal OS Test Answer: a) Internet of Things Explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

  5. Cloud computing delivers? a) Services over internet b) Only rain weather joke c) Only local floppy d) Only printer Answer: a) Services over internet Explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

  6. Big data refers to? a) Large complex datasets b) Big monitor c) Big keyboard d) Big mouse Answer: a) Large complex datasets Explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7.
