modules.title: Module 12 — CCC Exam Prep & Mock Tests

chapters.title: 15-Day Study Plan
chapters.heading: 15-Day Study Plan
chapters.contentHtml:
15-Day CCC Study Plan — Pass with Good Marks

For: NITA Classes DCA / CCC students
Daily study: 4–6 hours (theory + practical)
Goal: Complete syllabus + 3 mock tests before NIELIT CCC exam

Days 1–2: Computer Fundamentals (Module 1)

Study: Evolution of computers, hardware vs software, CPU, RAM, ROM, storage, input/output, mobile apps, IT gadgets
Practical: Label PC parts; convert decimal ↔ binary; create folder with student details
Practice: Module 1 MCQs (50) — check answers after completing

Important questions:

  • Brain of computer? → CPU
  • Temporary memory? → RAM
  • Device to enter data? → Keyboard (input device)

Days 3–4: Operating System (Module 2)

Study: Windows & Linux basics, desktop, taskbar, shortcuts, files/folders, install/remove programs
Practical: Create/rename/copy/move/delete folders; zip folder; Win+L lock; Task Manager
Practice: Module 2 MCQs

Practice tasks: Create Folder · Rename · Copy/Move/Delete files

Days 5–6: Word Processing (Module 3)

Study: MS Word / Writer — formatting, tables, images, mail merge, PDF export
Practical: Create resume + formal letter + 4×3 table
Practice: Module 3 MCQs

Shortcuts: Ctrl+S Save · Ctrl+B Bold · Ctrl+Enter Page break

Days 7–8: Spreadsheet (Module 4)

Study: Excel/Calc — formulas, SUM, AVERAGE, MAX, MIN, COUNT, IF, charts, sort, filter
Practical: Marks sheet 8 students × 3 subjects with Total, Average, Pass/Fail
Practice: Module 4 MCQs

Functions: =SUM() · =AVERAGE() · =MAX() · =MIN() · =COUNT()

Days 9–10: Presentation (Module 5)

Study: PowerPoint/Impress — slides, themes, animation, transition, slideshow
Practical: 5-slide presentation: About Yourself · Education · Skills · Career Goal · Thank You
Practice: Module 5 MCQs

Run slideshow: F5

Days 11–12: Internet & Communication (Modules 6–7)

Study: Browsers, search, downloads, HTTPS; email, attachments, cloud, video meetings
Practical: Bookmark sites; send email with attachment; upload file to Google Drive
Practice: Module 6 + 7 MCQs

Terms: WWW · URL · HTTP/HTTPS · CC · BCC

Day 13: Digital Financial Services (Module 8)

Study: UPI, BHIM, NPCI, net banking, wallets, AePS, QR payments, OTP safety
Practical: Demo UPI app screens (no real PIN entry in class); identify phishing SMS
Practice: Module 8 MCQs

Important: UPI = Unified Payments Interface

Day 14: E-Governance & Cyber Security (Modules 9–10)

Study: DigiLocker, UMANG, .gov.in portals; virus, phishing, strong passwords, 2FA
Practical: Explore India.gov.in; create strong password; list 5 phishing red flags
Practice: Module 9 + 10 MCQs

Strong password example style: Nita@2026#AI

Day 15: Future Skills + Mock Tests (Modules 11 + Exam Prep)

Study: AI basics, IoT, cloud, digital literacy (Module 11)
Morning: Revise 50 Most Important MCQs
Afternoon: Mock Test 1 (100 MCQ, 90 min timed)
Evening: Check Mock Test 1 answer key; revise wrong topics

After Day 15 (Exam Week)

Day Activity
+1 Mock Test 2 + answer key review
+2 Mock Test 3 + answer key review
+3 Full revision weak modules only
+4 Rest + light MCQ drill
+5 CCC Exam

Daily Checklist

  • Read study notes chapter
  • Complete lab tasks on PC
  • Solve practice MCQs (without peeking)
  • Mark chapter complete in NITA LMS
  • Unlock and check answer key only after attempt

NITA Classes — Good luck for CCC!

chapters.title: CCC Exam Pattern & Passing Criteria
chapters.heading: CCC Exam Pattern & Passing Criteria
chapters.contentHtml:
NIELIT CCC Exam Pattern (Revision 4)

Applicable from: October 2023 onward
Course: Course on Computer Concepts (CCC)
Aligned DCA course at NITA: 90 Hours (30 Theory + 60 Practical)

Examination Format

Item Detail
Question type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ) only
Total questions 100
Duration 90 minutes
Negative marking No
Passing marks 50% (minimum 50 correct)
Mode Online at NIELIT-approved exam centres

Syllabus Weightage (11 Modules)

  1. Introduction to Computer
  2. Operating System
  3. Word Processing (MS Word / LibreOffice Writer)
  4. Spreadsheet (MS Excel / LibreOffice Calc)
  5. Presentation (PowerPoint / Impress)
  6. Internet & Web Browsing
  7. Communication & Collaboration
  8. Digital Financial Services
  9. E-Governance Services
  10. Cyber Security
  11. Future Skills

Questions are distributed across all modules. Practical skills (Word, Excel, PPT) are tested conceptually via MCQs.

Passing Criteria

  • Score 50 or more out of 100
  • No section-wise minimum in standard CCC pattern (confirm with latest NIELIT notification)
  • Certificate issued after successful completion of course hours + exam

Exam Day Tips

  1. Read all four options before selecting
  2. Attempt easy questions first; mark doubtful for review
  3. Manage time: ~54 seconds per question average
  4. Do not leave questions blank (no negative marking)
  5. Revise 50 Most Important MCQs and 3 mock tests before exam

NITA Internal Assessment (Optional)

Assessment Marks When
Module practice MCQs Self-check after each unit Weekly
Mock Test 1–3 100 MCQ each Week 14–15
Lab practicals Trainer sign-off During 60 practical hours

Official references: NIELIT CCC Revised Syllabus PDF · CCC Detailed Syllabus PDF

chapters.title: Mock Test 1 — 100 MCQs (90 min)
chapters.heading: Mock Test 1 — 100 MCQs (90 min)
chapters.contentHtml:
CCC Mock Test 1 (100 MCQs)

Time: 90 minutes · Pass: 50%

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit

  2. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer

  3. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD

  4. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register

  5. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker

  6. Output device? a) Printer b) Mouse c) Scanner d) Microphone

  7. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes

  8. Binary uses digits? a) 0 and 1 b) 0 to 9 c) 1 to 9 d) A to F

  9. Software example? a) MS Word b) CPU c) Monitor d) Keyboard

  10. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus

  11. Pen drive is? a) Storage device b) Input device c) Output device d) ALU

  12. Full form of RAM? a) Random Access Memory b) Read Access Memory c) Run All Memory d) Random Active Memory

  13. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus

  14. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver

  15. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email

  16. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z

  17. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P

  18. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+N d) Ctrl+O

  19. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email

  20. Win+L? a) Lock computer b) Log off always c) Open Word d) Print

  21. F2 key? a) Rename b) Refresh c) Save d) Print

  22. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network

  23. Shift+Delete? a) Permanent delete b) Recycle Bin c) Copy d) Rename

  24. Task Manager opens with? a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc b) F5 c) Alt+H d) Win+P

  25. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb

  26. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt

  27. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P

  28. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N

  29. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L

  30. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5

  31. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter

  32. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver

  33. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only

  34. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only

  35. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N

  36. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K

  37. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

  38. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word

  39. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @

  40. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print

  41. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join

  42. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date

  43. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text

  44. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets

  45. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart

  46. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM

  47. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type

  48. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email

  49. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt

  50. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel

  51. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print

  52. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview

  53. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7

  54. Transition applies to? a) Between slides b) Objects only c) Notes d) Handouts only

  55. Animation applies to? a) Objects on slide b) Between slides c) Printer d) BIOS

  56. Title slide layout has? a) Title + subtitle placeholders b) Blank only c) Excel table d) Database

  57. Theme changes? a) Colors fonts effects b) CPU speed c) RAM d) Internet

  58. Widescreen slide ratio often? a) 16:9 b) 4:3 only c) 1:1 only d) None

  59. Duplicate slide? a) Right-click Duplicate b) F5 c) Delete d) Esc

  60. Presenter View shows? a) Notes and timer b) BIOS c) Excel d) Email only

  61. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard

  62. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide

  63. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access

  64. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator

  65. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU

  66. HTTPS indicates? a) Secure connection b) Slow site c) No images d) Free Wi-Fi

  67. HTTP stands for? a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol b) High Transfer Text Program c) Hyper Tool Transfer d) Home Text Protocol

  68. Download means? a) Save from internet to PC b) Upload c) Delete d) Print

  69. Upload means? a) Send from PC to internet b) Download c) Shutdown d) Format

  70. Google is a? a) Search engine b) Operating system c) Word processor d) Printer

  71. Bookmark saves? a) Favorite website b) Deletes site c) Formats PC d) Installs OS

  72. Ctrl+T in browser? a) New tab b) Close tab c) Print d) Save page

  73. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail

  74. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only

  75. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home

  76. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy

  77. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy

  78. Reply sends to? a) Original sender b) Everyone BCC hidden c) Nobody d) Printer

  79. Forward sends? a) Copy to new recipient b) Deletes original c) Formats PC d) Installs OS

  80. Gmail provided by? a) Google b) Microsoft only c) Apple only d) Government only

  81. Outlook email by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Linux kernel

  82. Compose email needs? a) To, Subject, Body b) CPU serial only c) Printer model d) BIOS version

  83. Email etiquette includes? a) Clear subject line b) ALL CAPS shouting OK always c) Share passwords d) No greeting ever required wrong

  84. Cloud storage example? a) Google Drive b) CPU cache c) RAM stick d) Printer tray

  85. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index

  86. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only

  87. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always

  88. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator

  89. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart

  90. QR code payment scans? a) Merchant QR to pay b) CPU barcode c) Printer test page d) BIOS screen

  91. OTP in banking means? a) One Time Password b) Open Transfer Protocol c) Online Text Payment d) Only Total Price

  92. Never share OTP with? a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent b) Your own memory c) Bank official phone asking wrong scam d) Secure app entry yourself OK

  93. IMPS is? a) Immediate payment service b) Internet mail print c) Image media file d) Internal memory

  94. NEFT is? a) Electronic fund transfer b) Network email file c) New excel format d) National education test

  95. RTGS for? a) Large value real-time transfer b) Email attachment c) Word mail merge d) PPT animation

  96. Debit card deducts from? a) Your bank account b) Credit loan always wrong for debit c) Email account d) Cloud drive

  97. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips

  98. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor

  99. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only

  100. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Word file extension?
questions.explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: LibreOffice Writer extension?
questions.explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Bold shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

questions.question: Italic shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Underline shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Save shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Page break shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge used for?
questions.explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge tab in Word?
questions.explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Justify alignment?
questions.explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Find shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Replace shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Excel extension?
questions.explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Calc is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Formula starts with?
questions.explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

questions.question: SUM() used for?
questions.explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

questions.question: AVERAGE() returns?
questions.explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MAX() returns?
questions.explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MIN() returns?
questions.explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: COUNT() counts?
questions.explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cell A1 is?
questions.explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Absolute reference?
questions.explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: IF function checks?
questions.explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Pie chart best for?
questions.explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: PowerPoint extension?
questions.explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Impress is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: F5 starts slideshow from?
questions.explanation: F5 starts the PowerPoint/Impress slideshow from the first slide. Shift+F5 starts from the current slide.

questions.question: Shift+F5 from?
questions.explanation: Use Current slide for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: New slide shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

questions.question: Transition applies to?
questions.explanation: Between slides — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Animation applies to?
questions.explanation: Objects on slide — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Title slide layout has?
questions.explanation: Title + subtitle placeholders is the correct answer. Blank only and Excel table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Theme changes?
questions.explanation: Colors fonts effects is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Widescreen slide ratio often?
questions.explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Duplicate slide?
questions.explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Presenter View shows?
questions.explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Internet is?
questions.explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

questions.question: WWW stands for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

questions.question: Browser example?
questions.explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: URL stands for?
questions.explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

questions.question: ISP provides?
questions.explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: HTTPS indicates?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

questions.question: HTTP stands for?
questions.explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

questions.question: Download means?
questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

questions.question: Upload means?
questions.explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

questions.question: Google is a?
questions.explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

questions.question: Bookmark saves?
questions.explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Ctrl+T in browser?
questions.explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Email stands for?
questions.explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

questions.question: Email format?
questions.explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

questions.question: Attachment icon?
questions.explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: CC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: BCC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: Reply sends to?
questions.explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Forward sends?
questions.explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Gmail provided by?
questions.explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Outlook email by?
questions.explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Compose email needs?
questions.explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Email etiquette includes?
questions.explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cloud storage example?
questions.explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

questions.question: UPI stands for?
questions.explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

questions.question: BHIM app developed by?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

questions.question: Net banking allows?

options.text: Only phone call branch only always

questions.explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: Digital wallet example?
questions.explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

questions.question: AEPS uses?
questions.explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

questions.question: QR code payment scans?
questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.question: OTP in banking means?
questions.explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

questions.question: Never share OTP with?

options.text: Bank official phone asking wrong scam

questions.explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

questions.question: IMPS is?
questions.explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

questions.question: NEFT is?
questions.explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

questions.question: RTGS for?
questions.explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

questions.question: Debit card deducts from?

options.text: Credit loan always wrong for debit

questions.explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

questions.question: DigiLocker stores?
questions.explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

questions.question: UMANG app provides?
questions.explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

questions.question: e-Governance means?
questions.explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

questions.question: Aadhaar used for?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

chapters.title: Mock Test 1 — Answer Key
chapters.heading: Mock Test 1 — Answer Key
chapters.contentHtml:
Mock Test 1 — Answer Key

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit Answer: a) Central Processing Unit Explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

  2. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer Answer: a) CPU Explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

  3. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD Answer: a) RAM Explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

  4. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register Answer: a) ROM Explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

  5. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker Answer: a) Keyboard Explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

  6. Output device? a) Printer b) Mouse c) Scanner d) Microphone Answer: a) Printer Explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

  7. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes Answer: a) 1024 bytes Explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

  8. Binary uses digits? a) 0 and 1 b) 0 to 9 c) 1 to 9 d) A to F Answer: a) 0 and 1 Explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

  9. Software example? a) MS Word b) CPU c) Monitor d) Keyboard Answer: a) MS Word Explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

  10. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus Answer: a) Keyboard Explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

  11. Pen drive is? a) Storage device b) Input device c) Output device d) ALU Answer: a) Storage device Explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

  12. Full form of RAM? a) Random Access Memory b) Read Access Memory c) Run All Memory d) Random Active Memory Answer: a) Random Access Memory Explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

  13. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus Answer: a) Operating System Explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

  14. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver Answer: a) Open source OS Explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

  15. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email Answer: a) File management Explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

  16. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z Answer: a) Ctrl+C Explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

  17. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+V Explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

  18. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+N d) Ctrl+O Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

  19. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email Answer: a) File Explorer Explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

  20. Win+L? a) Lock computer b) Log off always c) Open Word d) Print Answer: a) Lock computer Explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

  21. F2 key? a) Rename b) Refresh c) Save d) Print Answer: a) Rename Explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

  22. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network Answer: a) Recycle Bin Explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

  23. Shift+Delete? a) Permanent delete b) Recycle Bin c) Copy d) Rename Answer: a) Permanent delete Explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

  24. Task Manager opens with? a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc b) F5 c) Alt+H d) Win+P Answer: a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc Explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

  25. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb Answer: a) .docx Explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt Answer: a) .odt Explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  27. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+B Explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

  28. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+I Explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  29. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L Answer: a) Ctrl+U Explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  30. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5 Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  31. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter Answer: a) Ctrl+Enter Explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  32. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver Answer: a) Bulk personalized letters Explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  33. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only Answer: a) Mailings Explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  34. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only Answer: a) Both edges straight Explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  35. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+F Explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  36. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K Answer: a) Ctrl+H Explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  37. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt Answer: a) .xlsx Explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  39. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @ Answer: a) = Explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

  40. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print Answer: a) Addition/total Explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

  41. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join Answer: a) Mean value Explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  42. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date Answer: a) Largest value Explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  43. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text Answer: a) Smallest value Explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  44. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets Answer: a) Numeric cells Explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  45. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart Answer: a) Column A row 1 Explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  46. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM Answer: a) $A$1 Explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  47. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type Answer: a) Condition true/false Explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

  48. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email Answer: a) Parts of whole Explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  49. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt Answer: a) .pptx Explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  50. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  51. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print Answer: a) Beginning Explanation: F5 starts the PowerPoint/Impress slideshow from the first slide. Shift+F5 starts from the current slide.

  52. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview Answer: a) Current slide Explanation: Use Current slide for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  53. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7 Answer: a) Ctrl+M Explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

  54. Transition applies to? a) Between slides b) Objects only c) Notes d) Handouts only Answer: a) Between slides Explanation: Between slides — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  55. Animation applies to? a) Objects on slide b) Between slides c) Printer d) BIOS Answer: a) Objects on slide Explanation: Objects on slide — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  56. Title slide layout has? a) Title + subtitle placeholders b) Blank only c) Excel table d) Database Answer: a) Title + subtitle placeholders Explanation: Title + subtitle placeholders is the correct answer. Blank only and Excel table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  57. Theme changes? a) Colors fonts effects b) CPU speed c) RAM d) Internet Answer: a) Colors fonts effects Explanation: Colors fonts effects is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  58. Widescreen slide ratio often? a) 16:9 b) 4:3 only c) 1:1 only d) None Answer: a) 16:9 Explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  59. Duplicate slide? a) Right-click Duplicate b) F5 c) Delete d) Esc Answer: a) Right-click Duplicate Explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  60. Presenter View shows? a) Notes and timer b) BIOS c) Excel d) Email only Answer: a) Notes and timer Explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  61. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard Answer: a) Global network of computers Explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

  62. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide Answer: a) World Wide Web Explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

  63. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access Answer: a) Google Chrome Explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  64. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator Answer: a) Uniform Resource Locator Explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

  65. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU Answer: a) Internet connection Explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  66. HTTPS indicates? a) Secure connection b) Slow site c) No images d) Free Wi-Fi Answer: a) Secure connection Explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

  67. HTTP stands for? a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol b) High Transfer Text Program c) Hyper Tool Transfer d) Home Text Protocol Answer: a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol Explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

  68. Download means? a) Save from internet to PC b) Upload c) Delete d) Print Answer: a) Save from internet to PC Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

  69. Upload means? a) Send from PC to internet b) Download c) Shutdown d) Format Answer: a) Send from PC to internet Explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

  70. Google is a? a) Search engine b) Operating system c) Word processor d) Printer Answer: a) Search engine Explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

  71. Bookmark saves? a) Favorite website b) Deletes site c) Formats PC d) Installs OS Answer: a) Favorite website Explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  72. Ctrl+T in browser? a) New tab b) Close tab c) Print d) Save page Answer: a) New tab Explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  73. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail Answer: a) Electronic Mail Explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

  74. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only Answer: a) user@domain.com Explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

  75. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home Answer: a) Paperclip Explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  76. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy Answer: a) Carbon copy visible Explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

  77. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy Answer: a) Blind carbon copy hidden Explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

  78. Reply sends to? a) Original sender b) Everyone BCC hidden c) Nobody d) Printer Answer: a) Original sender Explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  79. Forward sends? a) Copy to new recipient b) Deletes original c) Formats PC d) Installs OS Answer: a) Copy to new recipient Explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  80. Gmail provided by? a) Google b) Microsoft only c) Apple only d) Government only Answer: a) Google Explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  81. Outlook email by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Linux kernel Answer: a) Microsoft Explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  82. Compose email needs? a) To, Subject, Body b) CPU serial only c) Printer model d) BIOS version Answer: a) To, Subject, Body Explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  83. Email etiquette includes? a) Clear subject line b) ALL CAPS shouting OK always c) Share passwords d) No greeting ever required wrong Answer: a) Clear subject line Explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  84. Cloud storage example? a) Google Drive b) CPU cache c) RAM stick d) Printer tray Answer: a) Google Drive Explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

  85. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index Answer: a) Unified Payments Interface Explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

  86. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only Answer: a) NPCI / Government initiative Explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

  87. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always Answer: a) Banking via internet Explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

  88. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator Answer: a) Paytm, PhonePe Explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

  89. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart Answer: a) Aadhaar authentication for payments Explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

  90. QR code payment scans? a) Merchant QR to pay b) CPU barcode c) Printer test page d) BIOS screen Answer: a) Merchant QR to pay Explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

  91. OTP in banking means? a) One Time Password b) Open Transfer Protocol c) Online Text Payment d) Only Total Price Answer: a) One Time Password Explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

  92. Never share OTP with? a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent b) Your own memory c) Bank official phone asking wrong scam d) Secure app entry yourself OK Answer: a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent Explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  93. IMPS is? a) Immediate payment service b) Internet mail print c) Image media file d) Internal memory Answer: a) Immediate payment service Explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

  94. NEFT is? a) Electronic fund transfer b) Network email file c) New excel format d) National education test Answer: a) Electronic fund transfer Explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

  95. RTGS for? a) Large value real-time transfer b) Email attachment c) Word mail merge d) PPT animation Answer: a) Large value real-time transfer Explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

  96. Debit card deducts from? a) Your bank account b) Credit loan always wrong for debit c) Email account d) Cloud drive Answer: a) Your bank account Explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

  97. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips Answer: a) Digital certificates documents Explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

  98. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor Answer: a) Government services unified Explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

  99. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only Answer: a) Government services via ICT Explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

  100. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme Answer: a) Identity authentication Explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.
