questions.explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Duplicate slide?
questions.explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Presenter View shows?
questions.explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Internet is?
questions.explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

questions.question: WWW stands for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

questions.question: Browser example?
questions.explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: URL stands for?
questions.explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

questions.question: ISP provides?
questions.explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: HTTPS indicates?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

questions.question: HTTP stands for?
questions.explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

questions.question: Download means?
questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

questions.question: Upload means?
questions.explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

questions.question: Google is a?
questions.explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

questions.question: Bookmark saves?
questions.explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Ctrl+T in browser?
questions.explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Email stands for?
questions.explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

questions.question: Email format?
questions.explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

questions.question: Attachment icon?
questions.explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: CC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: BCC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: Reply sends to?
questions.explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Forward sends?
questions.explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Gmail provided by?
questions.explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Outlook email by?
questions.explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Compose email needs?
questions.explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Email etiquette includes?
questions.explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cloud storage example?
questions.explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

questions.question: UPI stands for?
questions.explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

questions.question: BHIM app developed by?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

questions.question: Net banking allows?

options.text: Only phone call branch only always

questions.explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: Digital wallet example?
questions.explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

questions.question: AEPS uses?
questions.explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

questions.question: QR code payment scans?
questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.question: OTP in banking means?
questions.explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

questions.question: Never share OTP with?

options.text: Bank official phone asking wrong scam

questions.explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

questions.question: IMPS is?
questions.explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

questions.question: NEFT is?
questions.explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

questions.question: RTGS for?
questions.explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

questions.question: Debit card deducts from?

options.text: Credit loan always wrong for debit

questions.explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

questions.question: DigiLocker stores?
questions.explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

questions.question: UMANG app provides?
questions.explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

questions.question: e-Governance means?
questions.explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

questions.question: Aadhaar used for?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

chapters.title: Mock Test 2 — 100 MCQs (90 min)
chapters.heading: Mock Test 2 — 100 MCQs (90 min)
chapters.contentHtml:
CCC Mock Test 2 (100 MCQs)

Time: 90 minutes · Pass: 50%

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus

  2. Online PAN apply via? a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals b) Random WhatsApp c) Unknown email EXE d) Pop-up ad

  3. Passport application online? a) Passport Seva portal b) Facebook form c) Instagram DM d) Random APK

  4. Voter ID services online? a) NVSP portal b) Unknown blog c) Pirate site d) Spam email link

  5. Income tax e-filing? a) Official incometax.gov.in b) Random .com similar phishing caution c) Word doc macro d) Excel only offline always wrong

  6. GST registration online? a) gst.gov.in b) Unknown mirror site c) Telegram bot only wrong d) Email attachment exe

  7. EPFO member portal for? a) Provident fund services b) Games c) Music streaming d) PPT templates

  8. Ayushman Bharat digital mission? a) Health records ecosystem b) Word spell check c) Excel pivot only d) Printer driver

  9. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature

  10. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong

  11. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache

  12. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam

  13. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only

  14. OTP should be? a) Kept secret one-time use b) Shared on Facebook c) Emailed to stranger d) Posted public

  15. Firewall helps? a) Block unauthorized access b) Print documents c) Edit Word d) Make charts

  16. HTTPS for banking? a) Encrypted connection prefer b) Optional never matters wrong c) Slower so avoid wrong d) Replaces password wrong

  17. Ransomware? a) Encrypts data demands payment b) Free backup tool c) Word feature d) Excel sort

  18. Spyware? a) Secretly monitors user b) Official antivirus c) Browser bookmark d) Email signature

  19. Trojan horse? a) Disguised malicious program b) Greek history only no malware wrong c) Hardware GPU d) Printer font

  20. Worm spreads? a) Self across network b) Only USB always wrong only one way c) Never copies wrong d) Word table

  21. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink

  22. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol

  23. ChatGPT type tool is? a) Generative AI assistant b) Antivirus c) Spreadsheet d) Operating system

  24. IoT means? a) Internet of Things b) Internet of Text c) Input Output Tool d) Internal OS Test

  25. Cloud computing delivers? a) Services over internet b) Only rain weather joke c) Only local floppy d) Only printer

  26. Big data refers to? a) Large complex datasets b) Big monitor c) Big keyboard d) Big mouse

  27. Blockchain known for? a) Distributed ledger technology b) Word formatting c) Excel chart d) PPT animation

  28. Digital literacy means? a) Use technology effectively safely b) Avoid all tech wrong c) Only games wrong d) Only social media wrong narrow

  29. Coding teaches? a) Logical problem solving b) Only typing speed wrong only c) Only painting wrong d) Only printing wrong

  30. Python used for? a) Programming AI data scripts b) Word processing only wrong c) Printer driver only wrong d) BIOS only wrong

  31. 3D printing creates? a) Physical objects layer by layer b) Only PDF c) Only email d) Only music

  32. Virtual reality VR? a) Immersive simulated environment b) Email feature c) Word table d) Excel pivot only

  33. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit

  34. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer

  35. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD

  36. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register

  37. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker

  38. Output device? a) Printer b) Mouse c) Scanner d) Microphone

  39. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes

  40. Binary uses digits? a) 0 and 1 b) 0 to 9 c) 1 to 9 d) A to F

  41. Software example? a) MS Word b) CPU c) Monitor d) Keyboard

  42. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus

  43. Pen drive is? a) Storage device b) Input device c) Output device d) ALU

  44. Full form of RAM? a) Random Access Memory b) Read Access Memory c) Run All Memory d) Random Active Memory

  45. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus

  46. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver

  47. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email

  48. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z

  49. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P

  50. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+N d) Ctrl+O

  51. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email

  52. Win+L? a) Lock computer b) Log off always c) Open Word d) Print

  53. F2 key? a) Rename b) Refresh c) Save d) Print

  54. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network

  55. Shift+Delete? a) Permanent delete b) Recycle Bin c) Copy d) Rename

  56. Task Manager opens with? a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc b) F5 c) Alt+H d) Win+P

  57. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb

  58. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt

  59. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P

  60. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N

  61. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L

  62. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5

  63. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter

  64. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver

  65. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only

  66. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only

  67. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N

  68. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K

  69. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

  70. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word

  71. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @

  72. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print

  73. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join

  74. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date

  75. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text

  76. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets

  77. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart

  78. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM

  79. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type

  80. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email

  81. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt

  82. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel

  83. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print

  84. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview

  85. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7

  86. Transition applies to? a) Between slides b) Objects only c) Notes d) Handouts only

  87. Animation applies to? a) Objects on slide b) Between slides c) Printer d) BIOS

  88. Title slide layout has? a) Title + subtitle placeholders b) Blank only c) Excel table d) Database

  89. Theme changes? a) Colors fonts effects b) CPU speed c) RAM d) Internet

  90. Widescreen slide ratio often? a) 16:9 b) 4:3 only c) 1:1 only d) None

  91. Duplicate slide? a) Right-click Duplicate b) F5 c) Delete d) Esc

  92. Presenter View shows? a) Notes and timer b) BIOS c) Excel d) Email only

  93. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard

  94. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide

  95. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access

  96. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator

  97. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU

  98. HTTPS indicates? a) Secure connection b) Slow site c) No images d) Free Wi-Fi

  99. HTTP stands for? a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol b) High Transfer Text Program c) Hyper Tool Transfer d) Home Text Protocol

  100. Download means? a) Save from internet to PC b) Upload c) Delete d) Print

questions.question: India.gov.in is?
questions.explanation: India.gov.in is the official national portal linking to government websites and services.

questions.question: Online PAN apply via?
questions.explanation: NSDL/UTIITSL portals is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp and Unknown email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Passport application online?
questions.explanation: Passport Seva portal is the correct answer. Facebook form and Instagram DM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Voter ID services online?
questions.explanation: NVSP portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Income tax e-filing?
questions.explanation: Official incometax.gov.in is the correct answer. Random .com similar phishing caution and Word doc macro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: GST registration online?
questions.explanation: gst.gov.in is the correct answer. Unknown mirror site and Telegram bot only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: EPFO member portal for?
questions.explanation: Provident fund services is the correct answer. Games and Music streaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ayushman Bharat digital mission?
questions.explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Malware means?
questions.explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

questions.question: Phishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Antivirus protects from?
questions.explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Strong password example style?
questions.explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

questions.question: OTP should be?
questions.explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Firewall helps?
questions.explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

questions.question: HTTPS for banking?
questions.explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ransomware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Spyware?
questions.explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Trojan horse?

options.text: Greek history only no malware wrong

questions.explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Worm spreads?

options.text: Only USB always wrong only one way

questions.explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: AI stands for?
questions.explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

questions.question: Machine learning is?
questions.explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

questions.question: ChatGPT type tool is?
questions.explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

questions.question: IoT means?
questions.explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

questions.question: Cloud computing delivers?
questions.explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

questions.question: Big data refers to?
questions.explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Blockchain known for?
questions.explanation: Distributed ledger technology is the correct answer. Word formatting and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Digital literacy means?
questions.explanation: Digital literacy means using computers, Internet, and mobile apps safely and effectively in daily life.

questions.question: Coding teaches?
questions.explanation: Logical problem solving is the correct answer. Only typing speed wrong only and Only painting wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Python used for?
questions.explanation: Python is a popular programming language used for AI, data analysis, automation, and web development.

questions.question: 3D printing creates?
questions.explanation: Physical objects layer by layer is the correct answer. Only PDF and Only email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virtual reality VR?
questions.explanation: Immersive simulated environment is the correct answer. Email feature and Word table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Word file extension?
questions.explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: LibreOffice Writer extension?
questions.explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Bold shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

questions.question: Italic shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Underline shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Save shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Page break shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge used for?
questions.explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge tab in Word?
questions.explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Justify alignment?
questions.explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Find shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Replace shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Excel extension?
questions.explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Calc is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Formula starts with?
questions.explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

questions.question: SUM() used for?
questions.explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

questions.question: AVERAGE() returns?
questions.explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MAX() returns?
questions.explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MIN() returns?
questions.explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: COUNT() counts?
questions.explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cell A1 is?

questions.question: Absolute reference?
questions.explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: IF function checks?
questions.explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Pie chart best for?
questions.explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: PowerPoint extension?
questions.explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.explanation: F5 starts the PowerPoint/Impress slideshow from the first slide. Shift+F5 starts from the current slide.

questions.question: Shift+F5 from?
questions.explanation: Use Current slide for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: New slide shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

questions.question: Transition applies to?
questions.explanation: Between slides — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Animation applies to?
questions.explanation: Objects on slide — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Title slide layout has?
questions.explanation: Title + subtitle placeholders is the correct answer. Blank only and Excel table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Theme changes?
questions.explanation: Colors fonts effects is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Widescreen slide ratio often?
questions.explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Duplicate slide?
questions.explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Presenter View shows?
questions.explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Internet is?
questions.explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

questions.question: WWW stands for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

questions.question: Browser example?
questions.explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: URL stands for?
questions.explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

questions.question: ISP provides?
questions.explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: HTTPS indicates?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

questions.question: HTTP stands for?
questions.explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

questions.question: Download means?
questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

chapters.title: Mock Test 2 — Answer Key
chapters.heading: Mock Test 2 — Answer Key
chapters.contentHtml:
Mock Test 2 — Answer Key

MCQ Answer Key

  1. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus Answer: a) National portal Explanation: India.gov.in is the official national portal linking to government websites and services.

  2. Online PAN apply via? a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals b) Random WhatsApp c) Unknown email EXE d) Pop-up ad Answer: a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals Explanation: NSDL/UTIITSL portals is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp and Unknown email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  3. Passport application online? a) Passport Seva portal b) Facebook form c) Instagram DM d) Random APK Answer: a) Passport Seva portal Explanation: Passport Seva portal is the correct answer. Facebook form and Instagram DM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  4. Voter ID services online? a) NVSP portal b) Unknown blog c) Pirate site d) Spam email link Answer: a) NVSP portal Explanation: NVSP portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  5. Income tax e-filing? a) Official incometax.gov.in b) Random .com similar phishing caution c) Word doc macro d) Excel only offline always wrong Answer: a) Official incometax.gov.in Explanation: Official incometax.gov.in is the correct answer. Random .com similar phishing caution and Word doc macro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  6. GST registration online? a) gst.gov.in b) Unknown mirror site c) Telegram bot only wrong d) Email attachment exe Answer: a) gst.gov.in Explanation: gst.gov.in is the correct answer. Unknown mirror site and Telegram bot only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7. EPFO member portal for? a) Provident fund services b) Games c) Music streaming d) PPT templates Answer: a) Provident fund services Explanation: Provident fund services is the correct answer. Games and Music streaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  8. Ayushman Bharat digital mission? a) Health records ecosystem b) Word spell check c) Excel pivot only d) Printer driver Answer: a) Health records ecosystem Explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature Answer: a) Malicious software Explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

  10. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong Answer: a) Malicious software general Explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

  11. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache Answer: a) Fraudulent data theft attempt Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  12. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam Answer: a) Malware Explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  13. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only Answer: a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars Explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

  14. OTP should be? a) Kept secret one-time use b) Shared on Facebook c) Emailed to stranger d) Posted public Answer: a) Kept secret one-time use Explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. Firewall helps? a) Block unauthorized access b) Print documents c) Edit Word d) Make charts Answer: a) Block unauthorized access Explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

  16. HTTPS for banking? a) Encrypted connection prefer b) Optional never matters wrong c) Slower so avoid wrong d) Replaces password wrong Answer: a) Encrypted connection prefer Explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Ransomware? a) Encrypts data demands payment b) Free backup tool c) Word feature d) Excel sort Answer: a) Encrypts data demands payment Explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

  18. Spyware? a) Secretly monitors user b) Official antivirus c) Browser bookmark d) Email signature Answer: a) Secretly monitors user Explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  19. Trojan horse? a) Disguised malicious program b) Greek history only no malware wrong c) Hardware GPU d) Printer font Answer: a) Disguised malicious program Explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. Worm spreads? a) Self across network b) Only USB always wrong only one way c) Never copies wrong d) Word table Answer: a) Self across network Explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  21. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink Answer: a) Artificial Intelligence Explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

  22. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol Answer: a) Computers learn from data Explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

  23. ChatGPT type tool is? a) Generative AI assistant b) Antivirus c) Spreadsheet d) Operating system Answer: a) Generative AI assistant Explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

  24. IoT means? a) Internet of Things b) Internet of Text c) Input Output Tool d) Internal OS Test Answer: a) Internet of Things Explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

  25. Cloud computing delivers? a) Services over internet b) Only rain weather joke c) Only local floppy d) Only printer Answer: a) Services over internet Explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

  26. Big data refers to? a) Large complex datasets b) Big monitor c) Big keyboard d) Big mouse Answer: a) Large complex datasets Explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  27. Blockchain known for? a) Distributed ledger technology b) Word formatting c) Excel chart d) PPT animation Answer: a) Distributed ledger technology Explanation: Distributed ledger technology is the correct answer. Word formatting and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. Digital literacy means? a) Use technology effectively safely b) Avoid all tech wrong c) Only games wrong d) Only social media wrong narrow Answer: a) Use technology effectively safely Explanation: Digital literacy means using computers, Internet, and mobile apps safely and effectively in daily life.

  29. Coding teaches? a) Logical problem solving b) Only typing speed wrong only c) Only painting wrong d) Only printing wrong Answer: a) Logical problem solving Explanation: Logical problem solving is the correct answer. Only typing speed wrong only and Only painting wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. Python used for? a) Programming AI data scripts b) Word processing only wrong c) Printer driver only wrong d) BIOS only wrong Answer: a) Programming AI data scripts Explanation: Python is a popular programming language used for AI, data analysis, automation, and web development.

  31. 3D printing creates? a) Physical objects layer by layer b) Only PDF c) Only email d) Only music Answer: a) Physical objects layer by layer Explanation: Physical objects layer by layer is the correct answer. Only PDF and Only email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Virtual reality VR? a) Immersive simulated environment b) Email feature c) Word table d) Excel pivot only Answer: a) Immersive simulated environment Explanation: Immersive simulated environment is the correct answer. Email feature and Word table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit Answer: a) Central Processing Unit Explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

  34. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer Answer: a) CPU Explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

  35. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD Answer: a) RAM Explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

  36. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register Answer: a) ROM Explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

  37. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker Answer: a) Keyboard Explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

  38. Output device? a) Printer b) Mouse c) Scanner d) Microphone Answer: a) Printer Explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

  39. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes Answer: a) 1024 bytes Explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

  40. Binary uses digits? a) 0 and 1 b) 0 to 9 c) 1 to 9 d) A to F Answer: a) 0 and 1 Explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

  41. Software example? a) MS Word b) CPU c) Monitor d) Keyboard Answer: a) MS Word Explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

  42. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus Answer: a) Keyboard Explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

  43. Pen drive is? a) Storage device b) Input device c) Output device d) ALU Answer: a) Storage device Explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

  44. Full form of RAM? a) Random Access Memory b) Read Access Memory c) Run All Memory d) Random Active Memory Answer: a) Random Access Memory Explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

  45. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus Answer: a) Operating System Explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

  46. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver Answer: a) Open source OS Explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

  47. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email Answer: a) File management Explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

  48. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z Answer: a) Ctrl+C Explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

  49. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+V Explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

  50. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+N d) Ctrl+O Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

  51. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email Answer: a) File Explorer Explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

  52. Win+L? a) Lock computer b) Log off always c) Open Word d) Print Answer: a) Lock computer Explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

  53. F2 key? a) Rename b) Refresh c) Save d) Print Answer: a) Rename Explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

  54. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network Answer: a) Recycle Bin Explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

  55. Shift+Delete? a) Permanent delete b) Recycle Bin c) Copy d) Rename Answer: a) Permanent delete Explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

  56. Task Manager opens with? a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc b) F5 c) Alt+H d) Win+P Answer: a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc Explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

  57. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb Answer: a) .docx Explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  58. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt Answer: a) .odt Explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  59. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+B Explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

  60. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+I Explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  61. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L Answer: a) Ctrl+U Explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  62. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5 Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  63. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter Answer: a) Ctrl+Enter Explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  64. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver Answer: a) Bulk personalized letters Explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  65. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only Answer: a) Mailings Explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  66. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only Answer: a) Both edges straight Explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  67. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+F Explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  68. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K Answer: a) Ctrl+H Explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  69. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt Answer: a) .xlsx Explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  70. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  71. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @ Answer: a) = Explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

  72. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print Answer: a) Addition/total Explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

  73. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join Answer: a) Mean value Explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  74. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date Answer: a) Largest value Explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  75. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text Answer: a) Smallest value Explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  76. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets Answer: a) Numeric cells Explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  77. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart Answer: a) Column A row 1 Explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  78. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM Answer: a) $A$1 Explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  79. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type Answer: a) Condition true/false Explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

  80. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email Answer: a) Parts of whole Explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  81. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt Answer: a) .pptx Explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  82. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  83. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print Answer: a) Beginning Explanation: F5 starts the PowerPoint/Impress slideshow from the first slide. Shift+F5 starts from the current slide.

  84. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview Answer: a) Current slide Explanation: Use Current slide for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  85. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7 Answer: a) Ctrl+M Explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

  86. Transition applies to? a) Between slides b) Objects only c) Notes d) Handouts only Answer: a) Between slides Explanation: Between slides — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  87. Animation applies to? a) Objects on slide b) Between slides c) Printer d) BIOS Answer: a) Objects on slide Explanation: Objects on slide — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  88. Title slide layout has? a) Title + subtitle placeholders b) Blank only c) Excel table d) Database Answer: a) Title + subtitle placeholders Explanation: Title + subtitle placeholders is the correct answer. Blank only and Excel table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  89. Theme changes? a) Colors fonts effects b) CPU speed c) RAM d) Internet Answer: a) Colors fonts effects Explanation: Colors fonts effects is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  90. Widescreen slide ratio often? a) 16:9 b) 4:3 only c) 1:1 only d) None Answer: a) 16:9 Explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  91. Duplicate slide? a) Right-click Duplicate b) F5 c) Delete d) Esc Answer: a) Right-click Duplicate Explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  92. Presenter View shows? a) Notes and timer b) BIOS c) Excel d) Email only Answer: a) Notes and timer Explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  93. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard Answer: a) Global network of computers Explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

  94. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide Answer: a) World Wide Web Explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

  95. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access Answer: a) Google Chrome Explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  96. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator Answer: a) Uniform Resource Locator Explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

  97. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU Answer: a) Internet connection Explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  98. HTTPS indicates? a) Secure connection b) Slow site c) No images d) Free Wi-Fi Answer: a) Secure connection Explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

  99. HTTP stands for? a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol b) High Transfer Text Program c) Hyper Tool Transfer d) Home Text Protocol Answer: a) Hyper Text Transfer Protocol Explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

  100. Download means? a) Save from internet to PC b) Upload c) Delete d) Print Answer: a) Save from internet to PC Explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

questions.question: Ayushman Bharat digital mission?
questions.explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Malware means?
questions.explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

questions.question: Phishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Antivirus protects from?
questions.explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Strong password example style?
questions.explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

questions.question: OTP should be?
questions.explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Firewall helps?
questions.explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

questions.question: HTTPS for banking?
questions.explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ransomware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Spyware?
questions.explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Trojan horse?

options.text: Greek history only no malware wrong

questions.explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Worm spreads?

options.text: Only USB always wrong only one way

questions.explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: AI stands for?
questions.explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

questions.question: Machine learning is?
questions.explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

questions.question: ChatGPT type tool is?
questions.explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

questions.question: IoT means?
questions.explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

questions.question: Cloud computing delivers?
questions.explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

questions.question: Big data refers to?
questions.explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Blockchain known for?
questions.explanation: Distributed ledger technology is the correct answer. Word formatting and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Digital literacy means?
questions.explanation: Digital literacy means using computers, Internet, and mobile apps safely and effectively in daily life.

questions.question: Coding teaches?
questions.explanation: Logical problem solving is the correct answer. Only typing speed wrong only and Only painting wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Python used for?
questions.explanation: Python is a popular programming language used for AI, data analysis, automation, and web development.

questions.question: 3D printing creates?
questions.explanation: Physical objects layer by layer is the correct answer. Only PDF and Only email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virtual reality VR?
questions.explanation: Immersive simulated environment is the correct answer. Email feature and Word table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Word file extension?
questions.explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: LibreOffice Writer extension?
questions.explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Bold shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

questions.question: Italic shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Underline shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Save shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Page break shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge used for?
questions.explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge tab in Word?
questions.explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Justify alignment?
questions.explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Find shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Replace shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Excel extension?
questions.explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Calc is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Formula starts with?
questions.explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

questions.question: SUM() used for?
questions.explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

questions.question: AVERAGE() returns?
questions.explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MAX() returns?
questions.explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MIN() returns?
questions.explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: COUNT() counts?
questions.explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cell A1 is?
questions.explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Absolute reference?
questions.explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: IF function checks?
questions.explanation: Condition true/false is the main purpose or function described in the question. Text color and Print area are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Pie chart best for?
questions.explanation: Parts of whole — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: PowerPoint extension?
questions.explanation: .pptx is the correct answer. .docx and .xlsx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Impress is part of?
questions.explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: F5 starts slideshow from?
questions.explanation: F5 starts the PowerPoint/Impress slideshow from the first slide. Shift+F5 starts from the current slide.

questions.question: Shift+F5 from?
questions.explanation: Use Current slide for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: New slide shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+M inserts a new slide in PowerPoint. Use it quickly while building presentations.

questions.question: Transition applies to?
questions.explanation: Between slides — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Animation applies to?
questions.explanation: Objects on slide — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Title slide layout has?
questions.explanation: Title + subtitle placeholders is the correct answer. Blank only and Excel table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Theme changes?
questions.explanation: Colors fonts effects is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Widescreen slide ratio often?
questions.explanation: 16:9 is the correct answer. 4:3 only and 1:1 only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Duplicate slide?
questions.explanation: Right-click Duplicate is the correct answer. F5 and Delete are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Presenter View shows?
questions.explanation: Notes and timer is the correct answer. BIOS and Excel are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Internet is?
questions.explanation: The Internet is a global network connecting millions of computers worldwide to share data and services.

questions.question: WWW stands for?
questions.explanation: WWW (World Wide Web) is the collection of web pages and links accessed through browsers on the Internet.

questions.question: Browser example?
questions.explanation: Google Chrome — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: URL stands for?
questions.explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is the web address you type in the browser — e.g. https://www.google.com.

questions.question: ISP provides?
questions.explanation: Internet connection is the correct answer. Word license and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: HTTPS indicates?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts data between your browser and the website — look for the padlock when entering passwords or paying online.

questions.question: HTTP stands for?
questions.explanation: HTTP/HTTPS are protocols browsers use to load web pages — HTTPS adds encryption for security.

questions.question: Download means?
questions.explanation: Downloading copies a file from the Internet to your computer (e.g. saving a PDF or photo).

chapters.title: Mock Test 3 — 100 MCQs (90 min)
chapters.heading: Mock Test 3 — 100 MCQs (90 min)
chapters.contentHtml:
CCC Mock Test 3 (100 MCQs)

Time: 90 minutes · Pass: 50%

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Upload means? a) Send from PC to internet b) Download c) Shutdown d) Format

  2. Google is a? a) Search engine b) Operating system c) Word processor d) Printer

  3. Bookmark saves? a) Favorite website b) Deletes site c) Formats PC d) Installs OS

  4. Ctrl+T in browser? a) New tab b) Close tab c) Print d) Save page

  5. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail

  6. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only

  7. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home

  8. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy

  9. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy

  10. Reply sends to? a) Original sender b) Everyone BCC hidden c) Nobody d) Printer

  11. Forward sends? a) Copy to new recipient b) Deletes original c) Formats PC d) Installs OS

  12. Gmail provided by? a) Google b) Microsoft only c) Apple only d) Government only

  13. Outlook email by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Linux kernel

  14. Compose email needs? a) To, Subject, Body b) CPU serial only c) Printer model d) BIOS version

  15. Email etiquette includes? a) Clear subject line b) ALL CAPS shouting OK always c) Share passwords d) No greeting ever required wrong

  16. Cloud storage example? a) Google Drive b) CPU cache c) RAM stick d) Printer tray

  17. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index
