  18. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only

  19. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always

  20. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator

  21. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart

  22. QR code payment scans? a) Merchant QR to pay b) CPU barcode c) Printer test page d) BIOS screen

  23. OTP in banking means? a) One Time Password b) Open Transfer Protocol c) Online Text Payment d) Only Total Price

  24. Never share OTP with? a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent b) Your own memory c) Bank official phone asking wrong scam d) Secure app entry yourself OK

  25. IMPS is? a) Immediate payment service b) Internet mail print c) Image media file d) Internal memory

  26. NEFT is? a) Electronic fund transfer b) Network email file c) New excel format d) National education test

  27. RTGS for? a) Large value real-time transfer b) Email attachment c) Word mail merge d) PPT animation

  28. Debit card deducts from? a) Your bank account b) Credit loan always wrong for debit c) Email account d) Cloud drive

  29. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips

  30. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor

  31. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only

  32. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme

  33. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus

  34. Online PAN apply via? a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals b) Random WhatsApp c) Unknown email EXE d) Pop-up ad

  35. Passport application online? a) Passport Seva portal b) Facebook form c) Instagram DM d) Random APK

  36. Voter ID services online? a) NVSP portal b) Unknown blog c) Pirate site d) Spam email link

  37. Income tax e-filing? a) Official incometax.gov.in b) Random .com similar phishing caution c) Word doc macro d) Excel only offline always wrong

  38. GST registration online? a) gst.gov.in b) Unknown mirror site c) Telegram bot only wrong d) Email attachment exe

  39. EPFO member portal for? a) Provident fund services b) Games c) Music streaming d) PPT templates

  40. Ayushman Bharat digital mission? a) Health records ecosystem b) Word spell check c) Excel pivot only d) Printer driver

  41. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature

  42. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong

  43. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache

  44. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam

  45. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only

  46. OTP should be? a) Kept secret one-time use b) Shared on Facebook c) Emailed to stranger d) Posted public

  47. Firewall helps? a) Block unauthorized access b) Print documents c) Edit Word d) Make charts

  48. HTTPS for banking? a) Encrypted connection prefer b) Optional never matters wrong c) Slower so avoid wrong d) Replaces password wrong

  49. Ransomware? a) Encrypts data demands payment b) Free backup tool c) Word feature d) Excel sort

  50. Spyware? a) Secretly monitors user b) Official antivirus c) Browser bookmark d) Email signature

  51. Trojan horse? a) Disguised malicious program b) Greek history only no malware wrong c) Hardware GPU d) Printer font

  52. Worm spreads? a) Self across network b) Only USB always wrong only one way c) Never copies wrong d) Word table

  53. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink

  54. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol

  55. ChatGPT type tool is? a) Generative AI assistant b) Antivirus c) Spreadsheet d) Operating system

  56. IoT means? a) Internet of Things b) Internet of Text c) Input Output Tool d) Internal OS Test

  57. Cloud computing delivers? a) Services over internet b) Only rain weather joke c) Only local floppy d) Only printer

  58. Big data refers to? a) Large complex datasets b) Big monitor c) Big keyboard d) Big mouse

  59. Blockchain known for? a) Distributed ledger technology b) Word formatting c) Excel chart d) PPT animation

  60. Digital literacy means? a) Use technology effectively safely b) Avoid all tech wrong c) Only games wrong d) Only social media wrong narrow

  61. Coding teaches? a) Logical problem solving b) Only typing speed wrong only c) Only painting wrong d) Only printing wrong

  62. Python used for? a) Programming AI data scripts b) Word processing only wrong c) Printer driver only wrong d) BIOS only wrong

  63. 3D printing creates? a) Physical objects layer by layer b) Only PDF c) Only email d) Only music

  64. Virtual reality VR? a) Immersive simulated environment b) Email feature c) Word table d) Excel pivot only

  65. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit

  66. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer

  67. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD

  68. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register

  69. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker

  70. Output device? a) Printer b) Mouse c) Scanner d) Microphone

  71. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes

  72. Binary uses digits? a) 0 and 1 b) 0 to 9 c) 1 to 9 d) A to F

  73. Software example? a) MS Word b) CPU c) Monitor d) Keyboard

  74. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus

  75. Pen drive is? a) Storage device b) Input device c) Output device d) ALU

  76. Full form of RAM? a) Random Access Memory b) Read Access Memory c) Run All Memory d) Random Active Memory

  77. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus

  78. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver

  79. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email

  80. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z

  81. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P

  82. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+N d) Ctrl+O

  83. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email

  84. Win+L? a) Lock computer b) Log off always c) Open Word d) Print

  85. F2 key? a) Rename b) Refresh c) Save d) Print

  86. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network

  87. Shift+Delete? a) Permanent delete b) Recycle Bin c) Copy d) Rename

  88. Task Manager opens with? a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc b) F5 c) Alt+H d) Win+P

  89. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb

  90. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt

  91. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P

  92. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N

  93. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L

  94. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5

  95. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter

  96. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver

  97. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only

  98. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only

  99. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N

  100. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K

questions.question: Upload means?
questions.explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

questions.question: Google is a?
questions.explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

questions.question: Bookmark saves?
questions.explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Ctrl+T in browser?
questions.explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Email stands for?
questions.explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

questions.question: Email format?
questions.explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

questions.question: Attachment icon?
questions.explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: CC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: BCC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: Reply sends to?
questions.explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Forward sends?
questions.explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Gmail provided by?
questions.explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Outlook email by?
questions.explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Compose email needs?
questions.explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Email etiquette includes?
questions.explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cloud storage example?
questions.explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

questions.question: UPI stands for?
questions.explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

questions.question: BHIM app developed by?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

questions.question: Net banking allows?

options.text: Only phone call branch only always

questions.explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: Digital wallet example?
questions.explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

questions.question: AEPS uses?
questions.explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

questions.question: QR code payment scans?
questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.question: OTP in banking means?
questions.explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

questions.question: Never share OTP with?

options.text: Bank official phone asking wrong scam

questions.explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

questions.question: IMPS is?
questions.explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

questions.question: NEFT is?
questions.explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

questions.question: RTGS for?
questions.explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

questions.question: Debit card deducts from?

options.text: Credit loan always wrong for debit

questions.explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

questions.question: DigiLocker stores?
questions.explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

questions.question: UMANG app provides?
questions.explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

questions.question: e-Governance means?
questions.explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

questions.question: Aadhaar used for?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

questions.question: India.gov.in is?
questions.explanation: India.gov.in is the official national portal linking to government websites and services.

questions.question: Online PAN apply via?
questions.explanation: NSDL/UTIITSL portals is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp and Unknown email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Passport application online?
questions.explanation: Passport Seva portal is the correct answer. Facebook form and Instagram DM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Voter ID services online?
questions.explanation: NVSP portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Income tax e-filing?
questions.explanation: Official incometax.gov.in is the correct answer. Random .com similar phishing caution and Word doc macro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: GST registration online?
questions.explanation: gst.gov.in is the correct answer. Unknown mirror site and Telegram bot only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: EPFO member portal for?
questions.explanation: Provident fund services is the correct answer. Games and Music streaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ayushman Bharat digital mission?
questions.explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Malware means?
questions.explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

questions.question: Phishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Antivirus protects from?
questions.explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Strong password example style?
questions.explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

questions.question: OTP should be?
questions.explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Firewall helps?
questions.explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

questions.question: HTTPS for banking?
questions.explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ransomware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Spyware?
questions.explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Trojan horse?

options.text: Greek history only no malware wrong

questions.explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Worm spreads?

options.text: Only USB always wrong only one way

questions.explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: AI stands for?
questions.explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

questions.question: Machine learning is?
questions.explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

questions.question: ChatGPT type tool is?
questions.explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

questions.question: IoT means?
questions.explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

questions.question: Cloud computing delivers?
questions.explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

questions.question: Big data refers to?
questions.explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Blockchain known for?
questions.explanation: Distributed ledger technology is the correct answer. Word formatting and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Digital literacy means?
questions.explanation: Digital literacy means using computers, Internet, and mobile apps safely and effectively in daily life.

questions.question: Coding teaches?
questions.explanation: Logical problem solving is the correct answer. Only typing speed wrong only and Only painting wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Python used for?
questions.explanation: Python is a popular programming language used for AI, data analysis, automation, and web development.

questions.question: 3D printing creates?
questions.explanation: Physical objects layer by layer is the correct answer. Only PDF and Only email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virtual reality VR?
questions.explanation: Immersive simulated environment is the correct answer. Email feature and Word table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Word file extension?
questions.explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: LibreOffice Writer extension?
questions.explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Bold shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

questions.question: Italic shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Underline shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Save shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Page break shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge used for?
questions.explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge tab in Word?
questions.explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Justify alignment?
questions.explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Find shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Replace shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

chapters.title: Mock Test 3 — Answer Key
chapters.heading: Mock Test 3 — Answer Key
chapters.contentHtml:
Mock Test 3 — Answer Key

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Upload means? a) Send from PC to internet b) Download c) Shutdown d) Format Answer: a) Send from PC to internet Explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

  2. Google is a? a) Search engine b) Operating system c) Word processor d) Printer Answer: a) Search engine Explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

  3. Bookmark saves? a) Favorite website b) Deletes site c) Formats PC d) Installs OS Answer: a) Favorite website Explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  4. Ctrl+T in browser? a) New tab b) Close tab c) Print d) Save page Answer: a) New tab Explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

  5. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail Answer: a) Electronic Mail Explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

  6. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only Answer: a) user@domain.com Explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

  7. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home Answer: a) Paperclip Explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  8. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy Answer: a) Carbon copy visible Explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

  9. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy Answer: a) Blind carbon copy hidden Explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

  10. Reply sends to? a) Original sender b) Everyone BCC hidden c) Nobody d) Printer Answer: a) Original sender Explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  11. Forward sends? a) Copy to new recipient b) Deletes original c) Formats PC d) Installs OS Answer: a) Copy to new recipient Explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

  12. Gmail provided by? a) Google b) Microsoft only c) Apple only d) Government only Answer: a) Google Explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  13. Outlook email by? a) Microsoft b) Google only c) LibreOffice d) Linux kernel Answer: a) Microsoft Explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  14. Compose email needs? a) To, Subject, Body b) CPU serial only c) Printer model d) BIOS version Answer: a) To, Subject, Body Explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. Email etiquette includes? a) Clear subject line b) ALL CAPS shouting OK always c) Share passwords d) No greeting ever required wrong Answer: a) Clear subject line Explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Cloud storage example? a) Google Drive b) CPU cache c) RAM stick d) Printer tray Answer: a) Google Drive Explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

  17. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index Answer: a) Unified Payments Interface Explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

  18. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only Answer: a) NPCI / Government initiative Explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

  19. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always Answer: a) Banking via internet Explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

  20. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator Answer: a) Paytm, PhonePe Explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

  21. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart Answer: a) Aadhaar authentication for payments Explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

  22. QR code payment scans? a) Merchant QR to pay b) CPU barcode c) Printer test page d) BIOS screen Answer: a) Merchant QR to pay Explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

  23. OTP in banking means? a) One Time Password b) Open Transfer Protocol c) Online Text Payment d) Only Total Price Answer: a) One Time Password Explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

  24. Never share OTP with? a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent b) Your own memory c) Bank official phone asking wrong scam d) Secure app entry yourself OK Answer: a) Anyone claiming to be bank/agent Explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

  25. IMPS is? a) Immediate payment service b) Internet mail print c) Image media file d) Internal memory Answer: a) Immediate payment service Explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

  26. NEFT is? a) Electronic fund transfer b) Network email file c) New excel format d) National education test Answer: a) Electronic fund transfer Explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

  27. RTGS for? a) Large value real-time transfer b) Email attachment c) Word mail merge d) PPT animation Answer: a) Large value real-time transfer Explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

  28. Debit card deducts from? a) Your bank account b) Credit loan always wrong for debit c) Email account d) Cloud drive Answer: a) Your bank account Explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

  29. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips Answer: a) Digital certificates documents Explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

  30. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor Answer: a) Government services unified Explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

  31. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only Answer: a) Government services via ICT Explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

  32. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme Answer: a) Identity authentication Explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

  33. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus Answer: a) National portal Explanation: India.gov.in is the official national portal linking to government websites and services.

  34. Online PAN apply via? a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals b) Random WhatsApp c) Unknown email EXE d) Pop-up ad Answer: a) NSDL/UTIITSL portals Explanation: NSDL/UTIITSL portals is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp and Unknown email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  35. Passport application online? a) Passport Seva portal b) Facebook form c) Instagram DM d) Random APK Answer: a) Passport Seva portal Explanation: Passport Seva portal is the correct answer. Facebook form and Instagram DM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  36. Voter ID services online? a) NVSP portal b) Unknown blog c) Pirate site d) Spam email link Answer: a) NVSP portal Explanation: NVSP portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  37. Income tax e-filing? a) Official incometax.gov.in b) Random .com similar phishing caution c) Word doc macro d) Excel only offline always wrong Answer: a) Official incometax.gov.in Explanation: Official incometax.gov.in is the correct answer. Random .com similar phishing caution and Word doc macro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. GST registration online? a) gst.gov.in b) Unknown mirror site c) Telegram bot only wrong d) Email attachment exe Answer: a) gst.gov.in Explanation: gst.gov.in is the correct answer. Unknown mirror site and Telegram bot only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  39. EPFO member portal for? a) Provident fund services b) Games c) Music streaming d) PPT templates Answer: a) Provident fund services Explanation: Provident fund services is the correct answer. Games and Music streaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  40. Ayushman Bharat digital mission? a) Health records ecosystem b) Word spell check c) Excel pivot only d) Printer driver Answer: a) Health records ecosystem Explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature Answer: a) Malicious software Explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

  42. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong Answer: a) Malicious software general Explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

  43. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache Answer: a) Fraudulent data theft attempt Explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

  44. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam Answer: a) Malware Explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  45. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only Answer: a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars Explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

  46. OTP should be? a) Kept secret one-time use b) Shared on Facebook c) Emailed to stranger d) Posted public Answer: a) Kept secret one-time use Explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  47. Firewall helps? a) Block unauthorized access b) Print documents c) Edit Word d) Make charts Answer: a) Block unauthorized access Explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

  48. HTTPS for banking? a) Encrypted connection prefer b) Optional never matters wrong c) Slower so avoid wrong d) Replaces password wrong Answer: a) Encrypted connection prefer Explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  49. Ransomware? a) Encrypts data demands payment b) Free backup tool c) Word feature d) Excel sort Answer: a) Encrypts data demands payment Explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

  50. Spyware? a) Secretly monitors user b) Official antivirus c) Browser bookmark d) Email signature Answer: a) Secretly monitors user Explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  51. Trojan horse? a) Disguised malicious program b) Greek history only no malware wrong c) Hardware GPU d) Printer font Answer: a) Disguised malicious program Explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  52. Worm spreads? a) Self across network b) Only USB always wrong only one way c) Never copies wrong d) Word table Answer: a) Self across network Explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  53. AI stands for? a) Artificial Intelligence b) Automatic Internet c) Advanced Input d) Applied Ink Answer: a) Artificial Intelligence Explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

  54. Machine learning is? a) Computers learn from data b) Manual typing only c) Printer setting d) Email protocol Answer: a) Computers learn from data Explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

  55. ChatGPT type tool is? a) Generative AI assistant b) Antivirus c) Spreadsheet d) Operating system Answer: a) Generative AI assistant Explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

  56. IoT means? a) Internet of Things b) Internet of Text c) Input Output Tool d) Internal OS Test Answer: a) Internet of Things Explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

  57. Cloud computing delivers? a) Services over internet b) Only rain weather joke c) Only local floppy d) Only printer Answer: a) Services over internet Explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

  58. Big data refers to? a) Large complex datasets b) Big monitor c) Big keyboard d) Big mouse Answer: a) Large complex datasets Explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  59. Blockchain known for? a) Distributed ledger technology b) Word formatting c) Excel chart d) PPT animation Answer: a) Distributed ledger technology Explanation: Distributed ledger technology is the correct answer. Word formatting and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  60. Digital literacy means? a) Use technology effectively safely b) Avoid all tech wrong c) Only games wrong d) Only social media wrong narrow Answer: a) Use technology effectively safely Explanation: Digital literacy means using computers, Internet, and mobile apps safely and effectively in daily life.

  61. Coding teaches? a) Logical problem solving b) Only typing speed wrong only c) Only painting wrong d) Only printing wrong Answer: a) Logical problem solving Explanation: Logical problem solving is the correct answer. Only typing speed wrong only and Only painting wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  62. Python used for? a) Programming AI data scripts b) Word processing only wrong c) Printer driver only wrong d) BIOS only wrong Answer: a) Programming AI data scripts Explanation: Python is a popular programming language used for AI, data analysis, automation, and web development.

  63. 3D printing creates? a) Physical objects layer by layer b) Only PDF c) Only email d) Only music Answer: a) Physical objects layer by layer Explanation: Physical objects layer by layer is the correct answer. Only PDF and Only email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  64. Virtual reality VR? a) Immersive simulated environment b) Email feature c) Word table d) Excel pivot only Answer: a) Immersive simulated environment Explanation: Immersive simulated environment is the correct answer. Email feature and Word table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  65. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit Answer: a) Central Processing Unit Explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

  66. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer Answer: a) CPU Explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

  67. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD Answer: a) RAM Explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

  68. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register Answer: a) ROM Explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

  69. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker Answer: a) Keyboard Explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

  70. Output device? a) Printer b) Mouse c) Scanner d) Microphone Answer: a) Printer Explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

  71. 1 KB equals? a) 1024 bytes b) 1000 bytes c) 1024 bits d) 8 bytes Answer: a) 1024 bytes Explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

  72. Binary uses digits? a) 0 and 1 b) 0 to 9 c) 1 to 9 d) A to F Answer: a) 0 and 1 Explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

  73. Software example? a) MS Word b) CPU c) Monitor d) Keyboard Answer: a) MS Word Explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

  74. Hardware example? a) Keyboard b) Windows c) Chrome d) Antivirus Answer: a) Keyboard Explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

  75. Pen drive is? a) Storage device b) Input device c) Output device d) ALU Answer: a) Storage device Explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

  76. Full form of RAM? a) Random Access Memory b) Read Access Memory c) Run All Memory d) Random Active Memory Answer: a) Random Access Memory Explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

  77. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus Answer: a) Operating System Explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

  78. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver Answer: a) Open source OS Explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

  79. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email Answer: a) File management Explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

  80. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z Answer: a) Ctrl+C Explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

  81. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+V Explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

  82. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+N d) Ctrl+O Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

  83. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email Answer: a) File Explorer Explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

  84. Win+L? a) Lock computer b) Log off always c) Open Word d) Print Answer: a) Lock computer Explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

  85. F2 key? a) Rename b) Refresh c) Save d) Print Answer: a) Rename Explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

  86. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network Answer: a) Recycle Bin Explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

  87. Shift+Delete? a) Permanent delete b) Recycle Bin c) Copy d) Rename Answer: a) Permanent delete Explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

  88. Task Manager opens with? a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc b) F5 c) Alt+H d) Win+P Answer: a) Ctrl+Shift+Esc Explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

  89. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb Answer: a) .docx Explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  90. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt Answer: a) .odt Explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  91. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+B Explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

  92. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+I Explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  93. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L Answer: a) Ctrl+U Explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  94. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5 Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  95. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter Answer: a) Ctrl+Enter Explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  96. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver Answer: a) Bulk personalized letters Explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  97. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only Answer: a) Mailings Explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  98. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only Answer: a) Both edges straight Explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  99. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+F Explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  100. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K Answer: a) Ctrl+H Explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Upload means?
questions.explanation: Uploading sends a file from your computer to the Internet (e.g. attaching a photo to email or cloud).

questions.question: Google is a?
questions.explanation: Google and Bing are search engines — they find web pages matching your search words. They are not operating systems.

questions.question: Bookmark saves?
questions.explanation: Favorite website — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Ctrl+T in browser?
questions.explanation: New tab — this is how browsers and Internet connectivity work in everyday use.

questions.question: Email stands for?
questions.explanation: Email (Electronic Mail) sends messages over the Internet to addresses like user@domain.com.

questions.question: Email format?
questions.explanation: A valid email has a username, @ symbol, and domain — e.g. student@gmail.com.

questions.question: Attachment icon?
questions.explanation: Paperclip — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: CC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: BCC means?
questions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to others — all recipients can see who received it.

questions.question: Reply sends to?
questions.explanation: Original sender — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Forward sends?
questions.explanation: Copy to new recipient — this is the standard meaning or action in email and online communication for CCC.

questions.question: Gmail provided by?
questions.explanation: Google is the correct answer. Microsoft only and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Outlook email by?
questions.explanation: Microsoft is the correct answer. Google only and LibreOffice are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Compose email needs?
questions.explanation: To, Subject, Body is the correct answer. CPU serial only and Printer model are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Email etiquette includes?
questions.explanation: Clear subject line is the correct answer. ALL CAPS shouting OK always and Share passwords are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Cloud storage example?
questions.explanation: Cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox) saves files online so you can access them from any device.

questions.question: UPI stands for?
questions.explanation: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) lets you transfer money instantly between bank accounts using a mobile app and UPI PIN.

questions.question: BHIM app developed by?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI app launched with support from NPCI — the organisation that runs UPI payment infrastructure in India.

questions.question: Net banking allows?

options.text: Only phone call branch only always

questions.explanation: Net banking (online banking) lets you check balance, transfer money, and pay bills through your bank's official website or app — without visiting a branch.

questions.question: Digital wallet example?
questions.explanation: Paytm and PhonePe are digital wallets/UPI apps used for cashless payments, recharges, and bill pay from your phone.

questions.question: AEPS uses?
questions.explanation: AEPS (Aadhaar Enabled Payment System) uses Aadhaar-based authentication so people can do banking with fingerprint verification.

questions.question: QR code payment scans?
questions.explanation: Scan & Pay reads a merchant's QR code with your phone camera and pays via UPI — no cash or card swipe needed.

questions.question: OTP in banking means?
questions.explanation: OTP (One Time Password) is a temporary code sent to your phone for secure login or payment — never share it with anyone.

questions.question: Never share OTP with?

options.text: Bank official phone asking wrong scam

questions.explanation: Anyone claiming to be bank/agent — safe digital payment practice: use official apps, protect PIN/OTP, and verify before you pay.

questions.question: IMPS is?
questions.explanation: IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) transfers money between bank accounts instantly, 24×7.

questions.question: NEFT is?
questions.explanation: NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) moves money between bank accounts — used for regular bank transfers.

questions.question: RTGS for?
questions.explanation: RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) is for high-value bank transfers that settle in real time during banking hours.

questions.question: Debit card deducts from?

options.text: Credit loan always wrong for debit

questions.explanation: A debit card deducts money directly from your savings/current account when you pay or withdraw.

questions.question: DigiLocker stores?
questions.explanation: DigiLocker stores verified digital documents (marksheets, certificates) issued by government authorities online.

questions.question: UMANG app provides?
questions.explanation: UMANG app provides many government services in one place — PAN, EPFO, utility bills, and more.

questions.question: e-Governance means?
questions.explanation: E-governance uses technology to deliver government services online — certificates, tax, schemes — to citizens.

questions.question: Aadhaar used for?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is a 12-digit identity number used for verification (eKYC) in banking and government services.

questions.question: India.gov.in is?
questions.explanation: India.gov.in is the official national portal linking to government websites and services.

questions.question: Online PAN apply via?
questions.explanation: NSDL/UTIITSL portals is the correct answer. Random WhatsApp and Unknown email EXE are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Passport application online?
questions.explanation: Passport Seva portal is the correct answer. Facebook form and Instagram DM are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Voter ID services online?
questions.explanation: NVSP portal is the correct answer. Unknown blog and Pirate site are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Income tax e-filing?
questions.explanation: Official incometax.gov.in is the correct answer. Random .com similar phishing caution and Word doc macro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: GST registration online?
questions.explanation: gst.gov.in is the correct answer. Unknown mirror site and Telegram bot only wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: EPFO member portal for?
questions.explanation: Provident fund services is the correct answer. Games and Music streaming are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ayushman Bharat digital mission?
questions.explanation: Health records ecosystem is the correct answer. Word spell check and Excel pivot only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virus is?
questions.explanation: A computer virus is harmful software that can copy itself and damage files or the system. It is not hardware.

questions.question: Malware means?
questions.explanation: Malware (including viruses) is malicious software designed to harm, steal data, or take control of your device.

questions.question: Phishing is?
questions.explanation: Phishing is a fraud trick using fake emails, links, or messages to steal passwords, OTP, or bank details.

questions.question: Antivirus protects from?
questions.explanation: Malware is the correct answer. Low RAM only and Slow typing are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Strong password example style?
questions.explanation: A strong password mixes upper/lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols — avoid names, birthdays, or "123456".

questions.question: OTP should be?
questions.explanation: Kept secret one-time use is the correct answer. Shared on Facebook and Emailed to stranger are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Firewall helps?
questions.explanation: A firewall monitors network traffic and blocks suspicious connections to protect your computer or network.

questions.question: HTTPS for banking?
questions.explanation: Encrypted connection prefer is the correct answer. Optional never matters wrong and Slower so avoid wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ransomware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Spyware?
questions.explanation: Secretly monitors user is the correct answer. Official antivirus and Browser bookmark are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Trojan horse?

options.text: Greek history only no malware wrong

questions.explanation: Disguised malicious program is the correct answer. Greek history only no malware wrong and Hardware GPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Worm spreads?

options.text: Only USB always wrong only one way

questions.explanation: Self across network is the correct answer. Only USB always wrong only one way and Never copies wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: AI stands for?
questions.explanation: AI (Artificial Intelligence) means computer systems that can perform tasks needing human-like learning or reasoning.

questions.question: Machine learning is?
questions.explanation: Machine learning lets computers improve from data examples without being explicitly programmed for every rule.

questions.question: ChatGPT type tool is?
questions.explanation: Generative AI tools (like ChatGPT) create text, answers, or content based on your prompts — always verify important facts.

questions.question: IoT means?
questions.explanation: IoT (Internet of Things) connects everyday devices (sensors, cameras, appliances) to the Internet for smart control.

questions.question: Cloud computing delivers?
questions.explanation: Cloud computing delivers storage, software, and processing over the Internet instead of only on your local PC.

questions.question: Big data refers to?
questions.explanation: Large complex datasets is the correct answer. Big monitor and Big keyboard are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Blockchain known for?
questions.explanation: Distributed ledger technology is the correct answer. Word formatting and Excel chart are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Digital literacy means?
questions.explanation: Digital literacy means using computers, Internet, and mobile apps safely and effectively in daily life.

questions.question: Coding teaches?
questions.explanation: Logical problem solving is the correct answer. Only typing speed wrong only and Only painting wrong are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Python used for?
questions.explanation: Python is a popular programming language used for AI, data analysis, automation, and web development.

questions.question: 3D printing creates?
questions.explanation: Physical objects layer by layer is the correct answer. Only PDF and Only email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Virtual reality VR?
questions.explanation: Immersive simulated environment is the correct answer. Email feature and Word table are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Full form of CPU?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit — the main processor chip that runs programs and calculations.

questions.question: Brain of the computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the brain of the computer because it executes instructions and controls all processing. Monitor, keyboard, and printer are separate devices.

questions.question: Which memory is temporary?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is temporary (volatile) memory — it holds data only while the computer is on and clears when power is off. ROM, hard disk, and DVD keep data permanently.

questions.question: Which memory stores boot instructions?
questions.explanation: ROM (Read Only Memory) stores permanent boot instructions that start the computer. RAM is temporary; cache and registers are too small for full boot code.

questions.question: Input device?
questions.explanation: A keyboard sends data into the computer, so it is an input device. Monitor, printer, and speaker are output devices.

questions.question: Output device?
questions.explanation: A printer produces hard copy output on paper. Mouse, scanner, and microphone send data in — they are input devices.

questions.question: 1 KB equals?
questions.explanation: In computing, 1 KB (kilobyte) = 1024 bytes (2¹⁰), not 1000. This is the standard used in CCC and most operating systems.

questions.question: Binary uses digits?
questions.explanation: The binary number system uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Decimal uses 0–9; hexadecimal uses 0–9 and A–F.

questions.question: Software example?
questions.explanation: MS Word is application software — a program that helps users create documents. CPU, monitor, and keyboard are hardware.

questions.question: Hardware example?
questions.explanation: A keyboard is a physical hardware device you can touch. Windows, Chrome, and antivirus are software programs.

questions.question: Pen drive is?
questions.explanation: A pen drive (USB flash drive) is a portable storage device used to save and carry files. It is not an input or output device.

questions.question: Full form of RAM?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — fast temporary memory used while programs are running.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Word file extension?
questions.explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: LibreOffice Writer extension?
questions.explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Bold shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

questions.question: Italic shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Underline shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Save shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Page break shortcut?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge used for?
questions.explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Mail merge tab in Word?
questions.explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Justify alignment?
questions.explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

questions.question: Find shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Replace shortcut?
questions.explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

chapters.title: 50 Most Important MCQs
chapters.heading: 50 Most Important MCQs
chapters.contentHtml:
50 Most Important CCC MCQs

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Full form of CPU? a) Central Processing Unit b) Computer Personal Unit c) Control Processing Unit d) Central Process Unit

  2. Brain of the computer? a) CPU b) Monitor c) Keyboard d) Printer

  3. Which memory is temporary? a) RAM b) ROM c) Hard disk d) DVD

  4. Which memory stores boot instructions? a) ROM b) RAM c) Cache d) Register

  5. Input device? a) Keyboard b) Monitor c) Printer d) Speaker

  6. Windows is a? a) Operating System b) Browser c) Spreadsheet d) Antivirus

  7. Linux is? a) Open source OS b) Closed browser c) Word processor d) Printer driver

  8. OS function includes? a) File management b) Only gaming c) Only printing d) Only email

  9. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z

  10. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+P

  11. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb

  12. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt

  13. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P

  14. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N

  15. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L

  16. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

  17. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word

  18. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @

  19. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print

  20. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join

  21. PowerPoint extension? a) .pptx b) .docx c) .xlsx d) .odt

  22. Impress is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Excel

  23. F5 starts slideshow from? a) Beginning b) Current slide only always c) End d) Print

  24. Shift+F5 from? a) Current slide b) Beginning always c) Last slide d) Print preview

  25. New slide shortcut? a) Ctrl+M b) Ctrl+N doc c) Ctrl+P d) F7

  26. Internet is? a) Global network of computers b) One PC c) Printer d) Keyboard

  27. WWW stands for? a) World Wide Web b) World Web Wide c) Wide World Web d) Web World Wide

  28. Browser example? a) Google Chrome b) MS Word c) Excel d) Access

  29. URL stands for? a) Uniform Resource Locator b) Universal Reference Link c) Unified Resource Line d) User Resource Locator

  30. ISP provides? a) Internet connection b) Word license c) Printer d) CPU

  31. Email stands for? a) Electronic Mail b) Electric Mail c) Easy Mail d) External Mail

  32. Email format? a) user@domain.com b) user.domain c) http://user d) @only

  33. Attachment icon? a) Paperclip b) Star c) Trash d) Home

  34. CC means? a) Carbon copy visible b) Hidden copy c) Delete copy d) Print copy

  35. BCC means? a) Blind carbon copy hidden b) Big copy center c) Browser copy d) Backup cloud copy

  36. UPI stands for? a) Unified Payments Interface b) Universal Payment Internet c) Unified Personal ID d) User Payment Index

  37. BHIM app developed by? a) NPCI / Government initiative b) Microsoft c) Google only d) Apple only

  38. Net banking allows? a) Banking via internet b) Only ATM cash c) Only postal mail d) Only phone call branch only always

  39. Digital wallet example? a) Paytm, PhonePe b) Notepad c) Paint d) Calculator

  40. AEPS uses? a) Aadhaar authentication for payments b) Email password only c) Word doc d) Excel chart

  41. DigiLocker stores? a) Digital certificates documents b) Physical cash c) Printer ink d) CPU chips

  42. UMANG app provides? a) Government services unified b) Games only c) Movies only d) Word processor

  43. e-Governance means? a) Government services via ICT b) Email only c) Games policy d) Private social media only

  44. Aadhaar used for? a) Identity authentication b) Word processing c) Spreadsheet d) Presentation theme

  45. India.gov.in is? a) National portal b) Private shop c) Game site d) Antivirus

  46. Virus is? a) Malicious software b) Hardware part c) Input device d) Browser feature

  47. Malware means? a) Malicious software general b) Helpful tool c) OS update d) Printer driver always safe wrong

  48. Phishing is? a) Fraudulent data theft attempt b) Fishing hobby only c) Antivirus brand d) Browser cache

  49. Antivirus protects from? a) Malware b) Low RAM only c) Slow typing d) Printer jam

  50. Strong password example style? a) Nita@2026#AI mixed chars b) 123456 c) password d) yourname only

questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.
