F. Viva Questions (10)

  1. What is an operating system?
  2. What is the difference between GUI and CLI?
  3. Why is Linux called open source?
  4. What is the use of Task Manager?
  5. What is the use of Recycle Bin?
  6. Why should software be installed from official websites?
  7. What is a device driver?
  8. What is the difference between copy and move?
  9. What is the use of Win + L?
  10. Why are backups important?

G. Case Studies (5)

  1. A student deletes an assignment by mistake. Explain how to restore it and how to avoid future loss.
  2. A computer is slow after startup. Explain how Task Manager can help identify the problem.
  3. An employee cannot install software using a standard account. Explain why and what should be done.
  4. A user downloads cracked software and the computer starts showing unwanted popups. Identify the risk and prevention steps.
  5. A school wants to use old computers for basic learning. Explain why Ubuntu or Linux Mint may be useful.

Answer Keys

MCQ Answers

1-b | 2-b | 3-a | 4-b | 5-b | 6-a | 7-a | 8-a | 9-b | 10-a | 11-b | 12-b | 13-a | 14-a | 15-a | 16-a | 17-a | 18-a | 19-a | 20-a | 21-a | 22-a | 23-a | 24-a | 25-a

True/False Answers

1-True | 2-False | 3-True | 4-True | 5-False | 6-True | 7-False | 8-True | 9-True | 10-True | 11-True | 12-False | 13-True | 14-True | 15-False

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. Operating System
  2. Graphical User Interface
  3. Command Line Interface
  4. Linux
  5. Win + E
  6. Win + L
  7. Task Manager
  8. Recycle Bin
  9. .pdf
  10. network/IP configuration
  11. device driver
  12. Administrator
  13. zip
  14. malware
  15. backup

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-d | 3-a | 4-c | 5-e | 6-f | 7-g | 8-h | 9-i | 10-j

Case Study Model Answers

  1. The student should check Recycle Bin, select the file, and click Restore. Future loss can be avoided by keeping backups and using clear file names.
  2. Open Task Manager with Ctrl + Shift + Esc. Check CPU, Memory, Disk, and Startup apps. End only the frozen app if needed.
  3. Installation often needs Administrator permission because it changes system files. The employee should contact IT/admin and install only approved software.
  4. Cracked software may contain malware, adware, spyware, or ransomware. Prevention: uninstall suspicious apps, scan with antivirus, update Windows, and use official sources.
  5. Ubuntu or Linux Mint can run well on many older computers, are free/open source, include useful apps, and are suitable for basic learning and internet use.

Final Self-Check

  • I can define an operating system.
  • I can explain OS functions with examples.
  • I can compare Windows and Linux.
  • I can explain Linux and Ubuntu.
  • I can use File Explorer for file operations.
  • I can use common keyboard shortcuts.
  • I can install and uninstall software safely.
  • I can use Notepad, Calculator, Recycle Bin, Snipping Tool, Command Prompt, PowerShell, and Task Manager.
  • I can explain malware, updates, backups, and cyber hygiene.
  • I can answer OS viva and practical questions confidently.

chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 02: Operating System

Section A — MCQ (80 questions)

  1. Windows is a? a) Antivirus b) Operating System c) Browser d) Spreadsheet

  2. Linux is? a) Word processor b) Printer driver c) Open source OS d) Closed browser

  3. OS function includes? a) Only gaming b) Only printing c) Only email d) File management

  4. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z

  5. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+V d) Ctrl+C

  6. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+P b) Ctrl+N c) Ctrl+O d) Ctrl+S

  7. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email

  8. Win+L? a) Print b) Lock computer c) Log off always d) Open Word

  9. F2 key? a) Refresh b) Save c) Print d) Rename

  10. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network

  11. Shift+Delete? a) Rename b) Permanent delete c) Recycle Bin d) Copy

  12. Task Manager opens with? a) Alt+H b) Win+P c) Ctrl+Shift+Esc d) F5

  13. Default drive letter for Windows? a) C: b) A: c) Z: d) M:

  14. Folder contains? a) Nothing b) Files and subfolders c) Only CPU d) Only RAM

  15. Ctrl+X is? a) Paste b) Undo c) Cut d) Copy

  16. Ctrl+Z is? a) Redo b) Save c) Print d) Undo

  17. Alt+Tab? a) Save b) Switch windows c) Close all d) Shutdown

  18. Zip folder purpose? a) Print files b) Email only c) Compress files d) Delete files

  19. Desktop wallpaper changed via? a) BIOS only b) Notepad c) Calculator d) Personalize/Settings

  20. Uninstall program via? a) Settings → Apps b) Delete .exe only c) Recycle Bin d) Paint

  21. GUI OS uses? a) No monitor b) Telegraph c) Icons and mouse d) Only commands

  22. File path shows? a) CPU speed b) RAM size c) Printer ink d) Location of file

  23. Invalid filename character? a) * b) _ c) - d) 0

  24. Snipping Tool for? a) Database b) Screenshot c) Virus scan d) Spreadsheet

  25. Administrator account has? a) Full system control b) No install rights c) Guest only d) No login

  26. Linux mascot/tradition often? a) None b) Penguin Tux c) Apple only d) Windows logo

  27. Install program on Windows? a) Rename file b) F5 c) Run installer / Store / setup.exe d) Delete folder only

  28. Remove program properly? a) Delete shortcut only b) Hide icon c) Lock PC d) Uninstall from Settings

  29. System tray shows? a) Hard disk b) Clock, network, volume c) CPU chip d) Keyboard keys

  30. Start menu used to? a) Destroy data b) Remove OS c) Launch programs d) Format disk only

  31. Ctrl+A? a) Save all b) Print all c) Delete all d) Select all

  32. Copy vs Move: Copy? a) Original remains b) Original deleted c) Both deleted d) Neither exists

  33. Restore deleted file from? a) Keyboard b) Recycle Bin c) CPU d) Monitor

  34. Hibernate saves session to? a) Cloud only b) Printer c) Disk then low power d) RAM only always

  35. Windows Update purpose? a) Delete files b) Remove OS c) Slow internet d) Security patches

  36. Pen drive appears as drive letter when? a) Plugged in USB b) Always C: c) Never d) Only on Linux

  37. Search in File Explorer finds? a) Only emails b) BIOS c) Files in folder/search scope d) Only internet

  38. Notepad creates? a) Excel sheets b) Database c) Video d) Plain text files

  39. Control Panel / Settings for? a) Cable only b) System configuration c) Games only d) Hardware repair physical

  40. Shortcut to desktop? a) Format C: b) Uninstall OS c) Send to Desktop shortcut d) Delete file

  41. Safe eject USB why? a) Speed up CPU b) Add RAM c) Install OS d) Prevent data corruption

  42. Linux distributions include? a) Ubuntu, Fedora b) Word, Excel only c) Chrome only d) None

  43. Open source means? a) No GUI b) Source code can be studied/modified c) Always free hardware d) No internet

  44. Taskbar location usually? a) On printer b) In BIOS c) Bottom of screen d) Inside CPU

  45. File extension identifies? a) CPU model b) RAM size c) User name d) File type

  46. Win+D shows? a) Desktop b) Delete files c) Download d) Database

  47. Increase list indent in Explorer sort? a) Email b) Sort by name/date/size c) Print d) Scan

  48. Lock PC when away using? a) F5 b) Ctrl+P c) Win+L d) Delete

  49. Guest account typically? a) Full admin b) No login ever c) Root on Linux always d) Limited permissions

  50. Antivirus on Windows often? a) Windows Defender built-in b) Not available c) Part of CPU d) Part of mouse

  51. Main purpose of an operating system is to? a) Increase monitor size b) Manage hardware and provide user interface c) Only play videos d) Replace application software

  52. Ubuntu is? a) A virus b) A spreadsheet c) A Linux distribution d) A printer driver only

  53. GUI stands for? a) General User Internet b) Global Utility Input c) Graphical Unit Instruction d) Graphical User Interface

  54. CLI uses? a) Typed commands b) Only touch gestures c) Only pictures d) Printer output

  55. Which OS is commonly used on Apple computers? a) DOS only b) macOS c) Ubuntu only d) Android

  56. Android is mainly used on? a) Speakers only b) Projectors only c) Smartphones and tablets d) Printers only

  57. Which is a mobile operating system? a) Notepad b) Calculator c) PowerPoint d) iOS

  58. Device drivers help the OS communicate with? a) Hardware devices b) Wallpapers only c) Documents only d) Fonts only

  59. A process is? a) A printer ink level b) A running program c) A folder name d) A keyboard key

  60. Memory management allocates? a) Monitor to files b) Printer to BIOS c) RAM to programs d) CPU to folders

  61. Win+I opens? a) Internet Explorer only b) Task Manager c) Recycle Bin d) Settings

  62. Alt+F4 does? a) Close active window b) Open File Explorer c) Lock computer d) Rename file

  63. Win+Shift+S is used for? a) Sort files b) Screenshot selection c) Save file d) Shutdown

  64. Ctrl+Y is? a) Cut b) Paste c) Redo d) Undo

  65. Deleting a desktop shortcut usually? a) Uninstalls the program b) Formats the drive c) Removes Windows d) Does not delete the original program

  66. Microsoft Windows is? a) Closed source / proprietary b) Open source kernel c) A Linux distro d) A mobile app only

  67. Linux kernel is? a) A file extension b) Core part of the operating system c) A spreadsheet app d) A printer driver only

  68. Ransomware? a) Creates backups b) Updates Windows c) Encrypts files and demands payment d) Speeds up the PC

  69. Trojan malware? a) Is built into Windows b) Only affects printers c) Is a keyboard shortcut d) Pretends to be useful software

  70. A backup is? a) Extra copy of data b) Deleted virus only c) Shortcut icon only d) Temporary wallpaper

  71. Standard user account is generally? a) Required to delete Windows folder b) Safer for daily work c) More powerful than administrator d) Used to replace OS

  72. MS-DOS mainly used? a) Mobile apps only b) Cloud storage c) Command line interface d) Touchscreen gestures

  73. Batch processing systems? a) Use only smartphones b) Have no OS c) Replace file management d) Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction

  74. Command dir in Command Prompt? a) Lists files and folders b) Deletes all files c) Installs software d) Opens Settings

  75. Command ipconfig shows? a) File extensions b) Network configuration c) CPU temperature d) Printer status

  76. File extension .exe usually means? a) Image file b) Spreadsheet c) Windows installer/program d) Plain text file

  77. Hidden files in Windows are? a) Always viruses b) Deleted automatically c) Only on Linux d) Not shown by default to prevent accidental changes

  78. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) adds? a) Extra verification step after password b) A second monitor c) More RAM d) Automatic file delete

  79. Deleting a program folder manually is? a) Required for updates b) Not the same as proper uninstall c) The recommended method d) Same as Settings uninstall

  80. Linux is popular on servers because it is? a) A mobile-only OS b) Stable, secure, and customizable c) Only for games d) Not connected to networks

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. What is an operating system?
  2. Write any five functions of an operating system.
  3. Differentiate between GUI and CLI with examples.
  4. Differentiate between Windows and Linux.
  5. What is Ubuntu?
  6. What is a device driver?
  7. What is the difference between copy and move?
  8. What happens when you press Shift + Delete?
  9. Why should software be installed from official websites?
  10. Explain why Windows updates are important.
  11. What is Task Manager used for?
  12. What is the difference between administrator and standard user?
  13. What is open-source software? Give two examples.
  14. Write five useful keyboard shortcuts for Windows.
  15. Why is backup important for files and folders?

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Open File Explorer with Win+E; identify Navigation Pane, Address Bar, and Search Box.
  2. Create folder structure Documents\CCC\Unit2\Notes and save a Notepad file.
  3. Rename a file with F2; copy and move files using Ctrl+C/X and Ctrl+V.
  4. Delete a test file and restore it from Recycle Bin.
  5. Take a screenshot with Snipping Tool and save as PNG.
  6. Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc) and note CPU and memory usage.
  7. Create a compressed (zip) folder and compare file size.
  8. Open Settings (Win+I) and check Windows Update status.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Default drive letter for Windows?
questions.explanation: Windows is usually installed on drive C:. Drive A: was for floppy disks; other letters are for extra partitions or USB drives.

questions.question: Folder contains?
questions.explanation: Files and subfolders is the correct answer. Nothing and Only CPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ctrl+X is?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+X cuts — removes the selection and places it on the clipboard to paste elsewhere.

questions.question: Ctrl+Z is?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Z undoes your last action. Very useful if you make a mistake while typing or editing.

questions.question: Alt+Tab?
questions.explanation: Alt+Tab switches between open application windows quickly without using the mouse.

questions.question: Zip folder purpose?
questions.explanation: Compress files is the main purpose or function described in the question. Print files and Email only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Desktop wallpaper changed via?
questions.explanation: Personalize/Settings is the correct answer. BIOS only and Notepad are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Uninstall program via?
questions.explanation: Use Settings → Apps → Uninstall to remove a program properly. Deleting only the desktop shortcut does not uninstall it.

questions.question: GUI OS uses?
questions.explanation: Icons and mouse is the correct answer. No monitor and Telegraph are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: File path shows?
questions.explanation: Location of file is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM size are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Invalid filename character?
questions.explanation: * is the correct answer. _ and - are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Snipping Tool for?
questions.explanation: Screenshot is the correct answer. Database and Virus scan are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Administrator account has?
questions.explanation: Full system control is the correct answer. No install rights and Guest only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Linux mascot/tradition often?
questions.explanation: Penguin Tux is the correct answer. None and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Install program on Windows?
questions.explanation: Run installer / Store / setup.exe is the correct answer. Rename file and F5 are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Remove program properly?
questions.explanation: Uninstall from Settings is the correct answer. Delete shortcut only and Hide icon are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: System tray shows?
questions.explanation: Clock, network, volume is the correct answer. Hard disk and CPU chip are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Start menu used to?
questions.explanation: Launch programs is the main purpose or function described in the question. Destroy data and Remove OS are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Ctrl+A?
questions.explanation: Use Select all for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Copy vs Move: Copy?
questions.explanation: Original remains is the correct answer. Original deleted and Both deleted are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Restore deleted file from?
questions.explanation: Deleted files go to Recycle Bin first. Open it and choose Restore to get the file back to its original place.

questions.question: Hibernate saves session to?
questions.explanation: Disk then low power is the correct answer. Cloud only and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Windows Update purpose?
questions.explanation: Security patches is the main purpose or function described in the question. Delete files and Remove OS are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Pen drive appears as drive letter when?
questions.explanation: Plugged in USB is the correct answer. Always C: and Never are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Search in File Explorer finds?
questions.explanation: Files in folder/search scope is the correct answer. Only emails and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Notepad creates?
questions.explanation: Plain text files is the correct answer. Excel sheets and Database are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Control Panel / Settings for?

questions.explanation: Use Send to Desktop shortcut for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Safe eject USB why?
questions.explanation: Safely ejecting USB (Eject option) finishes writing data before you unplug — prevents file corruption.

questions.question: Linux distributions include?
questions.explanation: Ubuntu, Fedora is the correct answer. Word, Excel only and Chrome only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Open source means?
questions.explanation: Open source means the program's source code is publicly available to study, modify, and share (e.g. Linux, LibreOffice).

questions.question: Taskbar location usually?
questions.explanation: Bottom of screen is the correct answer. On printer and In BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: File extension identifies?
questions.explanation: File type is the correct answer. CPU model and RAM size are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Win+D shows?
questions.explanation: Use Desktop for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Increase list indent in Explorer sort?
questions.explanation: Sort by name/date/size is the correct answer. Email and Print are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Lock PC when away using?
questions.explanation: Win+L is the correct answer. F5 and Ctrl+P are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Guest account typically?
questions.explanation: Limited permissions is the correct answer. Full admin and No login ever are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Antivirus on Windows often?
questions.explanation: Windows Defender built-in is the correct answer. Not available and Part of CPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.explanation: Manage hardware and provide user interface is the main purpose or function described in the question. Increase monitor size and Only play videos are not the right choices here.

questions.question: CLI uses?

questions.explanation: macOS is correct for this question. DOS only and Ubuntu only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Device drivers help the OS communicate with?
questions.explanation: Hardware devices is the correct answer. Wallpapers only and Documents only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: A process is?
questions.explanation: A running program is the correct answer. A printer ink level and A folder name are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Memory management allocates?
questions.explanation: RAM to programs is the correct answer. Monitor to files and Printer to BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Win+I opens?
questions.explanation: Use Settings for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Alt+F4 does?
questions.explanation: Use Close active window for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Win+Shift+S is used for?

options.text: Core part of the operating system

questions.explanation: Core part of the operating system is the correct answer. A file extension and A spreadsheet app are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Trojan malware?
questions.explanation: Pretends to be useful software is the correct answer. Is built into Windows and Only affects printers are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

options.text: Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction

questions.explanation: Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction is the correct answer. Use only smartphones and Have no OS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Command `dir` in Command Prompt?
questions.explanation: Lists files and folders is the correct answer. Deletes all files and Installs software are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.explanation: Network configuration is the correct answer. File extensions and CPU temperature are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

chapters.description: NITA CCA · Foundation modules (shared study pack)
chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 02: Operating System

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Windows is a? a) Antivirus b) Operating System c) Browser d) Spreadsheet Answer: b) Operating System Explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system that manages hardware and runs applications.

  2. Linux is? a) Word processor b) Printer driver c) Open source OS d) Closed browser Answer: c) Open source OS Explanation: Linux is open source — its source code can be studied and modified under license.

  3. OS function includes? a) Only gaming b) Only printing c) Only email d) File management Answer: d) File management Explanation: The OS organizes files and folders on storage devices.

  4. Shortcut Copy? a) Ctrl+C b) Ctrl+V c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+Z Answer: a) Ctrl+C Explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected items to the clipboard.

  5. Shortcut Paste? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+V d) Ctrl+C Answer: c) Ctrl+V Explanation: Ctrl+V pastes copied or cut items.

  6. Shortcut Save? a) Ctrl+P b) Ctrl+N c) Ctrl+O d) Ctrl+S Answer: d) Ctrl+S Explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file in most applications.

  7. Win+E opens? a) File Explorer b) Excel c) Edge only d) Email Answer: a) File Explorer Explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer for browsing files and folders.

  8. Win+L? a) Print b) Lock computer c) Log off always d) Open Word Answer: b) Lock computer Explanation: Win+L locks the computer — use it when stepping away.

  9. F2 key? a) Refresh b) Save c) Print d) Rename Answer: d) Rename Explanation: F2 renames the selected file or folder.

  10. Delete sends file to? a) Recycle Bin b) Permanent delete always c) Desktop d) Network Answer: a) Recycle Bin Explanation: The Delete key sends files to Recycle Bin for possible recovery.

  11. Shift+Delete? a) Rename b) Permanent delete c) Recycle Bin d) Copy Answer: b) Permanent delete Explanation: Shift+Delete bypasses Recycle Bin and deletes permanently.

  12. Task Manager opens with? a) Alt+H b) Win+P c) Ctrl+Shift+Esc d) F5 Answer: c) Ctrl+Shift+Esc Explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager directly.

  13. Default drive letter for Windows? a) C: b) A: c) Z: d) M: Answer: a) C: Explanation: Windows is usually installed on drive C:.

  14. Folder contains? a) Nothing b) Files and subfolders c) Only CPU d) Only RAM Answer: b) Files and subfolders Explanation: Folders organize files and can contain subfolders.

  15. Ctrl+X is? a) Paste b) Undo c) Cut d) Copy Answer: c) Cut Explanation: Ctrl+X cuts — the item is moved when pasted.

  16. Ctrl+Z is? a) Redo b) Save c) Print d) Undo Answer: d) Undo Explanation: Ctrl+Z undoes the last action in supported apps.

  17. Alt+Tab? a) Save b) Switch windows c) Close all d) Shutdown Answer: b) Switch windows Explanation: Alt+Tab switches between open applications.

  18. Zip folder purpose? a) Print files b) Email only c) Compress files d) Delete files Answer: c) Compress files Explanation: Zip folders reduce size or bundle multiple files together.

  19. Desktop wallpaper changed via? a) BIOS only b) Notepad c) Calculator d) Personalize/Settings Answer: d) Personalize/Settings Explanation: Wallpaper and themes are changed via Personalize or Settings.

  20. Uninstall program via? a) Settings → Apps b) Delete .exe only c) Recycle Bin d) Paint Answer: a) Settings → Apps Explanation: Uninstall programs properly from Settings → Apps → Installed apps.

  21. GUI OS uses? a) No monitor b) Telegraph c) Icons and mouse d) Only commands Answer: c) Icons and mouse Explanation: GUI uses graphical elements like icons, windows, and a mouse pointer.

  22. File path shows? a) CPU speed b) RAM size c) Printer ink d) Location of file Answer: d) Location of file Explanation: A file path shows where a file is stored on a drive.

  23. Invalid filename character? a) * b) _ c) - d) 0 **Answer: a) *** Explanation: Characters like * ? \ / : " < > | cannot be used in Windows file names.

  24. Snipping Tool for? a) Database b) Screenshot c) Virus scan d) Spreadsheet Answer: b) Screenshot Explanation: Snipping Tool captures screenshots for tutorials or proof.

  25. Administrator account has? a) Full system control b) No install rights c) Guest only d) No login Answer: a) Full system control Explanation: Administrator accounts can install software and change system settings.

  26. Linux mascot/tradition often? a) None b) Penguin Tux c) Apple only d) Windows logo Answer: b) Penguin Tux Explanation: Tux the penguin is the well-known mascot of Linux.

  27. Install program on Windows? a) Rename file b) F5 c) Run installer / Store / setup.exe d) Delete folder only Answer: c) Run installer / Store / setup.exe Explanation: Install software using official installers, Microsoft Store, or setup.exe.

  28. Remove program properly? a) Delete shortcut only b) Hide icon c) Lock PC d) Uninstall from Settings Answer: d) Uninstall from Settings Explanation: Proper removal uses Settings → Apps, not just deleting shortcuts.

  29. System tray shows? a) Hard disk b) Clock, network, volume c) CPU chip d) Keyboard keys Answer: b) Clock, network, volume Explanation: The system tray shows time, network, volume, battery, and background icons.

  30. Start menu used to? a) Destroy data b) Remove OS c) Launch programs d) Format disk only Answer: c) Launch programs Explanation: The Start menu launches apps, search, power options, and settings.

  31. Ctrl+A? a) Save all b) Print all c) Delete all d) Select all Answer: d) Select all Explanation: Ctrl+A selects all items in the current window or document.

  32. Copy vs Move: Copy? a) Original remains b) Original deleted c) Both deleted d) Neither exists Answer: a) Original remains Explanation: Copy (Ctrl+C/V) keeps the original; move (Ctrl+X/V) removes it.

  33. Restore deleted file from? a) Keyboard b) Recycle Bin c) CPU d) Monitor Answer: b) Recycle Bin Explanation: The Delete key sends files to Recycle Bin for possible recovery.

  34. Hibernate saves session to? a) Cloud only b) Printer c) Disk then low power d) RAM only always Answer: c) Disk then low power Explanation: Hibernate saves the session to disk and uses very low power.

  35. Windows Update purpose? a) Delete files b) Remove OS c) Slow internet d) Security patches Answer: d) Security patches Explanation: Windows Update delivers security fixes, stability improvements, and features.

  36. Pen drive appears as drive letter when? a) Plugged in USB b) Always C: c) Never d) Only on Linux Answer: a) Plugged in USB Explanation: USB drives appear as removable drive letters when connected.

  37. Search in File Explorer finds? a) Only emails b) BIOS c) Files in folder/search scope d) Only internet Answer: c) Files in folder/search scope Explanation: File Explorer search finds files within the selected folder or scope.

  38. Notepad creates? a) Excel sheets b) Database c) Video d) Plain text files Answer: d) Plain text files Explanation: Notepad creates simple .txt plain text files without formatting.

  39. Control Panel / Settings for? a) Cable only b) System configuration c) Games only d) Hardware repair physical Answer: b) System configuration Explanation: Settings and Control Panel configure display, network, apps, and accounts.

  40. Shortcut to desktop? a) Format C: b) Uninstall OS c) Send to Desktop shortcut d) Delete file Answer: c) Send to Desktop shortcut Explanation: Right-click → Send to → Desktop (create shortcut) makes a shortcut icon.

  41. Safe eject USB why? a) Speed up CPU b) Add RAM c) Install OS d) Prevent data corruption Answer: d) Prevent data corruption Explanation: Safely eject USB so files finish writing before removal.

  42. Linux distributions include? a) Ubuntu, Fedora b) Word, Excel only c) Chrome only d) None Answer: a) Ubuntu, Fedora Explanation: Ubuntu and Fedora are popular Linux distributions.

  43. Open source means? a) No GUI b) Source code can be studied/modified c) Always free hardware d) No internet Answer: b) Source code can be studied/modified Explanation: Open source allows viewing and modifying source code under license.

  44. Taskbar location usually? a) On printer b) In BIOS c) Bottom of screen d) Inside CPU Answer: c) Bottom of screen Explanation: The taskbar is usually at the bottom of the Windows desktop.

  45. File extension identifies? a) CPU model b) RAM size c) User name d) File type Answer: d) File type Explanation: Extensions like .txt, .pdf, and .exe identify file types.

  46. Win+D shows? a) Desktop b) Delete files c) Download d) Database Answer: a) Desktop Explanation: Win+D minimizes windows and shows the desktop.

  47. Increase list indent in Explorer sort? a) Email b) Sort by name/date/size c) Print d) Scan Answer: b) Sort by name/date/size Explanation: File Explorer can sort files by name, date modified, type, or size.

  48. Lock PC when away using? a) F5 b) Ctrl+P c) Win+L d) Delete Answer: c) Win+L Explanation: Win+L — important CCC exam point from Module 2 notes.

  49. Guest account typically? a) Full admin b) No login ever c) Root on Linux always d) Limited permissions Answer: d) Limited permissions Explanation: Guest accounts have restricted access compared to administrators.

  50. Antivirus on Windows often? a) Windows Defender built-in b) Not available c) Part of CPU d) Part of mouse Answer: a) Windows Defender built-in Explanation: Windows includes Microsoft Defender antivirus in Windows Security.

  51. Main purpose of an operating system is to? a) Increase monitor size b) Manage hardware and provide user interface c) Only play videos d) Replace application software Answer: b) Manage hardware and provide user interface Explanation: The OS manages resources and provides GUI/CLI for users.

  52. Ubuntu is? a) A virus b) A spreadsheet c) A Linux distribution d) A printer driver only Answer: c) A Linux distribution Explanation: Ubuntu bundles the Linux kernel with apps, desktop, and package manager.

  53. GUI stands for? a) General User Internet b) Global Utility Input c) Graphical Unit Instruction d) Graphical User Interface Answer: d) Graphical User Interface Explanation: GUI = Graphical User Interface — icons, menus, and windows.

  54. CLI uses? a) Typed commands b) Only touch gestures c) Only pictures d) Printer output Answer: a) Typed commands Explanation: CLI (Command Line Interface) uses typed commands like dir and cd.

  55. Which OS is commonly used on Apple computers? a) DOS only b) macOS c) Ubuntu only d) Android Answer: b) macOS Explanation: macOS is Apple's operating system for Mac computers.

  56. Android is mainly used on? a) Speakers only b) Projectors only c) Smartphones and tablets d) Printers only Answer: c) Smartphones and tablets Explanation: Android powers most smartphones and many tablets.

  57. Which is a mobile operating system? a) Notepad b) Calculator c) PowerPoint d) iOS Answer: d) iOS Explanation: iOS is Apple's mobile operating system for iPhone and iPad.

  58. Device drivers help the OS communicate with? a) Hardware devices b) Wallpapers only c) Documents only d) Fonts only Answer: a) Hardware devices Explanation: Device drivers let the OS communicate with printers, scanners, and other hardware.

  59. A process is? a) A printer ink level b) A running program c) A folder name d) A keyboard key Answer: b) A running program Explanation: A process is a program currently executing in memory.

  60. Memory management allocates? a) Monitor to files b) Printer to BIOS c) RAM to programs d) CPU to folders Answer: c) RAM to programs Explanation: Memory management assigns RAM to running programs and frees it when they close.

  61. Win+I opens? a) Internet Explorer only b) Task Manager c) Recycle Bin d) Settings Answer: d) Settings Explanation: Win+I opens the modern Settings app for system configuration.

  62. Alt+F4 does? a) Close active window b) Open File Explorer c) Lock computer d) Rename file Answer: a) Close active window Explanation: Alt+F4 closes the currently active window or app.

  63. Win+Shift+S is used for? a) Sort files b) Screenshot selection c) Save file d) Shutdown Answer: b) Screenshot selection Explanation: Win+Shift+S opens the screenshot/snipping selection tool in Windows.

  64. Ctrl+Y is? a) Cut b) Paste c) Redo d) Undo Answer: c) Redo Explanation: Ctrl+Y redoes an action that was undone with Ctrl+Z.

  65. Deleting a desktop shortcut usually? a) Uninstalls the program b) Formats the drive c) Removes Windows d) Does not delete the original program Answer: d) Does not delete the original program Explanation: A shortcut is only a link — deleting it does not uninstall the program.

  66. Microsoft Windows is? a) Closed source / proprietary b) Open source kernel c) A Linux distro d) A mobile app only Answer: a) Closed source / proprietary Explanation: Windows source code is private; users cannot freely modify it.

  67. Linux kernel is? a) A file extension b) Core part of the operating system c) A spreadsheet app d) A printer driver only Answer: b) Core part of the operating system Explanation: The Linux kernel is the core that talks to hardware.

  68. Ransomware? a) Creates backups b) Updates Windows c) Encrypts files and demands payment d) Speeds up the PC Answer: c) Encrypts files and demands payment Explanation: Ransomware encrypts files and demands payment to restore access.

  69. Trojan malware? a) Is built into Windows b) Only affects printers c) Is a keyboard shortcut d) Pretends to be useful software Answer: d) Pretends to be useful software Explanation: Trojans disguise themselves as helpful programs but cause harm.

  70. A backup is? a) Extra copy of data b) Deleted virus only c) Shortcut icon only d) Temporary wallpaper Answer: a) Extra copy of data Explanation: Backup keeps a safe copy to recover from loss or failure.

  71. Standard user account is generally? a) Required to delete Windows folder b) Safer for daily work c) More powerful than administrator d) Used to replace OS Answer: b) Safer for daily work Explanation: Standard users have limited permissions — safer for everyday tasks.

  72. MS-DOS mainly used? a) Mobile apps only b) Cloud storage c) Command line interface d) Touchscreen gestures Answer: c) Command line interface Explanation: MS-DOS was a command-line OS — users typed text commands.

  73. Batch processing systems? a) Use only smartphones b) Have no OS c) Replace file management d) Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction Answer: d) Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction Explanation: Batch systems collected jobs and processed them together.

  74. Command dir in Command Prompt? a) Lists files and folders b) Deletes all files c) Installs software d) Opens Settings Answer: a) Lists files and folders Explanation: The dir command lists directory contents in Command Prompt.

  75. Command ipconfig shows? a) File extensions b) Network configuration c) CPU temperature d) Printer status Answer: b) Network configuration Explanation: ipconfig displays IP address and network adapter details.

  76. File extension .exe usually means? a) Image file b) Spreadsheet c) Windows installer/program d) Plain text file Answer: c) Windows installer/program Explanation: .exe and .msi files are common Windows program/installer formats.

  77. Hidden files in Windows are? a) Always viruses b) Deleted automatically c) Only on Linux d) Not shown by default to prevent accidental changes Answer: d) Not shown by default to prevent accidental changes Explanation: Hidden/system files are concealed to avoid accidental damage.

  78. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) adds? a) Extra verification step after password b) A second monitor c) More RAM d) Automatic file delete Answer: a) Extra verification step after password Explanation: 2FA adds OTP, app code, or biometric verification after the password.

  79. Deleting a program folder manually is? a) Required for updates b) Not the same as proper uninstall c) The recommended method d) Same as Settings uninstall Answer: b) Not the same as proper uninstall Explanation: Manual folder delete leaves registry entries and shortcuts behind.

  80. Linux is popular on servers because it is? a) A mobile-only OS b) Stable, secure, and customizable c) Only for games d) Not connected to networks Answer: b) Stable, secure, and customizable Explanation: Linux is widely used on servers for stability, security, and flexibility.

Short Answer Key

  1. What is an operating system? Ans: System software that manages hardware and software resources and provides an interface for users and applications.

  2. Write any five functions of an operating system. Ans: Process management, memory management, file management, device management, security management, user interface management (any five).

  3. Differentiate between GUI and CLI with examples. Ans: GUI uses icons, menus, and mouse (Windows desktop). CLI uses typed commands (Command Prompt, PowerShell, Terminal).

  4. Differentiate between Windows and Linux. Ans: Windows is proprietary, widely used on desktops. Linux is open source, used in distros like Ubuntu, popular on servers and cloud.

  5. What is Ubuntu? Ans: A beginner-friendly Linux distribution with GUI, file manager, apps, and Terminal — used on desktop and servers.

  6. What is a device driver? Ans: Software that helps the OS communicate with hardware such as printer, scanner, or network adapter.

  7. What is the difference between copy and move? Ans: Copy (Ctrl+C/V) keeps the original; move/cut (Ctrl+X/V) removes the item from the original location.

  8. What happens when you press Shift + Delete? Ans: The file is permanently deleted without going to Recycle Bin.

  9. Why should software be installed from official websites? Ans: Official sources reduce malware risk; cracked/pirated software may contain viruses, spyware, or ransomware.

  10. Explain why Windows updates are important. Ans: Updates fix security weaknesses, improve stability, remove bugs, and add features.

  11. What is Task Manager used for? Ans: View running programs, check CPU/RAM/disk/network usage, end frozen apps, and manage startup programs.

  12. What is the difference between administrator and standard user? Ans: Administrator has full system control and can install software; standard user has limited permissions and is safer for daily use.

  13. What is open-source software? Give two examples. Ans: Software whose source code can be studied/modified under license. Examples: Linux, Ubuntu, LibreOffice, Firefox.

  14. Write five useful keyboard shortcuts for Windows. Ans: Win+E (File Explorer), Win+L (lock), Ctrl+C/V/X (copy/paste/cut), Ctrl+Shift+Esc (Task Manager), Alt+Tab (switch apps) — any five from notes.

  15. Why is backup important for files and folders? Ans: Backup protects against accidental deletion, device failure, virus attack, and corruption.

Practical Checklist Key

  1. Open File Explorer with Win+E; identify Navigation Pane, Address Bar, and Search Box. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  2. Create folder structure Documents\CCC\Unit2\Notes and save a Notepad file. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  3. Rename a file with F2; copy and move files using Ctrl+C/X and Ctrl+V. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  4. Delete a test file and restore it from Recycle Bin. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  5. Take a screenshot with Snipping Tool and save as PNG. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  6. Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc) and note CPU and memory usage. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  7. Create a compressed (zip) folder and compare file size. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  8. Open Settings (Win+I) and check Windows Update status. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

questions.question: Windows is a?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Windows is an operating system (OS) that manages hardware and runs applications. It is not a browser or spreadsheet.

questions.question: Linux is?
questions.explanation: Linux is an open-source operating system — its source code can be freely used and modified. Ubuntu and Fedora are Linux distributions.

questions.question: OS function includes?
questions.explanation: File management is the main purpose or function described in the question. Only gaming and Only printing are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Shortcut Copy?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+C copies selected text or files to the clipboard without removing the original. Ctrl+V pastes; Ctrl+X cuts.

questions.question: Shortcut Paste?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+V pastes content from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+S saves the file.

questions.question: Shortcut Save?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+S saves the current file quickly. Always save your work often to avoid losing changes.

questions.question: Win+E opens?
questions.explanation: Win+E opens File Explorer so you can browse folders and files on your computer.

questions.question: Win+L?
questions.explanation: Win+L locks the computer immediately — you must sign in again. Use this when stepping away from your desk.

questions.question: F2 key?
questions.explanation: F2 renames a selected file or folder in Windows File Explorer. F5 refreshes the view.

questions.question: Delete sends file to?
questions.explanation: Normal Delete (without Shift) moves the file to Recycle Bin — you can restore it later.

questions.question: Shift+Delete?
questions.explanation: Shift+Delete permanently deletes a file without sending it to Recycle Bin — recovery is difficult.

questions.question: Task Manager opens with?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager to view running programs, CPU/RAM use, and end stuck tasks.

questions.question: Default drive letter for Windows?
questions.explanation: Windows is usually installed on drive C:. Drive A: was for floppy disks; other letters are for extra partitions or USB drives.

questions.question: Folder contains?
questions.explanation: Files and subfolders is the correct answer. Nothing and Only CPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ctrl+X is?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+X cuts — removes the selection and places it on the clipboard to paste elsewhere.

questions.question: Ctrl+Z is?
questions.explanation: Ctrl+Z undoes your last action. Very useful if you make a mistake while typing or editing.

questions.question: Alt+Tab?
questions.explanation: Alt+Tab switches between open application windows quickly without using the mouse.

questions.question: Zip folder purpose?
questions.explanation: Compress files is the main purpose or function described in the question. Print files and Email only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Desktop wallpaper changed via?
questions.explanation: Personalize/Settings is the correct answer. BIOS only and Notepad are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Uninstall program via?
questions.explanation: Use Settings → Apps → Uninstall to remove a program properly. Deleting only the desktop shortcut does not uninstall it.

questions.question: GUI OS uses?
questions.explanation: Icons and mouse is the correct answer. No monitor and Telegraph are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: File path shows?
questions.explanation: Location of file is the correct answer. CPU speed and RAM size are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Invalid filename character?
questions.explanation: * is the correct answer. _ and - are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Snipping Tool for?
questions.explanation: Screenshot is the correct answer. Database and Virus scan are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Administrator account has?
questions.explanation: Full system control is the correct answer. No install rights and Guest only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Linux mascot/tradition often?
questions.explanation: Penguin Tux is the correct answer. None and Apple only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Install program on Windows?
questions.explanation: Run installer / Store / setup.exe is the correct answer. Rename file and F5 are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Remove program properly?
questions.explanation: Uninstall from Settings is the correct answer. Delete shortcut only and Hide icon are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: System tray shows?
questions.explanation: Clock, network, volume is the correct answer. Hard disk and CPU chip are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Start menu used to?
questions.explanation: Launch programs is the main purpose or function described in the question. Destroy data and Remove OS are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Ctrl+A?
questions.explanation: Use Select all for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Copy vs Move: Copy?
questions.explanation: Original remains is the correct answer. Original deleted and Both deleted are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Restore deleted file from?
questions.explanation: Deleted files go to Recycle Bin first. Open it and choose Restore to get the file back to its original place.

questions.question: Hibernate saves session to?
questions.explanation: Disk then low power is the correct answer. Cloud only and Printer are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Windows Update purpose?
questions.explanation: Security patches is the main purpose or function described in the question. Delete files and Remove OS are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Pen drive appears as drive letter when?
questions.explanation: Plugged in USB is the correct answer. Always C: and Never are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Search in File Explorer finds?
questions.explanation: Files in folder/search scope is the correct answer. Only emails and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Notepad creates?
questions.explanation: Plain text files is the correct answer. Excel sheets and Database are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Control Panel / Settings for?
questions.explanation: System configuration is the correct answer. Cable only and Games only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Shortcut to desktop?
questions.explanation: Use Send to Desktop shortcut for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Safe eject USB why?
questions.explanation: Safely ejecting USB (Eject option) finishes writing data before you unplug — prevents file corruption.

questions.question: Linux distributions include?
questions.explanation: Ubuntu, Fedora is the correct answer. Word, Excel only and Chrome only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Open source means?
questions.explanation: Open source means the program's source code is publicly available to study, modify, and share (e.g. Linux, LibreOffice).

questions.question: Taskbar location usually?
questions.explanation: Bottom of screen is the correct answer. On printer and In BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: File extension identifies?
questions.explanation: File type is the correct answer. CPU model and RAM size are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Win+D shows?
questions.explanation: Use Desktop for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Increase list indent in Explorer sort?
questions.explanation: Sort by name/date/size is the correct answer. Email and Print are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Lock PC when away using?
questions.explanation: Win+L is the correct answer. F5 and Ctrl+P are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Guest account typically?
questions.explanation: Limited permissions is the correct answer. Full admin and No login ever are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Antivirus on Windows often?
questions.explanation: Windows Defender built-in is the correct answer. Not available and Part of CPU are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Main purpose of an operating system is to?

options.text: Manage hardware and provide user interface

questions.explanation: Manage hardware and provide user interface is the main purpose or function described in the question. Increase monitor size and Only play videos are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Ubuntu is?
questions.explanation: A Linux distribution is the correct answer. A virus and A spreadsheet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: GUI stands for?
questions.explanation: Graphical User Interface is the correct expansion or meaning. Learn both the short form and full form for the exam.

questions.question: CLI uses?
questions.explanation: Typed commands is the correct answer. Only touch gestures and Only pictures are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Which OS is commonly used on Apple computers?
questions.explanation: macOS is correct for this question. DOS only and Ubuntu only are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Android is mainly used on?
questions.explanation: Smartphones and tablets is the correct answer. Speakers only and Projectors only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Which is a mobile operating system?
questions.explanation: iOS is correct for this question. Notepad and Calculator are not the right choices here.

questions.question: Device drivers help the OS communicate with?
questions.explanation: Hardware devices is the correct answer. Wallpapers only and Documents only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: A process is?
questions.explanation: A running program is the correct answer. A printer ink level and A folder name are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Memory management allocates?
questions.explanation: RAM to programs is the correct answer. Monitor to files and Printer to BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Win+I opens?
questions.explanation: Use Settings for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Alt+F4 does?
questions.explanation: Use Close active window for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Win+Shift+S is used for?
questions.explanation: Use Screenshot selection for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Ctrl+Y is?
questions.explanation: Use Redo for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Deleting a desktop shortcut usually?

options.text: Does not delete the original program

questions.explanation: Use Does not delete the original program for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

questions.question: Microsoft Windows is?
questions.explanation: Closed source / proprietary is the correct answer. Open source kernel and A Linux distro are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Linux kernel is?

options.text: Core part of the operating system

questions.explanation: Core part of the operating system is the correct answer. A file extension and A spreadsheet app are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Ransomware?
questions.explanation: Ransomware locks or encrypts your files and demands payment — regular backups help you recover without paying.

questions.question: Trojan malware?
questions.explanation: Pretends to be useful software is the correct answer. Is built into Windows and Only affects printers are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: A backup is?
questions.explanation: Extra copy of data is the correct answer. Deleted virus only and Shortcut icon only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Standard user account is generally?
questions.explanation: Safer for daily work is the correct answer. Required to delete Windows folder and More powerful than administrator are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: MS-DOS mainly used?
questions.explanation: Command line interface is the correct answer. Mobile apps only and Cloud storage are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Batch processing systems?

options.text: Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction

questions.explanation: Process jobs in groups without direct user interaction is the correct answer. Use only smartphones and Have no OS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Command `dir` in Command Prompt?
questions.explanation: Lists files and folders is the correct answer. Deletes all files and Installs software are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Command `ipconfig` shows?
questions.explanation: Network configuration is the correct answer. File extensions and CPU temperature are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: File extension `.exe` usually means?
questions.explanation: Windows installer/program is the correct answer. Image file and Spreadsheet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Hidden files in Windows are?

options.text: Not shown by default to prevent accidental changes

questions.explanation: Not shown by default to prevent accidental changes is the correct answer. Always viruses and Deleted automatically are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) adds?
questions.explanation: Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds a second step (OTP, app code, fingerprint) after your password for stronger security.

questions.question: Deleting a program folder manually is?

options.text: Not the same as proper uninstall

questions.explanation: Not the same as proper uninstall is the correct answer. Required for updates and The recommended method are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

questions.question: Linux is popular on servers because it is?
questions.explanation: Stable, secure, and customizable is the correct answer. A mobile-only OS and Only for games are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.description: NITA CCA · Foundation modules (shared study pack)
chapters.contentHtml:
Module 3: Word Processing with MS Word (12 Hours)

Course: CCC 90-Hour DCA | Module duration: 12 hours
Primary software: Microsoft Word
Alternative reference: LibreOffice Writer as an open-source equivalent

Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Explain what word processing is and why MS Word is used in education, offices, and professional communication.
  • Create, open, save, print, and export documents using Microsoft Word.
  • Apply professional text formatting such as font, size, bold, italic, underline, color, highlight, and clear formatting.
  • Format paragraphs using alignment, indentation, line spacing, spacing before/after, bullets, and numbering.
  • Use page layout tools such as margins, orientation, paper size, page breaks, columns, and print setup.
  • Insert and format tables, pictures, shapes, WordArt, headers, footers, and page numbers.
  • Use Find, Replace, Word Count, Spell Check, Grammar tools, and proofreading features.
  • Apply styles, themes, and templates to create consistent professional documents.
  • Prepare formal letters, leave letters, application letters, complaint letters, business letters, cover letters, resumes, and bio-data.
  • Perform basic mail merge in MS Word using a main document, data source, and merge fields.
  • Export documents as PDF and follow professional document design standards.
  • Use essential MS Word keyboard shortcuts for faster office work.
  • Complete MS Word practical labs suitable for CCC, DCA, vocational training, office work, and job readiness.

1. Introduction to Word Processing

Explain What It Is

Word processing means creating, editing, formatting, storing, printing, and sharing text-based documents using software. Microsoft Word is a word processing application used to prepare letters, resumes, reports, notices, certificates, invoices, assignments, project files, and official documents.

A word processor is different from a plain text editor such as Notepad. Notepad mainly stores simple text, while MS Word supports formatting, tables, images, page layout, headers, footers, spell check, templates, styles, references, and PDF export.

Explain Why It Is Used

MS Word is used because documents in schools, colleges, offices, banks, hospitals, government departments, and businesses must be clear, professional, printable, and easy to edit. Word helps users prepare documents faster and with better formatting.

Demonstrate in MS Word

  1. Click Start.
  2. Search Word.
  3. Open Microsoft Word.
  4. Click Blank document.
  5. Type a heading: Uses of Computer.
  6. Press Ctrl + S.
  7. Choose a folder and save as uses-of-computer.docx.

Writer Equivalent

LibreOffice Writer is an open-source alternative. It can create similar documents and usually saves in .odt format, though it can also open and save .docx with possible layout differences.

Real-World Usage

  • School: assignments, notices, question papers.
  • College: project reports, seminar documents.
  • Government office: applications, memos, letters.
  • Bank: customer forms and internal reports.
  • Hospital: medical certificates and patient instructions.
  • Corporate office: proposals, meeting notes, policy documents.
  • Freelancing: resume writing, content writing, typing work.

Practice Exercise

Create a blank Word document titled My First MS Word Document. Type five lines about yourself, save the file, close Word, reopen the file, and add one more paragraph.

2. MS Word Interface and Screen Layout

Explain What It Is

The MS Word interface is the working environment where users type, edit, format, and manage documents. It contains the ribbon, tabs, groups, document area, ruler, status bar, and zoom controls.

MS Word Screen Areas

Area Purpose
Title Bar Shows document name and window controls
Quick Access Toolbar Provides quick Save, Undo, Redo commands
Ribbon Main command area with tabs such as Home, Insert, Layout, Review
Tabs Organize commands into categories
Groups Smaller command groups such as Font, Paragraph, Styles
Ruler Helps set margins, tabs, and indents
Document Area Main typing and editing space
Status Bar Shows page number, word count, language, and view options
Zoom Slider Increases or decreases display size

Demonstrate in MS Word

  1. Open Word.
  2. Observe the Home tab.
  3. Identify the Font, Paragraph, and Styles groups.
  4. Turn ruler on: View -> Ruler.
  5. Check word count on the status bar.
  6. Change zoom using the slider at the bottom-right.

Writer Equivalent

Writer uses menus, toolbars, and a sidebar instead of Word's ribbon-centered layout. The features are similar, but command locations may differ.

Common Mistakes

  • Typing without saving the document.
  • Not noticing the file name in the title bar.
  • Confusing zoom level with actual font size.
  • Using spaces repeatedly instead of alignment or indents.

Practice Exercise

Open MS Word and identify at least ten interface elements. Write their names and uses in a two-column table.

3. Document Management in MS Word

Explain What It Is

Document management includes creating, opening, saving, saving as, printing, sharing, and exporting documents. These are basic operations every Word user must master.

Why It Is Used

Proper document management prevents data loss, maintains file organization, and ensures documents can be shared professionally.

MS Word Method

New Document

  1. File -> New.
  2. Choose Blank document or a template.
  3. Shortcut: Ctrl + N.

Open Document

  1. File -> Open.
  2. Browse the saved location.
  3. Select the file.
  4. Shortcut: Ctrl + O.

Save Document

  1. File -> Save or press Ctrl + S.
  2. Choose folder.
  3. Enter file name.
  4. Choose file type .docx.

Save As

Use Save As when you want a new copy, a new file name, or a different format such as PDF.

Steps:

  1. File -> Save As.
  2. Choose location.
  3. Enter new file name.
  4. Choose file type.
  5. Click Save.

Print

  1. File -> Print or Ctrl + P.
  2. Select printer.
  3. Choose pages and copies.
  4. Check preview.
  5. Click Print.

Export PDF

  1. File -> Save As.
  2. Choose file type: PDF.
  3. Click Save.

Writer Equivalent

Writer uses File -> New, Open, Save, Save As, Print, and Export As -> Export as PDF.

Real-World Usage

  • Saving a resume as .docx for editing and PDF for submission.
  • Printing an application letter.
  • Saving project reports in subject-wise folders.
  • Creating separate versions such as Resume_Draft.docx and Resume_Final.pdf.

Best Practices

  • Save every 5-10 minutes.
  • Use meaningful names: Leave_Application_Rasmita.docx.
  • Keep editable .docx and final .pdf copies.
  • Avoid names like new final latest final.docx.

Practice Exercise

Create a document called document-management-practice.docx. Save it, use Save As to make a second copy, export it as PDF, and print preview it.

4. Basic Text Entry and Editing

Explain What It Is

Text entry means typing content into a Word document. Editing means correcting, deleting, moving, copying, and improving that content.

Why It Is Used

Every office document requires accurate typing and editing. Mistakes in official letters, resumes, or applications can create a poor impression.

MS Word Method

Task Method Shortcut
New paragraph Press Enter Enter
Line break in same paragraph Press Shift + Enter Shift + Enter
Delete left character Backspace Backspace
Delete right character Delete Delete
Select all Home -> Select -> Select All Ctrl + A
Copy Home -> Copy Ctrl + C
Cut Home -> Cut Ctrl + X
Paste Home -> Paste Ctrl + V
Undo Quick Access Toolbar -> Undo Ctrl + Z
Redo Quick Access Toolbar -> Redo Ctrl + Y

Real-World Usage

  • Correcting a spelling mistake in a leave application.
  • Moving a paragraph in a report.
  • Copying address details into multiple letters.
  • Undoing accidental deletion.

Common Mistakes

  • Pressing Enter many times to create space instead of using paragraph spacing.
  • Using Spacebar repeatedly to align text.
  • Forgetting to select text before applying formatting.
  • Deleting content without using Undo.

Practice Exercise

Type a paragraph on Importance of Computer Education. Copy the first sentence, paste it at the end, undo the paste, redo it, and then delete it.

5. Essential MS Word Keyboard Shortcuts

Keyboard shortcuts save time and are important for CCC/DCA practical exams and office jobs.

Document Management

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + N New document
Ctrl + O Open document
Ctrl + S Save document
F12 Save As
Ctrl + P Print
Ctrl + W Close document

Editing

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + C Copy
Ctrl + V Paste
Ctrl + X Cut
Ctrl + Z Undo
Ctrl + Y Redo
Ctrl + A Select all
Delete Delete selected text or character to right
Backspace Delete character to left

Formatting

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + B Bold
Ctrl + I Italic
Ctrl + U Underline
Ctrl + Shift + > Increase font size
Ctrl + Shift + < Decrease font size
Ctrl + E Center align
Ctrl + L Left align
Ctrl + R Right align
Ctrl + J Justify
Ctrl + 1 Single line spacing
Ctrl + 2 Double line spacing
Ctrl + 5 1.5 line spacing

Navigation

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + Home Move to beginning of document
Ctrl + End Move to end of document
Ctrl + Left Arrow Move one word left
Ctrl + Right Arrow Move one word right
Ctrl + Up Arrow Move one paragraph up
Ctrl + Down Arrow Move one paragraph down
Page Up Move up one screen
Page Down Move down one screen

Selection

Shortcut Use
Shift + Arrow Keys Select character/line gradually
Ctrl + Shift + Arrow Keys Select word/paragraph sections
Ctrl + A Select entire document
Shift + Home Select to beginning of line
Shift + End Select to end of line

Review

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + F Find
Ctrl + H Replace
F7 Spelling and Grammar
Ctrl + Alt + M Insert comment in newer Word versions

Practical Shortcut Drills

Drill 1: Document Management

Create a new document using Ctrl + N, type five lines, save using Ctrl + S, and open print preview using Ctrl + P.

Drill 2: Editing

Type a paragraph, select a sentence, copy it, paste it below, undo, and redo.

Drill 3: Formatting

Select heading text and apply Ctrl + B, Ctrl + E, and Ctrl + Shift + >.

Drill 4: Navigation

Create a two-page document. Use Ctrl + Home and Ctrl + End to move quickly.

6. MS Word Feature Deep-Dive

6.1 Fonts

Explain What It Is

A font is the design style of text. Examples include Calibri, Arial, Times New Roman, Cambria, and Verdana.

Purpose

Fonts improve readability, professionalism, and visual identity.

MS Word Steps

  1. Select text.
  2. Home -> Font group.
  3. Choose font name.
  4. Choose size.

Use Cases

  • Resume: Calibri, Arial, or Aptos.
  • Formal letter: Times New Roman or Arial.
  • Notice: clear bold heading with readable body.

Common Mistakes

  • Using too many fonts in one document.
  • Using decorative fonts in official documents.
  • Using very small or very large body text.

Best Practices

  • Use 1-2 fonts only.
  • Body text: 11 or 12 pt.
  • Headings: 14-18 pt.
  • Keep official documents simple.

Practice Exercise

Create a paragraph and apply three different fonts. Then choose the most professional one and explain why.

6.2 Bold, Italic, Underline, Color, and Highlight

Explain What It Is

Character formatting changes the appearance of selected text.

Purpose

It helps highlight important information such as headings, dates, subject lines, names, and key points.

MS Word Steps

  1. Select text.
  2. Home -> Font group.
  3. Click Bold, Italic, Underline, Font Color, or Text Highlight Color.

Shortcuts:

  • Bold: Ctrl + B
  • Italic: Ctrl + I
  • Underline: Ctrl + U

Use Cases

  • Resume section headings.
  • Subject line in formal letters.
  • Important dates in notices.
  • Highlighting key terms in notes.

Common Mistakes

  • Underlining too much text.
  • Using bright colors in official documents.
  • Highlighting entire paragraphs unnecessarily.

Best Practices

  • Bold headings instead of underlining everything.
  • Use black text for formal documents.
  • Use highlight only for review or study notes.

Practice Exercise

Create a notice and apply bold to heading, underline to date, and highlight only one important instruction.

6.3 Paragraph Formatting

Explain What It Is

Paragraph formatting changes the layout of complete paragraphs, including alignment, spacing, indentation, and list style.

Purpose

It makes documents organized and easy to read.

MS Word Steps

  1. Select paragraph.
  2. Home -> Paragraph group.
  3. Choose alignment, line spacing, bullets, numbering, indent, or borders.

Use Cases

  • Justified paragraphs in formal letters.
  • Centered titles in reports.
  • Bulleted lists in resumes.
  • Numbered steps in instructions.

Common Mistakes

  • Pressing Spacebar for indentation.
  • Pressing Enter repeatedly for spacing.
  • Mixing many alignments without reason.

Best Practices

  • Use paragraph spacing instead of blank lines.
  • Use consistent line spacing.
  • Use justify alignment for formal body text when appropriate.

Practice Exercise

Type three paragraphs and apply left, center, right, and justify alignment. Observe the difference.

6.4 Alignment

Purpose

Alignment controls how text is positioned between the left and right margins.

Alignment Shortcut Use
Left Ctrl + L Normal body text
Center Ctrl + E Titles and headings
Right Ctrl + R Dates or reference numbers
Justify Ctrl + J Formal paragraphs and reports

MS Word Steps

Home -> Paragraph group -> choose alignment button.

Practice Exercise

Create a formal letter and align the title center, date right, and body justified.

6.5 Line Spacing

Explain What It Is

Line spacing is the vertical distance between lines in a paragraph.

Purpose

Proper spacing improves readability and appearance.

MS Word Steps

  1. Select paragraph.
  2. Home -> Paragraph group -> Line and Paragraph Spacing.
  3. Choose 1.0, 1.15, 1.5, or 2.0.

Use Cases

  • Resume: 1.0 or 1.15.
  • Assignments: 1.5.
  • Draft proofreading: 2.0.

Practice Exercise

Type the same paragraph three times and apply 1.0, 1.5, and 2.0 spacing.

6.6 Bullets

Explain What It Is

Bullets are symbols used to present unordered list items.

Purpose

They make points easy to scan.

MS Word Steps

Home -> Paragraph group -> Bullets.

Use Cases

  • Resume skills.
  • Notice instructions.
  • Shopping lists.
  • Project features.

Common Mistakes

  • Using bullets for long paragraphs.
  • Mixing bullet styles randomly.

Practice Exercise

Create a bulleted list of five computer skills.

6.7 Numbering

Explain What It Is

Numbering creates ordered lists.

Purpose

It is used when sequence matters.

MS Word Steps

Home -> Paragraph group -> Numbering.

Use Cases

  • Steps in a practical exam.
  • Rules and procedures.
  • Agenda items.
  • Application instructions.

Practice Exercise

Write five steps to save a Word document using numbering.

6.8 Tables

Explain What It Is

A table organizes information into rows and columns.

Purpose

Tables help present marks, schedules, fees, lists, comparison data, and office records clearly.

MS Word Steps

  1. Insert -> Table.
  2. Select number of rows and columns.
  3. Type data.
  4. Use Table Design for borders and shading.
  5. Use Layout for adding rows/columns, merging cells, and alignment.

Use Cases

  • Student marksheet.
  • Attendance sheet.
  • Fee receipt.
  • Timetable.
  • Resume education section.

Common Mistakes

  • Using spaces instead of tables.
  • Making uneven columns.
  • Forgetting table headings.
  • Overusing colors.

Best Practices

  • Use header row.
  • Keep borders clean.
  • Align numbers properly.
  • Use consistent column width.

Practice Exercise

Create a 5-column marks table: Roll No, Name, Subject, Marks, Grade.

6.9 Images

Explain What It Is

Images are pictures inserted into a document.

Purpose

Images make documents visual and support explanation.

MS Word Steps

  1. Insert -> Pictures.
  2. Choose image from device.
  3. Resize using corner handles.
  4. Use Picture Format -> Wrap Text.

Use Cases

  • Project reports.
  • ID forms.
  • Posters.
  • Certificates.
  • Tutorials.

Common Mistakes

  • Stretching image from side handles and distorting it.
  • Using low-quality images.
  • Placing images without text wrapping.

Best Practices

  • Resize from corners.
  • Use Square or Tight wrap when needed.
  • Compress images for smaller file size.
  • Use relevant images only.

Practice Exercise

Insert one image into a document and apply Square text wrap.

6.10 Shapes

Explain What It Is

Shapes are drawing objects such as lines, arrows, rectangles, circles, and callouts.

Purpose

They help create diagrams, flowcharts, labels, and visual emphasis.

MS Word Steps

Insert -> Shapes -> choose shape -> draw on page.

Use Cases

  • Flowchart.
  • Certificate border.
  • Process diagram.
  • Highlight box.

Practice Exercise

Create a simple flowchart: Start -> Type Document -> Save -> Print -> End.

6.11 WordArt

Explain What It Is

WordArt creates decorative text effects.

Purpose

It is used for creative headings, posters, invitations, and certificates.

MS Word Steps

Insert -> WordArt -> choose style -> type text.

Common Mistakes

  • Using WordArt in formal letters or resumes.
  • Choosing overly colorful styles for official documents.

Practice Exercise

Create a birthday invitation heading using WordArt.

6.12 Header

Explain What It Is

A header is content repeated at the top of pages.

Purpose

It displays institute name, document title, report title, or department name.

MS Word Steps

Insert -> Header -> choose style -> type header text.

Use Cases

  • Project report title.
  • School/institute name.
  • Company document title.
