Add header NITA Classes - Word Processing Practical.

6.13 Footer

Explain What It Is

A footer is content repeated at the bottom of pages.

Purpose

It commonly contains page number, address, confidentiality note, or contact details.

MS Word Steps

Insert -> Footer -> choose style -> type footer text.

Practice Exercise

Add footer Prepared by: Your Name.

6.14 Page Number

Explain What It Is

Page numbers automatically number pages.

Purpose

They help readers navigate multi-page documents.

MS Word Steps

Insert -> Page Number -> choose position.

Use Cases

  • Project reports.
  • Manuals.
  • Assignments.
  • Office reports.

Practice Exercise

Create a three-page document and insert bottom-center page numbers.

6.15 Styles

Explain What It Is

Styles are predefined formatting sets such as Normal, Heading 1, Heading 2, and Title.

Purpose

Styles keep document formatting consistent and help create automatic tables of contents.

MS Word Steps

  1. Select heading text.
  2. Home -> Styles group.
  3. Choose Heading 1 or Heading 2.

Use Cases

  • Project reports.
  • Manuals.
  • Training materials.
  • Long assignments.

Common Mistakes

  • Manually formatting every heading separately.
  • Using headings only visually without applying styles.

Practice Exercise

Create a document with three headings and apply Heading 1 style to each.

6.16 Themes

Explain What It Is

A theme is a set of fonts, colors, and effects applied to a document.

Purpose

Themes give a consistent visual design.

MS Word Steps

Design -> Themes -> choose a theme.

Use Cases

  • Reports.
  • Business proposals.
  • Project documents.

Practice Exercise

Apply two different themes to the same report and compare appearance.

6.17 Templates

Explain What It Is

A template is a ready-made document layout.

Purpose

Templates save time and help beginners create professional documents.

MS Word Steps

File -> New -> search template such as resume, letter, certificate, or report.

Use Cases

  • Resume.
  • Cover letter.
  • Brochure.
  • Certificate.
  • Business report.

Common Mistakes

  • Using a template without editing placeholder text.
  • Choosing a colorful resume template for formal jobs.

Open a resume template and replace all sample text with your own details.

7. Page Layout and Print Setup

Explain What It Is

Page layout controls the physical appearance of the document page, including margins, paper size, orientation, columns, and page breaks.

MS Word Method

Feature Steps Use
Margins Layout -> Margins Controls blank space around page
Orientation Layout -> Orientation Portrait or Landscape
Size Layout -> Size A4, Letter, Legal
Columns Layout -> Columns Newsletter or newspaper style
Page Break Insert -> Page Break or Ctrl + Enter Starts new page

Real-World Usage

  • A4 portrait for letters and resumes.
  • Landscape for wide tables.
  • Narrow margins for content-heavy reports.
  • Page breaks before new chapters.

Best Practices

  • Use A4 for Indian academic and office documents.
  • Keep normal margins for formal letters.
  • Do not use manual Enter repeatedly to force a new page; use page break.

Practice Exercise

Create a two-page report. Use A4 size, normal margins, portrait orientation, and a page break before the second section.

8. Find, Replace, Spell Check, and Review Tools

Find

Explain What It Is

Find locates words or phrases inside a document.

MS Word Steps

Home -> Editing -> Find or press Ctrl + F.

Practice

Replace

Explain What It Is

Replace changes one word or phrase into another throughout a document.

MS Word Steps

Home -> Editing -> Replace or press Ctrl + H.

Real-World Use

Changing NITA Class to NITA Classes in a full document.

Spell Check and Grammar

Explain What It Is

Spell Check identifies spelling and grammar issues.

MS Word Steps

Review -> Editor/Spelling and Grammar or press F7.

Common Mistakes

  • Accepting all suggestions without reading.
  • Ignoring names and technical terms.
  • Not setting correct language.

Practice

Type a paragraph with five spelling errors and correct them using F7.

Word Count

Review -> Word Count. Used for assignments, essays, reports, and content writing.

9. Professional Document Creation

9.1 Writing Formal Letters

Structure

Sender Address
Date

Receiver Name and Designation
Organization Address

Subject: Clear one-line subject

Respected Sir/Madam,

Body Paragraph 1: Purpose
Body Paragraph 2: Details/request
Body Paragraph 3: Closing line

Yours faithfully,
Name
Contact

Formatting Standards

  • Paper: A4.
  • Font: Times New Roman 12 pt or Arial 11 pt.
  • Alignment: body justified or left aligned.
  • Subject: bold.
  • Tone: polite and formal.

Common Mistakes

  • Missing subject.
  • Using casual language.
  • Writing too long.
  • Forgetting date or signature.

Sample Formal Letter

At/PO: Example Village
Dist: Example District
21 June 2026

The Principal
NITA Classes
Odisha

Subject: Request for study leave

Respected Sir/Madam,

I respectfully request three days of study leave from 24 June to 26 June 2026 because I need to prepare for my upcoming examination.

I will complete pending classwork and practical assignments after returning. Kindly grant me permission for the above period.

Yours faithfully,
Rasmita Sahu
Contact: 9XXXXXXXXX

Practice Exercise

Create a one-page formal letter requesting a bonafide certificate.

9.2 Resume Creation

Purpose

A resume presents education, skills, experience, and contact details for a job or internship.

Structure

  1. Name and contact.
  2. Career objective.
  3. Education.
  4. Skills.
  5. Projects/practical experience.
  6. Certifications.
  7. Personal details if required.
  8. Declaration if needed.

Formatting Standards

  • One page for freshers.
  • Use clear headings.
  • Use bullets for skills.
  • Export as PDF before sending.
  • Avoid spelling errors.

Common Mistakes

  • Adding unnecessary personal details.
  • Using too many colors.
  • Making it more than two pages for fresher level.
  • Forgetting phone number or email.

Sample Resume Format

RASMlTA SAHU
Phone: 9XXXXXXXXX | Email: example@email.com | City: Odisha

Career Objective
To obtain an entry-level computer operator position where I can use my MS Office, typing, and communication skills.

Education
Class 12 | CHSE Odisha | 2026 | 82%

Skills
- MS Word document formatting
- MS Excel basic formulas
- PowerPoint presentation
- Internet and email
- English and Odia typing

Certification
CCC / DCA Computer Course - NITA Classes

Declaration
I hereby declare that the above information is true to the best of my knowledge.

Practice Exercise

Create your own one-page resume in MS Word and export it as PDF.

10. Document Design Best Practices

Professional Formatting Rules

  • Keep formatting consistent.
  • Use proper margins.
  • Avoid unnecessary colors.
  • Use headings and subheadings.
  • Use tables for structured data.
  • Proofread before printing or sending.

Font Selection

  • Formal documents: Times New Roman, Arial, Calibri, Aptos.
  • Body size: 11 or 12 pt.
  • Heading size: 14-18 pt.
  • Avoid decorative fonts in resumes or letters.

Heading Hierarchy

Level Example
Title Project Report on Computer Education
Heading 1 Introduction
Heading 2 Objectives
Heading 3 Methodology

White Space Usage

White space means blank space around text. It improves readability. Do not fill every part of the page with text.

Alignment Rules

  • Titles: center.
  • Body: left or justified.
  • Dates: left or right, depending on format.
  • Tables: text left, numbers center/right.

Print-Friendly Design

  • Use black or dark text.
  • Avoid low-contrast colors.
  • Check print preview.
  • Use A4 page size.
  • Keep margins clear.

PDF Export Standards

  • Export final documents as PDF.
  • Check file name before sharing.
  • Open PDF once to verify layout.
  • Use professional names such as Rasmita_Sahu_Resume.pdf.

11. Mail Merge in MS Word

What Is Mail Merge?

Mail Merge is an MS Word feature used to create many personalized documents from one common template and a list of records.

Why Organizations Use It

Organizations use mail merge to save time and reduce typing mistakes when sending similar documents to many people.

Components

Component Meaning
Main Document The common letter/template
Data Source List of names, addresses, marks, salary, etc.
Merge Fields Placeholders such as Name, Address, Roll No

Complete MS Word Mail Merge Walkthrough

Example: Invitation Letter

  1. Create an Excel list with columns: Name, Address, Event, Date.
  2. Open MS Word.
  3. Type the common invitation letter.
  4. Go to Mailings -> Start Mail Merge -> Letters.
  5. Click Select Recipients -> Use an Existing List.
  6. Select the Excel file.
  7. Place cursor where name should appear.
  8. Click Insert Merge Field -> Name.
  9. Insert Address, Event, and Date fields.
  10. Click Preview Results.
  11. Click Finish & Merge -> Edit Individual Documents or Print Documents.

Real Examples

  • Admit cards.
  • Salary slips.
  • Certificates.
  • Bulk letters.
  • Invitations.
  • Fee reminders.
  • Appointment letters.

Common Mistakes

  • Column names in Excel are unclear.
  • Merge fields inserted in wrong position.
  • Data source file moved after linking.
  • Not previewing before printing.

Practice Exercise

Create a data source of five students and generate five personalized invitation letters.

Writer Equivalent

Writer includes Tools -> Mail Merge Wizard. It can perform similar tasks, but this chapter teaches the MS Word Mailings tab as the primary method.

12. Industry-Oriented MS Word Skills

Schools

MS Word is used for lesson plans, question papers, notices, attendance sheets, certificates, and student reports.

Colleges

Colleges use Word for assignments, project reports, seminar documents, applications, and office notices.

Government Offices

Government offices use Word for official letters, circulars, reports, forms, orders, and public notices.

Banks

Banks use Word for customer letters, internal memos, loan documents, forms, and reports.

Hospitals

Hospitals use Word for patient instructions, certificates, staff notices, discharge summaries, and administrative letters.

Corporate Offices

Companies use Word for proposals, meeting minutes, HR letters, policy documents, contracts, and reports.

Freelancing

Freelancers use Word for typing work, formatting resumes, preparing reports, content writing, proofreading, and document conversion.

Content Writing

Writers use Word for drafts, editing, word count, comments, spell check, and final PDF delivery.

Documentation Teams

Documentation teams use Word for manuals, SOPs, user guides, training material, and technical documentation.

15. Quick Revision Sheet

Most Important MS Word Commands

Command Location
Save File -> Save
Save As File -> Save As
Print File -> Print
Font formatting Home -> Font
Paragraph formatting Home -> Paragraph
Table Insert -> Table
Pictures Insert -> Pictures
Header/Footer Insert -> Header/Footer
Page Number Insert -> Page Number
Spell Check Review -> Editor/Spelling
Mail Merge Mailings tab
Export PDF File -> Save As -> PDF

Most Important Shortcuts

Ctrl + N, Ctrl + O, Ctrl + S, Ctrl + P, Ctrl + C, Ctrl + X, Ctrl + V, Ctrl + Z, Ctrl + Y, Ctrl + B, Ctrl + I, Ctrl + U, Ctrl + E, Ctrl + J, Ctrl + F, Ctrl + H, F7, Ctrl + Home, Ctrl + End.

Frequently Used Features

  • Save and Save As.
  • Font formatting.
  • Paragraph alignment.
  • Bullets and numbering.
  • Tables.
  • Images.
  • Headers and footers.
  • Page numbers.
  • Spell check.
  • PDF export.

Exam Tips

  • Learn shortcuts: Ctrl + S, Ctrl + P, Ctrl + B, Ctrl + I, Ctrl + U, Ctrl + F, Ctrl + H, F7.
  • For formal letters, remember address, date, receiver, subject, salutation, body, closing.
  • For resumes, keep one page and export as PDF.
  • Use tables for structured information.
  • Use Mailings tab for mail merge.

Interview Questions

  1. What is MS Word?
  2. What is the difference between Save and Save As?
  3. What is mail merge?
  4. What are merge fields?
  5. What is the use of styles?
  6. What is the shortcut for spell check?
  7. Why should resumes be exported as PDF?
  8. What is the use of header and footer?
  9. How do you insert a table in Word?
  10. What is the difference between bullets and numbering?

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Not saving work regularly.
  • Using too many fonts and colors.
  • Pressing Enter many times for page breaks.
  • Using spaces to align text.
  • Forgetting to proofread.
  • Sending editable .docx when PDF is required.
  • Not checking print preview.
  • Using WordArt in formal documents.
  • Not replacing template placeholder text.
  • Using unclear file names.

28. Bullets are used for:

  • a) Unordered lists
  • b) Printing only
  • c) Saving files
  • d) Closing Word

29. Numbering is used when:

  • a) Sequence matters
  • b) No order is needed
  • c) Only images are inserted
  • d) Font color changes

30. LibreOffice Writer is:

  • a) Open-source alternative to MS Word
  • b) A hardware device
  • c) A printer
  • d) A virus

B. True/False (20)

  1. MS Word is used to create formatted documents.
  2. Ctrl + S is used to print a document.
  3. Ctrl + P opens print options.
  4. Bold makes text more prominent.
  5. A footer appears at the top of a page.
  6. Mail Merge can create personalized letters.
  7. A data source contains recipient information.
  8. F7 opens spelling and grammar tools.
  9. PDF export helps preserve layout.
  10. WordArt should be used heavily in formal resumes.
  11. Tables organize data into rows and columns.
  12. Ctrl + H is used for Replace.
  13. Ctrl + F is used for Find.
  14. Page breaks should be made by pressing Enter many times.
  15. Styles help keep formatting consistent.
  16. A resume should contain contact details.
  17. Templates save document creation time.
  18. Writer is an open-source alternative to Word.
  19. Header appears at the top of pages.
  20. Proofreading is not needed after spell check.

C. Fill in the Blanks (15)

  1. MS Word is a __________ software.
  2. The default modern Word extension is __________.
  3. Ctrl + B applies __________ formatting.
  4. Ctrl + I applies __________ formatting.
  5. Ctrl + U applies __________ formatting.
  6. Ctrl + J applies __________ alignment.
  7. Spell check shortcut is __________.
  8. Find shortcut is __________.
  9. Replace shortcut is __________.
  10. Mail Merge is found in the __________ tab.
  11. The common letter in mail merge is called the __________ document.
  12. Recipient list in mail merge is called the __________ source.
  13. A header appears at the __________ of a page.
  14. A footer appears at the __________ of a page.
  15. Final documents are often exported as __________.

D. Match the Following (10)

Column A Column B
1. Ctrl + S a. Spell check
2. Ctrl + P b. Save
3. F7 c. Print
4. Ctrl + B d. Bold
5. Ctrl + H e. Replace
6. Ctrl + F f. Find
7. Insert -> Table g. Rows and columns
8. Mail Merge h. Personalized bulk letters
9. Header i. Top of page
10. Footer j. Bottom of page

E. Viva Questions (15)

  1. What is MS Word?
  2. What is word processing?
  3. What is the difference between Save and Save As?
  4. What is the shortcut for Save?
  5. What is the use of Bold?
  6. What is paragraph alignment?
  7. What is the difference between bullets and numbering?
  8. What is the use of a table?
  9. What is the use of header and footer?
  10. What is mail merge?
  11. What is a data source?
  12. What is a merge field?
  13. Why should a resume be exported as PDF?
  14. What is the use of spell check?
  15. What is LibreOffice Writer?

F. Practical Questions (10)

  1. Create a one-page essay with heading, justified text, and 1.5 line spacing.
  2. Create a formal leave application in MS Word.
  3. Create a one-page resume and export it as PDF.
  4. Create a student marksheet table with five students.
  5. Insert an image and apply Square text wrap.
  6. Add header, footer, and page number to a document.
  7. Use Find and Replace in a document.
  8. Create a document using a built-in resume template.
  9. Create a mail merge letter for five recipients.
  10. Create a zipped project submission folder containing Word and PDF files.

G. Mini Projects (5)

  1. Prepare a complete student portfolio with cover page, resume, achievement list, and certificate page.
  2. Create a school notice file with header, footer, table, and signature section.
  3. Prepare a job application package with cover letter, resume, and bio-data.
  4. Create a class marksheet report with table formatting and PDF export.
  5. Generate five personalized invitation letters using Mail Merge.

H. Case Studies (5)

Case Study 1: Leave Application

A student needs to request three days of leave for exam preparation. Prepare the document professionally.

Questions:

  1. Which document type is needed?
  2. Which alignment is suitable for the body?
  3. What should be bold?
  4. Which format should be used for final sharing?

Case Study 2: Job Resume

A fresher wants to apply for a computer operator job.

Questions:

  1. Which Word document should be created?
  2. What sections should be included?
  3. Why should it be one page?
  4. Why export as PDF?

Case Study 3: Student Marksheet

A teacher needs a clean table for student marks.

Questions:

  1. Which MS Word feature is required?
  2. What should be included in the header row?
  3. How should numbers be aligned?
  4. Why are borders useful?

Case Study 4: Invitation Letters

An institute wants to send personalized invitation letters to 50 students.

Questions:

  1. Which MS Word feature saves time?
  2. What is the main document?
  3. What is the data source?
  4. Why preview results before printing?

Case Study 5: PDF Submission

A student has completed an assignment and must email it professionally.

Questions:

  1. Why should the document be exported as PDF?
  2. What file name is professional?
  3. What should be checked before sending?
  4. Why keep the .docx copy?

Answer Keys

MCQ Answers

1-a | 2-a | 3-a | 4-a | 5-a | 6-a | 7-a | 8-a | 9-a | 10-a | 11-a | 12-a | 13-a | 14-a | 15-a | 16-a | 17-a | 18-a | 19-a | 20-a | 21-a | 22-a | 23-a | 24-a | 25-a | 26-a | 27-a | 28-a | 29-a | 30-a

True/False Answers

1-True | 2-False | 3-True | 4-True | 5-False | 6-True | 7-True | 8-True | 9-True | 10-False | 11-True | 12-True | 13-True | 14-False | 15-True | 16-True | 17-True | 18-True | 19-True | 20-False

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. word processing
  2. .docx
  3. bold
  4. italic
  5. underline
  6. justify
  7. F7
  8. Ctrl + F
  9. Ctrl + H
  10. Mailings
  11. main
  12. data
  13. top
  14. bottom
  15. PDF

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-c | 3-a | 4-d | 5-e | 6-f | 7-g | 8-h | 9-i | 10-j

Case Study Model Answers

  1. Leave application; justify or left align body; subject line should be bold; final sharing should be PDF.
  2. Resume; include contact, objective, education, skills, certifications; one page is easier for freshers; PDF preserves layout.
  3. Use Table; header row should include Roll No, Name, Subject, Marks, Grade; numbers can be center/right aligned; borders improve readability.
  4. Use Mail Merge; main document is the common invitation letter; data source is the recipient list; preview prevents wrong names/addresses.
  5. PDF preserves layout; use a clear name such as Rasmita_Sahu_Assignment.pdf; check spelling/layout; keep .docx for future editing.

Final Self-Check

  • I can create, save, open, print, and export Word documents.
  • I can format fonts, paragraphs, alignment, spacing, bullets, and numbering.
  • I can insert tables, images, shapes, WordArt, headers, footers, and page numbers.
  • I can use Find, Replace, Spell Check, and Word Count.
  • I can create formal letters, resumes, bio-data, application letters, leave letters, complaint letters, business letters, and cover letters.
  • I can perform basic mail merge in MS Word.
  • I can follow professional formatting and PDF export standards.
  • I can answer MS Word practical, viva, and exam questions confidently.

chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 03: Word Processing

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb

  2. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt

  3. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P

  4. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N

  5. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L

  6. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5

  7. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter

  8. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver

  9. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only

  10. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only

  11. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N

  12. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K

  13. Spell check key? a) F7 b) F5 c) F2 d) F12

  14. Insert table from? a) Insert tab b) Review only c) Mailings only d) Help

  15. Header/footer from? a) Insert tab b) Home font c) View only d) Draw

  16. Page numbers inserted via? a) Insert → Page Number b) Home Bold c) Review d) File Close

  17. Export Word to PDF? a) Save As PDF b) Cannot export c) Print only paper d) Insert PDF

  18. Portrait orientation? a) Vertical page b) Horizontal page c) Circular d) None

  19. Landscape orientation? a) Horizontal page b) Vertical page c) Square only d) None

  20. Wrap text for image? a) Picture Format → Wrap Text b) Home font color c) Review d) Mailings

  21. Heading styles used for? a) Document structure b) Virus removal c) Internet d) CPU

  22. Line spacing changed in? a) Home → Paragraph b) Insert Picture c) Review d) Draw

  23. Tab in table moves to? a) Next cell b) New document c) Internet d) Shutdown

  24. Merge cells? a) Combine cells into one b) Delete table c) Print d) Save PDF

  25. Template file type often? a) .dotx b) .mp4 c) .exe d) .zip only

  26. Ctrl+P in Word? a) Print b) Paste c) Paragraph d) Picture

  27. Bullets in list from? a) Home tab b) Insert Chart c) References only d) Mailings only

  28. Page margins set in? a) Layout tab b) Insert c) Review d) Draw

  29. Columns in document? a) Layout → Columns b) Home font c) Insert table only d) None

  30. Thesaurus shortcut? a) Shift+F7 b) F1 c) F12 d) Ctrl+T

  31. Word count in? a) Review tab b) Insert c) Draw d) Help only

  32. Clear formatting? a) Home → Clear All Formatting b) Delete key only c) BIOS d) Task Manager

  33. Section break for? a) Different headers in sections b) Virus c) Email d) CPU upgrade

  34. Ctrl+Z in Word? a) Undo b) Redo c) Zoom d) Cut

  35. Hyperlink insert shortcut? a) Ctrl+K b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+L d) Ctrl+U

  36. Footnote used for? a) Reference at page bottom b) Header text c) Footer page number only d) Mail merge

  37. Resume created using? a) Word/Writer templates b) Excel only c) Access only d) Paint only

  38. Mail merge data source often? a) Excel list b) Printer c) Scanner d) CPU

  39. Print preview before? a) Printing to check layout b) Deleting file c) Formatting C: d) Uninstall

  40. Double spacing means? a) 2.0 line spacing b) Two pages c) Two columns always d) Two files

  41. Insert picture from? a) Insert → Pictures b) Review c) Mailings only d) References only

  42. Writer is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Antivirus

  43. MS Word is? a) Word processor b) Spreadsheet c) Database d) Browser

  44. Cut shortcut? a) Ctrl+X b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+V d) Ctrl+Z

  45. Paste shortcut? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+S

  46. Select all? a) Ctrl+A b) Ctrl+S c) Ctrl+P d) Ctrl+F

  47. Red underline in Word often means? a) Spelling error b) Saved file c) Printed d) Merged

  48. Green underline may mean? a) Grammar suggestion b) Virus c) Network error d) Low ink

  49. Envelopes printed via? a) Mailings / Envelopes b) Insert Chart c) Review d) Draw

  50. Table of Contents needs? a) Heading styles b) Only bold text c) Images only d) Videos only

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Word Processing.
  2. List five important terms from Word Processing.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Word Processing.
  4. Why is Word Processing important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Word Processing.
  6. Define key concept in Word Processing.
  7. List five important terms from Word Processing.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Word Processing.
  9. Why is Word Processing important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Word Processing.
  11. Define key concept in Word Processing.
  12. List five important terms from Word Processing.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Word Processing.
  14. Why is Word Processing important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Word Processing.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Word Processing study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Word Processing study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

chapters.heading: Practical Lab Pack — Word
chapters.description: NITA CCA · Foundation modules (shared study pack)
chapters.contentHtml:
Word Processing — Practical Lab Pack (CCC)

Software: MS Word 2016+ or LibreOffice Writer
Total labs: 6 · Time: ~12 hours practical

Lab 1: Basic Document (2 hrs)

  1. Open blank document
  2. Title "My Introduction" — Heading 1, centre align
  3. Three paragraphs about yourself — justify, 1.5 line spacing
  4. Save as Lab1_Introduction.docx (or .odt)

Check: Font size 12 body, title larger, file opens correctly

Lab 2: Formatting (2 hrs)

  1. Open Lab 1 file
  2. Bold your name; italic your school/institute; underline email
  3. Apply bullet list of 5 hobbies
  4. Numbered list of 3 goals for CCC course
  5. Save As PDF

Lab 3: Table (2 hrs)

  1. Create 4×4 table — Student Details
  2. Rows: Name, Address, Phone, Email (Field | Detail)
  3. Shade header row; borders all cells
  4. Save as Lab3_StudentTable.docx

Lab 4: Formal Letter (2 hrs)

  1. Create one-page application letter to Principal/Director
  2. Subject: Admission to DCA/CCC course
  3. Date top right; proper greeting; signature line
  4. Header: institute name; footer: page number
  5. Save + print preview

Lab 5: Resume (2 hrs)

  1. Use template or create from scratch
  2. Sections: Objective, Education, Skills, Contact
  3. Insert small photo (optional) with wrap text
  4. Export PDF for submission practice

Lab 6: Mail Merge (2 hrs)

  1. Create Excel list: 5 names + cities
  2. Word/Writer mail merge letter: "Dear «Name», Greetings from «City»..."
  3. Preview and generate 5 letters
  4. Print or save merged PDF

Trainer Sign-off Sheet

Lab Student Name Date Initial
1
2
3
4
5
6

chapters.contentHtml:
Answer Key — Module 03: Word Processing

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Word file extension? a) .docx b) .xlsx c) .pptx d) .accdb Answer: a) .docx Explanation: .docx is the correct answer. .xlsx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  2. LibreOffice Writer extension? a) .odt b) .docx only c) .xls d) .ppt Answer: a) .odt Explanation: .odt is the correct answer. .docx only and .xls are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  3. Bold shortcut? a) Ctrl+B b) Ctrl+I c) Ctrl+U d) Ctrl+P Answer: a) Ctrl+B Explanation: Ctrl+B applies bold formatting to selected text in Word, Writer, and many other apps.

  4. Italic shortcut? a) Ctrl+I b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+S d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+I Explanation: Ctrl+I — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  5. Underline shortcut? a) Ctrl+U b) Ctrl+B c) Ctrl+K d) Ctrl+L Answer: a) Ctrl+U Explanation: Ctrl+U — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  6. Save shortcut? a) Ctrl+S b) Ctrl+P c) Ctrl+O d) F5 Answer: a) Ctrl+S Explanation: Use Ctrl+S for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  7. Page break shortcut? a) Ctrl+Enter b) Enter c) Shift+Enter d) Alt+Enter Answer: a) Ctrl+Enter Explanation: Ctrl+Enter — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  8. Mail merge used for? a) Bulk personalized letters b) Virus scan c) Database query d) Print driver Answer: a) Bulk personalized letters Explanation: Bulk personalized letters — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  9. Mail merge tab in Word? a) Mailings b) Insert only c) View only d) Draw only Answer: a) Mailings Explanation: Mailings — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  10. Justify alignment? a) Both edges straight b) Center only c) Left only d) Right only Answer: a) Both edges straight Explanation: Both edges straight — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  11. Find shortcut? a) Ctrl+F b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+G d) Ctrl+N Answer: a) Ctrl+F Explanation: Use Ctrl+F for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  12. Replace shortcut? a) Ctrl+H b) Ctrl+R c) Ctrl+J d) Ctrl+K Answer: a) Ctrl+H Explanation: Use Ctrl+H for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  13. Spell check key? a) F7 b) F5 c) F2 d) F12 Answer: a) F7 Explanation: F7 is the correct answer. F5 and F2 are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  14. Insert table from? a) Insert tab b) Review only c) Mailings only d) Help Answer: a) Insert tab Explanation: Insert tab is the correct answer. Review only and Mailings only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  15. Header/footer from? a) Insert tab b) Home font c) View only d) Draw Answer: a) Insert tab Explanation: Insert tab is the correct answer. Home font and View only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  16. Page numbers inserted via? a) Insert → Page Number b) Home Bold c) Review d) File Close Answer: a) Insert → Page Number Explanation: Insert → Page Number is the correct answer. Home Bold and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  17. Export Word to PDF? a) Save As PDF b) Cannot export c) Print only paper d) Insert PDF Answer: a) Save As PDF Explanation: Save As PDF is the correct answer. Cannot export and Print only paper are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  18. Portrait orientation? a) Vertical page b) Horizontal page c) Circular d) None Answer: a) Vertical page Explanation: Vertical page is the correct answer. Horizontal page and Circular are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  19. Landscape orientation? a) Horizontal page b) Vertical page c) Square only d) None Answer: a) Horizontal page Explanation: Horizontal page is the correct answer. Vertical page and Square only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  20. Wrap text for image? a) Picture Format → Wrap Text b) Home font color c) Review d) Mailings Answer: a) Picture Format → Wrap Text Explanation: Picture Format → Wrap Text is the correct answer. Home font color and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  21. Heading styles used for? a) Document structure b) Virus removal c) Internet d) CPU Answer: a) Document structure Explanation: Document structure is the main purpose or function described in the question. Virus removal and Internet are not the right choices here.

  22. Line spacing changed in? a) Home → Paragraph b) Insert Picture c) Review d) Draw Answer: a) Home → Paragraph Explanation: Home → Paragraph is the correct answer. Insert Picture and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  23. Tab in table moves to? a) Next cell b) New document c) Internet d) Shutdown Answer: a) Next cell Explanation: Next cell is the correct answer. New document and Internet are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  24. Merge cells? a) Combine cells into one b) Delete table c) Print d) Save PDF Answer: a) Combine cells into one Explanation: Combine cells into one is the correct answer. Delete table and Print are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  25. Template file type often? a) .dotx b) .mp4 c) .exe d) .zip only Answer: a) .dotx Explanation: .dotx is the correct answer. .mp4 and .exe are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  26. Ctrl+P in Word? a) Print b) Paste c) Paragraph d) Picture Answer: a) Print Explanation: Use Print for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  27. Bullets in list from? a) Home tab b) Insert Chart c) References only d) Mailings only Answer: a) Home tab Explanation: Home tab is the correct answer. Insert Chart and References only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  28. Page margins set in? a) Layout tab b) Insert c) Review d) Draw Answer: a) Layout tab Explanation: Layout tab is the correct answer. Insert and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  29. Columns in document? a) Layout → Columns b) Home font c) Insert table only d) None Answer: a) Layout → Columns Explanation: Layout → Columns is the correct answer. Home font and Insert table only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  30. Thesaurus shortcut? a) Shift+F7 b) F1 c) F12 d) Ctrl+T Answer: a) Shift+F7 Explanation: Use Shift+F7 for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  31. Word count in? a) Review tab b) Insert c) Draw d) Help only Answer: a) Review tab Explanation: Review tab is the correct answer. Insert and Draw are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  32. Clear formatting? a) Home → Clear All Formatting b) Delete key only c) BIOS d) Task Manager Answer: a) Home → Clear All Formatting Explanation: Home → Clear All Formatting is the correct answer. Delete key only and BIOS are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  33. Section break for? a) Different headers in sections b) Virus c) Email d) CPU upgrade Answer: a) Different headers in sections Explanation: Different headers in sections is the correct answer. Virus and Email are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  34. Ctrl+Z in Word? a) Undo b) Redo c) Zoom d) Cut Answer: a) Undo Explanation: Use Undo for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  35. Hyperlink insert shortcut? a) Ctrl+K b) Ctrl+H c) Ctrl+L d) Ctrl+U Answer: a) Ctrl+K Explanation: Use Ctrl+K for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  36. Footnote used for? a) Reference at page bottom b) Header text c) Footer page number only d) Mail merge Answer: a) Reference at page bottom Explanation: Reference at page bottom is the main purpose or function described in the question. Header text and Footer page number only are not the right choices here.

  37. Resume created using? a) Word/Writer templates b) Excel only c) Access only d) Paint only Answer: a) Word/Writer templates Explanation: Word/Writer templates is the correct answer. Excel only and Access only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  38. Mail merge data source often? a) Excel list b) Printer c) Scanner d) CPU Answer: a) Excel list Explanation: Excel list — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.

  39. Print preview before? a) Printing to check layout b) Deleting file c) Formatting C: d) Uninstall Answer: a) Printing to check layout Explanation: Printing to check layout is the correct answer. Deleting file and Formatting C: are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  40. Double spacing means? a) 2.0 line spacing b) Two pages c) Two columns always d) Two files Answer: a) 2.0 line spacing Explanation: 2.0 line spacing is the correct answer. Two pages and Two columns always are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  41. Insert picture from? a) Insert → Pictures b) Review c) Mailings only d) References only Answer: a) Insert → Pictures Explanation: Insert → Pictures is the correct answer. Review and Mailings only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  42. Writer is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows OS c) Chrome d) Antivirus Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows OS and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  43. MS Word is? a) Word processor b) Spreadsheet c) Database d) Browser Answer: a) Word processor Explanation: Word processor is the correct answer. Spreadsheet and Database are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  44. Cut shortcut? a) Ctrl+X b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+V d) Ctrl+Z Answer: a) Ctrl+X Explanation: Use Ctrl+X for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  45. Paste shortcut? a) Ctrl+V b) Ctrl+C c) Ctrl+X d) Ctrl+S Answer: a) Ctrl+V Explanation: Use Ctrl+V for this action. Practice the shortcut on your keyboard during lab sessions so it becomes automatic.

  46. Select all? a) Ctrl+A b) Ctrl+S c) Ctrl+P d) Ctrl+F Answer: a) Ctrl+A Explanation: Ctrl+A is the correct answer. Ctrl+S and Ctrl+P are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  47. Red underline in Word often means? a) Spelling error b) Saved file c) Printed d) Merged Answer: a) Spelling error Explanation: Spelling error is the correct answer. Saved file and Printed are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  48. Green underline may mean? a) Grammar suggestion b) Virus c) Network error d) Low ink Answer: a) Grammar suggestion Explanation: Grammar suggestion is the correct answer. Virus and Network error are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  49. Envelopes printed via? a) Mailings / Envelopes b) Insert Chart c) Review d) Draw Answer: a) Mailings / Envelopes Explanation: Mailings / Envelopes is the correct answer. Insert Chart and Review are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  50. Table of Contents needs? a) Heading styles b) Only bold text c) Images only d) Videos only Answer: a) Heading styles Explanation: Heading styles is the correct answer. Only bold text and Images only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

Short Answer Key

  1. Define key concept in Word Processing. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  2. List five important terms from Word Processing. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  3. Name three practical tasks for Word Processing. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  4. Why is Word Processing important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  5. Give two exam tips for Word Processing. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  6. Define key concept in Word Processing. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  7. List five important terms from Word Processing. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  8. Name three practical tasks for Word Processing. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  9. Why is Word Processing important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  10. Give two exam tips for Word Processing. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

  11. Define key concept in Word Processing. Ans: See study notes summary section.

  12. List five important terms from Word Processing. Ans: Refer module study notes vocabulary.

  13. Name three practical tasks for Word Processing. Ans: See Section C practical checklist.

  14. Why is Word Processing important for CCC exam? Ans: CCC tests digital literacy including this module.

  15. Give two exam tips for Word Processing. Ans: Practice MCQs daily; do hands-on lab tasks.

Practical Checklist Key

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Word Processing study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  2. Complete lab task 2 from Word Processing study notes. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  6. Peer review exercise in lab. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

  8. Document steps in notebook. Verify: Trainer initials & date when demonstrated.

chapters.title: Study Notes
chapters.heading: Study Notes
chapters.description: NITA CCA · Foundation modules (shared study pack)
chapters.contentHtml:
Module 4: Spreadsheet with MS Excel (12 Hours)

Course: CCC 90-Hour DCA | Module duration: 12 hours
Primary software: Microsoft Excel
Alternative reference: LibreOffice Calc as an open-source equivalent

Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Explain what a spreadsheet is and why MS Excel is used in offices, schools, banks, and businesses.
  • Navigate workbook, worksheet, rows, columns, cells, ranges, formula bar, name box, and sheet tabs.
  • Enter and format text, numbers, currency, dates, time, percentage, and custom formats.
  • Write formulas and use functions such as SUM, AVERAGE, MIN, MAX, COUNT, COUNTA, ROUND, IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF, TODAY, CONCAT, AND, and OR.
  • Apply relative, absolute, and mixed cell references using F4.
  • Identify and fix common formula errors such as #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #NAME?, and #REF!.
  • Sort and filter data for reports and analysis.
  • Apply conditional formatting for fail marks, high performers, low stock, and overdue payments.
  • Create and customize column, bar, line, pie, area, and scatter charts.
  • Design professional spreadsheets with headers, borders, freeze panes, data validation, and cell protection.
  • Prepare marksheets, attendance registers, salary sheets, fee reports, inventory sheets, and expense trackers.
  • Build simple Excel dashboards using totals, charts, and KPI-style summaries.
  • Set print area, print titles, page setup, scaling, and PDF export.
  • Use essential MS Excel keyboard shortcuts for faster office work.
  • Connect Excel skills with careers such as data entry operator, MIS executive, accountant, HR executive, and business analyst.

Note: This module focuses on spreadsheet skills. Database tools such as MS Access or LibreOffice Base are not covered here.

1. Introduction to Spreadsheets

What It Is

A spreadsheet is application software used to organize data in rows and columns, perform calculations, analyze information, create charts, and prepare reports. Microsoft Excel is the most widely used spreadsheet program in offices, schools, colleges, banks, shops, hospitals, and government departments.

Why It Is Used

Excel saves time, reduces manual calculation errors, and helps users make quick decisions. Instead of calculating totals on paper, Excel can instantly sum hundreds of rows, find averages, apply conditions, sort data, and create visual charts.

Where It Is Used

  • Schools and colleges: marksheets, attendance, fee records.
  • Banks: transaction summaries, loan calculations, reports.
  • Offices: salary sheets, expense tracking, inventory lists.
  • Shops: billing summaries, stock tracking, sales reports.
  • Government: data collection, monitoring, and reporting.
  • Freelancing: data entry, MIS reports, client spreadsheets.

MS Excel Method

  1. Click Start.
  2. Search Excel.
  3. Open Microsoft Excel.
  4. Click Blank workbook.
  5. Press Ctrl + S and save as my-first-workbook.xlsx.

Calc Equivalent

LibreOffice Calc is an open-source spreadsheet. It uses similar formulas and saves in .ods format. Most Excel formulas work in Calc, but menus and some advanced features differ.

Real-World Example

A school clerk uses Excel to enter marks of 40 students, calculate totals and percentages, identify pass/fail students, and print a marksheet for the principal.

Practice Exercise

Create a blank Excel workbook, type your name in cell A1, save the file, close Excel, reopen it, and verify the data.

2. Spreadsheet Fundamentals

Understanding Excel terminology is essential for practical work and exams.

Workbook

What It Is

A workbook is the entire Excel file you create, open, save, and share.

Why It Is Used

It holds one or more worksheets together in one file.

Real-World Example

Class10_Marksheet_2026.xlsx is one workbook containing sheets for different subjects or terms.

Worksheet

What It Is

A worksheet (or sheet) is a single tab inside a workbook where data is entered in rows and columns.

Why It Is Used

Different sheets can store different data such as January sales, February sales, or subject-wise marks.

MS Excel Method

  • View sheet tabs at the bottom: Sheet1, Sheet2, Sheet3.
  • Add sheet: click + or press Shift + F11.
  • Rename sheet: double-click tab name.

Row

What It Is

A row is a horizontal line of cells numbered 1, 2, 3, 4…

Use

Each student, employee, or product is often entered in one row.

Column

What It Is

A column is a vertical line of cells labeled A, B, C… Z, AA, AB…

Use

Each field such as Name, Roll No, Marks, Salary is usually placed in one column.

Cell

What It Is

A cell is the intersection of one row and one column.

Example

Cell D10 is where column D and row 10 meet.

Range

What It Is

A range is a group of selected cells forming a rectangle.

Examples

  • A1:C10 — from A1 to C10.
  • B2:B20 — column B from row 2 to 20.

Active Cell

What It Is

The active cell is the currently selected cell with a dark border.

Why It Matters

Formulas and formatting apply to the active cell or selected range.

Cell Address

What It Is

A cell address identifies a cell using column letter + row number.

Examples

  • A1
  • C15
  • F100

Formula Bar

What It Is

The formula bar appears below the ribbon and shows the content of the active cell, including formulas.

Why It Is Used

It helps view and edit long formulas easily.

Name Box

What It Is

The name box shows the address of the active cell and can be used to jump to a cell quickly.

Example

Type D15 in the name box and press Enter to go to that cell.

Sheet Tabs

What It Is

Sheet tabs appear at the bottom and let you switch between worksheets in the same workbook.

Status Bar

What It Is

The status bar at the bottom shows information such as Average, Count, and Sum of selected cells.

Visual Layout Diagram

+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Title Bar | Quick Access Toolbar | Ribbon Tabs           |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Name Box [A1] | Formula Bar [=SUM(B2:B10)]                |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|      |  A   |  B   |  C   |  D   |                       |
|------+------+------+------+------|                       |
|  1   | Name | Roll | Math | Total|  <- Row 1              |
|  2   | Rina | 101  |  85  |      |                       |
|  3   | Amit | 102  |  72  |      |                       |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
| Sheet1 | Sheet2 | Sheet3 | +                              |
| Status Bar: Average | Count | Sum                         |
+----------------------------------------------------------+

Fill Handle

What It Is

The fill handle is a small square at the bottom-right corner of the active cell.

MS Excel Method

  1. Enter 1 in A1 and 2 in A2.
  2. Select A1:A2.
  3. Drag the fill handle down to extend the series.

Use Cases

  • Copy formulas.
  • Extend number series.
  • Extend month names.

Practice Exercise

Label a diagram with workbook, worksheet, row, column, cell, range, formula bar, name box, and sheet tabs.

3. Data Entry and Formatting

Text Data

What It Is

Text data includes names, roll numbers stored as text, cities, and descriptions.

Why It Is Used

Text identifies records and labels columns.

MS Excel Method

  1. Click a cell.
  2. Type text such as Rasmita Sahu.
  3. Press Enter.

Real-World Example

Student names, employee IDs, department names.

Numeric Data

What It Is

Numeric data includes marks, quantities, amounts, and scores.

MS Excel Method

Enter numbers directly. Excel aligns numbers to the right by default.

Real-World Example

Marks: 85, Salary: 25000, Quantity: 120.

Currency

What It Is

Currency format displays amounts with currency symbol.

MS Excel Method

  1. Select cells.
  2. Home -> Number -> Currency or Accounting.
  3. Or press Ctrl + 1 -> Currency.

Real-World Example

₹ 25,000 salary, ₹ 1,250 fee, ₹ 45,000 sales.

Dates

What It Is

Date values represent calendar dates.

MS Excel Method

  1. Enter date such as 15-08-2026.
  2. Home -> Number -> Short Date.
  3. Or Format Cells -> Date.

Real-World Example

Joining date, fee due date, exam date.

Time

What It Is

Time values represent clock time.

Real-World Example

Attendance in-time, shift start time.

Percentage

What It Is

Percentage shows part of a whole out of 100.

MS Excel Method

  1. Enter decimal or fraction.
  2. Home -> Number -> Percentage.
  3. Or use formula: =B2/C2*100.

Real-World Example

85% attendance, 72% marks, 15% discount.

Scientific Numbers

What It Is

Scientific format displays very large or very small numbers in compact form.

Use

Advanced science and engineering data.

Custom Formats

What It Is

Custom formats let users control how values appear without changing actual values.

MS Excel Method

  1. Select cells.
  2. Press Ctrl + 1.
  3. Choose Custom.
  4. Apply format code if required.

Format Cells Dialog (Ctrl + 1)

Tab Use
Number General, Number, Currency, Date, Percentage
Alignment Horizontal, vertical, wrap text, merge
Font Font style, size, bold, color
Border Table borders
Fill Background color
Protection Lock/hide cells

Common Formatting Tools

Tool Use
Bold Headings
Borders Tables
Merge & Center Titles
Wrap Text Long text in one cell
Increase/Decrease Decimal Money and percentages

MS Excel Method — Professional Table Formatting

  1. Enter headings in row 1.
  2. Apply bold to headings.
  3. Add borders.
  4. Apply currency or number format.
  5. Freeze top row if needed.

Calc Equivalent

Calc uses Format -> Cells or Ctrl + 1 with similar tabs and options.

Practice Exercise

Create a table with columns: Name, Salary (₹), Joining Date, Attendance %. Format each column correctly.

4. Essential MS Excel Shortcuts

Workbook Management

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + N New workbook
Ctrl + O Open workbook
Ctrl + S Save workbook
F12 Save As
Ctrl + P Print / Print Preview
Ctrl + W Close workbook

Navigation

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + Arrow Keys Jump to edge of data region
Ctrl + Home Go to cell A1
Ctrl + End Go to last used cell
Page Up Move up one screen
Page Down Move down one screen
Arrow Keys Move one cell
Tab Move right
Enter Move down

Editing

Shortcut Use
F2 Edit active cell
Delete Clear cell content
Ctrl + Z Undo
Ctrl + Y Redo
Ctrl + C Copy
Ctrl + X Cut
Ctrl + V Paste

Selection

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + A Select all
Shift + Arrow Keys Extend selection
Ctrl + Shift + Arrow Keys Select to edge of data

Formatting

Shortcut Use
Ctrl + 1 Format Cells dialog
Ctrl + B Bold
Ctrl + I Italic
Ctrl + U Underline

Formula Shortcuts

Shortcut Use
Alt + = AutoSum
F4 Toggle absolute/relative reference
Ctrl + ` Show formulas in worksheet
Ctrl + Shift + U Expand formula bar in some versions

Shortcut Drills

Drill 1

Create a workbook using Ctrl + N, enter data, save with Ctrl + S, and open Format Cells with Ctrl + 1.

Drill 2

Enter numbers in A1:A5 and use Alt + = in A6 to calculate total.

Drill 3

Write a formula with B1 reference, press F4 repeatedly to see $B$1, B$1, $B1, and B1.

Drill 4

Press Ctrl + ` to show formulas, then press again to hide them.

5. Formulas and Functions — Deep Dive

Every Excel formula begins with =.

SUM Function

What It Is

Adds values together.

Why It Is Used

To calculate totals quickly.

Excel Syntax

=SUM(A1:A10)

Arguments

Range of numbers or cell references.

Real Example

=SUM(B2:D2) adds three subject marks for one student.

Common Errors

  • Forgetting =.
  • Selecting wrong range.
  • Including text cells accidentally.

Practice

Create a marksheet and calculate total marks for 10 students.

AVERAGE Function

Purpose

Finds mean value.

Syntax

=AVERAGE(B2:B20)

Real Example

Average marks of a class.

Practice

Calculate average marks for each subject.

MIN Function

Purpose

Finds smallest value.

Syntax

=MIN(B2:B20)

Real Example

Lowest mark in a subject.

MAX Function

Purpose

Finds highest value.

Syntax

=MAX(B2:B20)

Real Example

Highest sales amount in a month.

COUNT Function

Purpose

Counts cells containing numbers only.

Syntax

=COUNT(B2:B20)

Real Example

Count how many numeric marks are entered.

COUNTA Function

Purpose

Counts non-empty cells.

Syntax

=COUNTA(A2:A20)

Real Example

Count how many students have names entered.

ROUND Function

Purpose

Rounds a number to specified decimal places.

Syntax

=ROUND(A2,2)

Real Example

Round percentage to 2 decimal places.

IF Function

Purpose

Returns one value if condition is true, another if false.

Syntax

=IF(logical_test, value_if_true, value_if_false)

Real Example

=IF(C2>=40,"Pass","Fail")

Nested IF Example

=IF(B2>=60,"First",IF(B2>=45,"Second","Fail"))

Practice

Add Pass/Fail and Grade columns to a marksheet.

COUNTIF Function

Purpose

Counts cells that meet a condition.

Syntax

=COUNTIF(range, criteria)

Real Example

=COUNTIF(B2:B20,">=40") counts passes.

Practice

Count number of students with attendance above 75%.

SUMIF Function

Purpose

Sums values that meet a condition.

Syntax

=SUMIF(range, criteria, sum_range)

Real Example

=SUMIF(A2:A20,"Delhi",B2:B20) sums sales for Delhi only.

TODAY Function

Purpose

Returns current date.

Syntax

=TODAY()

Real Example

Attendance sheet date, invoice date.

CONCAT Function

Purpose

Joins text from multiple cells.

Syntax

=CONCAT(A2," ",B2) or =A2&" "&B2

Real Example

Combine first name and last name.

AND Function

Purpose

Returns TRUE only if all conditions are true.

Syntax

=AND(C2>=40,D2>=40,E2>=40)

Real Example

Check pass in all three subjects.

OR Function

Purpose

Returns TRUE if any condition is true.

Syntax

=OR(C2>=40,D2>=40,E2>=40)

Real Example

Pass if student passes in at least one subject under special rule.

Important Formula Summary Table

Function Syntax Use
SUM =SUM(B2:B10) Total
AVERAGE =AVERAGE(B2:B10) Mean
MIN =MIN(B2:B10) Lowest
MAX =MAX(B2:B10) Highest
COUNT =COUNT(B2:B10) Count numbers
COUNTA =COUNTA(A2:A10) Count non-empty
ROUND =ROUND(A2,2) Round decimals
IF =IF(C2>=40,"Pass","Fail") Condition
COUNTIF =COUNTIF(B2:B20,">=40") Conditional count
SUMIF =SUMIF(A:A,"A",B:B) Conditional sum
TODAY =TODAY() Current date
CONCAT =A2&" "&B2 Join text
AND =AND(C2>=40,D2>=40) All true
OR =OR(C2>=40,D2>=40) Any true

6. Formula Troubleshooting

Excel shows error messages when a formula cannot be calculated correctly.

#DIV/0!

Why It Occurs

Division by zero.

Example

=A1/B1 when B1 is 0.

Fix

Check denominator; use IF to avoid divide-by-zero:

=IF(B1=0,"NA",A1/B1)

#VALUE!

Why It Occurs

Wrong data type used in calculation.

Example

Adding text to numbers incorrectly.

Fix

Check cell values; convert text numbers to numeric format.

#NAME?

Why It Occurs

Excel does not recognize function or range name.

Example

=SUMA1:A10) or misspelled function.

Fix

Check spelling; ensure function name is valid.

#REF!

Why It Occurs

Invalid cell reference, often after deleting rows/columns.

Fix

Correct formula references.

Why It Occurs

Column is too narrow to display value.

Fix

Widen column or reduce decimal places.

Best Practices

  • Always start formulas with =.
  • Check cell references carefully.
  • Use parentheses properly in IF.
  • Preview formulas with Ctrl + `.
  • Do not type commas in Indian number style inside formulas unless formatted properly.

7. Relative, Absolute, and Mixed References

Relative Reference

What It Is

A reference that changes when copied to another cell.

Example

=B2*C2 copied down becomes =B3*C3, =B4*C4.

Use

Row-wise calculations such as marks × weight for each student.

Absolute Reference

What It Is

A reference that remains fixed when copied.

Example

=$B$1

MS Excel Method

Type reference and press F4 until $ signs appear on both column and row.

Real Office Example

  • GST rate in B1 applied to all amounts.
  • Tax rate in one cell.
  • Discount rate in one cell.
  • Salary calculation using fixed allowance in one cell.

Mixed Reference

What It Is

Only part of reference is fixed.

Examples

  • =$B1 — column fixed, row changes.
  • =B$1 — row fixed, column changes.

Visual Example

If tax rate is in cell F1:

Amount in C2: =B2*$F$1
Copy down: C3 = B3*$F$1
Copy right: D2 = B2*$F$1

Practice Exercise

Place GST rate 18% in F1. Calculate GST for 10 invoice amounts using absolute reference.

Calc Equivalent

F4 works similarly in Calc for toggling absolute references.

8. Professional Spreadsheet Design

Titles

Use clear workbook and sheet titles such as Student Marksheet March 2026.

Headers

Row 1 should contain bold column headings: Name, Roll No, Marks, Total, Grade.

Borders

Use borders to separate table data from other content.

Colors

Use light colors for headers; avoid too many bright colors in official reports.

Alignment

  • Text: left.
  • Numbers: right or center.
  • Headings: center.

Freeze Panes

Purpose

Keeps headings visible while scrolling.

MS Excel Method

View -> Freeze Panes -> Freeze Top Row.

Wrap Text

Home -> Wrap Text for long headings or addresses.

Merge and Center

Use for report title only; avoid merging data cells unnecessarily.

Data Validation

Purpose

Restricts data entry to valid values.

MS Excel Method

Data -> Data Validation -> List / Whole Number / Date.

Example

Allow only Pass/Fail in a column.

Cell Protection

Protect sheet structure after formatting so users edit only input cells.

Best Practices

  • One row = one record.
  • One column = one field.
  • No blank rows inside table.
  • Use consistent formats.
  • Keep formulas in separate columns from raw data when possible.

9. Sorting and Filtering

Single Column Sort

MS Excel Method

  1. Select data including headers.
  2. Data -> Sort.
  3. Choose column and A to Z or Z to A.

Multi-Level Sort

Sort by Class first, then by Total marks descending.

Text, Number, and Date Sorting

Excel sorts according to data type automatically if data is entered correctly.

Custom Sort

Used for non-alphabetical order such as S, A, B, C grade sequence.

Filters

MS Excel Method

  1. Select data.
  2. Data -> Filter.
  3. Click dropdown arrow in header.
  4. Choose values to display.

Search Filters

Use filter search box to find names quickly.

Advanced Filter Concepts

Used for complex criteria such as marks > 40 and attendance > 75%.

Real Office Scenarios

  • Sort employees by salary descending.
  • Filter students who failed in Maths.
  • Filter fee records due this month.
  • Sort inventory by quantity low to high.

Practice Exercise

Sort 10 students by total marks descending and filter students with total > 200.

10. Conditional Formatting — Deep Dive

Conditional formatting automatically changes cell appearance based on rules.

Highlight Cell Rules

MS Excel Method

Home -> Conditional Formatting -> Highlight Cell Rules.

Examples

  • Greater than 75 -> green.
  • Less than 40 -> red.
  • Between 40 and 75 -> yellow.

Top/Bottom Rules

Highlight top 10 marks or bottom 10 performers.

Data Bars

Visual bars inside cells showing relative values.

Color Scales

Heat-map style coloring from low to high values.

Icon Sets

Traffic lights, arrows, stars for quick visual analysis.

Real Examples

Scenario Rule
Fail students Marks < 40 -> red fill
High performers Marks > 75 -> green fill
Low inventory Quantity < 10 -> red text
Overdue payments Due date passed -> orange fill

Practice Exercise

Apply conditional formatting to marks column: fail red, distinction green.

Calc Equivalent

Format -> Conditional in Calc provides similar rule-based formatting.

11. Charts and Graphs

Why Charts Matter

Charts turn numbers into visuals so managers, teachers, and decision-makers can understand trends quickly.

Choosing the Right Chart

Chart Best For Limitation
Column Compare categories Too many categories look crowded
Bar Long category names Less common for time trends
Line Trends over time Not ideal for parts of whole
Pie Parts of a whole Too many slices become unclear
Area Volume over time Can look cluttered
Scatter Relationship between two variables Needs numeric pairs on both axes

Column Chart

Purpose

Compare marks, sales, or expenses across categories.

MS Excel Method

  1. Select data with labels.
  2. Insert -> Column Chart.
  3. Add chart title and axis labels.

Bar Chart

Similar to column chart but horizontal.

Line Chart

Used for monthly sales, attendance trend, or growth over time.

Pie Chart

Used for pass/fail proportion or expense category share.

Area Chart

Shows cumulative or volume trends.

Scatter Chart (Introduction)

Shows relationship between two numeric variables such as study hours vs marks.

Practice Exercise

Create a column chart of subject-wise marks and a pie chart of pass/fail count.

13. Mini Dashboard Introduction

A simple dashboard summarizes key information on one sheet.

Beginner Dashboard Components

  • Total sales.
  • Total expenses.
  • Net profit.
  • Pass count / fail count.
  • Attendance percentage.
  • Charts for trends.
  • Conditional formatting for alerts.

Simple Example

Create a sheet with:

  • Cell B2: Total Sales =SUM(C2:C20)
  • Cell B3: Total Expenses =SUM(D2:D20)
  • Cell B4: Profit =B2-B3

  • Red highlight for low stock items.

Build a school performance dashboard showing total students, pass count, fail count, average marks, and one chart.

14. Data Analysis Basics

Businesses rely on Excel because it supports quick analysis without advanced software.

Totals

Use SUM for revenue, expenses, marks, stock.

Averages

Use AVERAGE for class performance, monthly sales, attendance.

Trends

Use line charts and month-wise data.

Comparisons

Compare subject marks, branch sales, or employee performance.

Summaries

Use pivot-style thinking even in beginner sheets: totals by category, count by status.

Decision Making

Managers use Excel to decide reorder levels, identify weak students, and monitor budgets.

15. Print and Reporting

Print Area

MS Excel Method

Select range -> Page Layout -> Print Area -> Set Print Area.

Print Titles

Repeat header row on every printed page: Page Layout -> Print Titles -> Rows to repeat at top.

Page Setup

Set margins, orientation, and paper size.

Headers and Footers

Insert page number, date, file name, and institute name.

Orientation

Portrait for tall lists; Landscape for wide tables.

Scaling

Fit sheet on one page when needed.

PDF Export

File -> Save As -> PDF.

Print Preview

Ctrl + P before final printing.

Professional Reporting Standards

  • Include title and date.
  • Use clear headers.
  • Align numbers properly.
  • Print preview before printing.
  • Export PDF for submission.

16. Multiple Sheets and 3D Formulas

Add, Rename, Delete Sheets

  • Add: click + or Shift + F11.
  • Rename: double-click tab.
  • Delete: right-click tab -> Delete.

Link Between Sheets

Reference another sheet: =Sheet2!A1

3D Formula

Sum same cell across sheets:

=SUM(Jan:Mar!B2)

Use Case

Quarterly sales totals across Jan, Feb, Mar sheets.

Most Important Formulas

=SUM(), =AVERAGE(), =MIN(), =MAX(), =COUNT(), =COUNTA(), =ROUND(), =IF(), =COUNTIF(), =SUMIF(), =TODAY()

Most Important Shortcuts

Ctrl + N, Ctrl + O, Ctrl + S, Ctrl + P, Ctrl + 1, Ctrl + B, Alt + =, F4, F2, Ctrl + `, Ctrl + Z, Ctrl + Y

Common Formula Errors

#DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #NAME?, #REF!, #####

Important Exam Facts

  • Cell address = column letter + row number.
  • Every formula starts with =.
  • $B$1 is absolute reference.
  • F4 toggles reference type.
  • COUNT counts numbers only.
  • COUNTA counts non-empty cells.
  • IF checks condition.
  • Ctrl + 1 opens Format Cells.
  • Workbook contains worksheets.
  • .xlsx is Excel file extension.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is the difference between workbook and worksheet?
A: Workbook is the file; worksheet is one tab inside it.

Q: What is AutoSum?
A: Quick tool to insert SUM formula, shortcut Alt + =.

Q: Why use charts?
A: To visualize data and understand trends quickly.

  8. Create a column chart of totals.
  9. Set print area and repeat header row.
  10. Export worksheet as PDF.

G. Case Studies (5)

Case Study 1: Class Marksheet

A teacher has marks of 30 students in three subjects and needs total, average, grade, and a chart.

Questions:

  1. Which Excel features are required?
  2. Which functions will you use?
  3. How will you identify fail students?
  4. Which chart is suitable?

Case Study 2: Salary Sheet

An office must calculate net salary for 20 employees using fixed allowance in one cell.

Questions:

  1. Which reference type is needed for allowance cell?
  2. Which functions are useful?
  3. Why use absolute reference?
  4. How will you format currency?

Case Study 3: Inventory Alert

A shop wants to highlight items with stock below 10.

Questions:

  1. Which feature highlights low stock?
  2. Which column should be checked?
  3. Why is sorting useful?
  4. How can a chart help?

Case Study 4: Fee Due Report

A school wants to see only students with pending fees.

Questions:

  1. Which feature shows selected records?
  2. Which formula finds total due amount?
  3. Why use date format?
  4. Why export PDF?

Case Study 5: Monthly Expense Analysis

A student tracks monthly expenses by category.

Questions:

  1. Which function finds total expense?
  2. Which chart shows category share?
  3. Why use budget vs actual columns?
  4. How can dashboard cells help?

H. Mini Projects (5)

  1. School Marksheet System with formulas, grades, chart, and PDF export.
  2. Personal Monthly Budget with categories, totals, and pie chart.
  3. Inventory Management Sheet with low-stock conditional formatting.
  4. Employee Salary Register with absolute reference for allowance/tax.
  5. Sales Dashboard with totals, KPI cells, and one chart.

Answer Keys

MCQ Answers

1-a | 2-a | 3-b | 4-b | 5-a | 6-a | 7-a | 8-b | 9-a | 10-a | 11-a | 12-a | 13-a | 14-c | 15-a | 16-a | 17-a | 18-a | 19-a | 20-a | 21-a | 22-a | 23-a | 24-a | 25-a | 26-a | 27-a | 28-a | 29-a | 30-a

True/False Answers

1-True | 2-False | 3-True | 4-True | 5-True | 6-True | 7-True | 8-False | 9-True | 10-True | 11-True | 12-True | 13-True | 14-False | 15-True | 16-True | 17-True | 18-True | 19-True | 20-False

Fill in the Blanks Answers

  1. spreadsheet
  2. worksheets
  3. letter
  4. =
  5. Ctrl + S
  6. Ctrl + 1
  7. SUM
  8. AVERAGE
  9. COUNT
  10. COUNTA
  11. $B$1
  12. F4
  13. #DIV/0!
  14. Filter
  15. .xlsx

Match the Following Answers

1-b | 2-c | 3-e | 4-a | 5-d | 6-f | 7-g | 8-h | 9-i | 10-j

Case Study Model Answers

  1. Use SUM, AVERAGE, IF, sort, filter, conditional formatting, chart; identify fail using IF or conditional formatting; column chart suitable.
  2. Use absolute reference for allowance; SUM and arithmetic formulas; absolute reference keeps allowance fixed; currency format for salary columns.
  3. Conditional formatting; stock quantity column; sorting helps see lowest stock first; chart shows stock comparison.
  4. Filter; SUM for due amounts; date format for due dates; PDF for professional sharing.
  5. SUM; pie chart; budget vs actual helps control spending; dashboard totals give quick summary.

Final Self-Check

  • I can explain workbook, worksheet, cell, range, and active cell.
  • I can enter and format text, numbers, currency, dates, and percentages.
  • I can use SUM, AVERAGE, MIN, MAX, COUNT, COUNTA, ROUND, IF, COUNTIF, SUMIF, TODAY, CONCAT, AND, and OR.
  • I can use relative, absolute, and mixed references with F4.
  • I can identify and fix common formula errors.
  • I can sort, filter, and apply conditional formatting.
  • I can create column, bar, line, pie, and area charts.
  • I can prepare marksheets, salary sheets, fee reports, and inventory sheets.
  • I can set print area, print titles, and export PDF.
  • I can answer Excel practical, viva, interview, and exam questions confidently.

chapters.contentHtml:
Practice — Module 04: Spreadsheet

Section A — MCQ (50 questions)

  1. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

  2. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word

  3. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @

  4. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print

  5. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join

  6. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date

  7. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text

  8. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets

  9. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart

  10. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM

  11. IF function checks? a) Condition true/false b) Text color c) Print area d) Chart type

  12. Pie chart best for? a) Parts of whole b) Time trend only c) Large database d) Email

  13. Column chart for? a) Comparing categories b) Parts of whole only c) Email d) Boot

  14. Line chart for? a) Trend over time b) Pie parts c) Database keys d) Virus scan

  15. Sort data? a) Data tab → Sort b) Insert Chart c) Review d) Draw

  16. Filter adds? a) Dropdown arrows in headers b) Virus c) RAM d) Printer

  17. Conditional formatting? a) Format by rules b) Delete rows c) Install OS d) Send email

  18. Print area sets? a) Range to print b) RAM size c) CPU d) Monitor

  19. Print titles repeat? a) Header rows each page b) Footer only c) Delete data d) Chart only

  20. Workbook contains? a) Worksheets b) Only one cell c) Only charts d) Emails

  21. Worksheet is? a) Tab/sheet in workbook b) CPU c) Printer d) Browser

  22. Fill handle used to? a) Copy formula/extend series b) Print c) Shutdown d) Email

  23. Ctrl+1 opens? a) Format Cells b) Save c) Print d) New book

  24. Merge cells? a) Combine into one cell b) Delete sheet c) Scan d) Boot

  25. COUNTIF counts? a) Cells meeting criteria b) All sheets c) Printers d) Folders

  26. SUMIF adds? a) Values matching criteria b) All text c) Charts d) BIOS

  27. TODAY() returns? a) Current date b) Sum c) Average d) Text hello

  28. ROUND(3.456,2) gives? a) 3.46 b) 3.45 c) 3.5 always wrong d) 3456

  29. Percentage formula example? a) =B2/C2*100 b) =B2+C2 c) =SUM(text) d) =IF color

  30. Remove Duplicates? a) Data tab b) Home font c) Insert d) View

  31. 3D formula SUM(Sheet1:Sheet3!B2)? a) Sum B2 across sheets b) One cell typo c) Chart d) Email

  32. Link to Sheet2 A1? a) =Sheet2!A1 b) =A1Sheet2 c) =LINK(A1) d) =WEB(A1)

  33. Relative reference when copied down? a) Adjusts row/column b) Stays fixed always c) Deletes d) Prints

  34. F4 in Excel toggles? a) Absolute/relative refs b) Save c) Print d) Close

  35. Gridlines print option in? a) Page Layout b) Insert c) Review d) Mailings

  36. Chart inserted from? a) Insert tab b) File Close c) BIOS d) Task Manager

  37. Legend in chart shows? a) Series labels b) CPU c) RAM d) Password

  38. #DIV/0! error means?

a) Divide by zero b) Success c) Saved d) Printed

  1. Currency format shows? a) ₹ or $ amounts b) Only text c) Only dates d) Charts only

  2. Bar chart similar to? a) Column chart horizontal b) Pie only c) Line only d) Email

  3. AutoSum button does? a) Quick SUM formula b) Average only c) Print d) Save PDF

  4. Freeze panes keeps visible? a) Header/label rows while scroll b) CPU c) Printer d) Desktop

  5. Data validation restricts? a) Allowed cell input b) Internet c) OS install d) Email

  6. Concatenate joins? a) Text strings b) Sheets c) Printers d) Folders

  7. Ctrl+S in Excel? a) Save workbook b) Sum c) Sort d) Print

  8. Active cell shown in? a) Name box / formula bar b) Taskbar only c) BIOS d) Printer

  9. Shift+F11 adds? a) New worksheet b) New workbook always c) Chart d) Email

  10. Calc compatible with? a) Excel formats mostly b) Only video c) Only audio d) Only PDF edit

  11. Sorting Z to A is? a) Descending b) Ascending c) Random d) Print order

  12. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt

Section B — Short Answer (15)

  1. Define key concept in Spreadsheet.
  2. List five important terms from Spreadsheet.
  3. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet.
  4. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam?
  5. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet.
  6. Define key concept in Spreadsheet.
  7. List five important terms from Spreadsheet.
  8. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet.
  9. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam?
  10. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet.
  11. Define key concept in Spreadsheet.
  12. List five important terms from Spreadsheet.
  13. Name three practical tasks for Spreadsheet.
  14. Why is Spreadsheet important for CCC exam?
  15. Give two exam tips for Spreadsheet.

Section C — Practical (8)

  1. Complete lab task 1 from Spreadsheet study notes.
  2. Complete lab task 2 from Spreadsheet study notes.
  3. Demonstrate skill to trainer on PC.
  4. Screenshot or checklist signed by trainer.
  5. Revise wrong MCQs from practice test.
  6. Peer review exercise in lab.
  7. Timed 10-minute drill on key shortcuts.
  8. Document steps in notebook.

chapters.title: Practical Lab Pack — Excel
chapters.heading: Practical Lab Pack — Excel
chapters.description: NITA CCA · Foundation modules (shared study pack)
chapters.contentHtml:
Spreadsheet — Practical Lab Pack (CCC)

Software: MS Excel or LibreOffice Calc
Total labs: 6 · Time: ~12 hours practical

Lab 1: Data Entry (2 hrs)

  1. Create workbook CCC_Marks.xlsx
  2. Columns: Name, Roll, Sub1, Sub2, Sub3
  3. Enter 8 students with sample marks (0–100)
  4. Format header row bold, centre

Lab 2: Formulas (2 hrs)

  1. Column Total = sum of 3 subjects
  2. Column Average = average of 3 subjects
  3. Column Result = IF average >= 40, "Pass", else "Fail"
  4. Verify by changing one mark — formulas update

Lab 3: Functions (2 hrs)

  1. Below table calculate:
    • Highest marks (MAX)
    • Lowest marks (MIN)
    • Class average (AVERAGE)
    • Count pass students (COUNTIF)
  2. Label each result cell

Lab 4: Sort & Filter (1.5 hrs)

  1. Sort table by Total descending
  2. Enable filter — show only Pass students
  3. Filter one subject marks > 75

Lab 5: Conditional Formatting (1.5 hrs)

  1. Highlight marks < 40 in red
  2. Highlight marks >= 75 in green
  3. Data bars on Total column (optional)

Lab 6: Chart (3 hrs)

  1. Column chart — student names vs Total marks
  2. Pie chart — Pass vs Fail count
  3. Add chart titles and axis labels
  4. Set print area; repeat header row; print preview

Trainer Sign-off Sheet

Lab Student Name Date Initial
1–6

Answer Key — Module 04: Spreadsheet

MCQ Answer Key

  1. Excel extension? a) .xlsx b) .docx c) .pptx d) .odt Answer: a) .xlsx Explanation: .xlsx is the correct answer. .docx and .pptx are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  2. Calc is part of? a) LibreOffice b) Windows c) Chrome d) Word Answer: a) LibreOffice Explanation: LibreOffice is the correct answer. Windows and Chrome are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  3. Formula starts with? a) = b) + c) # d) @ Answer: a) = Explanation: Every Excel/Calc formula must begin with = (equals sign), e.g. =SUM(A1:A10). Without it, text is stored as plain text.

  4. SUM() used for? a) Addition/total b) Average c) Count text d) Print Answer: a) Addition/total Explanation: SUM() adds numbers in the selected range — the most common function for totals in spreadsheets.

  5. AVERAGE() returns? a) Mean value b) Largest c) Smallest d) Text join Answer: a) Mean value Explanation: Mean value is the correct answer. Largest and Smallest are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  6. MAX() returns? a) Largest value b) Smallest c) Count d) Date Answer: a) Largest value Explanation: Largest value is the correct answer. Smallest and Count are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  7. MIN() returns? a) Smallest value b) Largest c) Sum d) Average text Answer: a) Smallest value Explanation: Smallest value is the correct answer. Largest and Sum are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  8. COUNT() counts? a) Numeric cells b) All text c) Blank only d) Sheets Answer: a) Numeric cells Explanation: Numeric cells is the correct answer. All text and Blank only are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  9. Cell A1 is? a) Column A row 1 b) Row A column 1 c) Invalid d) Chart Answer: a) Column A row 1 Explanation: Column A row 1 is the correct answer. Row A column 1 and Invalid are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  10. Absolute reference? a) $A$1 b) A1 only always c) #REF d) SUM Answer: a) $A$1 Explanation: $A$1 is the correct answer. A1 only always and #REF are not the right choices here. This is an important CCC exam point — memorise it.

  14. Line chart for? a) Trend over time b) Pie parts c) Database keys d) Virus scan Answer: a) Trend over time Explanation: Trend over time — this is the correct Office feature, shortcut, or chart type for the task described.
